definition.en: Consciousness, yours and mine, is quite independent of both time and space.
definition.zh: 意识，你我的，完全独立于时间和空间。

definition.en: My consciousness is an attribute to be used by me. This applies to each of the readers of this book, even though the knowledge may be hidden. Soul or entity, then, is more than consciousness.
definition.zh: 我的意识是我可用的一个属性。这适用于本书的每一位读者，即使这知识可能被隐藏。因此，灵魂或存有，超越了意识。

definition.en: All consciousness, including your own, is highly mobile.
definition.zh: 所有意识，包括你自己的，都是高度流动的。

definition.en: Your consciousness is a part of an infinitely original creative process.
definition.zh: 你的意识是一个无限原创的创造过程的一部分。

definition.en: The body consciousness is not 'unconscious,' but for working purposes in your terms, [the body] possesses its own system of consciousness that to some extent, now (underlined), is separated from what you think of as your own normal consciousness.
definition.zh: 身体意识并非“无意识”，但为了在你术语中的运作目的，[身体]拥有它自己的意识系统，在某种程度上，现在（强调），与你认为的正常意识是分离的。

explanation: 意识是一种高度流动、独立于时空的本质。它并非固定于身体或大脑，而是可以从肉体中脱离，在睡眠或特殊状态下探索其他现实。日常清醒意识只是意识的一种聚焦模式，其背后是更广阔、更具创造力的意识场域。身体本身也拥有自己的意识体系，这是内在自我赋予的、与日常意识平行运作的智能。意识具有心灵感应和超视觉能力，但在清醒时往往被自我过滤掉。意识的基本单位（CU）通过转化为电磁能量单元（EE）进入物质现实。灵魂或存有并非等同于意识，而是使用意识作为工具的存在。真正的意识状态始终充满创造力，所谓的“无意识”其实也是意识，只是不被自我所承认。意识的本质是变化和不可预测的，正是这种不可预测性为所有有序现实提供了可能。

quotes.text: From the "chaotic" bed of your dreams springs your ordered daily organized action. In your reality, the behavior of your consciousness and of your molecules are highly connected. Your type of consciousness presupposes a molecular consciousness, and your kind of consciousness is inherent in molecular consciousness -- inherent within your system, but not basically predictable. Predictability is simply another word for significance. Unpredictability, looking at itself in a variety of different fashions, finds certain portions of itself significant, and forms certain orders, or ordered sequences, about itself. In one of our very early sessions, I told you that you perceive from a vast field only certain data that you find meaningful. That data could only arise from the bed of unpredictability. Only unpredictability can provide the greatest source of probable orders.
quotes.translation: 从你梦境那“混沌”的温床中，涌现出你井然有序的日常行动。在你的实相里，你意识的运作与你分子的运作高度相连。你的意识类型预设了一种分子意识，而你的意识类型内在于分子意识之中——内在于你的系统之内，但基本上无法预测。可预测性不过是“意义”的另一个说法。不可预测性以各种不同方式审视自身时，发现自身的某些部分具有意义，并围绕自身形成某种秩序或有序序列。在我们早期的一次课中，我告诉过你，你从一片广袤的场域中只感知到你觉得有意义的那部分资料。那些资料只能源自不可预测性的温床。唯有不可预测性才能提供最丰富的可能秩序之源。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1

quotes.text: In some of their own private dreams, many of my readers will have discovered a reality quite as vivid as the normal one, and sometimes more so. These experiences can give you some vague hint of the kind of existence I am speaking of.11 There are also physical apparatuses connected with the hibernation abilities of some animals that can give further clues as to the possible relationships of consciousness to the body. Under certain conditions, for example, consciousness can leave the corporal mechanism while it remains intact -- functioning, but at a maintenance level. When optimum conditions return, then the consciousness reactivates the body. Such behavior is possible not only with the animals. In systems different from your own, there are realities in which physical organisms are activated after what would seem to you to be centuries of inactivity12 -- again, when the conditions are right. To some extent your own life-and-death cycles are simply another aspect of the hibernation principle as you understand it. Your own consciousness leaves the body almost in the same way that messages leap the nerve ends.13 The consciousness is not destroyed in the meantime.
quotes.translation: 在我许多读者的私人梦境中，他们会发现一种与正常实相同样生动、有时甚至更为生动的实相。这些体验能让你对我所说的那种存在形态有一些模糊的暗示。此外，某些动物与冬眠能力相关的生理机制，也能为进一步揭示意识与身体之间可能的关系提供线索。例如，在某些条件下，意识可以离开肉身机制，而肉身机制则保持完好——仍在运作，但处于维持性水平。当最佳条件恢复时，意识便会重新激活身体。这种行为不仅动物可能做到。在与你们不同的系统中，存在着这样的实相：物理有机体在你们看来似乎已沉寂了数个世纪之后，再次被激活——同样，是在条件适宜之时。在某种程度上，你们自身的生死循环，不过是你们所理解的冬眠原理的另一个面向。你们自己的意识离开身体的方式，几乎与信息跃过神经末梢的方式相同。在此期间，意识并未被摧毁。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: Now all consciousness, including your own, is highly mobile. While you focus your attention primarily in your own world, certain portions of your consciousness are always straying. When you are sleeping, then, your consciousness often ventures into other realities, usually in a wandering fashion without tuning itself in to any precise frequencies. Beneath many seemingly chaotic dreams there are often valid experiences in which your consciousness "lights" in another reality, without being attuned to it with the necessary precision that would allow for clear perception. The information cannot be sifted or used effectively and is translated into dream images, as your consciousness returns toward your own home station. Therefore, it has been difficult to achieve any kind of clear picture of such other realities.
quotes.translation: 所有意识——包括你的——都具有高度流动性。当你将注意力主要聚焦于自身世界时，你意识的某些部分始终在游离。因此，当你入睡时，你的意识常会闯入其他实相，通常以漫游方式运作，未将自身调频至精确频率。在许多看似混乱的梦境之下，往往隐藏着有效的体验——你的意识"着陆"于另一实相，却因未以必要精度调频而无法清晰感知。这些信息无法被筛选或有效运用，当你的意识返回自身基地时，便转化为梦境意象。因此，要获得这类其他实相的清晰图景一直困难重重。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: He was filled with wonder as his own consciousness ever-newly came into being. He had not yet covered over that process with the kind of smooth continuity that your own consciousness has now achieved -- so when he thought a thought he was filled with curiosity: Where had it come from? His own consciousness, then, was forever a source of delight, its changing qualities as noticeable and apparent as the changing sky. The relative smoothness of your own consciousness -- in those terms, at least -- was gained at the expense of certain other experiences, therefore, that were possible otherwise. You could not live in your present world of time if your consciousness was as playful, curious, and creative as it was, for [then] time was also experienced far differently.
quotes.translation: 当他的意识不断以崭新形态诞生时，他心中充满惊叹。他尚未像你们现在的意识那样，用平滑的连续性覆盖这个过程——所以当他生起一个念头时，便充满好奇：它从何而来？于是，他的意识永远是喜悦的源泉，其变化的特质如同变幻的天空般显著可见。你们自身意识的相对平滑性——至少就这些术语而言——是以牺牲某些原本可能的其他体验为代价的。若你的意识仍如当初那般嬉戏、好奇且富有创造力，你便无法活在当前的时间世界中，因为（那时）时间也被以截然不同的方式体验。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: So far in our discussion, then, we have an inner self, dwelling primarily in a mental or psychic dimension, dreaming itself into physical form, and finally forming a body consciousness. To that body consciousness the inner self gives "its own body of physical knowledge," the vast reservoir of physical achievement that it has triumphantly produced. (Pause.) The body consciousness is not "unconscious," but for working purposes in your terms, [the body] possesses its own system of consciousness that to some extent, now (underlined), is separated from what you think of as your own normal consciousness. The body's consciousness is hardly to be considered less than your own, or as inferior to that of your inner self, since it represents knowledge from the inner self, and is a part of the inner self's own consciousness--the part delegated to the body.
quotes.translation: 那么，到目前为止在我们的讨论中，我们有一个内在自己，主要栖居于心智或心灵的维度，将自己梦入物质形态，并最终形成身体意识。内在自己赋予那个身体意识"它自身的物质知识体"，即它成功产生的庞大身体成就库。（停顿）身体意识并非"无意识"，但用你们的话来说，为了运作目的，[身体]拥有它自己的意识系统，在某种程度上，现在（强调），与你们认为的自身正常意识相分离。身体意识绝不应被视为不如你们自己的意识，或低于你们内在自己的意识，因为它代表了来自内在自己的知识，并且是内在自己意识的一部分——被指派给身体的那部分。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1

quotes.text: The consciousness of each reader of this book existed before the universe was formed--in parentheses: (in your terms)--but that consciousness was unmanifest. Your closest approximation--and it is an approximation only--of the state of being that existed before the universe was formed is the dream state. (Long pause.) In that state before the beginning, your consciousness existed free of space and time, aware of immense probabilities. This is extremely difficult to verbalize, yet it is very important that such an attempt be made. (Long pause.) Your consciousness is a part of an infinitely original creative process.
quotes.translation: 本书每位读者的意识在宇宙形成之前就已存在——括号内：（以你们的时间观而言）——但那种意识是未显化的。你们最接近宇宙形成前存在状态的概念——且仅是近似概念——是梦境状态。（长停顿。）在那初始前的状态中，你们的意识存在于时空之外，觉知着浩瀚的可能性。这极难用语言表述，但进行这种尝试非常重要。（长停顿。）你们的意识是无限原创性创造过程的一部分。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1

quotes.text: Now your consciousness is telepathic and clairvoyant, for example, even though you may not realize it. In sleep when (whene) you often presume yourself to be unconscious (unconsious) you may be far more conscious than you are now, but simply using abilities of consciousness that you do not accept as real or valid (valed) in waking (waping) life. You therefore shut (shup) them out of your conscious experience (experiencl). Consciousness, yours and mine, is quite independent of both (poth) time and space. And after death you are simply aware of the greater powers of consciousness that exist within you all the time.
quotes.translation: 例如，你们的意识具有心电感应和千里眼的能力，即使你们可能并未意识到这一点。在睡眠中，当你们常以为自己无意识时，你们可能比现在更有意识，只是单纯运用了你们在清醒生活中不承认为真实或有效的意识能力。因此你们将这些能力排除在意识经验之外。意识，无论是我还是你们的，都完全独立于时间和空间。死后你们只是意识到始终存在于你们内在的意识的更大力量。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: We also realize that permanency of form is an illusion, since all consciousness must be in a state of change. We can be, in your terms, in several places at once because we realize the true mobility of consciousness. Now whenever you think emotionally of another person, you send out a counterpart of yourself, beneath the intensity of matter, but a definite form. This form, projecting outward from your own consciousness, completely escapes your egotistical attention. When I think emotionally of someone else, I do the same thing, except that a portion of my consciousness is within the image, and can communicate.
quotes.translation: 我们也认识到形态的恒常性是一种幻相，因为所有意识都必须处于变化状态。以你们的话来说，我们可以同时出现在多个地方，因为我们认识到意识的真正流动性。每当你们带着情感想到另一个人时，你们就会发送出一个自己的对应体，它处于物质强度之下，却是一个明确的形态。这个形态从你们自己的意识向外投射，完全逃脱了你们自我中心的注意。当我带着情感想到别人时，我也做同样的事，只是我的一部分意识存在于这个影像中，并且能够沟通。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: It utilizes numberless methods of perception, and it has at its command many other kinds of consciousness. Your idea of the soul is indeed limited by your three-dimensional concepts. The soul can change the focus of its consciousness, and uses consciousness as you use the eyes in your head. Now in my level of existence I am simply aware of the fact, strange as it may seem, that I am not my consciousness. My consciousness is an attribute to be used by me. This applies to each of the readers of this book, even though the knowledge may be hidden. Soul or entity, then, is more than consciousness.
quotes.translation: 它运用无数种感知方法，并支配着许多其他种类的意识。你们对灵魂的观念确实受到三维概念的限制。灵魂可以改变其意识的焦点，并运用意识就像你们运用头上的眼睛一样。在我存在的层面，我只是意识到一个事实——尽管这听起来可能很奇怪——我不是我的意识。我的意识是我可运用的一个属性。这适用于本书的每一位读者，即使这知识可能被隐藏。因此，灵魂或存有，超越了意识。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: Again, when you are in a state that is not the normal waking one, when you have forsaken this daily self, you are nevertheless conscious and alert. You merely block out the memory from the normal waking ego. So when the attributes of consciousness are given, creativity is largely ignored. It is assigned instead primarily to the unconscious. My point is that the unconscious is conscious. Creativity is one of the most important attributes and aspects of consciousness. We will differentiate between normal ego consciousness then, and consciousness that only appears unconscious to that ego.
quotes.translation: 再次强调，当你处于非正常清醒状态、暂时放下日常自我时，你依然保持清醒与警觉。你只是阻断了正常清醒自我的记忆。因此当人们讨论意识属性时，创造力常被严重忽视，反而被主要归因于无意识。我的观点是：无意识本身具有意识。创造力是意识最重要属性与面向之一。我们将区分正常自我意识，以及仅对自我而言看似无意识的意识。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)

quotes.text: When consciousness is being specified, it always sees itself at the center of its world. All specifications of consciousness and all phenomenal appearances occur when the basic units of consciousness, the CU's, emerge into EE units, and hence into the dimensions of actuality in your terms. Your mainly accepted normal consciousness is within the matter of your body, and through it -- the body -- you view your world. There is nothing to prevent you from viewing your body from a standpoint outside of it, except that you have been taught that consciousness is imprisoned within the flesh.
quotes.translation: 当意识被具体化时，它始终视自身为世界的中心。所有意识的特定化与现象显现，都发生在意识基本单元（CU）转化为电磁能量单元（EE单元），进而进入你们所谓现实维度的过程中。你们主要接纳的正常意识存在于肉体物质中，并通过肉体观察世界。除非你被教导意识囚禁于血肉之躯，否则没有任何事物能阻止你从肉体之外的角度观察自身。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1

quotes.text: Cellular consciousness itself straddles, so to speak, various levels of sleep activity. Various aspects of it come to the forefront at definite times. This consciousness is constant, whether you wake or sleep. It existed before the ego's formation, and in many cases exists after the ego's organization is altered. In sleep cellular consciousness often intrudes into the dream process, appearing in the form of dream images. Cellular consciousness is highly codified in actuality, much more Emotional than visual, and the visual dream images are but translations of inner comprehensions.
quotes.translation: 细胞意识本身横跨睡眠活动的不同层次，其各个面向会在特定时刻凸显。无论你清醒或沉睡，这种意识始终恒定存在。它在自我形成之前就已存在，在许多情况下，当自我组织改变后依然存在。睡眠时，细胞意识常以梦境意象的形式侵入做梦过程。实际上细胞意识高度编码，其情感性远强于视觉性，而视觉梦境意象不过是内在理解的转译。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)

quotes.text: When you are proficient you will not be swept willy-nilly into other stages of consciousness as you sleep, but will be able to understand and direct these activities. Consciousness is an attribute of the soul, a tool that can be turned in many directions. You are not your consciousness. It is something that belongs to you and to the soul. You are learning to use it. To the extent that you understand and utilize the various aspects of consciousness, you will learn to understand your own reality, and the conscious self will truly become conscious.
quotes.translation: 当你熟练时，你不会在睡眠中不由自主地被卷入其他意识阶段，而是能够理解并引导这些活动。意识是灵魂的一种属性，是可以转向许多方向的工具。你不是你的意识。它是属于你和灵魂的东西。你正在学习使用它。你越能理解和运用意识的各种面向，就越能了解自己的实相，而有意识的自我将真正变得有意识。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: The cells combine into other patterns, forming finally into your physical organs. When the whole physical body is constructed, the individual personality- consciousness is again more than the sum of its component parts. It is more than the combined consciousness of its atoms. Here is your creation; this creation, occurring constantly, is as I have said one of the laws of the inner universe. We know now that the consciousness behind each atom and molecule gave physical construction to the atom and molecule. That is, the consciousness came first.
quotes.translation: 细胞组合成其他模式，最终形成你的身体器官。当整个肉体被构建完成时，个体人格-意识再次大于其组成部分的总和。它大于其原子的组合意识。这就是你的创造；这种不断发生的创造，正如我所说，是内在宇宙的法则之一。我们现在知道，每个原子和分子背后的意识赋予了原子和分子物理结构。也就是说，意识先于物质存在。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)

quotes.text: The beginnings of human consciousness, on the other hand, began as soon as multicellular groupings began to form in field patterns of a certain complexity. While there was no specific point of entry as far as human consciousness was concerned, there was a point before which human consciousness as such did not exist. Self-consciousness did exist. The consciousness of being human in your terms was fully developed in the caveman, but--and I cannot emphasize this enough--the human conception was alive in the fish.
quotes.translation: 人类意识的萌芽，另一方面，始于多细胞组合开始在特定复杂度的场域模式中形成之时。虽然就人类意识而言并无特定的切入点，但在某个时间点之前，人类意识本身并不存在。自我意识确实存在。在你们看来，成为人类的意识在穴居人身上已完全发展，但——我无论如何强调都不为过——人类的概念在鱼类身上就已活跃存在。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)

quotes.text: Since the conscious ego is composed of the gestalt molecular consciousness, then when it leaves the physical body it takes the molecular consciousness with it, and this molecular consciousness is therefore present to aid in future construction. However, since you must deal with physical laws while dwelling upon the physical plane, you cannot dispense entirely with the physical constructions, but in such extensions of consciousness you must utilize other physical molecules and atoms.
quotes.translation: 由于有意识自我由分子意识的完形所组成，因此当它离开肉体时，会带着分子意识一同离去，此分子意识因而存在于场以协助未来建构。然而，由于你们栖身物质层面时必须遵循物质法则，你们无法完全舍弃物质建构，但在这类意识扩展中，你们必须运用其他物理分子与原子。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)

quotes.text: The characteristics of consciousness are the same (seme) whether you are in a body or outside of one. The peaks and valleys of consciousness that I mentioned exist to some degree in all consciousness despite the form adopted (adepted) after death (deth). The nature of your consciousness is no different basically (bascially) than it is now, though you may not be aware of many of its characteristics.
quotes.translation: 不论你身处肉身之内或之外，意识的特性都是相同的（seme）。我所提及的意识之高峰与低谷，在某种程度上存在于所有意识中，无论死亡（deth）后采取何种形式（adepted）。你的意识本质基本上（bascially）与现在并无不同，尽管你可能尚未察觉其诸多特性。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: We will resume dictation. (Pause; one of many.) Consciousness has many characteristics, some of course known to you. Many of the characteristics of consciousness, however, are not so apparent, since presently you largely use your own consciousness in such a way that its perceptions appear in quite other than "natural" guises. You are aware of your own consciousness, in other words, through the medium of your own physical mechanism. You are not nearly as aware of your own consciousness when it is not operating primarily through the mediumship of the body, as it does in out-of-body states and some dissociated conditions.
quotes.translation: 我们将继续口述。（停顿；多次之一。）意识具有许多特性，其中一些你自然知晓。然而，意识的许多特性并不那么显而易见，因为目前你主要以一种方式运用自身意识，使其感知以远非"自然"的面貌呈现。换言之，你是通过自身物理机制的媒介来觉察自身意识的。当你的意识并非主要通过身体的媒介作用运作时——例如在出体状态和某些解离状态下——你对其觉察程度便远不及此。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: When you are born, you understand that you have a new consciousness. You explore its ramifications. It is your primary evidence that you exist in flesh. Basically, each person must confront the experience of reality through a direct encounter with it. This encounter takes place through the use of the physical senses, of course, as they are used to perceive and interpret physical data. The very utilization of those senses, however, is dependent upon the nature of your consciousness itself, and that consciousness is aware of its power and action through the exercise of its own properties.
quotes.translation: 当你出生时，你明白自己拥有一个新的意识。你探索它的分支。它是你存在于肉身的主要证据。基本上，每个人都必须通过直接面对经验来体验实相。这种面对当然是通过使用物质感官来进行的，因为它们被用来感知和解读物质资料。然而，这些感官的运用本身取决于你意识的性质，而那个意识通过行使自己的属性来觉察自身的力量与行动。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1

quotes.text: Now, the future is also present in cellular consciousness. The ego, again, simply censors dreams from the cellular consciousness level when they deal with time that is not yet physical in your terms. Cellular consciousness is usually considered as simply a repository for past knowledge having to do with personal or racial existence. Because of the spacious present however, cellular consciousness also contains blueprints of the future.
quotes.translation: 现在，未来也存在于细胞意识中。当梦境涉及在你们看来尚未物质化的时间时，自我再次只是从细胞意识层面审查这些梦境。细胞意识通常被认为仅仅是储存与个人或种族存在相关的过去知识的仓库。然而，由于广袤的当下，细胞意识也包含未来的蓝图。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)

quotes.text: "You" presently have a once-centered consciousness, in that "you" close off from your experience these other stages of consciousness in which other portions of your entire identity are intimately involved. These other stages of consciousness create their own realities as you create your own. The realities are, therefore, byproducts of consciousness itself. If you could become aware of these, they might appear to be other places to you, rather than realms or fields of different kinds of activities. If you probe into these realms you will be forced to perceive them with the root assumptions of your own system, translating feelings of warmth and comfort, for example, into images of warm shelters or buildings, or feelings of fear into images of demons.
quotes.translation: 你目前拥有一个以单一为中心的“意识”，因为你从自己的经验中“关闭”了其他那些你整体身份的其他部分密切参与的意识阶段。这些其他阶段的意识创造它们自己的实相，就像你创造你自己的实相一样。因此，实相是意识本身的副产品。如果你能意识到它们，它们对你而言可能看起来像是其他地方，而不是不同活动的领域或场域。如果你探究这些领域，你将被迫用你自己体系的基本假设来感知它们，例如，将温暖和舒适的感觉转化为温暖庇护所或建筑物的意象，或将恐惧的感觉转化为恶魔的意象。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: (11:08.) There are even now in your species a number of different kinds of consciousness; different in that the physical life-situation is qualitatively experienced in ways that are not native to you in your culture; different in that the entire fabric of meaning, interpretation, experience, and life itself is "alien" to the kind of experience with which you are familiar. This does not mean that such differences occur as the result of cultural backgrounds or situations, for some such individuals exist within your own culture, and some with your kind of consciousness exist in cultures where they are a minority. I am simply saying that on your earth now there are species of consciousness, though that is probably not the best term. You have been so obsessed with exterior differences, especially of color and nationality, that you have completely ignored these other far more important variations in the form that consciousness takes in relation to physical life within your race -- the race of man.
quotes.translation: (11:08分) 在你们人类中，现在甚至存在着许多不同种类的意识；其不同之处在于，物质生命情境被以你们文化中不熟悉的方式定性体验；不同之处在于，意义、诠释、体验和生命本身的整个结构，对你们所熟悉的那种体验来说是"陌生的"。这并不意味着这些差异是文化背景或情境的结果，因为有些这样的个体存在于你们自己的文化中，而有些具有你们这种意识的个体则存在于他们属于少数的文化中。我只是说，在你们的地球上现在存在着意识的物种，尽管这可能不是最好的术语。你们如此痴迷于外在差异，尤其是肤色和国籍，以至于完全忽略了这些更重要的变化——意识在你们种族（人类种族）中与物质生命相关的形式。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1

quotes.text: Give us a moment ... There are inner structures within matter. These are swirls of energy. They have more purposes than one. The structures are formed by organizations of consciousness, or CU's. You have the most intimate knowledge of the nature of a cell, for example, or of an atom. They compose your flesh. There is, in certain terms, a continuum of consciousness there of which your present physical life is a part. You are in certain kinds of communication and communion with your own cells, and at certain levels of consciousness you know this. A true physicist would learn to reach that level of consciousness at will. There were pictures drawn of cellular structures long before any technological methods of seeing them were available, in your terms.
quotes.translation: 给我们一点时间……物质内部存在着内在结构。这些是能量的漩涡。它们有不止一个目的。这些结构由意识单位（CU）的组织形成。例如，你们对细胞或原子的本质有着最亲密的了解。它们构成了你们的肉体。从某种角度来说，那里存在着意识的连续体，你们当前的物质生命是其中的一部分。你们与自己的细胞处于某种沟通和交融中，并且在某些意识层面上你们知道这一点。一个真正的物理学家会学会随意达到那个意识层面。在你们看来，早在任何观察细胞结构的技术方法出现之前，就已经有人画出了细胞结构的图画。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1

quotes.text: The creative act is your closest experience to direct cognition. While your consciousness thinks of itself in physical terms, whether you are living or dead, then you will still largely utilize thinking patterns with which you are familiar. Your consciousness is cellularly attuned in life, in that it perceives its own reality through cellular function that forms the bodily apparatus. The psyche is larger than that physically attuned consciousness, however. It is the larger context in which you exist. It is intertwined with your own reality as you think of it. On those occasions when you are able to alter your focus momentarily, then the psyche's greater experiences come into play. You are able to at least sense your existence apart from its cellular orientation. The experience, however, is circular, and therefore very difficult to verbalize or to organize into your normal patterns of information.
quotes.translation: 创造行为是你最接近直接认知的体验。当你的意识仍以物质形态看待自身时（无论你处于生者或死者状态），你仍会大量运用熟悉的思维模式。你的意识在生命状态中与细胞调频，通过构成身体机能的细胞功能感知自身实相。然而，心灵远大于这种物质调频的意识。它是你存在的更大脉络，与你所认知的自身实相交织。当你偶尔能暂时转换焦点时，心灵更宏大的体验便会发挥作用。你至少能感知到脱离细胞导向的自身存在。但这种体验是循环性的，因此极难用语言表述或纳入你惯常的信息模式。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche

quotes.text: These are creative distortions on your part, directly related to specializations of consciousness that cut you off from the greater concourse existing at other levels between the species and the land. Again, consciousness everywhere pervades the universe, and is aware of all conditions. The balance of nature upon your planet is no chance occurrence, but the result of constant, instant computations on the part of each most minute consciousness, whether it forms part of a rock, a person, an animal, a plant. Each invisibly "holds space together," whatever its station. This is a cooperative venture. Your own consciousness has its particular unique qualities, in that like other comparatively long-lived species, you associate your identity with your form far more rigidly. Other kinds of consciousness "leap in and out of forms" with greatest leeway. There is a biological understanding that exists, for example, when one animal kills another one for food. The consciousness of the prey leaves its body under the impetus of a kind of stimulus unknown to you.
quotes.translation: 这些是你们特有的创造性扭曲，直接源于意识的专门化——这种专门化使你们与物种及土地之间其他层面存在的更大交融隔绝。再次强调，意识无处不在，遍及宇宙，知晓一切状态。你们星球上的自然平衡绝非偶然，而是每个最微小的意识（无论构成岩石、人类、动物或植物）持续进行即时计算的结果。每个意识都无形中"维系着空间结构"，无论其处于何种位置。这是一项合作事业。你们的意识具有独特品质，如同其他相对长寿的物种，你们将身份与形态的关联绑定得更为严格。其他类型的意识则能以最大自由度"跃入跃出形态"。例如，当动物为食物猎杀同类时，存在着一种生物层面的理解。猎物的意识会在某种你们未知的刺激驱动下离开躯体。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche

quotes.text: For some time, in your terms, the sleepwalkers remained more or less at that level of activity, and for many centuries they used the surface of the earth as a kind of background for other activity.Their real life was what you would now call the dreaming one. They worked mentally while asleep, constructing in their individual minds and in their joint mental endeavors (long pause) all of the dazzling images that would later become a mental reservoir from which men could draw. In that multidimensional array, consciousness mentally learned to form itself into EE units, atoms and molecules, electrons and chromosomes. It mentally formed the patterns through which all physical life could flow. The world then came into physical existence. Those units of consciousness are indestructible and vitalized, regardless of the forms they take, and while men's forms were dream images, consciousness spun forms into physical material.
quotes.translation: 按你们的时间来说，有一段时期，梦游者或多或少停留在那种活动层次上，许多世纪以来，他们把地球表面当作其他活动的背景。他们真实的生活就是你们现在所谓的梦境生活。他们在睡眠中进行心智工作，在各自的头脑里以及共同的心智努力中（长停顿）构建出所有那些耀眼夺目的意象，这些意象后来成为人类可以汲取的心智宝库。在那多维的阵列中，意识在心智上学会了将自己塑造成EE单位、原子和分子、电子和染色体。它心智上形成了所有物理生命得以流经的模式。世界于是进入了物理存在。那些意识单位是不可毁灭且充满活力的，无论它们采取何种形式，而当人类的形体还是梦境意象时，意识便将形态纺入了物理物质。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1

quotes.text: (9:05.) Initially, then, the world was a dream, and what you think of as waking consciousness was the dreaming consciousness. In that regard the earth's entire environment was built mentally, atom by conscious atom--each atom, again, being initially formed by units of consciousness. I said that these units could operate as entities, and as forces, so we are not speaking of a mental mechanics but of entities in the true meaning of the word: entities of unimaginable creative and psychic properties, purposeful fragments propelled from the infinite mind as that mind was filled with the inspiration that gave light to the world. Those entities, in your terms so ancient, left fragments of themselves in trance (underlined), so to speak, that form the rocks and hills, the mountains, the air and the water, and all of the elements that exist on the face of the earth.
quotes.translation: （9:05分）因此，最初世界是一场梦，而你们所谓的清醒意识就是那做梦的意识。就此而言，整个地球环境是心智上构建的，一个意识原子接一个意识原子——再次强调，每个原子最初都是由意识单位形成的。我曾说过这些单位可以作为实体和力量运作，所以我们不是在谈论一种心智机制，而是在谈论这个词真正意义上的实体：具有难以想象的创造性和灵性属性的实体，是从那充满灵感的无限心智中推进出来的有目的性的碎片，那灵感赋予了世界光明。那些实体，按你们的时间来说如此古老，留下了它们自身的碎片处于出神状态（强调），可以说，这些碎片形成了岩石和山丘、山脉、空气和水，以及地球表面上存在的所有元素。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1

quotes.text: Here in a simple manner is a case in point. The molecules and atoms and even smaller particles, all contain their separate consciousness. They form into cells. Now, although the cells maintain individuality and do not lose any of their abilities, in this formation into cells there is actually a pooling of individual consciousness of atoms and molecules into, and to form, an individual cellular consciousness. Here the consciousness of each individual molecule or atom, by this psychic gestalt, gains immeasurably. The combination of individual consciousness into a combination forms a new, enlarged, more powerful cellular consciousness that is capable of much more experience and fulfillment than would be possible for the isolated atom or molecule.
quotes.translation: 这里有个简单的实例：分子、原子乃至更微小的粒子，都各自拥有独立的意识。它们组合成细胞。虽然细胞保持个体性且不丧失任何能力，但在形成细胞的过程中，原子与分子的个体意识实际上会汇聚融合，形成一种新的细胞意识。通过这种心灵完形，每个分子或原子的意识都获得了不可估量的增益。个体意识的组合形成了一种更强大、更具扩展性的细胞意识，其所能体验与实现的范畴，远非孤立的原子或分子所能企及。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)

quotes.text: If you remember that beneath all, each unit of consciousness is aware of the position of each other unit, and that these units form all physical matter, then perhaps you can intuitively follow what I mean, for whatever knowledge man attains, whatever experience any one person accumulates, whatever arts or sciences you produce, all such information is instantly perceived at other levels of activity by each of the other units of consciousness that compose physical reality--whether those units form the shape of a rock, a raindrop, an apple, a cat, a frog or a shoe. Manufactured products are also composed of atoms and molecules that ride upon units of consciousness transformed into EE units, and hence into physical elements.
quotes.translation: 若你记得在一切表象之下，每个意识单位都知晓其他单位的位置，且这些单位构成所有物质形态，那么你或许能凭直觉领会我的意思——无论人类获得何种知识、积累何种经验、创造何种艺术或科学，所有这些信息都会立即被构成物质实相的其他意识单位（无论它们形成岩石、雨滴、苹果、猫、青蛙还是鞋子的形态）在活动的其他层面所感知。人造物品同样由原子与分子构成，而这些原子分子承载着转化为EE单位的意识单位，进而形成物质元素。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1

quotes.text: This frequently happens in the dream state, although such a performance can be achieved in varying altered states of consciousness. At such times consciousness simply puts itself in a different relationship with time and space. The physical body cannot follow, however. It is by altering its own relationship with the physical universe that consciousness can best understand its own properties, and glimpse from another vantage point that physical universe, where it will be seen in a different light. Operating outside the body, consciousness can better perceive the properties of matter. It cannot (intently) experience matter, however, in the same fashion as it can when it is physically oriented.
quotes.translation: 这种情况经常发生在梦境状态中，尽管这种表现可以在各种变异的意识状态下实现。此时意识只是将自己置于与时空不同的关系中。然而肉体无法跟随。正是通过改变自身与物质宇宙的关系，意识才能最好地理解自身的特性，并从另一个有利角度瞥见那个物质宇宙——在那里它将以不同的光芒被看见。在身体之外运作时，意识能更好地感知物质的特性。但它无法（专注地）以与身体导向时相同的方式体验物质。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: As you have probably supposed by now, there is consciousness in everything. Visible or invisible to you, each fragment of the universe has a consciousness of its own. Pain and pleasure, the strongest aspects of all consciousness, are experienced strongly by every fragment, according to its degree. Differentiation is of course various, and it is in the degree of differentiation that consciousness is different.
quotes.translation: 正如你们现在可能已经推测到的，万物皆有意识。无论你们能否看见，宇宙的每个碎片都有其自身的意识。痛苦与快乐——所有意识最强烈的面向——每个碎片都根据其程度强烈体验着。分化当然各有不同，而意识的差异恰恰在于分化的程度。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)

quotes.text: The qualities of consciousness cannot be elucidated. These exercises will bring you in contact with other kinds of knowing, and acquaint you with different feelings of consciousness that are not familiar. Your consciousness itself will then have a different feel as the exercises are done. Certain questions that you may have asked may be answered in such a state, but not in ways that you can anticipate, nor can you necessarily translate the answers into known terms. The different modes of consciousness with which I hope to acquaint you are not alien, however. They are quite native, again, in dream states, and are always present as alternatives beneath usual awareness.
quotes.translation: 意识的特质无法被阐明。这些练习将使你接触到其他种类的认知方式，并让你熟悉那些不常见的意识感受。当你进行这些练习时，你的意识本身将产生不同的感受。你曾提出的某些问题或许能在这种状态下得到解答，但解答方式绝非你所能预料，你也未必能将答案转化为已知的术语。然而，我希望你熟悉的这些不同意识模式并非异类。它们其实非常自然——再次强调，在梦境状态中——并且始终作为替代选项存在于寻常意识之下。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche

quotes.text: (9:48.) Units of consciousness (CU's), transforming themselves into EE units, formed the environment and all of its inhabitants in the same process, in what you might call a circular manner rather than a serial one. And in those terms, of course, there are only various physical manifestations of consciousness, not a planet and its inhabitants, but an entire gestalt of awareized consciousness. In those terms (underlined), each portion of physically oriented consciousness sees reality and experience from its own privileged viewpoint, about which it seems all else revolves, even though this may involve a larger generalized field than your own, or a smaller one.
quotes.translation: （9:48）意识单位（CU）将自身转化为电磁能量单位（EE单位），以你们可称为循环而非序列的方式，在同一过程中形成了环境及其所有居民。用那些术语来说，当然只有意识的各种物质显化——并非一个行星及其居民，而是整个觉知意识的完形。用那些术语（强调），每个面向物质的意识部分都从其自身特权视角看待实相与经验，仿佛一切皆围绕此视角旋转——尽管这可能涉及比你们自身更广阔或更狭小的普遍场域。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1

quotes.text: "To your way of thinking, some lives are lived in a twinkling (in various systems), and others last for centuries. The perception of consciousness is not limited, however. I have told you, for example, that trees have their own consciousness. The consciousness of a tree is not as specifically focused as your own, yet to all intents and purposes, the tree is conscious of 50 years before its existence, and 50 years hence. Its sense of identity spontaneously goes beyond the change of its own form. It has no ego to cut the 'I' identification short. Creatures without the compartment of the ego can easily follow their own identity beyond any change of form."
quotes.translation: "在你们的认知里，有些生命转瞬即逝（在各种系统中），而另一些则持续数百年。然而，意识的感知并不受限。例如，我曾告诉过你们，树木拥有自己的意识。树木的意识不像你们的意识那样聚焦明确，但就所有实际目的而言，树木能感知到它存在之前的五十年，以及之后的五十年。它的身份认同感自发地超越了自身形态的变化。它没有自我来切断'我'的认同。没有自我这个隔间的生物，能够轻易地跟随自己的身份认同，超越任何形态的变化。"
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: There is communication between these other realities and your own in your dream states, and a constant interaction between both systems. If there is any point where your own consciousness seems to elude you or escape you, or if there is any point where your consciousness seems to end, then these are the points where you have yourselves set up psychological and psychic barriers, and these are precisely those areas that you should explore. Otherwise you feel as if your consciousness is enclosed within your skull, immobile and constricted, and every lost thought or forgotten memory at least symbolically seems like a small death. And such is not the case.
quotes.translation: 在你们的梦境状态中，这些其他实相与你们的实相之间存在沟通，两个系统之间也有持续的互动。如果你们的意识似乎在某个点上令你们难以捉摸或逃逸，或者如果你们的意识似乎在某个点上终结，那么这些正是你们自己设置了心理和心灵屏障的地方，而这些恰恰是你们应该探索的领域。否则，你们会感觉自己的意识被禁锢在头骨内，静止不动且受限，每一个遗失的念头或遗忘的记忆，至少象征性地像一次小小的死亡。但事实并非如此。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: It is theoretically possible, for example, for any of you to disperse your consciousness and become a part of any object in the room -- or to fly apart, to disperse yourself out into space -- without leaving your sense of identity. This is not practical in your terms, yet many of you do it to gain refreshment while you are sleeping. Consciousness by its very characteristics carries the burden of perception. This is the kind of consciousness you are used to thinking of. You cannot imagine it without perception in your terms; and yet consciousness can be vital and alive without your idea of perception. The last part of that sentence is important.
quotes.translation: 理论上来说，例如你们任何人都可以分散自己的意识，成为房间里任何物体的一部分——或者飞散开来，将自己散布到太空中——而不会失去你们的身份感。这在你们的说法中并不实际，但你们许多人在睡眠时确实这样做以获得恢复。意识以其特性承载着感知的重担。这是你们习惯思考的那种意识。在你们的说法中，你们无法想象没有感知的意识；然而意识可以在没有你们所认为的感知的情况下保持活力和生机。那句话的最后一部分很重要。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: This book had no chapters [in order] to further disrupt your accepted notions of what a book should be. There are different kinds of organizations present, however, and in any given section of the book, several levels of consciousness are appealed to at once. (Intently:) The threads of the work are interwoven so that various portions of your consciousness are sent out, so to speak, on separate journeys of thought and imagination. Yet these side trips are also related. They intertwine, not only through the psychic organization that I have given to "Unknown" Reality, but because of the great uniting nature within the consciousness of each reader.
quotes.translation: 这本书没有章节[按顺序]，以进一步打破你们对一本书应该是什么样子的既定观念。然而，这里存在着不同种类的组织，在书的任何特定部分，同时会唤起几个层次的意识。（专注地：）作品的线索交织在一起，以至于你们意识的各个部分，可以说，被派往思想和想象的不同旅程。然而这些旁支旅程也是相互关联的。它们交织在一起，不仅通过我赋予《未知的实相》的心灵组织，还因为每位读者意识内部伟大的统一本质。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: It would seem, therefore, that what you call consciousness might be the result of this combination. That is, it would seem that self-consciousness was the result of this combination. Each cell, however, has its own consciousness, and when I speak of consciousness as being an attribute of cellular life, I speak only for convenience, since actually cellular life is an attribute of consciousness.
quotes.translation: 因此，看起来你们所谓的意识可能是这种组合的结果。也就是说，看起来自我意识是这种组合的结果。然而，每个细胞都有其自己的意识，当我谈到意识是细胞生命的属性时，我只是为了方便起见，因为实际上细胞生命是意识的属性。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)

quotes.text: Your life seems synonymous with your consciousness. Therefore it appears that your knowledge of yourself grows gradually, as your self-consciousness develops from your birth. It appears, furthermore, that your consciousness will meet a death beyond which your self-consciousness will not survive. You may think longingly and with an almost hopeful nostalgia of the religion of your childhood, and remember a system of belief that ensured you of immortality. Yet most of you, my readers, yearn for some private and intimate assurances, and seek for some inner certainty that your own individuality is not curtly dismissed at death.
quotes.translation: 你的生命似乎与你的意识同义。因此，你的自我认知似乎随着自我意识从出生起逐渐发展而逐步增长。此外，你的意识似乎终将面临死亡，而你的自我意识无法超越死亡。你或许会带着近乎怀旧的渴望，追忆童年宗教中确保你永生的信仰体系。然而，我亲爱的读者们，你们大多数人都渴望某种私密而确切的保证，寻求内在的确定性，确信自己的个体性不会在死亡时被草率终结。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche

quotes.text: Consciousness of self involves a consciousness of self within, amid, and as a part of action. ego consciousness, on the other hand, involves a state in which consciousness of self attempts to divorce itself from action, an attempt on the part of consciousness to perceive action as an object. Here we see that ego consciousness, in this attempt, strives to perceive action not only as separate, but to perceive it in such a fashion that it appears to ego that action is not only separate from itself, that is separate from the ego, but that action is initiated by the ego, and a result rather than a cause of ego's own existence.
quotes.translation: 自我意识涉及对内在、处于行动之中并作为行动一部分的自我的觉知。而自我意识则是一种状态，其中自我意识试图与行动分离，是意识试图将行动视为客体的尝试。在此我们看到，自我意识在这种尝试中不仅竭力将行动视为分离的，更以某种方式感知行动，使其在自我看来不仅与自身（即与自我）分离，而且行动是由自我发起的，是自我存在的结果而非原因。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)

quotes.text: The unknown reality: Much of that reality is unknown simply because your beliefs close you off from your own knowledge. The reaches of your own consciousness are not limited. Because you accept the idea of a straight-line movement of time, you cannot see before or after what you think of as your birth or death,* yet your greater consciousness is quite aware of such experience. Ideally it is possible not only to remember "past" lives, but to plan future ones now. In greater terms, all such lives happen at once. Your present neurological structure makes this seem impossible, yet your inner consciousness is not so impeded.
quotes.translation: 未知的实相：这实相的大部分之所以不为人知，仅仅是因为你的信念使你无法触及自己的知识。你意识的疆界并无限制。由于你接受了时间直线运动的观念，你无法看见你所认为的出生或死亡之前或之后的一切，然而你的更大意识却完全知晓这类经验。理想上，你不仅能记住“过去”的转世，还能在当下规划未来的转世。从更广的角度看，所有这些转世都同时发生。你目前的神经结构使这看似不可能，但你的内在意识却不受此阻碍。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: The physical body is therefore an attribute of consciousness. The atoms and molecules possess a self-consciousness to some degree, as well as a generalized consciousness. The self awareness of a human being is partially composed of the combination of self aware individual atoms and molecules that compose it. The generalized consciousness which you call the subconscious, is composed of the combination of generalized consciousness from the individual cells and molecules, but now we come down to the source of the self aware conscious individual behind or within each molecule. Where did it come from to begin with?
quotes.translation: 因此，物质身体是意识的一种属性。原子和分子在某种程度上拥有自我意识，以及一种普遍意识。人类的自我觉知部分由构成它的、具有自我意识的个体原子和分子的组合所组成。你称之为潜意识的普遍意识，则由个体细胞和分子的普遍意识组合而成，但现在我们要追溯到每个分子背后或之内那个具有自我意识的个体意识的源头。它最初从何而来？
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)

quotes.text: The so-called stream of consciousness is simply that -- one small stream of thoughts, images, and impressions -- that is part of a much deeper river of consciousness that represents your own far greater existence and experience. You spend all your time examining this one small stream, so that you become hypnotized by its flow, and entranced by its motion. Simultaneously these other streams of perception and consciousness go by without your notice, yet they are very much a part of you, and they represent quite valid aspects, events, actions, emotions with which you are also involved in other layers of reality.
quotes.translation: 所谓意识流，不过如此——它只是思想、影像和印象的一条小小溪流——属于一条更深广的意识之河的一部分，这条河代表着你自身更宏大的存在与经验。你花费所有时间审视这条小溪流，以至于被它的流动催眠，被它的运动迷住。与此同时，这些其他的感知与意识之流从你身边流过而不被你注意，然而它们却是你的一部分，并且代表着你在其他实相层面也涉入的、相当真实有效的面向、事件、行动和情感。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: There are also what I will call body dreams. No consciousness, to whatever degree, is fully manifested in matter. There is always constant communication between all portions of the body, but when the conscious mind is diverted that activity often increases. Cellular consciousness at its own level then forms a body dream. These do not involve pictures or words, but are rather like the formations of electromagnetic intent, anticipating action to be taken, and these may then serve as initiators of therapeutic dreams, in which "higher" levels of consciousness are psychologically made aware of certain conditions.
quotes.translation: 还有我所谓的身体之梦。无论何种程度的意识，都无法完全显现在物质中。身体各部分之间始终存在持续沟通，但当意识心转移注意力时，这种活动往往增强。细胞意识在其自身层面会形成身体之梦。这些梦境不涉及图像或文字，而更像是电磁意图的成形，预演将要采取的行动，这些随后可能成为疗愈性梦境的启动器——在此类梦境中，"更高"层次的意识会从心理层面觉察到某些状况。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche

quotes.text: Now. Since the identity consciousness is composed of the combined and cooperating generalized consciousness of all the body's atoms and molecules, and the consciousness of the inner ego, these molecular consciousnesses that once or initially found expression in forming their physical construction, no longer do so; but are competent to do so again when the self-consciousness so demands.
quotes.translation: 现在。由于身份意识是由身体所有原子与分子的联合协作的普遍意识，以及内在自我的意识所组成，这些曾一度或最初通过形成其物质结构而获得表达的分子意识，如今已不再如此运作；但当自我意识提出要求时，它们仍具备再次如此运作的能力。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)

quotes.text: The various levels of consciousness discussed here may appear to be very divorced from ordinary waking ones. The divisions are quite arbitrary. These various stages all represent different attributes and directions inherent within your own soul; clues and hints of them, shadows and reflections appear even in the consciousness that you know. Even normal waking consciousness, then, is not innocent of all other traces of existence, or devoid of other kinds of awareness. It is only because you usually use your waking consciousness in limited ways that you do not encounter these clues with any regularity.
quotes.translation: 此处讨论的各个意识层次，可能看似与寻常清醒意识相去甚远。这些划分其实相当武断。这些不同阶段都代表着你灵魂内在固有的不同属性与方向；其线索与暗示、阴影与倒影，甚至在你所知的意识中亦有所显现。因此，即便是正常的清醒意识，也并非全然不沾染其他存在痕迹，或缺乏其他类型的觉知。只是因为你通常以有限方式运用清醒意识，才未能规律性地遇见这些线索。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: You like to think in terms of units and definitions, so even when you consider your own consciousness you think of it as "a thing," or a unit -- an invisible something that might be held in invisible hands perhaps. Instead consciousness is a particular quality of being. Each portion of "it" contains the whole, so theoretically as far as you are concerned, you can leave your body and be in it simultaneously. You are rarely aware of such experiences because you do not believe them possible, and it seems that even consciousness, particularly when individualized, must be in one place or another.
quotes.translation: 你们喜欢以单位和定义来思考，因此即使考虑自己的意识时，也将其视为"一个东西"或一个单位——一种或许能被无形之手握住的隐形之物。然而，意识其实是存在的一种特定品质。"它"的每个部分都包含着整体，所以理论上就你而言，你可以离开身体，同时又存在于身体之中。你很少意识到这类经验，因为你不相信它们可能发生，而且似乎连意识——尤其是当它个体化时——也必须存在于某个地方。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche

quotes.text: Root assumptions are those laws upon which you agree in any system of reality. You agree, for example, upon what objects are physical -- it makes little difference whether they are or not, as long as you agree upon this. Your consciousness belongs in a body. You would not be caught DEAD with your consciousness outside of your body. It is taboo! Now the fact is that your consciousness is not imprisoned within your body; but as long as you believe that it is, again, you will not be caught dead outside of it. And when you are caught dead outside of it, there will be some amazement, indeed.
quotes.translation: 根本假设是你们在任何实相系统中共同认可的那些法则。例如，你们一致同意物体是什么样才算物质——至于它们是否真的是物质并不重要，只要你们同意这一点就行。你们的意识属于身体之内。就算死了，你们也不会让意识跑出身体之外。这是禁忌！事实上，你们的意识并非被囚禁在身体里；但只要你们相信它被囚禁，那么即使死了，你们也不会发现自己存在于身体之外。而当你们真的发现死后意识在身体之外时，确实会大吃一惊。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: This data must be interpreted in such a way that the conscious mind then can give a prediction, say, but also in such a way that its reality exits simultaneously with consciousness, but never so replacing consciousness that the ego grows alarmed. This is often done by the use of symbols which are not immediately apparent to consciousness, and thus are allowed through. Then it is too late for the ego to close its gates against them. But the symbols chosen must be of a nature so that they will become meaningful to consciousness, to as large an extent as possible after emergence.
quotes.translation: 这些资料必须以这样一种方式被诠释：让意识心既能做出预测，同时又能让其实相与意识共存，但绝不能取代意识到让自我警觉的程度。这通常通过使用对意识而言并非立即显明的象征来完成，因此这些象征得以通过。等到自我想要关闭大门阻挡它们时，已经为时已晚。但所选象征的性质必须使其在浮现后，能尽可能对意识产生意义。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)

quotes.text: Your attentions are indeed focused elsewhere. You are in a trance as well as Ruburt is in a trance state now. This is far from unusual. I use you, dear Doctor Instream, only as an example. Consciousness of any kind is merely the direction in which the self looks. I told you this at our brief meeting. Consciousness is the focus, the direction of focus. Your ordinary consciousness is as much a trance state as any trance state induced through hypnotism. Therefore it is nearly impossible to convince a subject in trance that something he does not see exists.
quotes.translation: 你们的注意力确实集中在别处。你们正处于出神状态，就像鲁柏现在处于出神状态一样。这绝非罕见。我亲爱的英斯纯博士，我只是以你为例。任何种类的意识都仅仅是自我所注视的方向。在我们短暂的会面中，我告诉过你这一点。意识是焦点，是注意力的方向。你们平常的意识，与任何通过催眠诱导出的出神状态一样，都是一种出神状态。因此，几乎不可能说服一个处于出神状态的受试者，让他相信他看不见的东西是存在的。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)

quotes.text: Instead, consciousness formed matter. As I have said before, each atom and molecule has its own consciousness. Consciousness and matter and energy are one, but consciousness initiates the transformation of energy into matter. In those terms, the "beginning" of your universe was a triumph in the expansion of consciousness, as it learned to translate itself into physical form. The universe emerged into actuality in the same way (underlined), but to a different degree, that any idea emerges from what you think of as subjectivity into physical expression.
quotes.translation: 相反，是意识形成了物质。正如我以前说过的，每个原子和分子都有自己的意识。意识、物质和能量是一体的，但意识启动了能量转化为物质的过程。用这些术语来说，你们宇宙的“开端”是意识扩张的一次胜利，因为它学会了将自己转化为物质形态。宇宙以同样的方式（强调）进入实相，但程度不同——就像任何想法从你们所谓的主观性中浮现，进入物质表达一样。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1

quotes.text: I have spoken often concerning the importance of changing focus, altering the direction of consciousness. This does not involve a change in consciousness itself, merely a change in the direction in which consciousness is focused. This ability is obviously not only inherent within the human race, but it is used constantly in your ordinary life to some degree. Usually however this involves a rather surface change of focus, as your attentions are directed first upon specific physical areas, and then switched to other specific physical areas.
quotes.translation: 我常谈及改变焦点、调整意识方向的重要性。这并非改变意识本身，而仅是改变意识聚焦的方向。这种能力显然不仅为人类所固有，在日常生活中也常被不同程度地运用。然而通常这仅涉及较为表层的焦点转换——你的注意力先集中于特定物质领域，随后又转向其他特定物质领域。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)

quotes.text: (10:19.) These are called coordination points, where one camouflage merges into the other. Some of these are geographical in your system, but in all cases, a tuning-in of consciousness is a necessary preliminary. Such entries can only be made in an out-of-body condition. Each individual in his dreams has access to the information possessed by the Speakers. There are adjacent states of consciousness that occur within the sleep pattern, that cannot be picked up by your EEG's -- adjacent "corridors" through which your consciousness travels.
quotes.translation: （10:19）这些被称为协调点，是不同伪装系统相互融合之处。在你们的系统中，有些属于地理坐标，但无论如何，意识的调谐都是必要前提。这类入口唯有在出体状态下才能进入。每个人在梦境中都能接触到"代言者"所拥有的信息。睡眠模式中存在相邻的意识状态——那些意识穿行的相邻"走廊"，是脑电图无法捕捉的。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: Now. Priestley puts it somewhat differently but the results are the same. According to him the consciousness, the individual consciousness of time one, becomes something else at physical death, and the consciousness that is part of time two in physical life becomes dominant in the next existence. There is one large difference here between us however, and I believe an important one. Priestley's individual, after death, with his dominant time two consciousness, has available to him what was time one during physical life.
quotes.translation: 现在。普里斯特利的说法略有不同，但结论一致。依他所见，时间一的个体意识在肉体死亡时会转化为其他形态，而肉体生命中属于时间二的那部分意识将在下一世占据主导。然而我们之间存在一个重大分歧——我认为这至关重要。普里斯特利认为，个体死后以其主导的时间二意识，仍能获取生前属于时间一的记忆。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 5 of The Seth Material (Sessions 199-239)

quotes.text: All of these procedures are unnecessary to consciousness itself, however. They are necessary to develop communication between nonphysical consciousness and the physical form which it has adopted. Consciousness, in forming an image, or creating it, then responds to it creatively, setting up frameworks for further creative actions. Consciousness experiences reality directly, but having formed physical matter into a personal image, it must then creatively translate data to that physical brain. It must keep it informed.
quotes.translation: 然而，所有这些程序对意识本身而言都是不必要的。它们对于发展非物性意识与其所采用的物质形态之间的沟通是必需的。意识在形成或创造意象后，会创造性地回应它，为后续的创造性行动建立框架。意识直接体验实相，但既然已将物质塑造成个人意象，它就必须创造性地将数据转译给那个物质大脑，并持续为其提供信息。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)

quotes.text: On the other hand, more complicated organizations of consciousness are necessary to form, enter, and vitalize the more complicated physical structures. All structure is formed by consciousness. Defined in your terms, a fragment is a consciousness not as developed as your own. The living portions of nature are the result of your own creativity, projections and fragments of your own energy; energy that comes to you from All That Is and goes outward from you, forming its own image manifestations as you form yours.
quotes.translation: 另一方面，要形成、进入并活化更复杂的物质结构，就需要更复杂的意识组织。所有结构都由意识形成。用你们的术语来说，片段体是发展程度不及你们自身的意识。自然界中活生生的部分，是你们自身创造力、投射与能量片段的结果；这些能量来自一切万有，从你们身上向外辐射，形成其自身的意象显化，正如你们形成自己的意象一样。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: (10:55.) The fluctuations also follow seasonal changes. Events from any given layer of consciousness are reflected in all other areas, each being actualized according to the characteristics of the given layer. As one dream is like a stone thrown into the pool of dream consciousness, so any act appears in this pool also in its own guise. Alternate focus allows you to perceive the many manifestations of any given act, the true multidimensional reality of a given thought. It enriches the normal consciousness.
quotes.translation: （十点五十五分。）这些波动也随季节变化。任何特定意识层面的事件都会反映在所有其他领域，每个领域都根据其特性被具体化。正如一个梦境如同投入梦意识之池的石子，任何行为也会以自身伪装出现在这池中。交替焦点让你能感知任何特定行为的众多显化——某个念头真正的多维实相。它丰富了正常意识。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: Now: The senses that you use, in a very real manner, create the environment that you perceive. Your physical senses necessitate the perception of a three-dimensional reality. Consciousness is equipped with inner perceptors, however. These are inherent within all consciousness, regardless of its development. These perceptors operate quite independently from those that might be assumed when a given consciousness adopts a specialized form, such as a physical body, in order to operate in a particular system.
quotes.translation: 现在：你们所使用的感官，以非常真实的方式创造了你们所感知的环境。你们的物质感官必然导致对三维实相的感知。然而，意识配备了内在的感知器。这些感知器是所有意识固有的，无论其发展程度如何。当某个意识为了在特定系统中运作而采用特殊形态（如物质身体）时，这些感知器的运作完全独立于可能被假设的那些感知器。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: Again, your own consciousness triumphantly rides above those deaths that you do not recognize as such. In your chosen three-dimensional existence, however, and in those terms, your consciousness finally recognizes a death. From the outside it is nearly impossible to pinpoint that intersection of consciousness and the seeming separation from the body. There is a time when you, as a consciousness, decide that death will happen, when in your terms you no longer bridge the gap of minute deaths not accepted.
quotes.translation: 再次强调，你们自己的意识胜利地凌驾于那些你们不认为是死亡的死亡之上。然而，在你们所选择的三维存在中，用那些术语来说，你们的意识最终会承认一种死亡。从外部来看，几乎不可能精确指出意识与看似脱离身体的那个交汇点。有一个时刻，作为意识的你决定死亡将会发生，那时用你们的说法，你不再弥合那些未被接受的小死亡之间的间隙。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: In many ways you can compare your consciousness as you know it now to a firefly, for while it seems to you that your consciousness is continuous, this is not so. It also flickers off and on, though as we mentioned earlier, it is never completely extinguished. Its focus is not nearly as constant as you suppose, however. So as you are alive in the midst of your own multitudinous small deaths, so though you do not realize it, you are often "dead," even amid the sparkling life of your own consciousness.
quotes.translation: 在许多方面，你可以将你现在所知的意识比作一只萤火虫，因为虽然对你而言意识似乎是连续的，但事实并非如此。它也忽明忽暗地闪烁，不过正如我们先前所提，它从未完全熄灭。然而，它的焦点远不如你所认为的那样恒定。因此，当你活在自己众多微小死亡之中时，尽管你未察觉，你常常是“死”的，即便在你自身意识那闪耀的生命中也是如此。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: Emotion and imagination are being considered as far superior. The displaced powers of consciousness are still being assigned to the unconscious, and great efforts are being made to reach what seem to be normally inaccessible areas of awareness. To this end drugs are utilized, cults set up, and there are methods and training manuals galore. Period. Yet there is nothing basically inaccessible about such "inner knowledge or experience." It can all be quite conscious, and utilized to enrich the reality that you know. The conscious mind is not some prodigal child or poor relative of the self. It can quite freely focus into inner reality when you understand that it can. You, again, have a conscious mind. You can change the focus of your own consciousness.
quotes.translation: 情感和想象力被视为远为优越。意识中被置换的力量仍被归入无意识，人们正付出巨大努力去触及那些看似通常无法触及的意识领域。为此，药物被使用，教派被建立，还有大量方法和训练手册。句号。然而，这种“内在知识或经验”本质上并无不可触及之处。它完全可以是有意识的，并用于丰富你所知的现实。有意识心智并非自我的某个浪子或穷亲戚。当它理解自己能够聚焦于内在实相时，它便能相当自由地做到这一点。再次强调，你拥有一个有意识心智。你可以改变自身意识的焦点。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality

quotes.text: New paragraph. Basically, however, consciousness is freewheeling. Such realities therefore always exist -- in your own psyche -- outside of your "home station," and some portion of your own consciousness is always involved in them. Period. There are bleed-throughs, so to speak, in the form of unofficial perceptions that often occur, or "impossible" events that are seemingly beyond explanation. (Pause.) For now think of your own psyche, which is a consciousized identity, as a kind of "supernatural radio." All of the stations exist at once within the psyche. These do not come through with sound alone, but with all the living paraphernalia of the world. The "you" that you recognize is but one signal on one such station, tuned in to a certain frequency, experiencing that station's overall reality from your own viewpoint -- one that is unique and like no other, and yet contributing to the whole life of the station.
quotes.translation: 新段落。然而，从根本上说，意识是自由运转的。因此，这类实相始终存在于——你自身的心灵中——你的“本台”之外，而你自身意识的某部分也始终参与其中。句号。可以说，存在着以非官方感知形式出现的渗透现象，这些感知时常发生，或是看似无法解释的“不可能”事件。（停顿。）现在，将你自身的心灵——它是一个意识化的身份——视为一种“超自然收音机”。所有电台都同时存在于心灵之内。这些电台不仅通过声音传递，还带着世界所有活生生的附属物。你所认出的“你”只是这样一个电台上的一个信号，调谐到某个特定频率，从你自身的视角体验该电台的整体实相——这个视角独一无二，无可比拟，却又为电台的整体生命做出贡献。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: I also think that if asked Seth would point out that since the concept of quantum mechanics is based upon the idea that everything we "know"--matter, energy, our sensual information--is made up of quanta, or the interactions of insubstantial fields that in turn, and quite paradoxically, produce very active subatomic packets or particles, then quantum mechanics is at least analogous with his statements that basically the universe is composed of consciousness itself. But I think that the continuum of consciousness, or All That Is, contains not only the phenomena of quantum mechanics, but also Seth's nonphysical EE (electromagnetic energy) units, and his CU's (or units of consciousness). In those terms, then, quantum mechanics is a theory that doesn't penetrate deeply enough into basic reality, even if physicists these days are basing their unified field theories upon quantum thinking. (These theories are themselves quite incomplete, since at this time they incorporate only three of the four basic interactions in nature: electromagnetism, and the strong and weak nuclear forces. So far, gravitation remains outside all attempts at integration.)
quotes.translation: 我也认为，如果赛斯被问及，他会指出，既然量子力学的概念基于这样的理念——我们所"知道"的一切——物质、能量、我们的感官信息——都由量子构成，或由非实质场的相互作用构成，而这些场又相当矛盾地产生非常活跃的亚原子包或粒子——那么量子力学至少与他所说的宇宙基本上由意识本身构成的说法类似。但我认为，意识的连续体，或一切万有，不仅包含量子力学的现象，还包含赛斯的非物质EE（电磁能量）单元，以及他的CU（意识单元）。因此，从这些术语来看，量子力学是一种未能深入触及基本现实的理论，即使当今物理学家正基于量子思维构建他们的统一场论。（这些理论本身相当不完整，因为目前它们只包含了自然界四种基本相互作用中的三种：电磁力、强核力和弱核力。到目前为止，引力仍未被纳入任何整合尝试中。）
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1

quotes.text: "Your [Sumari] consciousness is that kind of consciousness, and so is mine, except that my boundaries are far less limited than your own, and I recognize them not as boundaries but as directions in which recognition of myself must grow. The same applies to the Sumari as such. In other words, this is not an undifferentiated consciousness that addresses you now, but one that understands the nature of its own identity.
quotes.translation: "你们（苏马利）的意识是那种意识，我的也是，只不过我的界限远不如你们自己的那么有限，而我不把它们视为界限，而是视为我必须认识自己的方向。苏马利本身也是如此。换句话说，现在对你们讲话的并非一种无差别的意识，而是一种理解自身身份本质的意识。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: The atoms that compose the fetus have their own kind of consciousness. The volatile awareness-consciousnesses that exist independently of matter, form matter according to their ability and degree. The fetus, therefore, has its own consciousness, the simple component consciousness made up of the atoms that compose it. This exists before any reincarnating personality enters it. The consciousness of matter is present in any matter -- a fetus, a rock, a blade of grass, a nail.
quotes.translation: 构成胎儿的原子有它们自己的意识。独立于物质存在的易变觉知-意识，根据它们的能力和程度形成物质。因此，胎儿有它自己的意识，即由构成它的原子组成的简单成分意识。这在任何转世人格进入它之前就已存在。物质的意识存在于任何物质中——胎儿、岩石、草叶、指甲。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: I have used the term "expansion of consciousness" here rather than the more frequently used "cosmic consciousness" (pause), because the latter implies an experience of proportions not available to mankind at this time. (Pause.) Intense expansions of consciousness by contrast to your normal state may appear to be cosmic in nature, but they barely hint at those possibilities of consciousness that are available to you now, much less begin to approach a true cosmic awareness.
quotes.translation: 我在此处使用了“意识扩展”一词，而非更常用的“宇宙意识”（停顿），因为后者暗示着一种目前人类尚无法企及的体验规模。（停顿。）与你的正常状态相比，强烈的意识扩展可能看似具有宇宙本质，但它们仅能隐约暗示你现在可获得的那些意识可能性，更遑论接近真正的宇宙觉知。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: To some extent the greater expression of consciousness can be experienced under usual waking conditions, but only when a personality is flexible enough and secure enough to alter the focus of consciousness. This way, other unperceived data become available. The unknown reality is not beyond your experience, therefore. Any of your scientific or religious disciplines could benefit from a study of the dreaming consciousness, for there the basic nature of reality exists as clearly as you can perceive it. The inner condition of dreaming is valid. You find yourselves in other times and places because basically neither time nor space exists as you suppose.7
quotes.translation: 在某种程度上，意识更充分的表达可在通常的清醒状态下体验，但只有当人格足够灵活且足够安全，能够改变意识的焦点时才能实现。这样，其他未被感知的数据才变得可得。因此，未知实相并非超出你的经验范围。任何科学或宗教训练都能从对梦境意识的研究中获益，因为在那里，实相的基本性质清晰得如同你能感知它一样。梦境的内在状态是有效的。你发现自己身处其他时空，因为基本上时间和空间都不像你所认为的那样存在。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: If you do not understand that in periods of sleep your consciousness actually does leave your body, then what I have said will be meaningless. Now your consciousness does return at times, to check upon the physical mechanisms, and the simple consciousness of atom and cell -- the body consciousness -- is always with the body, so it is not vacant. But the largely creative portions of the self do leave the body, and for large periods of time when you sleep.
quotes.translation: 如果你不了解在睡眠期间你的意识确实会离开身体，那么我所说的一切都将毫无意义。你的意识确实会不时返回，检查身体机制，而原子和细胞的简单意识——身体意识——始终与身体同在，因此身体并非空置。但自我中极具创造性的部分确实会离开身体，而且在你睡眠时，会离开很长一段时间。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: Give us a moment. This was an exercise, actually, in the manipulation of consciousness. Close to death, this same sort of thing happens in varying degrees when the consciousness realizes that it can no longer express itself through the mediumship of the body. If the dying person overidentifies with the body then he can easily panic, thinking that all expression is therefore cut off, and for that matter that his consciousness is about to be extinguished.
quotes.translation: 请稍等片刻。这实际上是一个操控意识的练习。濒临死亡时，当意识意识到它再也无法通过身体这个媒介来表达自己时，同样的事情会以不同程度发生。如果垂死之人过度认同身体，他很容易陷入恐慌，认为所有表达因此被切断，甚至认为他的意识即将被熄灭。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: (Slowly at 11:43:) While your consciousness is so engaged, your body consciousness performs many functions that are impossible for it during your waking hours. The greatest biological creativity takes place while you sleep, for example, and certain cellular functions10 are accelerated. Some such disengagement of your main consciousness and the body is therefore obviously necessary, or it would not occur. Sleeping is not a by-product of waking life.
quotes.translation: （11:43缓慢地：）当你的意识如此投入时，你的身体意识会执行许多在你清醒时不可能完成的功能。例如，最伟大的生物创造力发生在你睡眠期间，某些细胞功能会加速。因此，你的主要意识与身体的这种分离显然是必要的，否则它就不会发生。睡眠并非清醒生活的副产品。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: (9:53.) Give us a moment ... You distinguish between consciousness and your own version, which you consider consciousness of self. When I speak of atoms and molecules having consciousness, I mean that they possess a consciousness of themselves as identities. I do not mean that they love or hate, in your terms, but that they are aware of their own separateness, and aware of the ways in which that separateness cooperates to form other organizations.
quotes.translation: （9:53分。）请稍等片刻……你们将意识与你们自己的版本——即你们所谓的自我意识——区分开来。当我说原子和分子具有意识时，我的意思是它们拥有作为个体身份的自我意识。我并非指它们以你们的话来说会爱或恨，而是它们觉察到自身的独立性，并觉察到这种独立性如何合作形成其他组织的方式。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche

quotes.text: The ego is only one layer of the self that has self-consciousness. Being self-conscious, the ego attempts to be conscious only of itself. Self-consciousness results in an intense, but necessarily limited focus. It necessitates boundaries. It depends upon some sort of inner psychological decision as to what will be considered self, and therefore accepted by consciousness, and that which will be considered notself, and not accepted by consciousness.
quotes.translation: 自我只是具有自我意识的自我的一个层面。由于具有自我意识，自我试图只意识到自身。自我意识导致一种强烈但必然受限的焦点。它需要界限。它依赖于某种内在的心理决定，即什么将被视为自我，从而被意识接受，什么将被视为非我，而不被意识接受。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 5 of The Seth Material (Sessions 199-239)

quotes.text: Now basically, consciousness itself is a type of barrier, and anything that has consciousness experiences time to some degree. This degree may be so slight that to you it would not seem to exist at all. Self-consciousness presents a larger barrier, therefore the sense of time is greater. Psychological time is the lowest common denominator, so to speak, from your viewpoint. That is, many of the barriers disappear. Psychological time represents on your plane the closest you can come to the experience of timelessness as far as your physical laws are concerned.
quotes.translation: 现在，从根本上说，意识本身就是一种屏障，任何具有意识的事物都在某种程度上体验时间。这种程度可能如此轻微，以至于对你们而言似乎根本不存在。自我意识呈现出一个更大的屏障，因此时间感更强。从你们的观点来看，心理时间可以说是最低的共同分母。也就是说，许多屏障消失了。就你们的物理法则而言，心理时间在你们的层面上代表了你们所能接近的无时间性体验的最接近状态。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)

quotes.text: All obviously then exists at once. To your way of thinking some lives are lived in a twinkling, and others last for centuries, as some huge trees. The perception of consciousness is not limited however. I have told you for example that trees have their own consciousness. The consciousness of the tree is not as specifically focused however as your own. To all intents and purposes the tree is conscious of 50 years before and 50 years hence.
quotes.translation: 那么显然一切同时存在。以你们的思维方式，有些生命转瞬即逝，而另一些则持续数百年，如同某些巨树。然而意识的感知并不受限。例如我曾告诉你们，树木拥有自己的意识。但树木的意识并不像你们的那样高度聚焦。实际上，树木能意识到其存在前后各五十年的时光。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)

quotes.text: Now consciousness as you know it is used to these brief gaps of physical nonexistence mentioned earlier. Longer gaps disorient it to varying degrees, but these are not unusual. When the physical body sleeps, consciousness often leaves the physical system for fairly long periods, in your terms. But because the consciousness is not in the normally physically awake state, it is not aware of these gaps and is relatively unconcerned.
quotes.translation: 你们所知的意识已习惯于先前提到的肉体不存在时的短暂间隙。较长的间隙会使其在不同程度上迷失方向，但这并不罕见。当肉体睡眠时，意识常常离开物质系统相当长的时间——以你们的时间尺度而言。但由于意识并非处于正常的肉体清醒状态，它不会察觉这些间隙，也相对不以为意。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: In our next chapter I will speak of these in some detail. For now it is sufficient to realize that specific steps, definite alterations, occur as consciousness is shifted from the exterior to the interior reality, and that these changes are not random; that consciousness leaves through a very predictable route to its many destinations. Through the ages the Speakers have taught dreamers how to manipulate in these other environments. They have taught them how to bring back information that could be used for the good of the present personality. According to the intent, present purpose, and development, an individual may be aware of these travels to varying degrees. Some have excellent recall, for example, but often misinterpret their experience because of conscious ideas.
quotes.translation: 下一章我将详细讨论这些。目前只需明白：当意识从外在实相转向内在实相时，会发生特定的步骤与明确的转变，这些变化并非随机；意识会经由一条高度可预测的路径前往其众多目的地。历代传道者教导做梦者如何在这些其他环境中运作。他们教导他们带回可用于裨益当前人格的信息。根据个体的意图、当前目的与发展程度，一个人可能不同程度地意识到这些旅程。例如，有些人拥有极佳的回忆能力，却常因意识观念而误解自身经验。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: Some of this has to do with distorted ideas of both the conscious and unconscious minds, using your terms now. Generally speaking, in Western society the conscious mind is seen as coming into its own in early adulthood, as the self rises from the bed of childhood unconsciousness into its critical awareness and differentiation. The appreciation of distinctions and differences is considered one of the greatest characteristics of consciousness, and so those aspects of it are valued. On the other hand the equally significant assimilating, combining, correlating characteristics of consciousness are overlooked. In scholarly circles, and many that are not scholarly at all, the intellect is equated only with the critical faculties, so that the more diagnostic you are the more intellectual you are considered.
quotes.translation: 其中一部分与你们对意识与无意识心灵的扭曲观念有关，容我用你们的术语来说。一般而言，在西方社会，意识心被视为在成年早期才真正成形，自我从童年无意识的温床中升起，进入批判性觉知与分化。对差异与区别的辨识被认为是意识最伟大的特质之一，因此这些面向备受重视。然而，意识同样重要的同化、整合、关联特质却被忽略了。在学术圈乃至许多非学术领域，智力仅等同于批判能力，因此你越善于诊断，就被认为越有才智。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality

quotes.text: Beyond this there are experiences but seldom recalled, in which the usual identification of your consciousness with physical-life orientation is gone. (Pause.) Images as you think of them are based upon your own neurological structure, and your interpretations of these. When you consider survival after death, for instance, you imagine all the senses fully operating, though perhaps in a nonphysical body. Perception without images seems impossible in that context. Yet in some dream situations you enter a state of awareness quite divorced from that kind of sense data. Images as such are not involved, though later they may be manufactured unconsciously for the sake of translation. In those conditions you come close to an understanding of what your consciousness is when it is not physically oriented at all.
quotes.translation: 除此之外，还存在一些鲜少被忆起的经验，在其中，你们意识与物质生命导向的惯常认同消失了。（停顿）你们所认为的影像，是基于自身的神经结构及其诠释。例如，当你们思考死后存续时，会想象所有感官完全运作，尽管可能是在非物质的躯体中。在此脉络下，没有影像的感知似乎不可能存在。然而，在某些梦境情境中，你们会进入一种完全脱离此类感官数据的觉知状态。影像本身并不参与其中，尽管之后可能为了翻译而无意中制造出影像。在这些条件下，你们得以接近理解：当意识完全不导向物质时，它究竟是什么。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality

quotes.text: We must unfortunately often deal with analogies, because they can form bridgeworks between concepts. There are units of consciousness,3 then, as there are units of matter. I do not want you to think of these units as particles. There is a basic unit of consciousness that, expressed, will not be broken down, as once it was thought that an atom was the smallest unit and could not be broken down. The basic unit of consciousness obviously is not physical. It contains within itself innately infinite properties of expansion, development, and organization; yet within itself always maintains the kernel of its own individuality. Despite whatever organizations it becomes part of, or how it mixes with other such basic units, its own identity is not annihilated.
quotes.translation: 不幸的是，我们常常必须借助类比，因为它们能在概念之间搭建桥梁。那么，意识单位是存在的，正如物质单位存在一样。我不希望你们将这些单位视为粒子。有一种基本的意识单位，一旦被表达出来便无法再分解，正如过去人们认为原子是最小单位且不可再分一样。这个基本意识单位显然是非物质的。它内在包含无限的可扩展、可发展、可组织的特性；然而，它始终维持着自身个体性的核心。无论它成为何种组织的一部分，或如何与其他基本单位混合，其自身身份都不会被消灭。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1

quotes.text: The point of all this is that these units are unpredictable, and fulfill all probabilities of consciousness. Any concepts of gods or other beings that are based upon limited ideas of personhood will ultimately be futile. You view the fantastic variety of physical life -- its animals, insects, birds, fish, man and all his works -- with hardly a qualm; yet you must understand that the nature of consciousness itself is far more varied, and you must learn to think of an inner reality that is as infinite as the exterior one. These concepts alone do alter your present consciousness, and change it in degree. The present idea of the soul, you see, is a "primitive" idea that can scarcely begin to explain the creativity or reality from which mankind's being comes. You are multipersons (intently). You exist in many times and places at once. You exist as one person, simultaneously. This does not deny the independence of the persons, but your inner reality straddles their reality, while it also serves as a psychic world in which they can grow.3
quotes.translation: 这一切的重点在于，这些单位是不可预测的，它们实现了意识的所有可能性。任何基于有限人格概念的神祇或其他存有的观念，最终都将徒劳无功。你们看待物质生命那奇妙的多样性——它的动物、昆虫、鸟类、鱼类、人类及其所有作品——几乎毫无疑虑；然而你们必须明白，意识本身的本质远比这更加多样，你们必须学会思考一个与外在实相同样无限的内在实相。仅这些概念本身就会改变你们当前的意识，并在程度上改变它。你看，当前关于灵魂的观念是一种“原始”的观念，几乎无法开始解释人类存在所源自的创造力或实相。你们是多重人格（专注地）。你们同时存在于许多时间和地点。你们同时作为一个人存在。这并不否定这些人的独立性，但你们的内在实相跨越了他们的实相，同时它也是一个心灵世界，让他们得以在其中成长。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1

quotes.text: All of this presented a quite erroneous picture. Physically speaking, earth itself has its own kind of gestalt consciousness. If you must, then think of that earth consciousness as grading (spelled) upward in great slopes of awareness from relatively "inert" particles of dust and stone through the mineral, vegetable, and animal kingdoms. Even then, remember that those kingdoms are not so separate after all. Each one is highly related to each of the others. Nothing happens in one such kingdom that does not affect the others. A great, gracious cooperation exists between those seemingly separate systems, however. If you will remember that even atoms and molecules have consciousness, then it will be easier for you to understand that there is indeed a certain kind of awareness that unites these kingdoms.
quotes.translation: 这一切呈现了相当错误的图景。从物理层面来说，地球本身拥有其独特的完形意识。如果你必须如此理解，那么请将地球意识视为从相对"惰性"的尘埃与石块粒子，经由矿物界、植物界与动物界，以意识坡度向上攀升的层级。即便如此，请记住这些界域并非真正彼此分离。每一界域都与其他界域高度相关。任何界域中发生的事件都会影响其他界域。然而，在这些看似分离的系统之间存在着伟大而优雅的合作。如果你能记住连原子与分子都具有意识，那么你就更容易理解确实存在某种统合这些界域的意识。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: In your terms, consciousness of self did not develop because of any exterior circumstances in which your species won out, so to speak. In fact, that consciousness of self in any person is dependent upon the constant, miraculous cooperations that exist between the mineral, vegetable, and animal worlds.3 The inner intent always forms any exterior alteration. This applies on any scale you use. Consciousness forms the environment. The environment itself is conscious (forcefully). Atoms and molecules themselves operate in their own fields of probabilities. In their own ways, they "yearn" toward all probable developments. When they form living creatures they become a physical basis for species alteration. The body's adaptability is not simply an adjusting mechanism or quality. The cells have inner capabilities that you have not discovered. They contain within themselves memory of all the "previous" forms they have been a part of.
quotes.translation: 用你们的话来说，自我意识并非因为你们物种在外部环境中"胜出"而发展。事实上，任何个体中的自我意识都依赖于矿物界、植物界与动物界之间持续存在的奇迹般的合作。内在意图始终塑造着一切外在变化。这适用于你们使用的任何尺度。意识塑造环境。环境本身具有意识（强烈地）。原子与分子在各自的概率场中运作。它们以各自的方式"渴望"所有可能的发展。当它们形成生物时，便成为物种变化的物质基础。身体的适应性并非简单的调节机制或特质。细胞具有你们尚未发现的内在能力。它们自身包含着所有它们曾参与构成的"先前"形态的记忆。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: What he was sensing, however, was an entirely different kind of reality. He was beginning to recognize another synapse [neuronal] pattern not "native"; he was familiarizing himself with perceptions at a different set of coordinate points. Such activity automatically alters the nature of time in your experience, and is indicative of intersections of your consciousness with another kind of consciousness. That particular type of consciousness operates "at different speeds" than your own. Biologically, your own physical structures are quite able to operate at those same speeds, though as a species you have disciplined yourselves to a different kind of neurological reaction. By altering such neurological prejudice,10 however, you can indeed learn to become aware of other realities that coincide with yours. Period.
quotes.translation: 然而，他感知到的是一种截然不同的实相。他开始辨识出另一种非"原生"的突触（神经元）模式；他正在熟悉一组不同坐标点上的感知。这类活动会自动改变你经验中的时间本质，并标志着你的意识与另一种意识的交汇。那种特定类型的意识以与你不同的"速度"运作。从生物学角度而言，你自身的肉体结构完全能够以相同速度运作，尽管作为一个物种，你们已训练自己适应了另一种神经反应模式。但通过改变这种神经偏见，你确实能学会觉察与你的实相并存的其他实相。句号。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: The freshness of dream experience lies in its direct nature. Your cultural world view does not have any clear understanding of the nature of dreams, so that their direct, clear expression is not recalled often in the morning. (Pause.) At night you tune in to dreaming reality simply by closing out so-called waking reality, but the same kind of dream experience continues beneath your focus in waking life. Dreaming, you are still aware of your daily experience, but it is seemingly peripheral. Waking, your dream experience is peripheral also, but you are less aware of that condition. Both together represent the dimensions of your consciousness, and they exist simultaneously. You can and often do work out in dreams the challenges of daily life. In waking life you are also working out challenges set for yourselves in the dream state. Obviously, then, your consciousness is equipped to function in the known and unknown realities, and the divisions that you have set up are quite arbitrary.
quotes.translation: 梦境体验的新鲜感在于其直接性。你们的文化世界观对梦的本质缺乏清晰认知，因此其直接清晰的表达往往在清晨不被记起。（停顿。）夜晚你只需屏蔽所谓的清醒实相便能调频进入梦境实相，但同样的梦境体验在清醒生活中仍持续存在于你的焦点之下。做梦时你仍能感知日常经验，只是它看似处于边缘；清醒时你的梦境经验同样处于边缘，但你对这种状态觉察更少。两者共同代表了你意识的维度，且同时存在。你能够且经常在梦中处理日常生活的挑战，而在清醒生活中你也在处理梦境状态中为自己设定的挑战。显然，你的意识具备在已知与未知实相中运作的能力，你所设定的划分完全是武断的。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: In a way, the one-line kind of consciousness that you have developed can be correlated with your use of any one language. Experience is programmed, highly specialized, and attains a seemingly tight organization only because (intently) it limits so much of reality. In those terms, if you are bilingual you are somewhat better off, for your thoughts have a choice of two paths. Biologically, you are physically capable of speaking any language now in use on the face of the earth. You would consider it an achievement if you learned to speak many languages. You would not find it frightening or unnatural, though you would take it for granted that some training was involved.
