In the same way, your one-line kind of consciousness is but one of many "languages." The others are as native, as natural, as biologically feasible.
quotes.translation: 从某方面来说，你们发展出的单线式意识，与你们使用任何一种语言的方式是相互关联的。经验被程序化、高度专门化，并且看似具有紧密的组织结构，只因为它（专注地）限制了如此多的实相。用这些术语来说，如果你能说双语，你的处境会好一些，因为你的思想有两条路径可选。从生物学角度，你的身体有能力说出地球上目前使用的任何语言。如果你学会说多种语言，你会认为这是一项成就。你不会觉得这可怕或不自然，尽管你会理所当然地认为需要一些训练。同样，你的单线式意识只是众多“语言”之一。其他的语言同样与生俱来、同样自然、同样在生物学上可行。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: The English itself, however, then becomes charged, freshened with new concepts, carrying within a strangeness that itself alters the relationship of the words. This is a dream or trance language. It is as native to its level of consciousness as English is to your own -- or Indian, or Chinese, or whatever. The various focuses of consciousness will have their own "languages." Ruburt has discovered that beneath the Sumari there are deeper meanings.11 He has become aware of what he calls long and short sounds. Some come so quickly that he cannot keep track, or speak them quickly enough. Others are so slow that he feels a sentence would take a week to utter.12 These are the signatures of different focuses of consciousness as they are transposed in your space-time system.
quotes.translation: 然而，英语本身随后会变得充满活力，因新概念而焕然一新，携带着一种陌生感，这种陌生感本身改变了词语之间的关系。这是一种梦境或出神语言。它对其意识层面而言，就像英语对你的意识层面——或印度语、中文或任何语言——一样自然。意识的各种焦点会有它们自己的“语言”。鲁柏发现，在苏马利语之下还有更深层的含义。他意识到他所谓的“长音”和“短音”。有些声音来得如此之快，以至于他无法跟上，或说得足够快。另一些则如此缓慢，以至于他觉得一个句子需要一周才能说完。这些是不同意识焦点在你们时空系统中转译时的特征。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: Give us a moment ... (Then slowly:) It is difficult to explain on spiritual and psychic levels without speaking in terms of gradations of identity, for example, but in your terms even the smallest "particle" of identity is inviolate. It may grow, develop or expand, change alliances or organizations, and it does combine with others even as cells do. (Long pause.) Your body does not feel as if you invade it. Your consciousness and its consciousness are merged; yet it is composed of the multitudinous individual consciousnesses that form the tiniest physical particles within it. Those particles come and go, yet your body remains itself. What was physically a part of you last year is not today. Physically, you are a different person. Put simply, the stuff of the body is constantly returned to the earth,* where it forms again into physical actualization -- but always differently.
quotes.translation: 给我们一点时间……（然后缓慢地：）很难在不使用身份层级这类术语的情况下，从灵性与心灵层面解释，但用你们的说法，即使是最小的身份“粒子”也是不可侵犯的。它可以成长、发展或扩展，改变联盟或组织，并且它确实像细胞一样与其他粒子结合。（长停顿。）你的身体并不感觉像是你入侵了它。你的意识与它的意识是融合的；然而，它是由无数个体意识组成的，这些意识形成了其中最小的物理粒子。那些粒子来来去去，但你的身体仍然保持自身。去年在物理上是你一部分的东西，今天已不复存在。从物理上说，你是一个不同的人。简单来说，身体的物质不断回归大地，在那里再次形成物理显化——但总是以不同的方式。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: The psyche as you know it, then, is composed of a mixture of these families of consciousness. One is not superior to the others. They are just different, and they represent various ways of looking at physical life. (Pause.) A book would be needed to explain the dimensions of the psyche in relation to the different families of consciousness. Here, in this manuscript, I merely want to make the reader aware of the existence of these psychic groupings. I am alert to the fact that I am using many terms, and that it may seem difficult to understand the differences between probable and reincarnational selves, counterparts and families of consciousness. At times contradictions may seem to exist. You may wonder how you are you in the midst of such multitudinous psychic "variations."
quotes.translation: 因此，你所知的灵性是由这些意识家族的混合体所组成的。没有哪个家族比其他家族更优越。它们只是不同而已，代表着看待物质生命的各种方式。（停顿。）需要一本书才能解释灵性维度与不同意识家族之间的关系。在这份手稿中，我只想让读者意识到这些灵性群体的存在。我意识到自己使用了大量术语，而且可能很难理解可能自我、转世自我、对应人物和意识家族之间的区别。有时似乎会存在矛盾。你可能会疑惑，在如此众多的灵性"变体"中，你究竟是如何成为你自己的。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: In connection with material in this note, I think it quite interesting and revealing that several millennia before Darwin, man himself began playing the role of a designer within the framework of nature, through his selective breeding of animals and his hybridization of plants. These activities certainly represent evolution through conscious intent, guided by the same creature who insists that no sort of consciousness could have been responsible for the origin or development of "life," let alone the "dead" matter of his planet. Not only that: We read that even now in his laboratories man is trying hard to create some of that life itself. This is always done, of course, with the idea that the right combination of simple ingredients (water, methane, ammonia, et al.) in the test tube, stimulated by the right kind of energy under just the right conditions, will automatically produce life. It's confidently predicted that eventually at least one such experiment will succeed. I have yet to see in those accounts anything about the role consciousness will play in this truly miraculous conversion of dead matter into that of the living. Perhaps those involved in the experiments fear that the idea of consciousness will impugn the scientific "purity" of their work.
quotes.translation: 关于本注释中的资料，我认为相当有趣且具有启发性的是：在达尔文之前数千年，人类就已经开始通过选择性培育动物和杂交植物，在自然框架内扮演设计师的角色。这些活动无疑代表了通过有意识意图实现的进化，而指导这一切的正是那个坚持认为没有任何意识能够对"生命"的起源或发展负责的同一生物——更不用说其星球上的"无生命"物质了。不仅如此：我们读到，即使在今天，人类在实验室里仍在努力创造某些生命本身。当然，这始终基于这样的想法：在试管中将简单成分（水、甲烷、氨等）以正确组合，在恰当条件下用适当能量刺激，就会自动产生生命。人们自信地预测，最终至少会有一次这样的实验成功。在这些描述中，我尚未看到关于意识将在这种将无生命物质真正奇迹般地转化为生命物质的过程中扮演什么角色。也许参与实验的人担心，意识的概念会玷污他们工作的科学"纯粹性"。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: It also "stamps" or "impresses" the universe with its own imprint. No portion of the universe is inactive or passive, regardless of its seeming organization or its seeming lack of organization. Each consciousness, then, impresses the universe in its own fashion. Its very existence sets up a kind of significance, in whose light the rest of the universe will be interpreted. The universe knows itself through such significances. Each consciousness is endowed with creativity of a multidimensional nature, so that it will seek to create as many possible realities for itself as it can, using its own significance as a focus to draw into its experience whatever events are possible for it from the universe itself. It will then attract events from the universe, even as its own existence imprints the universe as an event with the indelible stamp of its own nature.
quotes.translation: 它同时以自己的印记"铭刻"或"烙印"于宇宙之上。宇宙中没有哪个部分是不活跃或被动的，无论其表面呈现组织性还是缺乏组织性。因此，每个意识都以自己的方式在宇宙中留下印记。它的存在本身就建立了一种意义，宇宙的其余部分将在此意义之光中被诠释。宇宙正是通过这样的意义来认识自身。每个意识都被赋予了多维本质的创造力，它会以自身意义为焦点，尽可能为自己创造更多可能的实相，从宇宙中汲取任何可能的事件纳入自身经验。它既会从宇宙中吸引事件，其存在本身也会作为事件将自身本质的不可磨灭印记烙在宇宙之上。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche

quotes.text: Consciousness within the body knows that its existence is within the body's context, and apart from it at the same time. In ordinary life during the day consciousness often takes a recess, so to speak -- it daydreams, or otherwise experiences itself as somewhat apart from the body's reality. At night, in sleep, the self's consciousness takes longer, freer recesses from physical reality, and does this as spontaneously as the body itself walks. These experiences are not hypothetical. They happen to each person. On such occasions, each person is to some extent aware of a kind of comprehension that is not dependent upon the accumulation of data, but of a deeper kind of experience and direct encounter with the reality from which the world emerges.
quotes.translation: 身体内的意识知道自己的存在既在身体脉络之中，同时又超越身体。在白天的日常生活中，意识常常会"休息"——它做白日梦，或以其他方式体验与身体实相稍许分离的状态。夜晚睡眠时，自我意识会从物质实相中享受更长久、更自由的休憩，其自然程度如同身体行走一般。这些体验绝非假设。它们发生在每个人身上。在这样的时刻，每个人都能在一定程度上体会到一种不依赖数据积累的领悟，而是通过更深层的体验，直接与那孕育世界的实相相遇。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche

quotes.text: In my opinion these are hardly predictions, but instead very conservative projections of already well-established phenomena: I don't for a minute think that any country, let alone our species as a whole, will give up on nuclear power. Nor will Iran, or the United States, or a number of other nations, dispense with fundamentalist religion of whatever kind. I believe that those particular aspects of scientific consciousness and religious consciousness will be with us for a very long time, for in our chosen earthly reality a larger consciousness--and, ultimately, All That Is--has opted for much long-range exploration of those two closely related portions of itself. In our probability we can create both very transcendent and very painful portions of that dual exploration. I think those particular aspects of mankind's search for answers will grow ever more powerful for a number of years, until their very excesses finally lead to their "evolution" into forces that are much more controlled and compassionate and understanding.
quotes.translation: 依我之见，这些几乎算不上预言，而是对早已确立的现象极为保守的推估：我丝毫不认为任何国家——更遑论全人类——会放弃核能。伊朗、美国或其他许多国家也不会摒弃任何形式的原教旨宗教。我相信，科学意识与宗教意识的这些特定面向将与我们共存极长一段时间，因为在我们所选择的地球实相中，一个更大的意识——最终是"一切万有"——已选择对其自身这两个密切相关的部分进行长程探索。在我们的或然性中，这场双重探索既能创造极为超越的部分，也能创造极为痛苦的部分。我认为人类寻求答案的这些特定面向将在未来多年间日益强大，直至其过度发展最终促使它们"演化"为更受控制、更具慈悲与理解的力量。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1

quotes.text: But in your terms this was still largely a dream world, though it was fully fashioned. It had, generally speaking, all of the species that you now know. These all correlated with the multitudinous kinds of consciousnesses that had clamored for release, and those consciousnesses were spontaneously endowed by All That Is with those forms that fit their requirements. You had the birth of individualized consciousness as you think of it into physical context. Those consciousnesses were individualized before the beginning, but not manifest. But individualized consciousness was not quite all that bold. It did not attach itself completely to its earthly forms at the start, but rested often within its "ancient" divine heritage. In your terms, it is as if the earth and all of its creatures were partially dreaming, and not as focused within physical reality as they are now.
quotes.translation: 但以你们的说法，这仍大体是个梦境世界，尽管它已被充分塑造。一般而言，它拥有你们现今所知的所有物种。这些物种皆与那些喧嚷着要释放的众多意识种类相互关联，而"一切万有"自发赋予那些意识符合其需求的形体。你们所认知的个体化意识在物质脉络中诞生了。那些意识在太初之前便已个体化，但尚未显化。然而个体化意识并非全然那般大胆。它起初并未完全依附于其地球形体，而是常驻于其"古老"的神圣传承中。以你们的说法，仿佛地球及其所有造物都处于半梦半醒之间，其专注程度不及现今这般聚焦于物质实相。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1

quotes.text: You read your own consciousness now in a kind of vertical fashion, identifying only with certain portions of it, and it seems to you that any other organization of perception, any other recognition of identity, would quite necessarily negate your own or render it inoperable. In the beginning of the world there were numberless groupings, however, and affiliations of consciousness, many other organizations of identity that were recognized, as well as the kind of psychological orientation you have now--but [your] kind of orientation was not the paramount one. While, generally speaking, earth's species existed from the beginning in the forms by which you now know them, consciousness of species was quite different, and all species were much more intimately related through various kinds of identification that have since gone into the underground of awareness.
quotes.translation: 你们如今以某种垂直方式解读自身意识，只认同其特定部分，因而认为任何其他感知组织、任何其他身份认同，都必然否定或瘫痪你们的身份认同。然而在世界之初，存在着无数意识群集与联结，许多其他身份组织被认知，与你们现今拥有的心理取向并存——但你们的取向并非首要的。虽然一般而言，地球物种自始便以你们现今所知的形态存在，但物种意识却大不相同，所有物种通过后来沉入觉知底层的各种认同方式，彼此间有着远为密切的关联。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1

quotes.text: I'm sure that Seth would be the first to agree that consciousness obviously contains an unlimited number of viewpoints, regardless of which ones we humans may choose to call "true" at any particular time. Consciousness is just as amenable to having some of its physical manifestations scientifically studied, its parts manipulated through "genetic engineering," as it is to encompassing Seth's material. All of our species' actions represent our keen and creative interests in studying ourselves in the finest details possible. That the scientific approach has limitations is obvious. So do all others in this physical realm. A discipline, of whatever nature and motivation, can erect barriers to "outside" influences--and those barriers are often artifacts growing almost automatically out of the very nature of the belief system in question.
quotes.translation: 我确信赛斯会首先同意，意识显然包含了无限的观点，无论我们人类在特定时期选择称哪些为"真实"。意识既允许其部分物理表现被科学地研究、其部分通过"基因工程"被操控，也允许包含赛斯的资料。我们人类的所有行为都代表了我们以最精细的方式研究自身的热切而富有创造性的兴趣。科学方法有其局限性是显而易见的。在这个物质领域中，所有其他方法也是如此。任何学科，无论其性质和动机如何，都可能筑起屏障阻挡"外部"影响——而这些屏障往往是几乎自动从所涉信念系统的本质中生长出来的产物。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2

quotes.text: There are countless relationships between species that go unrecognized. The generations of all species interact. The genetic cues are not triggered on the proposition, obviously, that a species exists alone on the planet, but also in response to genetic sequences that operate in all of the species combined. The genetic system, again, is not closed nearly as much as supposed. That is, again, because the basic units of consciousness that build up matter--that form matter--are themselves endowed with a subjective acuteness. This also accounts for my earlier statement, that in usually understood terms the environment and its creatures "evolve" together. (Long pause.) Your position on the scale of awareness inclines you to categorize consciousnesses so that only your own familiar brand seems to fit the definition--so again here I remind you that consciousness is everywhere in the deepest terms, because All That Is disperses itself throughout physical reality. All portions of that reality have their own rights to existence, and purposes within it. So of course do all peoples, and the races.
quotes.translation: 物种之间存在无数未被认识的关系。所有物种的世代都在相互作用。基因线索的触发显然并非基于某个物种独自存在于地球上的假设，而是响应于在所有物种中共同运作的基因序列。基因系统再次强调，并不像人们假设的那样封闭。这是因为构成物质——形成物质——的基本意识单位本身就被赋予了主观的敏锐性。这也解释了我先前的陈述，即按照通常理解，环境及其生物是"共同进化"的。（长停顿。）你在意识层级上的位置使你倾向于对意识进行分类，以至于只有你自己熟悉的那种才符合定义——所以在此我再次提醒你，在最深层的意义上，意识无处不在，因为一切万有将自身分散于整个物质实相中。该实相的所有部分都有其自身存在的权利，以及其中的目的。所有民族和种族当然也是如此。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2

quotes.text: They would seem to be the spirits of nature,2 as you would be more or less bound to interpret them from your viewpoint. They would certainly be psychological relatives, but with their own time schemes, languages, and psychological affiliations. These do exist along with the kinds of consciousness that you recognize within the structure of physical life. When you dream, however, you often come in contact with these cousins of consciousness. It is not simply that they communicate with you, or you with them, so much as it is that in sleep the conventional properties that you have learned are somewhat loosened and abandoned. You see "the lights around the corner," so to speak. You see a species of consciousness, a species that must remain unexplained in any normal explanations of evolution, and these hint at the communications that exist at all levels (intently), protecting not only the genetic references necessary to your own kind, but the combinations of other forms of organization that exist adjacent to your own, yet connected to them. You have often misread such references, and many of your legends of good and evil spirits, monsters and strange varieties of artificial creatures, appear in folklore.
quotes.translation: 它们似乎是自然之灵，从你的角度多少会这样解读。它们无疑是心理上的亲戚，却拥有自己的时间架构、语言和心理归属。这些意识确实存在，与你所认知的、存在于物质生命结构中的那些意识并存。然而，当你做梦时，你常常会接触到这些意识的表亲。与其说是它们与你沟通，或你与它们沟通，不如说是在睡眠中，你所习得的传统特性会稍微松动并被舍弃。你看到了“角落里的光”，可以这么说。你看到了一种意识物种，一种在任何正常的进化解释中都必然无法解释的物种，而这些暗示着存在于所有层面的沟通（专注地），不仅保护着你自身物种所需的基因参考，也保护着那些与你自身相邻却又相连的其他组织形式之组合。你常常误读这些参考，许多关于善灵与恶灵、怪物和奇特人造生物的传说，都出现在民间故事中。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2

quotes.text: The basic consciousness is never physical. Yet within your system it must collect experience within the physical system, hence the physical body. But experience itself is not physical, and cannot be contained within physical matter. Therefore this experience, collected within the physical field, is held in codified form by this inner self or astral identity. It is only by understanding the connection between the physical and nonphysical self, and the communication systems that operate here, that the true nature of human personality can be studied. While we are dealing now with your own species, it should be realized that all consciousness also possesses its own astral identity. The inner senses are part of this nonphysical self. They allow the personality to retain its relationship with nonphysical reality, permitting the material self to focus within its earthly environment.
quotes.translation: 基本意识从来不是物质的。然而，在你的系统内，它必须在物质系统内收集经验，因此才有了物质身体。但经验本身不是物质的，也无法被包含在物质之内。因此，这种在物质场域内收集的经验，以编码的形式被这个内在自己或星光体身份所持有。只有通过理解物质与非我之间的连接，以及在此运作的沟通系统，才能研究人类人格的真实本质。虽然我们现在处理的是你们自己的物种，但应该认识到，所有意识也拥有它自己的星光体身份。内在感官是这非我的一部分。它们允许人格保持与非物质实相的关系，让物质自己能够聚焦在其尘世环境内。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 5 of The Seth Material (Sessions 199-239)

quotes.text: There are five steps. (Pause.) It is possible to cover all of them at once, or the process may take you some time. First however imagine your consciousness out of your body in its astral form. Next, feeling that you are in the astral form, tell yourself that from that image you will be able to see the image of the person that you were in your immediately past life. I cannot tell you whether you will succeed at once, as you may, or whether you will find it difficult. You, Joseph, being proficient with images, may find it easier than Ruburt. Now when you feel that you have this new image of your past self before you, then imagine that your consciousness is moving from your astral form into this past self. You should not do this until you see the whole form.
quotes.translation: 共有五个步骤。（停顿。）你可以同时完成所有步骤，也可能需要一些时间。但首先，想象你的意识以星光体形态离开身体。接着，在感受到自己处于星光体形态后，告诉自己从这个影像中你将能看到你上一世那个人的影像。我无法告诉你是否能立刻成功——你可能成功，也可能发现这很困难。约瑟夫，你擅长处理影像，可能比鲁柏更容易做到。现在，当你感觉眼前出现了过去自我的新影像时，想象你的意识正从星光体移入这个过去的自我。在看清完整形态之前，不要进行这一步。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)

quotes.text: The only way to avoid facing the problem you see was to limit the amount of free energy allotted to the individual, and available en masse. Also safeguards were automatically built into the physical structure so that accumulations of energy could not be repressed--so no buildup could then emerge with violent overtones. Those in this system take further steps very slowly, and it will be more difficult for them to learn to handle large amounts of energy constructively. Those in your own system are more venturesome, more daring. Consciousness is far too varied. No one system could contain it or satisfy it. Generally speaking then, individual entities choose the system which suits their personalities and general leanings. Your system presents a rather abrupt, explosive entrance into largely organized consciousness, but on an individual basis.
quotes.translation: 避免面对你看到的问题的唯一方法，就是限制分配给个体及整体可用的自由能量总量。此外，物理结构本身内置了安全机制，使能量积累无法被压抑——这样就不会有积累的能量以暴力形式爆发。这个系统中的个体进展非常缓慢，他们学习建设性处理大量能量会更加困难。而你们系统中的个体则更冒险、更大胆。意识的多样性远超想象，没有任何一个系统能完全容纳或满足它。因此，一般来说，个体存有会选择符合其人格和总体倾向的系统。你们的系统呈现了一种相当突然、爆发性的方式进入高度组织化的意识，但这是基于个体层面的。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)

quotes.text: ("Now, if you had all been really paying attention to what I have been saying for some time about the simultaneous nature of time and existence, then you would have known that the theory of evolution is as beautiful a tale as the theory of biblical creation. Both are quite handy, and both are methods of telling stories, and both might seem to agree within their own systems, and yet, in larger respects they cannot be realities.... No -- no form of matter, however potent, will be self-evolved into consciousness, no matter what other bits of matter are added to it. Without the consciousness, the matter would not be there in the universe, floating around, waiting for another component to give it reality, consciousness, existence, or song."
quotes.translation: （"现在，如果你们真的认真关注我长期以来关于时间与存在同时性的论述，就会明白进化论与圣经创世论同样都是美妙的故事。两者都很方便，都是讲故事的方式，在各自系统内似乎自洽，但从更广阔的视角看，它们都不可能是实相……不——无论物质形态多么强大，都无法自我进化出意识，无论添加多少其他物质碎片。没有意识，物质根本不会存在于宇宙中，漂浮着等待另一个成分赋予它实相、意识、存在或歌声。"）
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: New paragraph: Consciousness then is not dependent upon physical perception, though this attribute does require an awareness immersed within a material form. While physical consciousness is sifted through the bodily apparatus, you are usually unaware of noncorporeal kinds because of that process. The general framework, properties and characteristics of the body exist, therefore, before its formation. In simple terms, you choose ahead of time the kind of body you will inhabit and impress. It may seem to you that you do not have any conscious control over your body's condition in life as you know it, much less before your birth. You have been taught that there is little connection between your thought and your body's activities.
quotes.translation: 意识并不依赖于物质感知，尽管这一特质确实需要一种沉浸于物质形态中的觉知。当物质意识通过身体机制被筛选时，你通常因这一过程而无法察觉非肉体的意识类型。因此，身体的一般框架、属性与特征在形成之前便已存在。简而言之，你预先选择了将要居住并注入活力的身体类型。在你看来，你似乎对自己生命中的身体状况毫无有意识的控制力，更不用说出生之前了。你一直被教导，你的思想与身体活动之间几乎没有关联。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality

quotes.text: Often you visit such areas of consciousness in the dream state where you fall into them spontaneously, remembering in the morning a fantastic dream. Consciousness must use all of its parts and activities, even as the body must. When you are sleeping, therefore, your consciousness turns itself in many of these directions, often perceiving, willy-nilly, bits and pieces of reality that are available to it at its different stages. This also happens beneath your normal physical focus to some extent, even as you go about your waking activities. The alternate presents of which I spoke are not simply alternate methods of perceiving one objective present. There are many alternate presents, with you focused only in one of them.
quotes.translation: 你们常在梦境状态自发地进入这类意识领域，早晨醒来时记得一个奇异的梦。意识必须运用其所有部分与活动，正如身体必须如此。因此，当你们睡眠时，意识会转向这些方向中的许多个，往往不由自主地感知到在其不同阶段可触及的零碎实相。这在某种程度上也发生在你们正常的物质焦点之下，即便在你们进行清醒活动时亦然。我所说的交替当下并非单纯是感知一个客观当下的交替方法。存在许多交替的当下，而你们只聚焦于其中之一。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: Putting this as simply as possible, your actual experience is far too vast for you to physically follow. Your particular kind of consciousness is the result of specialized focus within a particular area. You imagine it to be "absolute," in that it seems to involve an all-exclusive state that includes your identity as you think of it -- only you give it boundaries like a kingdom. It is, instead, a certain kind of organization that is indeed inviolate even while it is itself a portion of other kinds of consciousnesses, with their own points of focus. Your body itself is composed of self-aware organizations of consciousness that escape your notice and deal with perceptual material utterly alien to your own ways.
quotes.translation: 尽可能简单地说，你们实际的经验太过浩瀚，无法以物质方式追随。你们特定种类的意识，是专注于特定领域的结果。你们想象它是"绝对的"，因为它似乎包含一种排他性的状态，囊括了你们所认为的自我身份——但你们只给它划定了如王国般的边界。相反，它是一种确实不可侵犯的组织形式，即便它本身也是其他种类意识的一部分，而那些意识各有其焦点。你们的身体本身由具有自我意识的意识组织构成，这些组织逃过了你们的注意，并处理着与你们自身方式完全陌生的感知材料。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche

quotes.text: There is no end to our environment. In your terms there would be no lack of space or time in which to operate. Now this would put tremendous pressure on any consciousness without proper background and development. We do not have one simple, cozy universe in which to hide. We are still alert to other quite alien systems of reality that flash on the very outskirts of consciousness as we know it. There are far more various kinds of consciousness than there are physical forms, each with its own patterns of perception, dwelling within its own camouflage system. Yet all of these have inner knowledge of the reality that exists within all camouflage and that composes any reality, by whatever name it is called.
quotes.translation: 我们的环境没有尽头。以你们的说法，运作所需的空间或时间绝不会匮乏。这会对任何缺乏适当背景与发展的意识造成巨大压力。我们并非躲藏在一个简单舒适的宇宙中。我们仍对其它全然陌生的实相系统保持警觉，它们闪烁在我们所知的意识最边缘。存在的意识种类远比物质形态多得多，每种都有其感知模式，栖身于自身的伪装系统之中。然而，所有这些意识都内在知晓存在于所有伪装之内、并构成任何实相（无论以何名称呼）的那个实相。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: Such a primary construction allows consciousness to operate, manipulate and be perceived in the world of matter. The physical construction of consciousness never is complete as far as fulfilling the inner purpose is involved; that is, consciousness can never fully construct itself in matter, and to do so would indeed imprison such a consciousness so that it could not escape the transient nature of matter itself.
quotes.translation: 这种基本构造使意识得以在物质世界中运作、操控并被感知。就内在目的的实现而言，意识的物质构造永远无法完成；也就是说，意识永远无法在物质中完全构建自身，若真能如此，反而会囚禁这种意识，使其无法逃脱物质本身的短暂性。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)

quotes.text: Frameworks 1 and 2 obviously represent not only different kinds of reality in normal terms, but two different kinds of consciousness. To make this discussion as simple as possible for now, at least, think of these two frameworks or states of consciousness as being connected by "undifferentiated areas" in which sleep, dreaming, and certain trance states have their activity. Those undifferentiated areas are involved in the constant translation of one kind of consciousness into the other, and with energy transferences. You constantly process those data that come to you in your private life, and that information includes bulletins from all over the world, through your news broadcasts and so forth.
quotes.translation: 框架一与框架二显然不仅代表通常意义上的不同现实种类，更代表两种不同的意识。为便于当前讨论，至少可将这两种意识框架或状态视为由"未分化区域"相连，睡眠、梦境及某些出神状态的活动便发生于此。这些未分化区域参与着一种意识向另一种意识的持续转化，以及能量转换。你不断处理着私人生活中接收到的数据，这些信息包括通过新闻广播等渠道来自世界各地的简报。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: (Now here are some near-verbatim quotes from the information Seth gave Dick Bach and company on the evening of September 27, 1972: "Information does not exist by itself. Connected with it is the consciousness of all those who understand it, perceive it or originate it. So there are not records in terms of objective, forever-available banks of information into which you tune. Instead, the consciousness that held, or holds, or will hold the information attracts it like a magnet ... The information itself wants to move toward consciousness. It is not dead or inert. It is not something you grab for, it is also something that wants to be grabbed, and so it gravitates to those who seek it.
quotes.translation: （以下是赛斯在1972年9月27日晚间给予迪克·巴赫及其同伴的近乎逐字记录："信息并非独立存在。与之相连的是所有理解、感知或创造它之人的意识。因此并不存在客观的、永远可用的信息库供你调频接入。相反，曾经、正在或将要持有信息的意识会像磁铁般吸引信息……信息本身渴望趋向意识。它并非死寂或惰性之物。它不仅是你要去抓取的东西，更是渴望被抓住的存在，因此它会自然流向寻求它的人。"）
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality

quotes.text: In these sessions the full range of brain waves is utilized as you understand them. Here, in a highly creative, disciplined, and yet spontaneous performance, a situation is set up in which knowledge is obtained from the known frequencies, combined so that consciousness can use itself more fully, reaching into many areas closed to one range of consciousness alone. The various diverse, unique characteristics of each level of awareness are given play. In a way this is like an accelerated, chosen, well-organized "conscious" dream venture, in which Ruburt travels through mediums of consciousness until finally he, still being himself, is nevertheless no longer himself (humorously), but me.
quotes.translation: 在这些课程中，我们运用了你们所理解的全波段脑波。在此，通过高度创造性、纪律严明却又自发流畅的表现，我们建立了一种情境，从已知的频率中获取知识，并将其整合，使意识能够更充分地运用自身，触及那些单一意识范围无法企及的诸多领域。每一层觉知各自多样、独特的特质都得以展现。在某种程度上，这就像一场加速的、精心选择的、组织有序的“有意识”梦境探险，鲁柏在其中穿越意识的媒介，直到最终，他仍是自己，却也不再是自己（幽默地），而是我。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: To that extent, the Bible's interpretation is correct. Life was given, was free to develop according to its characteristic conditions. The planet was prepared, and endowed with life. Consciousness built the forms, so life existed within consciousness for all eternity. There was no point in which chemicals or atoms suddenly acquired life, for they always possessed consciousness, which is life's requirement.
quotes.translation: 就此而言，《圣经》的解释是正确的。生命被赋予，得以按其特有的条件自由发展。行星被预备好，并被赋予生命。意识构建了形态，因此生命在意识中永恒存在。从未有过某个时刻，化学物质或原子突然获得了生命，因为它们始终拥有意识，而意识正是生命所需。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: The consciousness always comes first, representing individualized, extremely potent bits of energy that compose the basic or inner universe. They materialize upon your plane, forming their blocks of construction. The creation that causes the whole to be more than the sum of its parts is merely the inner identities, the bulk of this consciousness not able to fully materialize upon the physical plane. In other words, no consciousness fully materializes upon the physical plane.
quotes.translation: 意识始终是第一位的，它代表着构成基本或内在宇宙的个体化、极其强大的能量单位。它们在你们的层面上具体化，形成自己的构建模块。那种使整体大于部分之和的创造，仅仅是内在身份，而意识的大部分无法在物质层面上完全具体化。换言之，没有任何意识能在物质层面上完全具体化。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)

quotes.text: Yet from the very day of the accident, this question has existed along with each step of the cleanup process, and will continue to do so: What to do with Three Mile Island, that enormously complicated human creation that now has its own consciousness, and that has in its own way exerted the force of that consciousness throughout our civilized world? To dismantle TMI seemingly would solve the "problem"--but only partially, for once born its consciousness will (like all others) continue to live. I repeat, however, that in this country no public citizen has been either seriously injured or killed in an accident at a commercial nuclear facility (as have a few workers).
quotes.translation: 然而从事故发生的当天起，这个问题就伴随着清理过程的每一步，并将持续存在：如何处理三里岛这个极其复杂的人造产物——它如今已拥有自己的意识，并以自己的方式将这种意识的力量施加于整个文明世界？拆除三里岛似乎能解决这个"问题"——但只是部分解决，因为一旦诞生，它的意识（如同所有其他意识一样）将继续存在。不过我要重申，在这个国家，没有普通公民在商业核设施事故中受重伤或死亡（虽然有少数工人遇难）。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2

quotes.text: The full personality consciousness indeed places an additional strain upon what you may call the overall body consciousness, and prolongs the sense of pain connected with that body consciousness. Each cell, as I told you, is aware. In a terminal illness, the personality consciousness, the I as you know it, bears down in panic upon the body consciousness when it does not understand the state of affairs.
quotes.translation: 完整的人格意识确实会对你所谓的整体身体意识施加额外压力，并延长与身体意识相关的痛感。如我所说，每个细胞都有觉知。在绝症中，人格意识——你所知的"我"——在不了解事态时，会恐慌地压迫身体意识。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)

quotes.text: You have, however, become so specialized in its use, so prejudiced in its favor, that your tendency is to examine all other kinds of consciousness using the reasoning mind as the only yardstick by which to judge intelligent life. You are surrounded everywhere by other kinds of consciousness whose validity you have largely ignored, whose psychic brotherhood you have dismissed--kinds of consciousness in the animal kingdom particularly, that deal with a different kind of knowing, but who share with you the reality of keen emotional experience, and who are innately aware of biological and psychic values, but in ways that have escaped your prejudiced examination.
quotes.translation: 然而你们在使用理性心智时已变得如此专精，如此偏袒它，以至于倾向于用理性心智作为唯一标准来审视所有其他类型的意识，判断它们是否为智慧生命。你们被其他类型的意识所包围，却大多忽视了它们的有效性，否定了它们与你们的心灵亲缘关系——特别是动物王国中的意识类型，它们处理着不同种类的认知，却与你们共享强烈情感体验的真实性，并且天生知晓生物与心灵的价值，只是以你们带有偏见的审视所无法理解的方式运作。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1

quotes.text: In our just-previous exercise, when I spoke of having you let your clear perception drop away, and told you to disconnect vision from hearing, you were drifting in terms of your own home station. Your consciousness was straying. This time begin with the point of your own finest focus, which you have established, then let your consciousness stray as given. Only let it stray in a particular direction -- to the right or the left, whichever seems most natural to you. In this way you are still directing it and learning to orient yourself. In the beginning, 15 minutes at most for this exercise; but let your awareness drift in whatever direction you have chosen.
quotes.translation: 在我们刚刚进行的练习中，当我让你们放下清晰的感知，并告诉你们切断视觉与听觉的联系时，你们正依循自身内在中心站的状态漂流。你们的意识正在游荡。这次请从你们已建立的最精微焦点开始，然后让意识如所述般游荡。只需让它朝特定方向——向右或向左，选择最自然的方向。如此你们仍在引导它，并学习定位自己。初期每次练习最多十五分钟；但请让觉知朝你选择的方向漂流。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: In other terms, certain portions of your own reality have long since "vanished" in the unrecognized death that your own sense of continuity has so nicely straddled. Your personal cluster of probable realities surrounds you, again, on a cellular basis, and biologically your physical body steers its own line, finding its balance operating in a cluster of probabilities while maintaining the focus that is your own. You can even learn to tune in to the cellular comprehension. It will help you realize that your consciousness is not as limited as you suppose. All realities emerge from the psyche, and from the CU's (the units of consciousness) that compose it.
quotes.translation: 换个说法，你们自身实相的某些部分早已在未被觉察的死亡中"消失"，而你们对连续性的感知却巧妙跨越了这些断层。你们个人可能实相的簇群再次以细胞为基础环绕着你们，生物层面上，肉体在概率簇中航行，在维持属于你们自己的焦点时找到平衡。你们甚至能学会调频至细胞的认知。这将帮助你们意识到意识并不如想象中受限。所有实相皆源自心灵，以及构成心灵的意识单位。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: The ego would if it could, stop personality's motion and development for the security of stability. The ego would drive the personality into preconceived channels. This consciousness-of-self--I suggest hyphens between consciousness- of-self--this consciousness-of-self is seen in man as personality, as the human personality. It appears, however, in all types of consciousness to one degree or another.
quotes.translation: 自我若有可能，会为了稳定的安全感而阻止人格的运动与发展。自我会将人格驱入预设的轨道。这种自我意识——我建议在"自我"与"意识"间加连字符——这种自我意识在人类身上显现为人格，即人类人格。然而，它以不同程度存在于所有类型的意识中。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)

quotes.text: "You" are not divorced from these other streams of consciousness in any basic way; only your focus of attention closes you off from them, and from the events in which they are involved. If you think of your stream of consciousness as transparent, however, then you can learn to look through and beneath it to others that lie in other beds of reality. You can also learn to rise above your present stream of consciousness and perceive others that run, for analogy's sake, parallel. The point is that you are only limited to the self you know if you think that you are, and if you do not realize that that self is far from your entire identity.
quotes.translation: “你”与其他意识流在根本层面上并未分离；只是你的注意力焦点将你与它们及其所涉事件隔离开来。然而，若你将自身意识流视为透明的，便能学会穿透并超越它，窥见其他存在于不同实相河床中的意识流。你也能学会超越当前意识流，感知那些——打个比方——平行流动的支流。关键在于：你之所以局限于已知的自我，只因你认定如此，且未意识到那个自我远非你的完整身份。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: Your own main consciousness has the ability to travel faster than light (as noted at 9:37), but those perceptions are too fast, and the neurologically structured patterns that you accept cannot capture them. For that matter, cellular comprehension and reaction are too fast for you to follow. The poised framework of physical existence requires a particular platform of experience that you accept as valid and real. At that level only is the universe that you know experienced. That platform or focus is the result of the finest cooperation. Your own free consciousness and your body consciousness form an alliance that makes this possible.
quotes.translation: 你自身的主要意识具备超光速旅行的能力（如9:37所述），但那些感知过于迅疾，你所接受的神经结构模式无法捕捉它们。同理，细胞的认知与反应也快到你无法追踪。物质存在的平衡框架需要一个特定的经验平台，你将其视为有效且真实。唯有在此层面上，你所知的宇宙才得以被体验。这个平台或焦点是精妙协作的成果——你自由的意识与身体意识结盟，使这一切成为可能。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: In this respect, you see, there is a personal god, if those are the words you use. There is a portion of All That Is, that is directed and focused upon every individual consciousness. A portion of All That Is resides within and is a part of every consciousness. Every consciousness is therefore cherished and protected individually. There are automatic electromagnetic connections that exist here.
quotes.translation: 就此而言，你看，若你使用“神”这个字眼，那么确实存在一位“人格神”。一切万有中有一部分，被导向并聚焦于每一个体意识。一切万有的一部分栖居于每个意识之内，并成为其组成部分。因此，每个意识都受到珍视与个别保护。此处存在着自动的电磁连接。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)

quotes.text: The experience of your species involves a certain kind of consciousness development, highly vital. (Pause.) This necessitated a certain kind of specialization, a certain "long-term" identification with form. Cellular structure maintains brilliant effectiveness in the body's present reality, but knows itself free of it. Man's particular kind of consciousness fiercely identified with the body. This was a necessity to focus energy toward physical manipulation. To some important extent the same applies to the animals. The cell might gladly "die," but the specifically oriented man-and-animal consciousness would not so willingly let go.
quotes.translation: 你们物种的经验涉及某种意识的发展，极具活力。（停顿。）这需要某种专门化，某种与形态的"长期"认同。细胞结构在身体的当前实相中维持着辉煌的效能，但自知不受其束缚。人类特有的意识强烈认同于身体。这是将能量聚焦于物质操控的必要条件。在相当重要的程度上，动物也是如此。细胞或许欣然"死去"，但特定导向的人类与动物意识却不会如此轻易放手。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1

quotes.text: Projection from a dream state is something else again, therefore, and when it is executed successfully then you have a fine example of the self as it changes the focus of its own awareness. Here the critical consciousness is quite apparent, while the body sleeps. Projections occur quite naturally under these conditions. For the development of the whole self however, and for the perfection of such experiences, it is beneficial that such projections be carried out by the conscious wish of the projector. You learn therefore to manipulate your own consciousness in different realities than the ones with which you are usually concerned.
quotes.translation: 因此，从梦境状态投射是另一回事。当成功执行时，你们便获得了一个绝佳范例：自我如何改变自身意识的焦点。在此，当身体沉睡时，批判性意识相当明显。在这些条件下，投射自然发生。然而，为了整体自我的发展，以及此类经验的完善，由投射者清醒的意愿来执行投射是有益的。你们因此学会在不同于平常关注的实相中操控自己的意识。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 6 of The Seth Material (Sessions 240-280)

quotes.text: All manner of insects, birds and beasts cooperate in this venture, producing the natural environment. This is as normal and inevitable as the fact that your breath causes a mist to form on glass if you breathe upon it. All consciousness creates the world, rising out of feeling-tone. It is a natural product of what your consciousness is. Feelings and emotions emerge into reality in certain specific ways. Thoughts appear, growing on the bed already laid. The seasons spring up, formed by ancient feeling-tones, having deep and abiding rhythms. They are the result, again, of innate creative aspects that are a portion of all life.
quotes.translation: 各类昆虫、鸟类与兽类皆参与这场冒险，共同营造自然环境。这正如你对着玻璃呼气必会结雾般自然且必然。一切意识皆从感受基调中涌现，创造着世界。这是你意识本质的自然产物。感受与情绪以特定方式显化为实相。思想萌生于已然铺就的沃土。季节由古老的感受基调催生，携带着深沉持久的韵律。它们再次源于一切生命共有的先天创造力。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality

quotes.text: The basis and firm groundwork of the material, and its primary contribution, lies in the concept that consciousness itself indeed creates matter, that consciousness is not imprisoned by matter but forms it, and that consciousness is not limited or bound by time or space; time and space in your terms being necessary distortions, or adopted conditions, forming a strata for physical existence.
quotes.translation: 物质世界的基础根基与首要贡献，在于意识确实创造物质这一概念——意识不被物质囚禁，反而塑造物质；意识不受时空限制或束缚；你们所谓的时空是必要的扭曲或适应条件，为物质存在构建了层叠结构。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)

quotes.text: Give us a moment ... Other focuses of consciousness besides your own have different concepts of time, and are actually more biologically correct, in that they have greater knowledge of both cellular and spiritual realities. There is nothing "wrong" with your present habitual kind of consciousness, any more than there is anything wrong with speaking only one language. There is within you, however, the impetus to explore, to expand, to create, and that will automatically lead you to explore inner lands of consciousness; as, in your terms, it has led you to explore the other countries of the physical world.
quotes.translation: 请稍等片刻……除了你们自己的意识之外，其他意识焦点拥有不同的时间概念，实际上更符合生物学真相，因为它们对细胞实相和灵性实相都有更深刻的认识。你们目前习惯性的意识形态并无任何"错误"，就像只会说一种语言也并无不妥。然而，你们内在存在着探索、扩展和创造的动力，这自然会引导你们探索意识的内在领域——正如用你们的话来说，这动力曾引导你们探索物质世界的其他国家。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: The fetus dreams. As its physical growth takes place in the womb, so the shaping of its consciousness is also extended by genetic dreams. These particular fetus-oriented dreams are most difficult to describe, for they are actually involved with forming the contours of the individual consciousness. Such dreams provide (pause) the subjective understanding from which thoughts are developed, and in those terms complete thoughts are possible before the brain itself is fully formed. It is the process of thinking that helps bring the brain into activity, and not the other way around (all quite intently).
quotes.translation: 胎儿会做梦。当胎儿在子宫中进行生理成长时，其意识的塑造也通过遗传梦境得以扩展。这些特定于胎儿的梦境极难描述，因为它们实际上参与塑造个体意识的轮廓。这类梦境提供（停顿）思想得以发展的主观理解，用那些术语来说，在大脑完全形成之前，完整的思想是可能存在的。正是思考过程帮助激活大脑，而非相反（语气相当专注）。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2

quotes.text: (Very actively delivered:) In your current beliefs, again, consciousness is equated in very limited terms with your conception of intellectual behavior: you consider this to be a peak of mental achievement, growing from the "undifferentiated" perceptions of childhood, and returning ignominiously to them again in old age. Such wake-sleep patterns as I have suggested would acquaint you with the great creative and energetic portions of psychological behavior -- that are not undifferentiated at all, but simply distinct from your usual concepts of consciousness; and these operate throughout your life.
quotes.translation: （非常活跃地传达：）在你们当前的信念中，意识再次被狭隘地等同于你们对智力行为的概念：你们视其为心智成就的巅峰，从童年"未分化"的感知中发展而来，又在老年时屈辱地回归这种状态。我建议的这类醒睡模式将使你们熟悉心理行为中那些伟大而充满活力的部分——这些部分绝非未分化，只是与你们通常的意识概念截然不同；并且它们贯穿你们整个生命。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality

quotes.text: There are also even deeper biological connections with the heart of nature. You are biological creatures. Your proud human consciousness rests on the vast "unconscious" integrity of your physical being. In that regard your consciousness is as natural as your toe. In terms of the species' integrity your mental states are, then, highly important. Despair or apathy is a biological "enemy." Social conditions, political states, economic policies, and even religious or philosophical frameworks that foster such mental states, bring about a biological retaliation. They act like fire applied to a plant.
quotes.translation: 还存在与自然之心更深层的生物联系。你们是生物性的造物。你们引以为傲的人类意识，建立在肉体存在的广大"无意识"完整性之上。就此而言，你们的意识与你们的脚趾一样自然。就物种的完整性而言，你们的心智状态极为重要。绝望或冷漠是生物性的"敌人"。助长这类心智状态的社会条件、政治体制、经济政策，甚至宗教或哲学框架，都会引发生物性的反击。它们就像施加在植物上的火焰。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: For one thing, while pain is unpleasant it is also a method of familiarizing the self against the edges of quickened consciousness. Any heightened sensation, pleasant or unpleasant, has a stimulating effect upon a consciousness to some degree. It is a strong awareness of activity and life. Where the stimulus may be extremely annoying, and humiliatingly unpleasant, certain portions of the psychological framework accept it indiscriminatingly because it is a sensation, and a vivid one. This acquiescence to even painful stimuli is a basic part of the nature of consciousness, and a necessary one.
quotes.translation: 首先，痛苦虽令人不快，却也是让自我熟悉意识加速边缘的方法。任何强烈的感受，无论愉悦与否，都在某种程度上对意识产生刺激作用。这是对活动与生命的强烈觉知。当刺激可能极度恼人且令人难堪时，心理结构的某些部分会不加区分地接纳它，因为这是一种感受，而且是鲜活的感受。这种对痛苦刺激的默许，是意识本质的基本组成部分，也是必要的组成部分。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)

quotes.text: The next adjacent level now would be A-1-c, which is an extension of the one just given, in which there is greater freedom of action, mobility and experience. Here to some extent there is some participation in the events perceived. There is no need to go deeply into any of these beyond this point, because ordinarily you will not be involved with them, and they lead into realities that have little reference to your own. They are states of consciousness too divorced, and under usual circumstances, this is as far as your present consciousness is able to go in that particular direction.
quotes.translation: 下一个相邻层次是A-1-c，这是对刚才所述层次的延伸，在此有更大的行动自由、机动性和体验。在此，对感知到的事件有某种程度的参与。无需深入探讨这些层次，因为通常你们不会涉及它们，它们会导向与你们自身现实关联甚少的实相。这些意识状态过于疏离，在通常情况下，这是你们当前意识在特定方向上所能达到的极限。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: Consciousness or action forms all realities. What is not simply represents a possibility which consciousness may bring to life. (Long pause.) Consciousness then formed out of itself a new dimension which was the physical one. The formation, the explosion, of energy, shattered consciousness into an infinity of parts, each with all the abilities in here within consciousness itself.
quotes.translation: 意识或行动形成了一切实相。未被实现的仅代表一种可能性，意识可将其赋予生命。（长停顿）意识随后从自身创造出物理维度这一新向度。能量的形成与爆发将意识粉碎为无限碎片，每个碎片都蕴含意识本身的所有能力。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)

quotes.text: The consciousness of that myth can indeed have no origin, for the myth precludes anything but a physically-oriented and physically-mechanized consciousness. Not only could that consciousness have no existence before or after death, but obviously it could have no access to knowledge that was not physically acquired. It is this myth that hampers your understanding most of all, and that closes you off from the greater nature of those events with which you are most intimately concerned. That myth also makes your own involvement with mass events sometimes appear incomprehensible.
quotes.translation: 那个神话中的意识确实没有起源，因为该神话排除了任何非物理导向、非物理机械化的意识。这种意识不仅在生前死后无法存在，显然也无法获取非物理习得的知识。正是这个神话最严重地阻碍了你的理解，使你与最切身相关事件的更大本质隔绝开来。这个神话也让你与群体事件的关联有时显得难以理解。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: Because you identify your experience with the regular line of consciousness with which you are familiar, you are rarely able to "bring in" any "other-self" material and hold it while retaining your own sense of identity. Such material may at times bleed or intrude into your own thought, where it blends and is not recognized. In such cases, it takes on the coloration of your own thought patterns. It adds to the overall atmosphere of your being. Without understanding or training, you would have to "lose" your own consciousness in order to perceive the "other-consciousness."
quotes.translation: 由于你将自身经验等同于所熟悉的常规意识流，你很少能在保持自我认同感的同时"纳入"任何"他我"资料。这类资料有时会渗入或侵入你的思想，在其中融合而未被识别。此时它会染上你自身思维模式的色彩，为你存在的整体氛围增添层次。未经理解或训练，你必须"失去"自身意识才能感知"他者意识"。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1

quotes.text: If life within the physical organism is to be maintained, then consciousness must return to it. This does not mean that consciousness is dependent upon the physical organism. There will be [an] endless series of projections in other existences, and these will have no chemical basis as you know it. Your traveling consciousness appears as an apparition within some other systems.
quotes.translation: 若要维持肉体有机体中的生命，意识必须回归其中。这并不意味着意识依赖于肉体有机体。在其他存在中会有无穷无尽的投射系列，这些投射没有你所知的化学基础。你旅行的意识在其他某些系统中显现为幻影。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 6 of The Seth Material (Sessions 240-280)

quotes.text: Consciousness is a way of perceiving the various dimensions of reality. Consciousness as you know it is highly specialized. The physical senses allow you to perceive the three-dimensional world, and yet by their very nature they can inhibit the perception of other equally valid dimensions. Most of you identify with your daily physically oriented self. You would not think of identifying with one portion of your body and ignoring all other parts, and yet you are doing the same thing (smile) when you imagine that the egotistical self carries the burden of your identity.
quotes.translation: 意识是感知实相各个维度的一种方式。你所知的意识是高度专门化的。肉体感官让你能感知三维世界，然而就其本质而言，它们也会抑制对其他同样有效维度的感知。你们大多数人认同于日常以肉体为导向的自己。你不会想到认同于身体的一部分而忽略所有其他部分，然而当你想象自我背负着你的身份重担时，你正在做同样的事（微笑）。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: The constant interchange that exists biologically means that the same physical stuff that composes a man or a woman may be dispersed, and later form a toad, a starfish, a dog or a flower. It may be distributed into numberless different forms. That arithmetic11 of consciousness is not annihilated. It is multiplied and not divided. Reminiscent within each form is the consciousness of all the other combinations, all of the other alliances, as identity continually forms new creative endeavors and gestalts of relatedness. There is no discrimination, no prejudice.
quotes.translation: 生物层面存在的持续交换意味着，构成男人或女人的相同物质材料可能会被分散，随后形成蟾蜍、海星、狗或花朵。它可能被分配到无数不同的形态中。意识的算术不会被消灭，它会被倍增而非分割。在每个形态中，都存有所有其他组合、所有其他联盟的意识记忆，如同身份认同持续形成新的创造性努力与关联性的完形。这里没有歧视，没有偏见。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: Ruburt wondered later if I dreamed. My own usual state of consciousness is far different from yours. I do not alternate between waking and sleeping as you do. Still, I have states of consciousness that could be compared to your dream state, in that I am myself not as involved in them as I am in others. If I said to you, "I control my dream state," you might have an idea of what I mean. Yet I do not control my dreams -- I fulfill them. What you could call my dreaming state is involved with the levels I spoke of that exist beneath your remembered dreams.
quotes.translation: 鲁柏后来好奇我是否做梦。我惯常的意识状态与你们大相径庭。我不像你们那样在清醒与睡眠间交替。不过，我确实拥有可类比于你们梦境状态的存在状态——区别在于，我自身对这些状态的投入程度不及其他状态。若我对你说"我掌控着自己的梦境"，你或许能理解我的意思。然而我并非掌控梦境——而是实现它们。你们可称之为我"做梦状态"的领域，与我提及的那些存在于你们记忆梦境之下的层次息息相关。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche

quotes.text: You think that your own consciousness is the only logical culmination of your body's reality. You read yourself in a certain accepted fashion. In the "entire book of life," however, just physically speaking, there are interrelationships on adjacent levels that you do not perceive, as other portions of your own biological consciousness or biological language relate to the entire living fabric of the world. In physical terms you are alive because of substructures -- psychic, spiritual, and biological -- of which you have hardly any comprehension at all.
quotes.translation: 你以为自己的意识是你身体实相唯一合乎逻辑的终极结果。你以某种公认的方式解读自己。然而，在"生命全书"中，仅就物质层面而言，存在着你无法感知的相邻层次间的相互关系，正如你自身生物意识或生物语言的其他部分与整个世界生命织体相关联。就物质层面来说，你之所以活着，是因为你几乎完全不了解的次结构——心灵的、灵性的和生物的。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche

quotes.text: (10:10.) There are affiliations of a most "sophisticated" fashion that leap even the boundaries of the species. You look upon your cultural world with its art and manufacture, its cities, technology, and the cultivated use of the intellectual mind. You count your religions, sciences, archeologies, and triumphs over the environment, and it seems to you that no other consciousness has wrought what man's has produced. Those "products" of your consciousness are indeed unique, creative, and form a characteristic mosaic that has its own beauty and elegance.
quotes.translation: （10:10。）存在着最为"精妙"的关联，甚至跨越了物种的界限。你审视自己的文化世界，包括其艺术与制造、城市、科技，以及智性心智的培育运用。你珍视自己的宗教、科学、考古学，以及战胜环境的成就，似乎没有其他意识能创造出人类所创造的成果。你意识的那些"产物"确实独特而富有创造力，形成了具有自身美感和优雅的特有马赛克。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche

quotes.text: Your world, again, is the result of a certain focus of consciousness, without which that world cannot be perceived. Period. The range of consciousness involved is obviously physically oriented, yet within it there are great varieties of consciousness, each experiencing that seemingly objective world from a private perspective. The physical environment is real in different terms to an animal, a fish, a man, or a rock, for example, and different portions of that environment are correspondingly unreal [to each of those forms]. This is highly important.
quotes.translation: 你的世界，再次强调，是特定意识焦点的结果，没有这个焦点就无法感知那个世界。句号。所涉及的意识范围显然以物质为导向，但其中存在着多种多样的意识，每种意识都从私密的视角体验那个看似客观的世界。物质环境对动物、鱼、人或岩石而言，其真实性各不相同，相应地，该环境的不同部分（对每种形态）也显得不真实。这一点极为重要。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: This may hardly be original thinking here, but these proliferations of consciousness imply some pretty fantastic abilities on the part of we humans--for such developments show that even though we live as small creatures within the incredible richness of an overall consciousness, or All That Is, still our actions can result in that great consciousness exploring new areas of itself. Quite awesome creative abilities on our part, I'd say, and ones that unknowingly we take for granted. We do this all of the time, of course, individually and collectively.
quotes.translation: 这或许算不上什么独创性思想，但这些意识的增殖暗示着人类自身具备某些相当惊人的能力——因为这类发展表明，即便我们作为渺小的生物存在于整体意识（或"一切万有"）那不可思议的丰饶之中，我们的行动仍能使那伟大意识探索自身的新领域。我得说，我们拥有相当令人敬畏的创造能力，而这些能力在不知不觉中被我们视为理所当然。当然，我们无时无刻不在个体与集体层面进行着这种创造。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1

quotes.text: The psyche is not male or female. In your system of beliefs, however, it is often identified as feminine, along with the artistic productions that emerge from its creativity. In that context, the day hours and waking consciousness are thought of as masculine, along with the sun -- while the nighttime, the moon, and the dreaming consciousness are considered feminine or passive. In the same manner, aggression is usually understood to be violent assertive action, male-oriented, while female elements are identified in terms of the nurturing principle.
quotes.translation: 心灵并无性别之分。然而在你们的信念体系中，它常被认定为阴性特质，连同其创造力所生发的艺术产物。在此脉络下，白昼与清醒意识被视为阳性特质，与太阳同属；而夜晚、月亮与梦境意识则被视为阴性或被动特质。同样地，攻击性通常被理解为暴力性的主动行为，偏向男性取向；而女性元素则被界定为滋养原则。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche

quotes.text: "Using these side pockets or pools where data are still unprocessed, in our terms, you can pick up several other strands of your own consciousness 'at once,' though retention may be difficult. Explaining the experience to the normal consciousness automatically helps expand it (the normal consciousness), so that each time the process becomes easier. Until, with practice, experience and data from several areas can be held simultaneously. The difficulty then is a translation in linear terms, hence Ruburt's trouble in the Saratoga episode."
quotes.translation: "利用这些储存未处理数据的侧袋或蓄水池（以我们的说法），你可以'同时'拾取自身意识的其他几股丝线，尽管记忆留存可能较为困难。向正常意识解释这种体验，会自动帮助扩展该意识（即正常意识），因此每次操作都会变得更容易。直到通过练习，来自多个领域的经验与数据能够被同时持有。此时的困难在于如何以线性方式进行转译——这正是鲁柏在萨拉托加事件中遭遇的困境。"
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1

quotes.text: Action may show itself as motion, but it is much more than motion in the terms which you usually use, and motion is but one small dimension within actions realm. All types of consciousness represent a different focus of energy's perception within itself. There is no past or future to action. All action is simultaneous. Identities, some identities and some forms of consciousness, particularly the ego, perceive a past or a present, but this is merely the result of the manner in which such identities and consciousness view available data.
quotes.translation: 行动可能表现为运动，但它远不止于你们通常所理解的运动，而运动只是行动领域内的一个微小维度。所有类型的意识都代表能量在其自身内部感知的不同焦点。行动没有过去或未来，所有行动都是同时发生的。某些身份和某些形式的意识，特别是自我，会感知到过去或现在，但这仅仅是这些身份和意识看待可用数据的方式所导致的结果。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)

quotes.text: Consciousness -- human consciousness -- is not dependent upon the tissues, and yet there is no physical matter that is not brought into being by some portion of consciousness. For example, when your individual consciousness has left the body in a way that I will shortly explain, then the simple consciousnesses of atoms and molecules remain, and are not annihilated.
quotes.translation: 意识——人类的意识——并不依赖于组织，然而没有任何物质不是由意识的某一部分所创造。例如，当你们的个体意识以我将要解释的方式离开身体时，原子和分子的简单意识仍然存在，并不会被消灭。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: Now in the same way that consciousness originated the physical dimension, you as portions of consciousness continue to maintain and create it anew. Your source of energy is that first creation, in which consciousness focused, where before it had not. You also then know and develop yourselves through your own creations, and you automatically create and maintain your physical environment in the same manner that you breathe. Your physical image and your physical environment are both materialized extensions of your inner selves.
quotes.translation: 正如意识创造了物质世界，你们作为意识的一部分，持续维系并重新创造它。你们的能量源头正是那最初的创造——意识在此聚焦，而此前它从未如此。你们也通过自己的创造来认识和发展自我，如同呼吸般自动创造并维系着物质环境。你们的形体与物质环境，皆是内在自我的物质化延伸。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)

quotes.text: In many ways this is a cozy concept, though to some it can be quite frightening with its connotations of eternal damnation. It is far too tidy an idea, however, to hint at the rich embellishments that are at the heart of divine creativity. The soul stands both within and without the fabric of physical life as you know it. You are not separated from the animals and the rest of existence by virtue of possessing an eternal inner consciousness. Such a consciousness is present within all living beings, and in all forms.
quotes.translation: 从许多方面看，这是个舒适的概念，尽管其永罚的暗示对某些人而言相当骇人。但这想法过于工整，无法触及神圣创造力核心那丰饶的装饰。灵魂既存在于你们所知的物质生命织锦之内，也超然于其外。你们并非因拥有永恒的内在意识而与动物及其他存在分离——这种意识存在于所有生灵与一切形态之中。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: In your probability you did allow the inner self some freedom. Therefore, the so-called egotistical consciousness was not given complete sway. It remained flexible enough so that even hidden in its god concepts4 there were symbols of greater reality. Your system deals with physical manipulability, again, and the translation of creativity into physical form. An exterior separation had to occur for a while, in which consciousness forgot, egotistically speaking, that it was a part of nature, and pretended to be apart.
quotes.translation: 在你的概率中，你确实允许内在自我拥有一些自由。因此，所谓的自我意识并未被赋予完全的主导权。它保持了足够的灵活性，以至于即使隐藏在其神祇概念中，也存在着更伟大实相的表征。你的系统再次涉及物理可操作性，以及将创造力转化为物理形态。必须暂时发生一种外在的分离，在这种分离中，从自我的角度来说，意识忘记了它是自然的一部分，并假装与之分离。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1

quotes.text: Now: In the same way you could not, practically speaking, experience such other-consciousness (with a hyphen) unless you learned to stand somewhat aside, like the writer in Ruburt's remarks. Period. But even if you did, the very experience of other-consciousness itself would supersede your living space. You would need another self, able to hold both lines of consciousness at once, lost in neither but maintaining footing in each. This would be a very difficult achievement in normal life in any sustained fashion.
quotes.translation: 现在：同样地，实际上，你无法体验这种他者意识（带连字符），除非你学会稍微退后一步，就像鲁柏笔记中的那位作家一样。句号。但即使你做到了，他者意识的体验本身也会超越你的生活空间。你需要另一个自我，能够同时持有两条意识线，既不迷失于其中任何一条，又在每条线上保持立足点。这在正常生活中以任何持续的方式都是一项非常困难的成就。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1

quotes.text: There are too many varieties of such dreams to discuss here, but they all involve consciousness dispersing, yet retaining its identity, consciousness making loops with itself. Such dreams involve other sequences than the ones with which you are familiar. They hint at the true dimensions of consciousness that are usually unavailable to you, for you actually form your own historical world in the same manner, in that above all other experiences that one world is predominant, and played on the screen of your brain.
quotes.translation: 此类梦境的变体太多，无法在此一一讨论，但它们都涉及意识的分散，同时保留其身份，意识与自己形成循环。这些梦境涉及与你所熟悉的序列不同的其他序列。它们暗示了通常对你不可用的意识的真实维度，因为你实际上以同样的方式形成了你自己的历史世界，在所有其他体验中，那个世界是主导的，并在你大脑的屏幕上播放。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche

quotes.text: "Imagine a body with a fully operating body consciousness, not diseased or defective, but without the overriding ego-directed consciousness that you have. The sleepwalkers' physical abilities surpassed yours. They were as agile as animals, their purpose simply to be. Their main points of consciousness were elsewhere, their primary focuses scarcely aware of the bodies they had created. Yet they learned 'through experience,' and began to 'awaken,' to become aware of themselves, to discover time, or to create it.
quotes.translation: 想象一个拥有完全运作的身体意识的身体，没有疾病或缺陷，但缺乏你所拥有的那种主导性的自我导向意识。梦游者的身体能力超越了你。他们像动物一样敏捷，其目的仅仅是存在。他们的意识主要点位于别处，主要焦点几乎不察觉他们自己所创造的身体。然而，他们“通过经验学习”，并开始“觉醒”，意识到自己，发现时间，或创造时间。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1

quotes.text: Energy or action is composed of an infinity of itself, and yet, forever acting upon itself, it forms forever new portions of itself. Each action causes another. Now until we have done more talking you will simply have to take my word, for the sake of our discussion--our one-way discussion--that any action or energy possesses consciousness seeking to know itself, therefore; and acting within itself it forms new consciousness that are individual and independent, and yet connected to every other consciousness.
quotes.translation: 能量或行动由自身的无限组成，然而，永远作用于自身，它形成自身永远新的部分。每个行动引发另一个行动。现在，在我们进行更多讨论之前，你只需暂且相信我的话——为了我们这场单向讨论——任何行动或能量都拥有寻求认识自身的意识；因此，在自身内部运作时，它形成新的意识，这些意识既个体独立，又与每一个其他意识相连。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)

quotes.text: Consciousness is composed of energy, with everything that implies. The psyche, then, can be thought of as a conglomeration of highly charged "particles" of energy, following rules and properties, many simply unknown to you. On other levels, laws of dynamics apply to the energy sources of the self. Think of a given "self" as a nucleus of an energy gestalt of consciousness. That nucleus, according to its intensity, will attract to it certain masses of the entire energy patterns available to a given identity.
quotes.translation: 意识由能量组成，包含其一切含义。那么，心灵可以被视为高度带电的能量“粒子”的集合体，遵循规则和属性，其中许多你根本不知道。在其他层面上，动力学定律适用于自我的能量来源。将某个给定的“自我”视为意识能量完形的核心。这个核心，根据其强度，会吸引特定身份可用的整个能量模式中的某些质量。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1

quotes.text: Now the period just before dawn often represents a crisis point for persons severely ill. Consciousness has been away from the body for too long a period, and such a returning consciousness then has difficulty dealing with the sick body mechanism. The practice in hospitals of giving drugs to patients so that they will sleep entirely throughout the night is detrimental for this reason. In many cases it is too great a strain on the part of the returning consciousness to take over again the ailing mechanism.
quotes.translation: 现在，黎明前的时段往往代表重病患者的危机点。意识离开身体的时间过长，这样的意识返回时便难以处理病弱的身体机制。医院让病人服药以整夜沉睡的做法，因此是有害的。在许多情况下，这会使返回的意识在重新接管病弱机制时承受过大的压力。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: Now the same sort of behavior occurs on a deep, basic, secret, and unexplored psychological level. The physically oriented consciousness, responding to one phase of the atom's activity, comes alive and awake to its particular existence, but in between are other fluctuations in which consciousness is focused upon entirely different systems of reality; each of these coming awake and responding, and each one having no sense of absence, and memory only of those particular fluctuations to which they respond.
quotes.translation: 同样的行为也发生在深层、基本、隐秘且未经探索的心理层面。以物质为导向的意识，响应原子活动的某一阶段，对其特定存在苏醒并清醒；但在其间还有其他的波动，其中意识聚焦于完全不同的实相系统；每一个这样的意识都苏醒并响应，每一个都没有缺席感，只记得它们所响应的那些特定波动。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: This process, you see, continues so smoothly that you are not aware of it. The pulses mentioned earlier are so short in duration that your consciousness skips over them merrily, yet your physical perception cannot seem to bridge the gap when the longer rhythm of pulsation occurs. And so this is the time that you perceive as death. What you want to know, therefore, is what happens when your consciousness is directed away from physical reality, and when momentarily it seems to have no image to wear.
quotes.translation: 你看，这个过程进行得如此顺畅，以至于你毫无察觉。先前提到的脉冲持续时间极短，你的意识能轻快地跳过它们，但当较长的脉动节奏出现时，你的物质感知似乎无法跨越这个间隙。因此，这就是你感知为死亡的时刻。所以你想知道的是，当你的意识被导向远离物质实相，且暂时似乎没有可穿戴的影像时，究竟会发生什么。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: All of the probabilities practically possible in human development are therefore present to some extent or another in each individual. Any biological or spiritual advancement that you might imagine will of course not come from any outside agency, but from within the heritage of consciousness made flesh. Generally, those alive in this century chose a particular kind of orientation. The species chose to specialize in certain kinds of physical manipulation, to devote its energies in certain directions. Those directions have brought forth a reality unique in its own fashion. Man has not driven himself down a blind alley, in other words. He has been studying the nature of his consciousness -- using it as if it were apart from the rest of nature, and therefore seeing nature and the world in a particular light.6 That light has finally made him feel isolated, alone, and to some extent relatively powerless (intently).
quotes.translation: 因此，人类发展中实际可能的所有可能性，都在某种程度上存在于每个个体之中。你所能想象的任何生物或灵性进步，当然不会来自任何外在力量，而是源于意识化为肉身的内在传承。一般而言，活在这个世纪的人们选择了特定的取向。这个物种选择专精于某些物质操控方式，将能量投入特定方向。这些方向已催生出一种独特形式的实相。换言之，人类并未将自己逼入死胡同。他一直在研究自身意识的本质——仿佛将其视为与自然其余部分分离的存在，因此以特定视角看待自然与世界。这种视角最终让他感到孤立、孤独，并在某种程度上相对无力（专注地）。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: (4. Seth also said that it would be "not practical" and "boring" for him to relive his life as a pope, then added: "In those terms, many people do choose to reexperience what you would think of as a past existence in order to change it as they go along." Yet this reexperiencing of a life is a different thing from the original one. Again in Seth Speaks, see Seth's material at 10:07 in the 539th session for Chapter 10: "You may perfect [that past life], in other words, but you cannot again enter into that frame of reference as a completely participating consciousness -- following -- say, the historic trends of the time, joining into the mass-hallucinated existence that resulted from the applied consciousness of your self and your 'contemporaries.'"
quotes.translation: 赛斯还说他若重活一次当教皇的人生会“不实际”且“无聊”，接着补充道：“就那些说法而言，许多人确实选择重新体验你们所谓的前世，以便在过程中改变它。”然而这种对人生的重新体验与原本的人生截然不同。在《赛斯书》第539节第10章10:07处的资料中再次提到：“换言之，你可以完善那（前世人生），但无法再以完全参与的觉知——跟随——比方说，当时的历史潮流，融入由你与‘同时代人’的运用意识所引发的群体幻觉存在。”
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: (I'm projecting my own ideas here, but I think that in all of its complexity DNA has motives for its physical existence [as mediated through Seth's CU's, or units of consciousness] that considerably enlarge upon its assigned function as the "master molecule" of life as we know it. Deoxyribonucleic acid may exist within its host, whether man, plant, or animal -- or bacteria or virus -- in cooperative altruistic ventures with its carrier that are quite beside purely survival ones. Some of those goals, such as the exploration of concepts like the moment point [see Note 11], or probabilities [and reincarnation16], really defy our ordinary conscious perception. In terms we can more easily grasp, social relationships within and between species may be explored, starting at that biochemical level and working "upward." Basically, then, an overall genetics of cooperation becomes a truer long-run concept than the postulated deadly struggle for survival of the fittest, whether between man and molecules, say, or among members of the same species. Once again we have consciousness seeking to know itself in as many ways as possible, while being aware all of the time, in those terms, of the forthcoming "death" of its medium of expression, DNA, and of DNA's host, or "physical machine."
quotes.translation: 我在此投射个人观点，但认为DNA在其复杂性中对其物质存在（经由赛斯的意识单位（CU）中介）怀有动机，这大大扩展了其作为生命“主分子”的既定功能。脱氧核糖核酸可能存在于其宿主（无论人类、植物、动物——或细菌、病毒）中，与载体进行超越纯粹生存的合作利他冒险。某些目标（如探索“瞬间点”或概率（及转世）概念）确实超越我们寻常的显意识感知。用更易理解的说法，物种内部及物种间的社会关系可从生化层面开始探索，并“向上”延伸。因此根本上，合作的整体遗传学比假设的适者生存致命斗争（无论分子间或同物种成员间）更符合长期概念。我们再次看到意识寻求以尽可能多的方式认识自身，同时始终知晓其表达媒介DNA及DNA宿主（即“物理机器”）即将到来的“死亡”。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: When the physical origin of your universe is finally discovered, your scientists will be no better off than they are now. They will immediately be up against the problem that above all others they have avoided for so long, that of the origin behind the origin. The simple fact needs restating. The physical universe, and everything in it, is the result of consciousness. It did not evolve consciousness. To the contrary, consciousness not only created the physical universe, but continues to do so.
quotes.translation: 当你们宇宙的物理起源最终被发现时，你们的科学家并不会比现在好到哪里去。他们将立即面对那个他们长久以来一直回避的问题——起源背后的起源。这个简单的事实需要重述：物质宇宙及其中的一切，都是意识的结果。意识并非由物质宇宙演化而来。恰恰相反，意识不仅创造了物质宇宙，而且仍在持续创造它。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)

quotes.text: Consider this analogy. For one instant your consciousness is "alive," focused in physical reality. Now for the next instant it is focused somewhere else entirely, in a different system of reality. It is unalive, or "dead" to your way of thinking. The next instant it is "alive" again, focused in your reality, but you are not aware of the intervening instant of unaliveness. Your sense of continuity therefore is built up entirely on every other pulsation of consciousness. Is that clear to you?
quotes.translation: 请考虑这个类比。在一瞬间，你的意识"活着"，聚焦于物质实相。在下一瞬间，它完全聚焦于别处，一个不同的实相系统。以你的思维方式，它"不活"或"死了"。再下一瞬间，它又"活"过来，聚焦于你的实相，但你并未察觉中间那个"不活"的瞬间。因此，你的连续感完全建立在意识每一次脉动的交替之上。这对你来说清楚吗？
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: The official line does have its role, of course -- but once again, by itself it must remain isolated from the deep, creative, healing functions of body consciousness. The official line of consciousness is really the "worrier," because it recognizes that it can only go so far, and usually it is not educated enough to realize it is itself sustained and supported -- and now I bid you a fond good afternoon, and I do indeed adjust those coordinates that do quicken Ruburt's healing processes.
quotes.translation: 当然，官方路线确实有其作用——但再次强调，它本身必然与身体意识的深层、创造性、疗愈功能隔绝。官方意识路线其实是"忧虑者"，因为它认识到自己只能走到某一步，而且通常它所受的教育不足以让它明白自己本身是被维持和支持的——现在我要向你们道一声愉快的下午好，我确实在调整那些加速鲁柏疗愈过程的坐标。
quotes.book: The Way Toward Health

quotes.text: What you consider your consciousness, or your self, or your thinking ego, represents of course only one portion of your entire consciousness, that part which you are using at this time. It is as if, for example, consciousness of any whole self were compared to a huge, and indeed almost infinite light, with the ability inherent in the light to focus in many directions; to be diffused, as if the light had many switches that would turn it to greater or lesser intensities and directions.
quotes.translation: 你所谓的意识、自我或思考的自我，当然只代表你整体意识的一部分，即你此刻正在运用的那一部分。举例来说，整体自我的意识好比一个巨大、甚至近乎无限的光源，其内在能力可向多个方向聚焦；也可扩散开来，仿佛这光源有许多开关，能调节其强度与方向。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)

quotes.text: It should be fairly easy to understand now how the second dilemma evolved from the first. I have said that the second dilemma resulted in, and constantly results in, consciousness of self. Now. Consciousness of self is not the same thing as consciousness of ego self. Consciousness of self is still consciousness directly connected with action.
quotes.translation: 现在应该很容易理解第二个困境是如何从第一个演变而来的了。我曾说过，第二个困境导致了、并持续导致着自我意识。请注意，自我意识与自我本位的意识并非同一回事。自我意识仍然是直接与行动相连的意识。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)

quotes.text: Latently, your consciousness is capable of performing these feats, but the work cannot be done with the part of your consciousness that is strongly attached to the space-time relationship. What you think of as your conscious mind is given the task of assessing the "facts" of daily living. It then forms beliefs about reality, and these are used in the dream state as one of the main yardsticks, so to speak, that activate the emergence of certain probable events rather than others.
quotes.translation: 你的意识潜在地能够完成这些壮举，但这项工作无法由你那强烈依附于时空关系的意识部分来完成。你所谓的显意识被赋予了评估日常"事实"的任务。它随后形成对实相的信念，而这些信念在梦境状态中被用作主要衡量标准之一——可以说，正是这些标准激活了某些可能事件而非其他事件的出现。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality

quotes.text: The drugs also insure that consciousness will not come back to the physical brain too quickly, to shock the system. There are methods by which the relative behavior and condition of the travelling consciousness is monitored at the other end. In case of any severe dangers the consciousness will be pulled back, but this is highly dangerous.
quotes.translation: 这些药物也确保意识不会太快回到物质大脑，以免冲击系统。在另一端，有方法监测旅行意识的相对行为与状态。若遇严重危险，意识会被拉回，但这极为危险。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)

quotes.text: 35. Seth's material in this excerpt reminded me strongly of certain passages of his (and mine) in the 702nd session in Volume 1: "As long as you think in terms of [subatomic] particles, you are basically off the track -- or even when you think in terms of waves. The idea of interrelated fields comes closer, of course, yet even here you are simply changing one kind of term for one like it, only slightly different. In all of these cases you are ignoring the reality of consciousness, and its gestalt formation and manifestations. Until you perceive the innate consciousness behind any 'visible' or 'invisible' manifestations, then, you put a definite barrier to your own knowledge."
quotes.translation: 35. 赛斯在此摘录中的材料强烈让我想起第一卷第702节中他（和我）的某些段落：“只要你们以[亚原子]粒子的方式思考，基本上就偏离了轨道——甚至当你们以波的方式思考时也是如此。相互关联的场域概念当然更接近，但即便如此，你们也只是用一种类似的术语替换另一种，仅有细微差别。在所有这些情况下，你们都忽略了意识的实相，及其完形形成与显现。直到你们感知到任何‘可见’或‘不可见’显现背后的固有意识，你们才会为自己的知识设置明确的障碍。”
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: A spoken language is, again, dependent upon all other languages that could possibly be spoken, and thus its sounds rise into prominence and order because of the silences and pauses between them; so your waking consciousness is dependent upon what you think of as sleeping or dreaming consciousness. It rises into prominence in somewhat the same fashion, riding upon other possible versions of itself; alert only because -- in your terms -- of hidden pauses within its alertness.
quotes.translation: 口语同样依赖于所有可能被说出的其他语言，因此它的声音之所以凸显并形成秩序，正是因为它们之间的静默与停顿；同样地，你们的清醒意识也依赖于你们所谓的睡眠或梦境意识。它以类似的方式凸显出来，乘载于自身其他可能的版本之上；之所以保持警觉——用你们的说法——仅仅是因为其警觉性中隐藏的停顿。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche

quotes.text: The consciousness is composed of the individual consciousness that make up the material that is within the painting, and yet there is also a gestalt consciousness that is the result of the artist's overall conception. Anything that an observer learns from looking at the painting is always living knowledge, that alters his own makeup.
quotes.translation: 意识由构成画作材料的个体意识组成，然而也存在一个完形意识，它是艺术家整体构思的结果。观察者从画作中习得的任何事物，都是活生生的知识，会改变其自身的构成。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)

quotes.text: (10:13.) Many probable systems have perceptive mechanisms far different from your own. In fact, some are based upon gestalts of awareness completely alien to you. Quite without realizing it, your ego is a result of group consciousness, for example; the one consciousness that most directly faces the exterior world, is dependent upon the minute consciousness that resides within each living cell of your body; and as a rule you are only aware of one ego -- at least at a time.
quotes.translation: （10:13。）许多可能系统的感知机制与你们的截然不同。事实上，有些系统基于完全异于你们的意识完形。例如，你们的自我是群体意识的结果，而你们却浑然不觉；那个最直接面对外在世界的单一意识，依赖于你们身体每个活细胞中存在的细微意识；通常你们只意识到一个自我——至少在同一时间如此。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: There are dreams of different import, some triggered genetically, that serve as sparks for particular kinds of behavior--dreams, in other words, that literally span the centuries in that regard, coiled latently in the very chromosomes; and no level of consciousness is without some kind of participation in dream states. In that regard even electrons, for example, dream. Dreaming touches upon both microscopic and macroscopic events, or realities, and is not simply a human characteristic, appropriately appearing within your own range or within your own species. It is instead one area of subjective experience that is everywhere prevailing within the universe.
quotes.translation: 存在不同意义的梦境，有些由基因触发，作为特定行为模式的火花——换言之，有些梦境在染色体中潜伏数世纪之久；没有任何意识层次不参与梦境状态。就此而言，连电子也会做梦。梦境既涉及微观事件也涉及宏观事件（或实相），并非仅是人类特征，也不仅出现在你们的范畴或物种中。它是宇宙中普遍存在的主观体验领域之一。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2

quotes.text: Trees and rocks possess their own consciousness, and also share a gestalt consciousness, even as the living portions of your body. The cells and organs have their own awarenesses, and a gestalt one. So the race of man also has individual consciousness and a gestalt or mass consciousness, of which you individually are hardly aware.
quotes.translation: 树木和岩石拥有自身意识，也共享一种完形意识，正如你们身体的有生命部分。细胞和器官各有其觉知，也共享一种完形觉知。同样地，人类种族既有个体意识，也有你们个体几乎未曾察觉的完形意识或群体意识。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality

quotes.text: (9:26.) They can be, and they are, everywhere at once. Sometimes they operate with mass and sometimes without it. Now you are composed of such invisible particles, and so is everything else that you can physically perceive. To that degree--to that degree (underlined)--portions of your own consciousness are everywhere at once. They are not lost, or spread out in some generalized fashion, but acutely responsive, and as highly alert as your familiar consciousness is now.
quotes.translation: （9点26分）它们可以且确实同时存在于各处。有时它们以质量运作，有时则无。现在你们由这类不可见的粒子构成，你们能感知的一切物质也是如此。在某种程度上——在某种程度上（强调）——你们自身意识的某些部分同时存在于各处。它们并未迷失或分散于某种泛化模式中，而是敏锐响应，与你们熟悉的当前意识同样高度警觉。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2

quotes.text: (10:54.) Your experience, in other words, follows your expectations. Now the same applies to after-death experience and to the dream experience, and to any out-of-body encounters. If you are obsessed with the idea of evil, then you will meet evil conditions. If you believe in devils, then you will encounter these. As I mentioned earlier, there is greater freedom when consciousness is not physically directed. Thoughts and emotions are constructed, again, into reality without the physical time lapse. So if you believe you will be met by a demon, you will create your own thought-form of one, not realizing that it is of your own creation.
quotes.translation: （10:54）换句话说，你的体验追随你的期待。这同样适用于死后体验、梦境体验以及任何出体遭遇。若你执念于邪恶的概念，便会遭遇邪恶的情境；若你相信魔鬼的存在，便会遇见它们。如我先前所述，当意识不受肉体引导时，存在更大的自由。思想与情感会直接构建为实相，无需经历物理时间差。因此，若你相信会有恶魔迎接你，便会创造出自我的思想形态，却浑然不觉这源于自身造作。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: Any gap of assimilation here can be most unfortunate, and sometimes disastrous. Consciousness of self, if you recall, is self-consciousness that still retains self as a part of action, self that perceives its existence within action. Ego, originally a part of this consciousness of self, splits off as previously explained, and attempts to dissociate itself from action, indeed to view action as a result of itself; that is, to view action as a result and not a cause.
quotes.translation: 此处任何同化上的落差都可能极为不幸，有时甚至带来灾难。若你记得，自我意识是仍将自我视为行动一部分的自觉意识，是感知自身存在于行动中的自我。原本作为自我意识一部分的自我，如先前解释般分裂而出，试图与行动分离，甚至将行动视为自身的结果——即把行动看作结果而非原因。
