quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)

quotes.text: To identify man with his poorest works is to purposefully seek out the mars, the mistakes, of a fine artist, and then to condemn him. To do this is to condemn yourselves personally. If a scientist says consciousness is the result of chance, or Darwin's theories say that basically man is a triumphant son of murderers, many people object. If you say, however, that men are idiots, or that they are not worth the ground they walk upon, you are saying the same thing. For you must be concerned with this reality as you know it; in those terms, to condemn man is to condemn the species as you know it, and the practical terms of your world.
quotes.translation: 将人类等同于其最拙劣的作品，无异于刻意寻找杰出画作中的瑕疵与败笔，继而加以谴责。这样做就是在谴责你们自身。若科学家声称意识源于偶然，或达尔文理论宣称人类本质上是谋杀者的胜利后裔，许多人会反对。但若你们说人类是白痴，或他们不配踏足脚下的土地，你们表达的其实是同一回事。因为你们必须关注自己所知的这个实相；用那些术语来说，谴责人类就是谴责你们所知的物种，以及你们世界的现实处境。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche

quotes.text: Our friend back here (Paul W.) could well appear, you see, as a UFO in another aspect of reality, and frighten the inhabitants. You forget that consciousness is the only true vehicle. No part of your consciousness is imprisoned within you. It materializes in one aspect or another. I use the word "materializes" because it makes sense to you, but it is distortive since it predisposes an appearance within matter. Yet all realities, as you know, are not physical.
quotes.translation: 我们这位朋友（保罗·W.）在实相的另一面很可能以飞碟的形象出现，并吓到那里的居民。你们忘记了意识才是唯一的载具。你们意识的任何部分都不被囚禁在你们体内。它以这样或那样的方式物质化。我使用"物质化"这个词是因为它对你们有意义，但这是一种扭曲，因为它预设了物质中的显现。然而，如你们所知，并非所有实相都是物质的。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: Extension of energy must have contraction as an after effect, and contraction will lead to extension. Therefore, if you begin in an attempt to contract your consciousness and energy, in the hopes of attaining communication with inner reality, then indeed will your consciousness, closed and contracted like a tight fist, open and expand. And if you seek to communicate by expansion of consciousness, then will this expansion come crushing and closing upon itself.
quotes.translation: 能量的扩展必然伴随收缩作为后续效应，而收缩将导致扩展。因此，如果你们试图收缩自己的意识与能量，以期与内在实相沟通，那么你们那紧闭收缩如紧握拳头的意识，反而会张开并扩展。而如果你们试图通过扩展意识来沟通，那么这种扩展反而会向内挤压并闭合自身。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)

quotes.text: I also know that in a couple of chapters for Dreams itself Seth referred to the genetic factors involving reincarnation. He said that basically both our genetic structure and our reincarnational history are systems of consciousness, that they're "intermixed." The former is physical, the latter is psychic, a part of our inner bank of knowledge. I don't doubt that he's right--that is, in our temporal lifetimes we call upon whatever systems of consciousness we desire to, at whatever "time": a matter of choice and free will operating within the broad parameters of our sexual orientation and other personality factors.
quotes.translation: 我也知道，在《梦》的几章中，赛斯提到了涉及转世的遗传因素。他说，基本上我们的遗传结构和转世历史都是意识系统，它们是"相互混合"的。前者是物质的，后者是心灵的，是我们内在知识库的一部分。我不怀疑他是对的——也就是说，在我们现世的生命中，我们可以在任何"时间"召唤我们想要的任何意识系统：这是在性别取向和其他人格因素的广泛参数内，由选择和自由意志运作的问题。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1

quotes.text: Concentrating upon your own camouflage universe, you are able to distinguish only the distortive pattern, and from this pattern you deduce your ideas of cause and effect, past, present and future, and ideas of an expanding universe that bloats. Consciousness takes up no space nor is it enclosed by time, as you know time. The camouflage patterns that seem to enclose consciousness are temporary, for short term only, and for limited but necessary purpose.
quotes.translation: 专注于你自己的伪装宇宙时，你只能辨别扭曲的模式，并从这个模式推导出你对因果、过去、现在和未来的概念，以及一个膨胀的宇宙的概念。意识不占据任何空间，也不被你所谓的时间所封闭。那些似乎包裹着意识的伪装模式是暂时的，仅用于短期，且出于有限但必要的目的。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)

quotes.text: Since these selves exist simultaneously, it is then possible for consciousness to enter, or really form, such an image, undergo experiences within the characteristic pattern of reality, and then project to another image. In all cases consciousness forms the image. When consciousness changes, or travels at certain frequencies, it automatically changes its form. It is indeed possible to be involved with more than one form at once as in usual projection.
quotes.translation: 由于这些自我同时存在，意识就有可能进入，或实际上形成这样一个影像，在现实的特征模式中经历体验，然后投射到另一个影像。在所有情况下，意识都形成影像。当意识改变，或以特定频率旅行时，它会自动改变其形式。确实有可能像通常的投射那样，同时涉及多个形式。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)

quotes.text: Your consciousness is not a thing that you possess. Your individuality is not a thing with limits. If you ask, "What is my individuality in all of this?" or, "Which 'I' am I?" then you are automatically thinking of yourself as a psychological entity with definite boundaries that must be protected at all costs. You may say, "I was born in a house on a certain street in a certain town, and no present belief to the contrary will change that fact." If, in the present, one past event can be altered within your neuronal structure, however, then basically no event is safe from such change.
quotes.translation: 你的意识并非你所拥有的某样东西。你的个体性也并非有界限的事物。如果你问："在这一切中，我的个体性是什么？"或"我是哪个'我'？"，那么你自动将自己视为一个必须不惜一切代价保护其明确界限的心理实体。你可能会说："我出生在某镇某街的某栋房子里，任何与此相悖的当前信念都无法改变这个事实。"然而，如果当下某个过去事件能在你的神经元结构中被改变，那么基本上没有任何事件能免于这种改变。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality

quotes.text: You know now that so-called inert form has consciousness. To some degree it even possesses self-consciousness, and so there is no point at which self-consciousness entered, so to speak, with the sound of trumpets. Consciousness, to a degree, was inherent in the first materialization physically upon your plane.
quotes.translation: 你现在知道所谓的惰性形态也拥有意识。某种程度上它甚至具备自我意识，因此自我意识并非在某个时刻如号角齐鸣般突然降临。意识在某种程度上，本就内在于你们物质层面上的首次物质化之中。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)

quotes.text: Now. A crisis, particularly in very early or very late life, may so shatter the personality's identification with the body that he vacates it temporarily. He may do one of many things. He may leave so completely that the body goes into coma, if the body consciousness has also suffered shock. If the shock is psychological and the body consciousness is still operating more or less normally, then he may revert to an earlier reincarnational personality.
quotes.translation: 现在。一场危机——尤其在生命早期或晚期——可能如此彻底地粉碎人格与身体的认同，以至于他暂时离开身体。他可能采取多种行动之一。如果身体意识也遭受了冲击，他可能完全离开，使身体陷入昏迷。如果冲击是心理层面的，而身体意识仍在或多或少正常运作，那么他可能退回到某个较早的转世人格。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: Give us a moment ... Your planet as you know it is a certain kind of focus point for consciousness. At your level you think it is divided into areas of land and water -- continents and oceans, islands and peninsulas, cities and woods -- because that is all you perceive. Your consciousness is tuned in to frequencies of perception that give you that impression. A cat's world, or an insect's or a plant's, are each far different, yet equally valid.
quotes.translation: 请稍等片刻……你们所认知的星球，是意识聚焦的特定场域。在你们的层面上，你们认为它被划分为陆地和海洋——大陆与海洋、岛屿与半岛、城市与森林——因为这是你们所能感知的全部。你们的意识调谐至特定的感知频率，从而获得这种印象。猫的世界、昆虫的世界或植物的世界，各自截然不同，却同样真实有效。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: There was no point at which consciousness was introduced, because consciousness was the illumination from which the first cells emerged. That illumination was everywhere then at every point aware of itself, and of the conditions formed by its presence. In your terms each species is aware of the conditions of each other species, and of the entire environment. In those terms the environment forms the species and the species forms the environment.
quotes.translation: 意识从未被"引入"过某个时间点，因为意识正是最初细胞涌现时的光芒。那光芒无处不在，在每个点上都觉察自身，并觉察其存在所形成的情境。用你们的术语来说，每个物种都觉察着其他物种的情境，以及整个环境。在这些术语中，环境塑造物种，物种也塑造环境。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche

quotes.text: The plants awakened before the animals--and there are reasons for these varying degrees of "wakefulness" that have nothing to do basically with the differentiations of specieshood as defined by science from the outside, but have to do with the inner affiliations of consciousness, and with species or families of consciousness. Those affiliations fell into being as all of the consciousnesses that were embarked upon physical reality divided up (long pause) the almost unimaginable creative achievements that would be responsible for the physically effective world.
quotes.translation: 植物比动物更早觉醒——这些不同程度的"觉醒"有其原因，根本上与科学从外部定义的物种分化无关，而是与意识的内在联结有关，与意识物种或意识家族有关。当所有投身于物质实相的意识体（长停顿）划分出那些几乎难以想象的、将造就物质有效世界的创造性成就时，这些联结便应运而生。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1

quotes.text: Because of my own experiences, particularly with out-of-body states, I'm convinced that consciousness is not dependent upon physical matter. Certainly physical expression is my main mode of existence right now, but I don't take this to infer that all consciousness must be so oriented. Only the most blind egotism, it seems to me, would dare define reality in its own terms or project its own limitations and experience upon the rest of existence.
quotes.translation: 由于我自身的经历，尤其是出体状态，我确信意识并不依赖于物质。当然，物质表达目前是我存在的主要模式，但我并不认为这意味着所有意识都必须如此定向。在我看来，只有最盲目的自我中心，才敢以自己的标准定义实相，或将自己的局限与经验投射到其他存在之上。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: (9:35.) You are as actively and vividly concerned in these realities as you are in the one in which your main attention is now focused. Now, as you are merely concerned with your physical body and physical self as a rule, you give your attention to the stream of consciousness that seems to deal with it. These other streams of consciousness, however, are connected with other self-forms that you do not perceive. The body, in other words, is simply one manifestation of what you are in one reality, but in these other realities you have other forms.
quotes.translation: （9:35。）你在这些实相中同样活跃而生动地参与着，就像在你目前主要关注的实相中一样。现在，正如你通常只关心你的物质身体和物质自我，你将注意力集中在似乎与之相关的意识流上。然而，这些其他的意识流与你未能感知的其他自我形态相连。换言之，身体只是你在一个实相中的一种显现，但在这些其他实相中，你拥有其他形态。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: In your terms, consciousness is able to hold its own sense of identity by accepting one probability, one physical life, for example, and maintaining its identity through a lifetime. Even then, certain events will be remembered and others forgotten. The consciousness also learns to handle alternate moments as it "matures." As it does so mature it forms a new, larger framework of identity, as the cell forms into an organ on another level.
quotes.translation: 用你们的说法，意识能够通过接受一种可能性（例如一次物质生命）来维持其自身的身份感，并在整个生命过程中保持其身份。即便如此，某些事件会被记住，而其他事件则会被遗忘。随着意识的“成熟”，它也学会处理交替的时刻。当它如此成熟时，它会形成一个全新的、更宏大的身份框架，就像细胞在另一层面上形成器官一样。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1

quotes.text: Each beta wave rides atop the other patterns. In normal sleep, the "conscious" wave rides beneath the others, with the face of consciousness turned inward, so to speak. All the recognized characteristics of consciousness are "inverted," probing other realities than the one you know. They are quite effective and lightning fast. In sleep the beta waves are not turned off -- the "conscious" part of you, with its beta rhythms, is elsewhere.
quotes.translation: 每个贝塔波都骑乘在其他模式之上。在正常睡眠中，"意识"波骑乘在其他模式之下，可以说，意识的面孔转向内在。所有公认的意识特征都被"反转"，探索着不同于你所知的现实。它们非常有效且快如闪电。在睡眠中，贝塔波并未关闭——你那个拥有贝塔节律的"意识"部分，正在别处。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: Consciousness therefore continually creates and maintains itself, and this includes the physical materiaizationl, the properties of the dimension, and yet basically there is no difference between the creator in these terms and the created. Nor between inner reality, which forms physical matter, and physical objects themselves, for the atoms which are manipulated to form objects are themselves a portion of consciousness, and alive in those terms. They respond to Emotional and psychic directives as the physical body responds to light.
quotes.translation: 因此，意识持续创造并维持自身，这包括物质的具体化、维度的属性，然而从根本上说，在这些术语中，创造者与被创造者之间并无区别。形成物质实相的内在现实与物质对象本身之间也无区别，因为被操控形成对象的原子本身就是意识的一部分，并且在这些术语中是活着的。它们响应情感和心灵指令，如同物质身体响应光线。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)

quotes.text: Consciousness, by its nature, continually expands. The nature of consciousness as you understand it as a species will, in one way or another, lead you beyond your limited ideas of reality, for your experience will set challenges that cannot be solved within your current framework. Those problems set by one level of consciousness will automatically cause breakthroughs into other areas of conscious activity, where solutions can be found.
quotes.translation: 意识按其本性持续扩展。你作为一个物种所理解的意识本质，将以某种方式引领你超越对现实的有限观念，因为你的经验将设置无法在当前框架内解决的挑战。由某一意识层面设定的问题，将自动引发突破进入其他意识活动领域，在那里可以找到解决方案。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1

quotes.text: (Rapidly:) He is learning how to use the light of his own consciousness, and discovering how far one particular method of using it can be counted upon. He is studying what he can do and not do with that particular focus. He is now discovering that he needs other lights also, in other words -- that he has been relying upon only a small portion of an entire inner searchlight that can be used in many directions. Let us look at some of those other directions that are native to man's consciousness, still waiting to be used effectively.
quotes.translation: （快速地说：）他正在学习如何运用自己意识的明光，并发现某种特定的运用方式究竟能依赖到何种程度。他正在研究以这种特定焦点能做什么、不能做什么。此刻他正发现还需要其他光源——换言之，他一直只依赖着整个内在探照灯的一小部分，而这盏灯本可向多个方向照射。让我们看看人类意识与生俱来的其他方向，它们仍在等待被有效运用。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: In certain basic and vital ways, your own consciousness is a portion of that divine gestalt. In the terms of your earthly experience, it is a metaphysical, a scientific, and a creative error to separate matter from consciousness, for consciousness materializes itself as matter in physical life.
quotes.translation: 在某些根本且关键的方面，你的意识正是那神圣完形的一部分。以你们尘世经验的术语而言，将物质与意识分离是形而上、科学及创造上的谬误，因为意识在物质生命中将自己具体化为物质。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1

quotes.text: The fly is intensely conscious, at every moment engrossed in itself and its environment, precisely tuned to elements of which you are "unconscious." There are simply different kinds of consciousness, and you cannot basically compare one to the other any more than you can compare, say, a toad to a star to an apple to a thought to a woman to a child to a native to a suburbanite to a spider to a cat. They are varieties of consciousness, each focused upon its own view of reality, each containing experience that others exclude.
quotes.translation: 苍蝇具有强烈的意识，每时每刻都全神贯注于自身及其环境，精准调频于那些你“无意识”的元素。只是存在不同种类的意识，你根本不能将它们相互比较，就像你不能将蟾蜍与星星、苹果与思想、女人与孩子、原住民与郊区居民、蜘蛛与猫相提并论。它们是意识的变体，每一种都聚焦于自己对实相的看法，每一种都包含着其他意识排除在外的经验。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: As all of this occurred, consciousness took on more and more specific orientations, greater organizations at your end. At the "other end," it disentangled itself from vaster fields of activity to allow for this specific behavior. All of these units of consciousness, again, operate as entities (or particles, or as waves or forces). In those terms, consciousness formed the experience of time--and not, of course, the other way around.
quotes.translation: 当这一切发生时，意识呈现出越来越具体的取向，在你这一端形成了更大的组织。而在“另一端”，它则从更广阔的活动场域中脱离出来，以允许这种特定行为的发生。所有这些意识单位，再次强调，都作为实体（或粒子、或波、或力）运作。用这些术语来说，意识形成了时间的经验——当然，并非反过来。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1

quotes.text: In our demonstration, to which of course Joseph gave his permission, he allowed his consciousness to retreat, and to some degree began to cut off its physical expression. He was not aware consciously of his permission, simply because this kind of demonstration could not be held if the normal waking consciousness knew. It would automatically be frightened. As I spoke about the dimming of consciousness, Joseph then experienced it.
quotes.translation: 在我们的演示中，约瑟夫当然给予了许可，他允许自己的意识退却，并在某种程度上开始切断其物理表达。他并未有意识地知晓自己的许可，仅仅因为如果正常的清醒意识知道，这种演示就无法进行——它会自动感到恐惧。当我谈到意识的暗淡时，约瑟夫随后便体验到了这一点。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: Consciousness is, among other things, a spontaneous exercise in creativity. You are learning now, in a three-dimensional context, the ways in which your emotional and psychic existence can create varieties of physical form. You manipulate within the psychic environment, and these manipulations are then automatically impressed upon the physical mold. Now our environment is in itself creative in a different manner than yours. Your environment is creative in that trees bear fruit, that there is a self-sustaining principle, that the earth feeds its own, for example. The naturally creative aspects are the materializations of the deepest psychic, spiritual, and physical inclinations of the species, set up in your terms eons ago, and a part of the racial bank of psychic knowledge.
quotes.translation: 意识本质上是创造力的自发练习。你正在三维情境中学习，你的情感与心灵存在如何能创造各种物质形态。你在心灵环境中进行操纵，这些操纵随后自动印刻于物质模具之上。我们的环境本身以不同于你们的方式具有创造力。例如，你们的环境具有创造力，体现在树木结果、存在自我维持原则、大地滋养其子民等方面。这些自然创造性的面向，是物种最深层的心灵、精神与物质倾向的物质化，按你们的时间观而言是亿万年前建立的，属于种族心灵知识库的一部分。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: In other portions of the Seth material these processes will be made very clear for those of you who desire to pursue the question further, or those who may be interested from a scientific point of view. Here, we are discussing such issues only because they touch upon the multidimensional aspect of personality. They allow you to materialize certain subjective experiences into three-dimensional reality. Before I leave the subject, however, let me remind you that these emanations in varying degrees rise from all consciousness, not simply your own. This includes cellular consciousness as well, so that an invisible network of electromagnetic units pervades your entire atmosphere; and upon this webwork and from it, the particles of physical matter are then formed.
quotes.translation: 在赛斯资料的其他部分，这些过程将为那些希望深入探究此问题或从科学角度感兴趣的人清晰阐明。此处我们讨论这些议题，仅因其涉及人格的多维面向。它们允许你将某些主观体验物质化为三维实相。然而在我离开此主题前，容我提醒：这些辐射以不同程度源于所有意识，而不仅限于你们自身。这包括细胞意识，因此电磁单位的无形网络弥漫于你们整个大气层；物质粒子的物理形态正是基于此网络结构并从中形成。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: (10:35.) In the most protected areas of sleep you are dealing with experience that is pure feeling or knowing, and disconnected from both words or images. As mentioned, these experiences are translated into dreams later, necessitating a return to areas of consciousness more familiar with physical data. Here a great creative synthesis and a great creative diversification takes place, in which any given dream image has meaning to various layers of the self -- on one level representing a truth you have lived and on other levels representing this truth as it is more specifically applied to various areas of experience or problems. There will be a metamorphosis, therefore, of one symbol turning into many, and the conscious mind may only perceive a chaos of various dream images, because the inner organization and unity is partially hidden in the other areas of consciousness through which the reasoning mind cannot follow.
quotes.translation: (10:35) 在睡眠最受保护的领域，你处理的是纯粹感受或认知的经验，与文字或意象完全无关。如前所述，这些经验后来被转译为梦境，必须返回更熟悉物质实相的意识领域。在此发生伟大的创造性综合与创造性分化，任何特定梦境意象对自我的不同层次都具有意义——在某一层面代表你曾活过的真理，在其他层面则代表该真理更具体应用于不同经验或问题领域。因此，一个象征会蜕变为多个象征，而意识心可能只感知到混乱的梦境意象群，因为内在组织与统一性部分隐藏于理性心智无法追随的其他意识领域。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: However, through traveling within yourselves, you will discover the unity of your consciousness with other consciousnesses. You will discover the multidimensional love and energy that gives consciousness to all things. This will not lead you to want to rest upon the proverbial blessed bosom. It will instead inspire you to take a better hand in the job of creation; and that feeling of divine presence you will find indeed, and feel indeed, for you will sense it behind the dance of the molecules, and in yourselves and in your neighbors. What so many want is a God who walks down the street and says, "Happy Sunday, I am I, follow me." But God is hidden craftily in his creations, so that he is what they are and they are what he is; and in knowing them, you know him.
quotes.translation: 然而，通过向内探索，你将发现自身意识与其他意识的统一性。你将发现赋予万物意识的多维之爱与能量。这不会让你想安息于传说中的福泽怀抱，反而会激励你更主动参与创造工作；你确实会找到并感受到神圣临在，因你将在分子舞蹈背后、在自身与邻人之中感知它。许多人渴望的神明是走在街上说："快乐星期天，我就是我，跟随我。"但神巧妙隐藏于其创造物中，以致祂即万物，万物即祂；认识万物，你便认识祂。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: The physical reality into which you are born is not nearly as solid or predetermined or definite as it appears to be. Instead there is a field of rich interaction. Your consciousness must be focused at one particular range of frequencies before it can even perceive matter, much less solidity. In sleep your consciousness fluctuates between ranges of intensities, literally flowing into and out of the physical-matter grouping, and forming from more plastic "pre-matter" (with a hyphen) stages, the final shape that matter will take in your world. The same applies to events, where some will be crystallized in physical terms and others will not. The deep portions of your own being are aware of those purposes and intents that are uniquely yours. Unconsciously, then, you have within you what you might think of as a set of blueprints for the particular kind of physical reality you want to materialize. You are the architect.
quotes.translation: 你们所出生的物质实相，远非表面看起来那般坚实、预定或确定。相反，它是一个充满丰富互动的场域。你们的意识必须聚焦于特定频率范围，才能感知物质，更遑论其坚实性。在睡眠中，意识在强度范围之间波动，实际上是在流入和流出物质群组，并从更具可塑性的"前物质"阶段，形成物质在你们世界中的最终形态。事件也是如此——有些会以物质形式结晶，有些则不会。你们自身存在的深层部分知晓那些独属于你的目的与意图。因此，在无意识层面，你们内在拥有可视为蓝图的一套方案，用于实现你们想要具体化的特定物质实相。你们就是建筑师。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality

quotes.text: Here's Seth from the 750th session, held on June 25, 1975, two months after he finished Volume 2. In it he not only sums up his motives in producing "Unknown" Reality, but comments on another one of his basic ideas that I think it important to stress every so often; this time, perception is involved. "The 'Unknown' Reality was written to give ... individuals glimpses into alternate patterns of reality. It was meant to serve as a map that would lead, not into another objectified universe per se, but into inner roads of consciousness. These inner roads or strands of consciousness bring elements into play so that it becomes possible to realize that the content of a given objectified universe may actually be perceived quite differently. You are part of what you perceive. When you alter the focus of your perception you automatically change the objectified world. It is not simply that you perceive it differently while it remains the same, regardless of your experience. The act of perception itself helps form the perceived event and is a part of it."
quotes.translation: 以下是赛斯在1975年6月25日第750节课的讲述，距他完成第二卷已过两个月。在这节课中，他不仅总结了自己创作《未知实相》的动机，还评论了另一个我认为需要时常强调的基本观点——这次涉及感知。"《未知实相》的写作目的是让个体得以窥见实相的其他模式。它旨在成为一张地图，不是导向另一个客体化的宇宙本身，而是导向意识的内在道路。这些内在道路或意识脉络会调动各种元素，使人有可能认识到：某个特定客体化宇宙的内容，实际上可能被以截然不同的方式感知。你是你所感知之物的一部分。当你改变感知的焦点时，你便自动改变了客体化的世界。并非世界保持不变而只是你的感知不同——感知行为本身就在帮助形成被感知的事件，并且是事件的一部分。"
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1

quotes.text: You can understand what is meant by saying that your consciousness fluctuates -- for each individual is aware of various intensities and concentrations. You are more alert, or, in your terms more conscious on some occasions than others. Now the same applies to these units of consciousness -- and to atoms, molecules, electrons, and other such phenomena. The world literally blinks off and on. This reality of fluctuation in no way bothers your own feeling of consistency, however. The "holes (spelled) of nonexistence" are plugged up by the process of selectivity. This process chooses significances then, again, around which experience is built, and around which "life" is felt. The very sensations of one kind of life then automatically set up barriers against other such "world-schemes" (hyphen) that do not correlate with their own.
quotes.translation: 你们可以理解“你们的意识会波动”这句话的含义——因为每个人都能觉察到不同强度与专注度。在某些场合，你们比在其他场合更警觉，或用你们的话说，更有意识。现在，同样的道理也适用于这些意识单元——以及原子、分子、电子和其他此类现象。这个世界确实在闪烁明灭。然而，这种波动的实相，丝毫不会干扰你们对自身连续性的感受。那些“不存在的空洞”被选择性过程所填补。这个过程再次选择意义，然后围绕这些意义构建经验，并围绕这些意义感受“生命”。一种生命的感觉，会自动对其他与其自身不相关的“世界体系”设置障碍。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1

quotes.text: While inhabited by the usual human consciousness, the living body operates as an intense focus point. The conglomeration of consciousnesses within it on all levels focuses its own network of communication. This private network is connected to all others like it. There are levels of interaction then simply between all bodies, electromagnetically and biologically. The network is more far-reaching than that, however. Not only can all cells respond to each other, but their mass activity triggers even higher centers of consciousness to respond to a given set of world conditions, rather than to other quite-as-legitimate world conditions that do not fit the accepted pattern. Probabilities to some extent, then, are determined along cellular lines. This should be obvious.
quotes.translation: 当活生生的身体被通常的人类意识所占据时，它运作成一个强烈的焦点。身体内各个层面的意识聚合体，聚焦于其自身的沟通网络。这个私密的网络与所有其他类似的网络相连。因此，仅仅在所有身体之间，就存在着电磁与生物层面的互动层次。然而，这个网络的影响远不止于此。不仅所有细胞能彼此响应，它们的集体活动还会触发更高层的意识中心，去响应一组特定的世界状况，而不是响应其他同样合理、却不符公认模式的世界状况。因此，在某种程度上，可能性是沿着细胞路线决定的。这应该是显而易见的。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1

quotes.text: As specified,2 ego consciousness grew. These inner patterns, native to the psyche of any species, turned into concepts, mental images -- intuitive projections that were all meant to give conscious direction. The gods served, then, as stimulators of development. Seemingly outside of the self, they were meant to lead the self into its greatest area of fulfillment. The god images would change as consciousness did. The various god concepts that have fallen by the wayside, so to speak, represent areas of development that were not chosen, in your terms, but they are still latent. The totem pole, for example, is a remnant from an era where there was much greater communication between man and the animals -- when, in fact, men went to the animals to learn, and from them first acquired knowledge of herbs and corrective medicinal behavior.3
quotes.translation: 如前所述，自我意识逐渐成长。这些内在模式——任何物种心灵所固有的——转化为概念、心象，即旨在提供有意识指引的直觉投射。诸神因此充当了发展的刺激物。它们看似存在于自我之外，实则意在引领自我进入其最圆满的成就领域。神祇意象会随意识变化而改变。可以说，那些被弃置的各种神祇概念，在你们看来代表着未被选择的发展领域，但它们依然处于潜伏状态。例如图腾柱，便是人类与动物存在更广泛沟通时代的遗存——那时人类会向动物学习，并首次从中获得草药知识与矫正性医疗行为。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1

quotes.text: Such leeway left room for many probabilities and for many "errors," but the developing consciousness had to be free to make its own judgments. It would not be programmed any more than necessary by "instinct." It was, however, biologically locked into earthly existence, and so meant to understand its natural heritage. It could not separate itself too much, then, or become overly arrogant. Its survival was so linked to the rest of nature that it would of necessity always have to return to that base. It responds to an inborn impetus for its own greatest fulfillment, and will automatically change directions in answer to its own experiments and experiences. There are great sweeping changes in religious concepts abroad in your times, and these represent man's innate knowledge. His consciousness -- his psyche -- is projecting greater images of his own probable fulfillment, and these are seen in his changing concepts of God.
quotes.translation: 这种余地允许许多可能性和许多"错误"存在，但发展中的意识必须拥有自由做出自己的判断。它不会受到超出必要程度的"本能"编程。然而，它在生物学上被锁定于地球生存，因此注定要理解其自然传承。它既不能过度脱离自然，也不能变得过于傲慢。其生存与自然界的其他部分紧密相连，以至于必然始终回归这个基础。它响应内在驱动力以实现自身最大成就，并会根据自身实验与经验自动调整方向。你们时代正经历宗教观念的剧烈变革，这正体现了人类与生俱来的认知。他的意识——他的心灵——正在投射出关于自身可能成就的更大意象，这反映在他对上帝观念的持续演变中。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1

quotes.text: Rising ego consciousness then would have its religious reasons for domination and control. The pope became God the Father personified, but that god had indeed changed from the old Jewish Jehovah. Christ, historically speaking, had altered that concept enough so that at least God the Father was not quite as capricious as Jehovah. (Pause.) Some mercy came to the forefront. Growing ego consciousness could not run rampant over nature. On the other hand, holy wars and ignorance would keep the population down. The church, however -- the Roman Catholic Church -- still held a repository of religious ideas and concepts that served as a bank of probabilities from which the race could draw. The religious ideas served as social organization, much needed, and many of the monks managed to preserve old manuscripts and knowledge underground. Those who were allied with religious principles, now, mainly survived, and brought forth communities and descendants who were protected. Psychic and religious ideas, then, despite many drawbacks, served as a method of species organization. They are far more important in terms of "evolution" than is recognized. Religious concepts from the beginning kept tribes together, provided social structures, and insured physical survival and the protection that made descendants most probable.
quotes.translation: 不断上升的自我意识随后会为其支配与控制寻找宗教理由。教皇成为天父的化身，但这位上帝确实已从古老的犹太耶和华转变。从历史角度看，基督已足够改变这一概念，至少使天父不再像耶和华那般反复无常。（停顿）某种慈悲开始凸显。日益增长的自我意识不能对自然肆意妄为。另一方面，圣战与无知将控制人口数量。然而教会——罗马天主教会——仍保存着宗教思想与概念的宝库，这些作为种族可汲取的可能性银行而存在。宗教思想充当了急需的社会组织功能，许多僧侣得以在地下保存古老手稿与知识。那些与宗教原则结盟者最终幸存下来，繁衍出受到保护的社群与后代。因此，尽管存在诸多缺陷，心灵与宗教思想仍作为物种组织方式发挥作用。就"进化"而言，它们的重要性远超人们所认知。宗教概念自始便维系部落团结，提供社会结构，确保肉体生存与使后代成为可能的保护机制。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1

quotes.text: Give us a moment ... Some inventors, writers, scientists, artists, who are used to dealing with creative material directly, are quite aware of the fact that many of their productive ideas came from the dream condition. They see the results of dream activity in practical physical life. Many others, though untrained, can clearly trace certain decisions made in waking life to dreams. Few understand, however, that private reality is like a finished product, rising out of the immense productivity that occurs in the dreaming condition. Ruburt calls this The Wonderworks,2 and with good reason. In waking life there are fluctuations in your consciousness, periods when you are more or less alert, in your terms, when your attention wanders from issues at hand; or when, instead, you are certainly brilliantly focused in the moment. So there are gradations of consciousness in the waking state. Usually you pay little attention to them.
quotes.translation: 请稍等片刻……有些发明家、作家、科学家和艺术家，习惯于直接处理创造性素材，他们很清楚许多富有成效的想法来自梦境状态。他们在实际的物质生活中看到了梦境活动的结果。许多其他人，即使未经训练，也能清晰地追溯出清醒生活中的某些决定源于梦境。然而，很少有人明白，私人实相就像一件成品，源自梦境状态中发生的巨大生产力。鲁柏称此为“奇迹工厂”，这名称恰如其分。在清醒生活中，你们的意识会有波动，有时你们或多或少保持警觉——按你们的说法——当注意力从手头事务游离开时；或者相反，当你们在当下高度专注时。因此，清醒状态中也有意识的层级之分。通常你们很少留意它们。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1

quotes.text: The picture is a relatively simple one, all in all -- one in which each consciousness is assumed to be directed toward a particular focus, is ensconced in one body, with its existence bounded by birth at one end and by death at the other. (Pause.) Unfortunately, that picture is as limited as any one of your photographs. You are used to examining your dream state from the viewpoint of your "waking" condition, but some time in the dream state try to examine your normal waking reality. Simply give yourself the instructions to do so. You may be quite surprised with the results. Speaking as simply as I can, and using concepts that you can understand, let me put it this way: From the other side -- within what is loosely called the dream state -- there is an existence quite as valid as your own, and from that viewpoint you can be considered as the dreamer. "You" are the part of you concentrating in this reality. You form it through information and through energy that on the one hand has its source outside this system, and that on the other constantly flows into this system -- and so in that respect the systems are united.
quotes.translation: 这幅图景相对简单——简而言之，它假设每个意识都指向一个特定焦点，安居于一个身体之中，其存在以出生为起点、以死亡为终点。（停顿。）不幸的是，这幅图景就像你们任何一张照片那样局限。你们习惯于从“清醒”状态的角度审视梦境状态，但有时不妨在梦境状态中尝试检视你们正常的清醒实相。只需给自己下达这样的指令。结果可能会让你们大吃一惊。尽可能简单地说，用你们能理解的概念来表达：从另一边——在所谓的梦境状态中——存在着一种与你们自身同样有效的存在，而从那个视角来看，你们可以被视为做梦者。“你”是专注于这个实相的那部分自己。你通过信息和能量形成它，这些信息和能量一方面源于这个系统之外，另一方面不断流入这个系统——因此，在这方面，这些系统是统一的。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1

quotes.text: "I had all kinds of sudden flashes about this when I was doing the dishes (less than half an hour ago) -- about Seth's book, and that in a strange way it was difficult for me to get this book material. It was new, maybe; it would involve concepts that by themselves went against the grain of usual conscious thought, which wants to go consecutively. It's as though my consciousness is trying to use a new kind of organization -- for me, for it -- and so there's a kind of unfamiliarity. No scientific language would be used -- not that I know any -- but that would structure what I'm trying to do; and unwittingly, perhaps, it might lead me into a scientific dogma without recognizing it. Besides, that would put an unnecessary burden on the reader, who might feel he or she needed a particular vocabulary. The use of a normal vocabulary would put the ideas within the reach of the ordinary person as much as possible. Although most people might need to work at it to understand the material, there'd be no automatic difficulties with words."
quotes.translation: 我在洗碗时（不到半小时前）突然闪过各种念头——关于赛斯的书，以及奇怪的是，我很难获取这本书的资料。也许它是全新的；它会涉及一些概念，这些概念本身违背了通常意识思维的习惯，后者喜欢按顺序进行。就好像我的意识在尝试使用一种新的组织方式——对我、对它而言——所以有种陌生感。不会使用任何科学语言——不是说我懂什么科学语言——但那会限制我正在尝试做的事；而且不经意间，也许它会引导我陷入某种科学教条而不自知。此外，那会给读者带来不必要的负担，让他们觉得自己需要特定的词汇。使用普通词汇能尽可能让普通人理解这些概念。虽然大多数人可能需要努力才能理解资料，但不会因为词汇本身而遇到自动的困难。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1

quotes.text: Think of your ideas about your own sexuality in connection with those about your being and consciousness. Regroup your ideas so that you automatically think of sexuality in relationship to your religions and sciences. You have associated the word "female" with the unconscious, while you have been working toward what you now think of as an egotistically based consciousness. There is more in what I am saying than you presently realize. You cannot, each of you, consider the real meaning of your own sexuality unless you understand your own religious history. Follow through, in your own minds, with your memories. Try to be honest with yourselves as to those early experiences in which you forced yourselves to behave differently than you were, because adults told you that you must ... You had better understand the beautiful, unique quality of your own individuality lest you project upon the other sex -- whichever sex you are -- those abilities and qualities that you are afraid are your own, or project upon them those abilities and qualities that you wish you possessed and fear you do not.
quotes.translation: 将你对自身性别的看法，与对你存在和意识的看法联系起来思考。重新组织你的想法，使你自动将性别与你的宗教和科学联系起来。你一直将"女性"这个词与无意识联系在一起，而你一直在朝向你目前认为的以自我为中心的意识努力。我所说的比你目前意识到的要多。你们每个人，除非了解自己的宗教历史，否则无法考虑自身性别的真正含义。在你们自己的心智中，跟随你们的记忆。诚实地面对那些早期经历，那时你们强迫自己表现得与真实自我不同，因为成年人告诉你们必须如此……你们最好理解自己个性的美丽而独特的特质，以免将那些你们害怕属于自己的能力和品质投射到异性身上——无论你是哪种性别——或者将那些你们希望拥有却害怕没有的能力和品质投射到他们身上。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1

quotes.text: Now in the case of an animal who hibernates, the body is in the same state. But in the greater hibernation of your own experience, the body as a whole becomes inoperable. The cells within you obviously die constantly. The body that you have now is not the one that you had 10 years ago; its physical composition has died completely many times since your birth, but, again, your consciousness bridges those gaps (with gestures). They could be accepted instead, in which case it would seem to you that you were, say, a reincarnated self at age 7 (intently), or 14 or 21. The particular sequence of your own awareness follows through, however. In basic terms the body dies often, and as surely as you think it dies but once in the death you recognize. On numerous occasions it physically breaks apart, but your consciousness rides beyond those "deaths." You do not perceive them. The stuff of your body literally falls into the earth many times, as you think it does only at the "end of your life."
quotes.translation: 以冬眠动物为例，其身体处于相同状态。但在你们经验中更重大的"冬眠"里，身体整体会丧失运作能力。你们体内的细胞显然在不断死亡。此刻拥有的身体已非十年前的躯体——自出生以来，其物质构成早已彻底死亡过无数次，但你们的意识始终跨越这些断层（伴随手势）。若选择接受这种认知，你们会觉得自己在七岁（专注地）、十四岁或二十一岁时便已是转世之身。然而，你们自我认知的特定序列始终贯穿始终。从根本上说，身体经常死亡，其确定性如同你们认为它只在公认的死亡中终结一次。它曾无数次在物质层面瓦解，但你们的意识始终凌驾于这些"死亡"之上。你们并未察觉这些过程。你们身体的物质成分曾多次真正回归大地，正如你们以为它只在"生命终点"才会如此。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: Some individuals find themselves with memories of other lives, which are other days to the soul. Such persons then become aware of a greater consciousness reaching over those gaps, and realize that earthly experience can contain [among other things] a knowledge of existence in more than one body. Inherently then consciousness, affiliated with the flesh, can indeed carry such comprehensions. The mind of man as you know it shows at least the potential ability for handling a kind of memory with which you are usually not acquainted. This means that even biologically the species is equipped to deal with different sequences of time, while still manipulating within one particular time scheme. This also implies a far greater psychological richness -- quite possible, again, within corporal reality -- in which many levels of relationships can be handled. Such inner knowledge is inherent in the cells, and in ordinary terms of evolution is quite possible as a "future" development.
quotes.translation: 有些人发现自己拥有其他生命的记忆——对灵魂而言那不过是其他日子。这类人于是意识到存在跨越这些断层的更广大意识，并领悟到尘世经验可以包含（在诸多可能性中）对多具身体存在的认知。因此，与肉体相连的意识本质上确实能承载这种理解。你们所知的"人类心智"至少展现出处理某种陌生记忆的潜在能力。这意味着即便在生物学层面，这个物种也具备处理不同时间序列的能力，同时仍能在特定时间架构内运作。这也暗示着远为丰富的心理维度——再次强调，这在物质实相中完全可能——使得多重关系层次得以被处理。这种内在知识本就存在于细胞中，以寻常进化论而言，完全可能作为"未来"的发展方向。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: That kind of structuring also does a disservice, however, for it prevents you from coming in contact with your own original concepts. There is no reason, for example, to encounter any demons or devils in any trance or out-of-body condition.3 (Pause.) In such cases your own hallucinations blind you to the environment within which they are projected. When your consciousness is not directly focused in physical reality, then, the great creativity of the psyche is given fuller play. All of its dimensions are faithfully and instantly produced as experience when you learn to take your "normally alert" conscious mind with you; and when you are free of such limiting ideas, then at those levels you can glimpse the inner powers of your own psyche, and watch the interplay of beliefs and symbols as they are manifested before your eyes. Until you learn to do this you will most certainly have difficulty, for you will not be able to tell the difference between your projections and what is happening in the inner environment.
quotes.translation: 然而这种结构模式也会造成损害，因为它阻碍你们接触自身原始概念。例如，在任何出神或出体状态中，根本没有理由遭遇任何恶魔或邪灵（停顿）。在这种情况下，你们的幻觉反而遮蔽了它们投射其上的环境。当意识不直接聚焦于物质实相时，心灵巨大的创造力便得以充分展现。当你们学会携带"正常清醒"的意识心智同行，并摆脱这些限制性观念时，所有维度都会忠实而即时地转化为经验；届时在这些层面上，你们将得以窥见自身心灵的内在力量，目睹信念与象征在眼前交织显现。在学会这点之前，你们必定会遭遇困难——因为你们将无法区分自身投射与内在环境中真实发生的事件。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: The other [Saturday] evening while he was in bed Ruburt had a somewhat surprising experience. He was not dreaming. His body was asleep but his consciousness was drifting. He clearly heard my voice. It seemed to come literally from out of the sky, down into another room outside of (next-door to, actually) the one in which his body slept. For a moment the power frightened him, for it sounded like a radio turned up to an incredible degree -- louder than thunder. At the time words were clearly distinguishable, though later he forgot what they said. For an instant he was tempted to interpret the power as anger, for in your world when someone is shouting they are usually angry. He realized, however, that something else was involved. He did not sense my presence, but only heard the thunder of the voice. It shocked him because he is used to hearing my words from within his head -- he had never before been aware of my voice as existing apart from him. In the dream state he has heard me giving him information. In these instances, however, he was the channel through which my voice came. He has often wondered about the nature of my own independence, and the kind of reality in which I exist.
quotes.translation: 上周六晚上，鲁柏躺在床上时经历了一次颇为惊人的体验。他并非在做梦。他的身体已入睡，但意识仍在飘荡。他清晰地听见我的声音。那声音仿佛从天空直落而下，传入他身体所卧房间之外（实际上是隔壁）的另一间房。一时间，这股力量令他惊恐，因为那声音就像收音机被调到难以置信的音量——比雷声更响。当时字句清晰可辨，尽管他后来忘记了内容。瞬间他差点将这股力量解读为愤怒，因为在你们的世界里，当有人大喊大叫时通常是在发怒。然而他意识到其中另有玄机。他并未感知到我的临在，只听见那如雷贯耳的声音。这令他震惊，因为他已习惯从自己脑海中听见我的话语——他从未意识到我的声音竟能独立于他而存在。在梦境状态中，他听过我传递信息。但在那些情况下，他是我的声音流经的通道。他常思索我自身独立性的本质，以及我所存在的实相类型。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: (10:18.) Now: If you alter that picture a little so that the images are somewhat scrambled -- and you do this by altering the focus of your consciousness -- then the familiar coordination is gone. Objects may appear blurred, ordinary sounds distorted. It seems as if you are on the outskirts of your own reality. In such a state, however, it is easy to see that your usual orientation may be but one of many frames of reference. (Pause.) If you did change the focus of your consciousness still further, you might then "bring in" another picture entirely. On the outside this would give you another reality. (Intently:) In it your "old" reality might still be somewhat perceivable as a ghost image,6 if you knew what to look for and remembered your former coordinates. On the inside, however, you would be traveling not around or about, but through one portion of the psyche with its reality, into another portion of the psyche with its reality. That kind of journey would not be any more imaginary than a trip from one city to another.
quotes.translation: （10:18分）现在：如果你稍微改变那幅画面，使影像有些混乱——你通过改变意识的焦点来做到这一点——那么熟悉的协调性便消失了。物体可能变得模糊，普通的声音被扭曲。仿佛你正站在自己实相的边缘。然而在这种状态下，很容易看出你惯常的取向可能只是众多参考框架之一。（停顿。）如果你进一步改变意识的焦点，你或许能"带入"另一幅完全不同的画面。从外在看，这会给你另一个实相。（专注地：）在其中，如果你知道该寻找什么并记得先前的坐标，你的"旧"实相或许仍能隐约感知为鬼影般的存在。但从内在看，你并非在周遭绕行，而是穿越心灵的一部分及其实相，进入心灵的另一部分及其实相。那种旅程绝不比从一座城市到另一座城市的旅行更虚幻。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: The material I picked up about my father's psychic intents was at first very bewildering. Hinted at was such a diffusion of consciousness that at the time individuality seemed to have little meaning. For I glimpsed Robert Butts, Sr. as he decided to disperse "himself" into a series of other personalities in both the past and the near future, so that I wondered how -- in that mélange of identities -- my father could possibly know himself. Seth's explanations in ESP class last night and in this evening's session helped clear my mind considerably, though: According to him, consciousness has no difficulty in making such alliances while maintaining continuity of identity, though its vast abilities are certainly almost impossible for us to grasp.
quotes.translation: 我收集到的关于父亲心灵意图的资料起初令人十分困惑。其中暗示的意识扩散如此广泛，以至于当时个体性似乎失去了意义。因为我瞥见老罗伯特·巴茨决定将"自己"分散成一系列过去和近未来的其他人格，以至于我怀疑——在那身份的大杂烩中——父亲如何能认识自己。不过，昨晚ESP课和今晚课上的赛斯解释大大澄清了我的思路：据他所说，意识在维持身份连续性的同时，建立这种联盟毫无困难，尽管其浩瀚能力对我们而言确实几乎无法理解。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: In the same way you form events, often without being aware that you do so. It seems that events happen as it seems words are spoken. You were taught how to construct sentences in school, and you learned how to speak from your elders. You were involved with event-making before the time of your birth, however. The psyche forms events in the same way that the ocean forms waves -- except that the ocean's waves are confined to its surface or to its basin, while the psyche's events are instantly translated, and splash out into mass psychological reality. In waking life you meet the completed event, so to speak. You encounter events in the arena of waking consciousness. In the dream state, and at other levels of consciousness, you deal more directly with the formation of events. You are usually as unaware of this process as you are in normal practice of the ways in which you form your sentences, which seem to flow from you so automatically.
quotes.translation: 同样地，你们塑造事件，却往往浑然不觉。事件的发生看似如同话语的说出。你们在学校学习如何构建句子，从长辈那里学会说话。然而在出生之前，你们就已参与事件制造。心灵塑造事件的方式如同海洋形成波浪——只是海洋的波浪局限于其表面或盆地，而心灵的事件则会瞬间转化，飞溅入集体心理现实。在清醒生活中，你们可以说是遭遇已完成的事件。你们在清醒意识的竞技场中遇见事件。而在梦境状态及其他意识层面，你们更直接地处理事件的形成。通常你们对此过程毫无觉察，正如平时对遣词造句的方式浑然不觉——那些句子仿佛自动从你们口中流淌而出。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche

quotes.text: Alter your time orientation in other such exercises. This will automatically allow you to break away from too narrow a focus. It will to some extent break apart the rigid interlocking of your perception into reality as you have learned how to perceive it. Children can play so vividly that they might, for example, imagine themselves parched under a desert sun, though they are in the middle of the coolest air-conditioned living room. They are on the one hand completely involved in their activity, yet on the other hand they are quite aware of their "normal" environment. Yet the adult often fears that any such playful unofficial alteration of consciousness is dangerous, and becomes worried that the imagined situation will supersede the real one.
quotes.translation: 在其他此类练习中改变你的时间取向。这将自动让你摆脱过于狭隘的焦点。它会在某种程度上打破你感知现实时那种僵硬的连锁结构——正如你所学到的那样。例如，孩子们可以玩得如此生动，以至于他们可能想象自己置身于沙漠烈日下，尽管他们身处最凉爽的空调客厅中。一方面，他们完全沉浸在自己的活动中，另一方面，他们又相当清楚自己的“正常”环境。然而，成年人常常害怕任何这种非正式的、游戏般的意识改变是危险的，并担心想象的情境会取代真实的情境。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche

quotes.text: (11:41.) These strands are like double dreams that continue. They also serve as a framework to the recognized self. In periods of stress or challenge the recognized self may sense these other strains of consciousness, and realize that a fuller experience is possible, a greater psychological thickness. On some occasions in the dream state the recognized self may then enlarge its perception enough to take advantage of these other portions of its own identity. Double or triple dreams may represent such encounters at times. Consciousness always seeks the richest, most creative form, while ever maintaining its own integrity. The imagination, playing, the arts and dreaming, allow it to enrich its activities by providing feedback other than that received in the physical environment itself.
quotes.translation: （11:41。）这些线索就像持续的双重梦境。它们也为被认可的自己提供了一个框架。在压力或挑战的时期，被认可的自己可能会感知到这些其他的意识流，并意识到一种更完整的体验是可能的，一种更大的心理厚度。有时在梦境状态中，被认可的自己可能会扩大其感知，足以利用其自身身份的这些其他部分。双重或三重梦境有时可能代表这样的相遇。意识总是寻求最丰富、最具创造性的形式，同时始终保持其自身的完整性。想象力、游戏、艺术和做梦，通过提供不同于物理环境本身所接收的反馈，使其能够丰富其活动。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche

quotes.text: In titling this chapter I used the word "mechanics," because mechanisms suggest smooth technological workings. While the world is not a machine -- its inner workings are such that no technology could ever copy them -- this involves a natural mechanics in which the inner dimensions of consciousness everywhere emerge to form a materialized, cohesive, physical existence. Again, your interpretations of identity teach you to focus awareness in such a way that you cannot follow the strands of consciousness that connect you with all portions of nature. In a way, the world is like a multidimensional, exotic plant growing in space and time, each thought, dream, imaginative encounter, hope or fear, growing naturally into its own bloom -- a plant of incredible variety, never for a moment the same, in which each smallest root, leaf, stem, or flower has a part to play and is connected with the whole.
quotes.translation: 在为本章节命名时，我使用了"机制"一词，因为机制暗示着顺畅的技术运作。虽然世界并非机器——其内在运作方式没有任何技术能够复制——但这涉及一种自然机制，其中意识的内在维度处处涌现，形成物质化、凝聚的物理实相。再次强调，你们对身份认同的诠释引导你们以特定方式聚焦觉知，以至于无法追踪那将你们与自然万物相连的意识脉络。从某种角度看，世界犹如一株生长在时空中的多维异域植物，每个念头、梦境、想象际遇、希望或恐惧，都自然绽放成独特花朵——这株植物拥有不可思议的多样性，瞬息万变，其中每根最细微的根须、叶片、茎干或花朵都扮演着角色，并与整体相连。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: "I've had several new experiences with altered states of consciousness," she wrote in labored script, "and these are quite different than anything I've done before. For this reason they are difficult to classify...." Also, she's indulged in long conversations with me--and on occasion with certain friends--when we apparently were present in out-of-body states. Related here are actions she thought she was participating in with me, say, yet when she "woke up," she discovered we hadn't done any of those things. She's referred often to "gaps in my consciousness" while dozing. "I don't know what I was doing in my chair," she said at 11:05 A.M. yesterday; she'd fallen asleep after telling me she had to use the commode. "I don't like the way the thyroid business is making me feel.... I feel like I'm in your way, or in life's way...." She had certainly been depressed on that occasion, and I'd tried to cheer her up.
quotes.translation: "我在意识转换状态中有了几次新体验，"她用潦草的字迹写道，"这些与我之前经历的任何体验都截然不同。正因如此，它们难以归类……"此外，她还与我进行了长谈——偶尔也与某些朋友交谈——当时我们显然以出体状态在场。这里涉及她以为与我共同参与的行动，但当她"醒来"时，却发现我们并未做过任何那些事。她常提到打盹时"意识中的间隙"。"我不知道自己在椅子上做了什么，"她昨天上午11:05这样说；她在告诉我需要使用便桶后便睡着了。"我不喜欢甲状腺问题带给我的感受……我感觉自己成了你的负担，或生命的负担……"当时她显然很沮丧，我曾试图让她振作起来。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1

quotes.text: But I note with some amusement that science absorbs such heresies by weaving them into and developing them out of current establishment thinking--concepts, say, like the many-worlds interpretation of quantum mechanics. Put very simplistically, this "quantum approach" allows for the theme that each of us inhabits but one of innumerable probable or parallel worlds. Even the theory of evolution is invoked, for those other worlds are said to evolve in parallel with the one we inhabit. Yet there is no answer within quantum mechanics as to how or why one's personal identity chooses to follow a certain probable pathway, and consciousness per se is not considered. (Some physicists, however, have implied that subatomic particles--photons--communicate with each other as they take their separate but "sympathetic" paths.) Pardon my irony here, but Seth has always dealt with the ramifications of consciousness and maintained also that we do not inhabit just one probable world, but constantly move among them by choice--and by the microsecond, if one chooses.
quotes.translation: 但我颇觉有趣地注意到，科学吸收这类异端邪说的方式，是将它们编织并发展自当前主流思想——比如量子力学的多世界诠释。简而言之，这种"量子方法"允许这样的主题：我们每个人只栖居于无数可能或平行世界中的一个。甚至进化论也被援引，因为据说那些其他世界与我们居住的世界平行演化。然而量子力学并未解答个人身份如何或为何选择遵循某条特定可能路径，且意识本身未被考虑。（不过有些物理学家暗示，亚原子粒子——光子——在各自沿着分离却"共鸣"的路径时彼此沟通。）请原谅我的讽刺，但赛斯始终处理意识的衍生结果，并坚持我们并非只居住于一个可能世界，而是通过选择——若愿意，可以微秒计——不断在其中移动。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1

quotes.text: If the hassles surrounding TMI have engendered forces of a scientifically oriented consciousness, then, certainly those in Iran have released a very strong religiously oriented consciousness. Religious drives of whatever nature are much more comprehensible to us than scientific ones: I think it quite safe to note that in ordinary terms our species began struggling with religious expression long before it began recording history. This year [1979], Iran has turned into a land in which all Western nations--but particularly the United States--have become anathema. Iran's religious leaders actually run the country now, operating behind a weak secular and probably temporary government appointed by its Western-leaning and departed leader before he fled his country last January. [Now, looking tired and ill, he travels the world with his expensive entourage, looking for a safe place to live after leading 25 years of savage oppression in his homeland.]
quotes.translation: 若围绕三哩岛事件产生的麻烦催生了科学导向意识的力量，那么伊朗无疑释放了非常强烈的宗教导向意识。无论何种性质的宗教驱动力，对我们而言都比科学驱动力更易理解：我认为可以稳妥地指出，按通常说法，人类在开始记录历史之前很久就已开始与宗教表达抗争。今年（1979年），伊朗已变成所有西方国家——尤其是美国——成为诅咒之地。伊朗宗教领袖如今实际掌控国家，运作在一个由亲西方且已离去的领袖去年一月逃离前指定的软弱世俗且可能暂时的政府背后。（如今，他带着昂贵的随从周游世界，面容疲惫病态，在领导祖国25年野蛮压迫后寻找安全居所。）
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1

quotes.text: However, if objective proof of that nature is considered the priority for facts, then as you know science cannot prove its version of the [universe's] origin either. It only sets up an hypothesis, which collects about it all data that agree, and again ignores what does not fit. Moreover, science's thesis meets with no answering affirmation in the human heart--and in fact arouses the deepest antipathy, for in his heart man well knows his own worth, and realizes that his own consciousness is no accident.5 The psyche, then, possesses within itself an inner affirmation, an affirmation that provides the impetus for physical emergence, an affirmation that keeps man from being completely blinded by his own mental edifices (all with much emphasis and fast delivery.
quotes.translation: 然而，若以那种性质的客观证据作为事实的优先考量，那么如你所知，科学也无法证明其关于[宇宙]起源的版本。它只是建立了一个假设，收集所有与之相符的数据，再次忽略不相符的部分。此外，科学的论点在人类心中得不到任何回应的肯定——实际上还激起最深层的反感，因为人类内心深知自身价值，明白自己的意识绝非偶然。因此，心灵自身拥有内在的肯定，这种肯定为物质显现提供了动力，使人类不至于被自己的心智建构完全蒙蔽（语气强调且语速极快）。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1

quotes.text: You are subjectively "alive" before your birth. You will be subjectively alive after your death. Your subjective life is now interpreted through the specialized state of consciousness that you call the waking one, in which you recognize as real only experience that falls within certain space and time coordinates. Your greater reality exists outside those coordinates, and so does the reality of the universe. (Pause.) You create lives for yourselves, changing them as you go along, as a writer might change a book, altering the circumstances, changing the plots. The writer only knows that he or she creates without understanding the spontaneous order with which the creativity happens. The processes occur at another level of consciousness (underlined).
quotes.translation: 你在出生前就已主观地“活着”。你死后仍将主观地活着。你的主观生命现在通过你称为清醒状态的特殊意识状态来诠释，在这种状态中，你只承认落在特定时空坐标内的经验为真实。你的更大实相存在于那些坐标之外，宇宙的实相也是如此。（停顿。）你为自己创造生命，在过程中不断改变它们，如同作家可能修改一本书，改变情境、调整情节。作家只知道自己在创造，却不了解创造力发生的自发秩序。这些过程发生在另一层意识层面（强调）。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1

quotes.text: Beyond certain levels it is almost meaningless to speak in terms of particles, but I will for now use the term "invisible particles" because you are familiar with it. Invisible particles, then, form the foundation of your world. The invisible particles that I am referring to, however, have the ability to transform themselves into mass,1 or to divest themselves of it. And the invisible particles of which I speak not only possess consciousness--but each one is, if you will, a seed that contains within itself a potential for an infinite number of gestalts. Each such invisible particle contains within itself the potential (pause) to embark upon an infinite number of probable variations of consciousness. To that degree such psychological particles are at that stage unspecialized, while they contain within themselves the innate ability to specialize in whatever direction becomes suitable.
quotes.translation: 超越某些层次后，用粒子来讨论几乎毫无意义，但我现在暂时使用“无形粒子”一词，因为你对它熟悉。那么，无形粒子构成了你世界的基础。然而，我所说的无形粒子能够将自身转化为质量，或摆脱质量。而且，我所说的无形粒子不仅拥有意识——而且每一个，如果你愿意，都是一颗种子，自身包含着无限数量完形模式的潜能。每一个这样的无形粒子自身都包含着（停顿）踏上无限数量意识可能变体的潜能。在那种程度上，这类心理粒子在那个阶段尚未专门化，而它们自身却包含着在任何合适方向专门化的先天能力。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2

quotes.text: Your stated universe emerged out of that kind of interval, emerging from a master event whose true nature remains uncaptured by your definitions--so there will be places in our book where I may say that an event known to you is true and untrue at the same time, or that it is both myth and fact. And in so doing I hope to lead you toward some psychic comprehension of a kind of event far too large for your usual categories of true and false. [Perhaps], then, you will let your imaginations play upon the usual events of your world, and glimpse at least in part that greater brilliance that illuminates them, so that it leads you intuitively to a feeling for the source of events and the source of your world. The units of consciousness that I have mentioned are (underlined) that, and they do behave as I have said. They are also in other terms entities, fragments of All That Is, if you prefer--divine fragments of power and majesty, containing (pause) all of the powers of consciousness as you think of it, concentrations without substance in your terms.
quotes.translation: 你所陈述的宇宙正是从那种间隙中浮现，源自一个其真实本质无法被你的定义所捕捉的主事件——因此，在我们的书中会有这样的地方，我可能会说，你所知的一个事件既真实又不真实，或者它既是神话又是事实。通过这样做，我希望引导你走向对一种远比你通常的真假范畴更为宏大的事件类型的某种心灵理解。那么，[或许]你可以让你的想象力在你世界的寻常事件上驰骋，至少部分瞥见那照亮它们的更伟大的光辉，从而直觉地引导你感受到事件的源头和你世界的源头。我所提到的意识单位（下划线强调）正是如此，它们确实如我所说的那样运作。用其他术语来说，它们也是实体，是万有之神的碎片（如果你愿意这样称呼）——神圣的力量与庄严的碎片，包含（停顿）你所认为的意识的所有力量，在你的术语中是没有实质的凝聚体。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2

quotes.text: At one time, then, you were more open in a fashion to the kinds of consciousness that you admitted into your circle of reality. At one time, in those terms, you did not draw the lines as finely as you do now. Instead you included such cousins of consciousness into your midst, accepting a kind of comradeship--for to some extent at least you could see the different versions of humanity that resulted from a change of focus, an adjacent affiliation of humanized energy with the environment. Quite simply, you felt that in certain terms you had other brothers and sisters in the world that were like you but unlike you, that put together the contents of the universe in their own fashions. Such species, of course, can nowhere appear within the dictates of evolution or be perceived as realities except under those conditions when you relax your usual conventions of perception and behavior.
quotes.translation: 那么，在某个时期，你在某种程度上对你所接纳进入现实圈子的意识种类更为开放。在某个时期，用那些术语来说，你并没有像现在这样划出如此精细的界限。相反，你将这样的意识表亲纳入你的领域，接受了一种同志情谊——因为至少在一定程度上，你能看到因焦点改变而产生的不同版本的人性，那是人化能量与环境的邻近关联。简单来说，你曾觉得，在某种层面上，你在世界上有其他兄弟姐妹，他们像你又不像你，以他们自己的方式组合宇宙的内容。当然，这样的物种在进化论的指令下无处可现，也无法被感知为现实，除非在你放松通常的感知和行为惯例的那些条件下。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2

quotes.text: Ironically, as individuals and nations we talk about casting off old beliefs while cherishing them as long as possible. Why have large segments of consciousness chosen to operate in such a fashion? I think we're creating a probable reality in which consciousness has the absolute freedom to explore all facets of itself--every one we can think of, and therefore create. Within our national orientations, within our religious and secular, scientific and artistic structures, we are choosing to go to the extremes of "good" and "bad," and to deal with the consequences, all stewing together in what seems like an impossible mix of reason and emotion, learning and joy, pain and violence, and life and death. Naturally, many of us don't like certain facets of our creations, yet we must deal with all of them if we are to make any sense out of our reality. Otherwise, our growing will be too limited; we'll remain slaves to our animosities.
quotes.translation: 讽刺的是，作为个体和国家，我们一边谈论抛弃旧信念，一边却尽可能长久地珍视它们。为何意识的大片领域选择以这种方式运作？我认为我们正在创造一个可能性的实相，在其中意识拥有绝对自由去探索自身的所有面向——每一个我们能想到并因此创造的面向。在我们的国家取向中，在我们的宗教与世俗、科学与艺术结构中，我们选择走向"善"与"恶"的极端，并处理其后果，这一切都混杂在看似不可能融合的理性与情感、学习与喜悦、痛苦与暴力、生与死的混合物中。自然，我们中许多人不喜欢自己创造的某些面向，但若要理解我们的实相，就必须面对所有面向。否则，我们的成长将过于受限；我们将沦为自身敌意的奴隶。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2

quotes.text: As far as these drugs are concerned there are a few points I would like to make. One of your purposes as I have said is to learn to organize energy units and to focus your own energy along those lines. That is, you are the energy that you use. The self-consciousness is not to be blotted out but taken along. It is so to speak to walk side by side with the inner self. The use of these drugs sweeps the personality off its feet. Often the personality does receive a rewarding glimpse of the inner reality, but more often the ego is merely sent tumbling through frightening images of chaotic phantom realities formed by a suddenly-released subconscious, and with no guide. The experience is often vivid and long remembered, but so unorganized and undisciplined that no inner order is arrived at, no other organization glimpsed, and the ordinary but necessary camouflage footing suddenly dispensed with.
quotes.translation: 关于这些药物，我想提出几点看法。如我所说，你们的目的之一是学习组织能量单元，并沿着这些路线聚焦自身能量。也就是说，你们就是自己所使用的能量。自我意识不应被抹除，而应被携带前行。可以说，它应与内在自我并肩而行。使用这些药物会使人格失去平衡。人格有时确实能瞥见内在实相的有益景象，但更常见的是，自我只是被抛入由突然释放的潜意识形成的混乱幻象实相中，在恐惧的意象中翻滚，且无人引导。这种体验往往生动且长久难忘，但如此缺乏组织与纪律，以致无法达成内在秩序，无法瞥见其他组织形态，而平常却必要的伪装基础突然被弃置不顾。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)

quotes.text: I have told you that some portion of our sessions involves projection. The degree varies from session to session. There will be a time when Ruburt will project out of his body during sessions and remember what transpires while I speak through his body. When projections occur now he is not consciously aware of them. These things are not forced, you see. On other occasions we meet, so to speak. I will go into this perhaps soon, for there are the similarities here and significances, involving states of consciousness used by both Ruburt and myself, and the type of projection achieved. These have bearing on the clearness or undistorted nature of the material. Less satisfactory projections cause distortions, you see, that are characteristic features of this state of consciousness at that time.
quotes.translation: 我曾告诉你们，我们课程的某些部分涉及投射。投射程度因课程而异。将来会有那么一个时刻，鲁柏在课程中会投射出身体，并记得当我通过他的身体说话时所发生的事。目前发生投射时，他并未有意识地察觉。你看，这些事并非强求而来。在其他场合，我们可以说会相遇。我或许很快会深入探讨这一点，因为这里存在相似性与重要性，涉及鲁柏和我所使用的意识状态，以及所达成的投射类型。这些因素关系到资料的清晰度或未扭曲的本质。你看，较不令人满意的投射会导致扭曲，而这些扭曲正是当时该意识状态的特征。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)

quotes.text: You should desire good health because it is a natural state of your being. You should trust in the innate intelligence of your own being... which produces good health. Health is a natural state of your being. Through your physical image the energy of the universe expresses itself. You as an individualized consciousness are a part of this, and you cannot express yourself fully nor fulfill your purpose as an identity, as an individual, if you are not in good health... for the effects of the body are felt in the mind... and the effects of the mind are felt in the body. You distort the picture. Now I did not intend a question and answer service this evening, but I seem to be involved in one. And some evening I will turn the tables. And when I ask you questions, I will expectat some answers. And they will not be easy questions... and they will not be general questions. And I will make you search for the answers... and the answers will form steps upon which you can walk and will serve as foundations within which you can travel within yourselves. But they will be your answers and not my answers.
quotes.translation: 你应当渴望健康，因为它是你存在的自然状态。你应当信任自身与生俱来的智慧……这智慧会带来健康。健康是你存在的自然状态。宇宙的能量通过你的物质形象表达自身。你作为一个个体化的意识，是这能量的一部分。如果你不健康，就无法充分表达自己，也无法完成你作为个体、作为身份的目的……因为身体的状态会影响心灵……而心灵的状态也会影响身体。你扭曲了这幅画面。今晚我本无意进行问答服务，但似乎已身陷其中。某个夜晚我会反客为主。当我向你们提问时，我期待得到答案。那些问题不会简单……也不会泛泛而谈。我会迫使你们寻找答案……这些答案将成为你们行走的阶梯，成为你们内在探索的基石。但那是你们的答案，而非我的答案。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)

quotes.text: At the same time the camouflage consciousness cannot be aware of the actual originator, and therefore must look for causes from the outside. In your dual system, that is, the two self-consciousness are more divided and alien to themselves than need be. The old idea of spirits pervading all physical matter actually represents an intuitive glimpse into reality that your sciences will finally arrive at in a long labored manner.
quotes.translation: 与此同时，伪装意识无法觉察真正的创造者，因此必须从外界寻找原因。在你们的二元系统中，两种自我意识之间的分裂与疏离程度超过了必要。古老观念中"灵体渗透所有物质"的说法，实际上是对实相的一种直觉洞见——你们的科学终将以漫长而艰辛的方式抵达这一认知。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)

quotes.text: You are, as I said earlier, a spirit now; and that spirit has a consciousness. The consciousness belongs to the spirit then, but the two are not the same. The spirit may turn its consciousness off and on. By its nature consciousness may flicker and fluctuate, but the spirit does not.
quotes.translation: 如我之前所说，你此刻就是灵体；这个灵体拥有意识。意识属于灵体，但二者并非同一。灵体可以开启或关闭其意识。就其本质而言，意识可能闪烁波动，但灵体不会。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: However there is a chemical energy used in all such out-of-body experiences. The chemical reaction results in an electromagnetic connection between the consciousness and the physical body, and without it there would be no return to the physical body, in your terms. Of course it is mental energy that makes the chemical energy possible, however. This freedom of consciousness is not dependent upon physical existence, you see.
quotes.translation: 然而，所有这类出体经验中都使用了一种化学能量。这种化学反应导致意识与肉体之间产生电磁连接，用你们的话说，没有这种连接就无法返回肉体。当然，是心智能量使化学能量成为可能。你看，这种意识的自由并不依赖于物质存在。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 6 of The Seth Material (Sessions 240-280)

quotes.text: If you could orbit your planet in a different kind of craft, you could view the psychic contents of the world, seeing the world consciousness shining far more brilliantly than any lighted city. You could spot the point of intense activity, see the birth of new myths and the death of old ones as certainly as you might be able to see a mountain slide or a tidal wave. The physical portions of earth are all related. So does consciousness form its own kind of inner structures from which, again, the physical ones emerge. You are indeed counterparts, then, each of the other. Yet as there is great variety to physical form, so counterparts follow a still more expansive inner freedom that finds an even greater diversity of characteristics.
quotes.translation: 如果你能乘坐另一种飞行器绕行你们的星球，就能看到世界的灵性内涵，看到世界意识的光芒比任何灯火通明的城市都要耀眼。你能发现剧烈活动的焦点，看到新神话的诞生和旧神话的消亡，就像你能看到山崩或海啸一样确定。地球的物质部分都是相互关联的。意识同样形成其自身的内在结构，物质结构再次从中涌现。因此，你们确实是彼此的对应体。然而，正如物质形态具有极大的多样性，对应体也遵循着一种更广阔的内在自由，这种自由展现出更加多样化的特性。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: "All That Is creates its reality as it goes along. Each world has its own impetus, yet all are ultimately connected. The true dimensions of a divine creativity would be unendurable for any one consciousness of whatever import, and so that splendor is infinitely dimensionalized (most intensely throughout), worlds spiraling outward with each 'moment' of a cosmic breath; with the separation of worlds a necessity, and with individual and mass comprehension always growing at such a rate that All That Is multiplies itself at microseconds, building both pasts and futures and other time scales you do not recognize. Each is a reality in itself, with its own potentials, and with no individual consciousness, however minute, ever lost.
quotes.translation: “一切万有”在行进中创造其现实。每个世界都有其自身的动力，但所有世界最终都相互关联。神圣创造力的真实维度，对任何意识——无论其重要性如何——都是无法承受的，因此那辉煌被无限维度化（最强烈地贯穿始终），世界随着宇宙呼吸的每个“瞬间”向外螺旋展开；世界的分离是必要的，个体与群体的理解始终以如此速度增长，以至于“一切万有”在微秒间自我倍增，构建着你们不认识的过去、未来及其他时间尺度。每个世界本身就是一个现实，拥有其自身潜能，没有任何个体意识——无论多么微小——会永远失落。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: It is seldom that you really question your biological origins, what they mean, and how you interpret them. Are you physically composed of murderous cells, then, each spontaneously out to get the others? If so, your physical being is more miraculous a product than even I have ever told you! If your cells did not cooperate so well, you would not be listening to this voice, and it would make no sound. As you listen to me, the cooperative, creative adventure within your bodies continues, and in terms of continuity reaches back prehistorically and into the future. Because consciousness creates form with joy, there is no murder that you have not projected out of misunderstanding and ignorance of the nature of that consciousness.
quotes.translation: 你们很少真正质疑自己的生物起源、其意义以及你们如何诠释它们。那么，你们的身体是由凶残的细胞组成，每个都自发地想要吞噬其他细胞吗？如果是这样，你们的物质存在比我曾告诉你们的还要神奇！如果你们的细胞不如此完美地合作，你们就不会听到这个声音，它也不会发出声响。当你们聆听我时，你们体内的合作性、创造性冒险仍在继续，就连续性而言，它回溯到史前并延伸至未来。因为意识以喜悦创造形态，所以没有谋杀不是你们出于对意识本质的误解与无知而投射出来的。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: And each self is endowed with the agony and the impetus, for it is a fabric from which All That Is made itself, and all that you know. Perhaps now you can understand why it is so difficult to try to explain matters to you in terms that you can understand. All That Is loves all that it has created, down to the least, for it realizes the dearness, and the uniqueness of each consciousness which had been wrest (spelled) from such a state and at such a price; and it is triumphant and joyful at each new development taken by each consciousness, for this is an added triumph against that first state, and an added security against that first state, and it revels and (takes) joy in the slightest creative act of each of its issue.
quotes.translation: 每个自我都被赋予了痛苦与动力，因为它是一块织锦——万有即由此织就自身，亦织就你所知的一切。或许此刻你方能明白，为何要以你能理解的措辞向你解释事物是如此困难。万有深爱它所创造的一切，哪怕最微末者亦不例外，因它深知每个意识从那种状态中挣脱而出时付出的代价何其珍贵、何其独特；每个意识每取得新的进展，它便欢欣鼓舞——因为这是对那原初状态的又一次胜利，是对那原初状态的又一次保障，它对自己每个造物最微小的创造行为都沉醉其中、欣然喜悦。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)

quotes.text: (10:08.) For one thing, while individualized consciousness was within the massive subjectivity of All That Is, it enjoyed, beside its own uniqueness, a feeling of supporting unity, a comforting knowledge that it was one with its source. So in the beginning of [your] world, consciousness fluctuated greatly, focusing gently at the start, but not quite as willing to be as fully independent as its first intent might seem.
quotes.translation: （10:08）其一，当个体化意识尚在万有的庞大主体性之内时，它除了自身独特性之外，还享有被整体支撑的感受——一种与源头合一的慰藉认知。因此在你们世界的开端，意识剧烈波动，起初只是轻柔地聚焦，却并不像其最初意图所显现的那般甘愿全然独立。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1

quotes.text: Throughout the ages some have recognized the fact that there is self consciousness and purpose in certain dream and sleep states, and have maintained, even in waking life, the sense of continuity of this inner self. To such people it is no longer possible to identify completely with the ego consciousness. They are too obviously aware of themselves as more. When such knowledge is gained, the ego can accept it, for it finds to its surprise that it is not less conscious, but more conscious, that its limitations are dissipated; now it is not true, and I emphasize this strongly, that so-called unconscious material, given any freedom, will draw energy away from the egotistically organized self in a normal personality.
quotes.translation: 历经万古，有人已认识到某些梦境与睡眠状态中存在自我意识与目的，甚至在清醒生活中仍保持着这内在自我的连续性感知。对这样的人而言，再也不可能完全认同于自我意识。他们太过清晰地意识到自己远不止于此。当获得这种认知时，自我能够接纳它——因为它惊讶地发现，自己非但没有减少意识，反而更加有意识，其局限已然消散；在此我要着重强调：所谓无意识材料，若给予任何自由，绝不会从正常人格中以自我为中心组织起来的自我那里抽走能量。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)

quotes.text: However, for every scientist bold enough to think this way, there are scores of others who vehemently disagree. For most scientific materialists only physical matter is real. For them consciousness is nothing more than an epiphenomenon, the passive by-product of the brain's physiology and chemical events. They believe that physical death is the end of everything, that ultimately all is pointless. They derisively call their rebellious colleagues "animists"--those who believe that all life forms and natural phenomena have a spiritual origin independent of physical matter. (Such heretics are also called "vitalists," a term related to animism, and one which also has a long history of scientific contempt behind it.)
quotes.translation: 然而，每有一位敢于如此思考的科学家，就有数十位强烈反对者。对大多数科学唯物论者而言，唯有物质才是真实的。对他们来说，意识不过是附带现象，是大脑生理与化学活动的被动副产品。他们相信肉体死亡即一切终结，最终万物皆无意义。他们轻蔑地称这些叛逆的同行为"泛灵论者"——那些相信所有生命形式与自然现象皆具有独立于物质的精神起源者。（这类异端也被称为"生机论者"，这个与泛灵论相关的术语，同样背负着科学界长期以来的鄙夷。）
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1

quotes.text: This is done automatically. The data is not radioed back (gesture, eyes closed) to the physical body, so to speak, for interpretation. Consciousness can adapt physical methods of procedure even when separated from the body. It forms its own pseudo physical apparatus, even as it originally forms the physical image itself. The consciousness is aware of seeing and observing more or less in physical terms, and so it is.
quotes.translation: 这是自动完成的。数据并非（手势，闭眼）被传回肉体进行解读。意识即使脱离身体，也能适应物质性的运作方式。它形成自己的拟物质装置，正如它最初形成物质形象本身。意识以近乎物质的方式感知观察，事实正是如此。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)

quotes.text: (10:45.) Give us a moment ... Now to some extent, you see, you are both involved, but in ways almost impossible for me to explain. Do not take this literally by any means. Portions of your consciousness are alive in mine, so you are to some extent carried along where I go, as motes of dust might be swept along with a brisk autumn wind from one area to another. (Humorously:) I am not comparing you with motes of dust by any means; to some extent, however, you do share in my journeys. You are carried above the land of your usual perception so that portions of you glimpse subjective states. These arouse your curiosity even when consciously you are not aware of perceiving them. That curiosity acts as impetus.
quotes.translation: （10:45分）稍等片刻……此刻你们二人某种程度上都参与其中，但方式几乎难以言喻。切莫按字面意思理解。你们意识的某些部分活跃于我的意识中，因此你们会随我同行，如同秋风中飞扬的尘埃从一处飘向另一处。（幽默地）我绝非将你们比作尘埃；但你们确实在某种程度上分享我的旅程。你们被托举至惯常感知的疆域之上，使部分自我得以瞥见主观状态。即便意识层面未曾察觉，这份好奇已悄然萌生，化作前行的动力。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: There are here also thrusts in all directions, with certain identifying pulsations that underlie all, and that have their origins within the inner self. As the vitality of the universe forms, of itself, the boundaries of the various fields of activity, and as vitality itself takes on the coloration of the various fields and forms the camouflage patterns within them, so also vitality, in the form of emotions that the camouflage patterns of the personality, even while this vitality forms both emotions and personality for every individual, every consciousness, may be thought of as a separate field of activity; and all the data relating to fields of activity may be seen to apply to any consciousness as well.
quotes.translation: 此处同样存在向各方向的推进，伴随某些贯穿一切且源自内在自我的特定识别脉动。正如宇宙的生命力自行形成各活动领域的边界，正如生命力本身染上各领域的色彩并形成其中的伪装模式，同样地，生命力以情感形式——即使它同时为每个个体、每个意识塑造情感与人格——可被视为独立的活动领域；而所有与活动领域相关的资料，亦可视为适用于任何意识。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)

quotes.text: Your universe cannot be its own source. Its inner mysteries--which are indeed the mysteries of consciousness, not matter--cannot be explained, and must remain incomprehensible, if you try to study them from the viewpoint of your objective experience alone. You must look to the source of that experience. You must look not to space but to the source of space, not to time but to the source of time--and most of all, you must look to the kind of consciousness that experiences space and time. You must look, therefore, to events that show themselves through historical action, but whose origins are elsewhere. None of this is really beyond your capabilities, as long as you try to enlarge your framework.
quotes.translation: 你们的宇宙并非其自身的源头。其内在奥秘——这些奥秘实为意识的奥秘，而非物质的奥秘——若仅从客观经验的角度去研究，便无法解释，且必然难以理解。你们必须探寻那经验的源头。你们必须看向空间，不如说看向空间的源头；看向时间，不如说看向时间的源头——而最重要的是，你们必须看向那体验空间与时间的意识类型。因此，你们必须看向那些通过历史行动显现自身，但其起源却别有所在的事件。只要你们尝试扩展自己的框架，这一切其实都在你们的能力范围之内。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2

quotes.text: When your friend Philip innocently asked at what point human consciousness entered the picture, his question was not pertinent. Human consciousness involves entity consciousness, and entity consciousness asserted itself at the very first phase of physical materialization.
quotes.translation: 当你的朋友菲利普天真地问及人类意识是在何时进入画面时，他的问题并不恰当。人类意识涉及存有意识，而存有意识在物质化的最初阶段便已彰显自身。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)

quotes.text: Think of the greater you -- call it the entity if you want to -- as forming a psychic structure quite as real as your physical one, but composed of many selves. As each cell of your body has its position within your corporeal space and boundaries, so each self within the entity is aware of its own "time" and dimension of activity. The body is a temporal structure. The cells, however, while a part of this body, are not aware of the entire dimension in which your consciousness dwells. They do not perceive all of the elements that are available even in three-dimensional experience, yet your present consciousness -- seemingly so much more sophisticated -- physically rests upon cellular awareness.
quotes.translation: 将那个更大的你——若你愿意，可称之为存有——视为一个与你的物质结构同样真实，却由众多自我组成的心理结构。正如你身体的每个细胞在你肉体空间与边界内各有其位，存有中的每个自我也知晓自身活动的"时间"与维度。身体是一个时间性的结构。然而，细胞虽是身体的一部分，却无法感知你意识所栖居的整个维度。它们甚至无法察觉三维经验中可用的所有元素，而你当前看似复杂得多的意识，在物质层面上却依赖于细胞的觉知。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality

quotes.text: I try to strongly state the pristine uniqueness of the individual. I also say that there are no limitations to the self. The two statements can appear to be contradictory. When you are a child, your sense of identity does not include old age in usual experience. When you are an old person, you do not identify yourself as a child. Your sense of identity, then, changes physically through the years. In a way it seems that you add on to yourself through experience, becoming "more than you were before." You move in and out of probable selfhoods, while at the same time -- usually with the greatest of ease -- you maintain an identity of yourself. The mosaics of consciousness are brilliant to behold.
quotes.translation: 我极力强调个体原初的独特性。我也说过自我没有限制。这两句话看似矛盾。当你是个孩子时，你的身份感通常不会包含老年体验。当你是个老人时，你也不会将自己认同为孩子。因此，你的身份感会随着岁月在肉体层面改变。从某种角度看，你似乎通过经验不断"超越过去的自己"。你在各种可能的自我形态中穿梭，同时——通常毫不费力地——维持着自身的身份认同。意识的马赛克璀璨夺目。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: Chemicals themselves will simply not give rise to consciousness or to life. Your scientists will simply have to face the facts that consciousness comes first, and evolves its own form. But this is, and involves, individuality, and also interdependence. The physical body that you imagine consists of some sort of separate consciousness, controlling a framework of completely unconscious parts, is quite farfetched.
quotes.translation: 化学物质本身绝不会产生意识或生命。你们的科学家终将不得不面对事实：意识先于一切，并演化出它自己的形态。但这涉及且包含个体性，也包含相互依存性。你们想象中那个由某种独立意识操控着一套完全无意识部件的物质身体，这种想法实在牵强。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)

quotes.text: Some waking states, of course, come very close to sleep states. These blend one into the other so that the rhythm often goes unnoticed. These gradations of consciousness are accompanied by changes in the physical organism. In the more sluggish periods of waking consciousness there is a lack of concentration, a cutting off of stimuli to varying degrees, an increase in accidents, and generally a lower body tone.
quotes.translation: 当然，某些清醒状态与睡眠状态非常接近。两者相互交融，以至于这种节奏常常不被察觉。这些意识层次的变化伴随着生理机体的改变。在清醒意识较为迟钝的时期，会出现注意力涣散、刺激输入不同程度的中断、事故增多，以及身体机能普遍下降。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: It is only because you have a highly limited conception of your own entity that you insist upon its being almost sterile in its singularity. There are millions of cells within your body, but you call your body a unit, and consider it your own. You do form it, from the inside out, and yet you form it from living substance, and each smallest particle has its own living consciousness.
