There are clumps of matter, and in that respect there are clumps of consciousness, each individual, with their own destiny and abilities and potentials. There are no limitations to your own entity: therefore, how can your entity or soul have boundaries, for boundaries would enclose it and deny it freedom.
quotes.translation: 仅仅因为你们对自己存有的认知极度局限，才会坚持认为它在单一性中近乎贫瘠。你们体内有数百万个细胞，但你们将身体称为一个单位，并视其为己有。你们确实从内而外地塑造它，然而你们是用活生生的物质塑造它，每个最微小的粒子都有其自身的活意识。存在物质的团块，就此而言也存在意识的团块，每个个体都有其自身的命运、能力和潜能。你们的存有没有任何限制：因此，你们的存有或灵魂怎么可能有边界？因为边界会将其封闭，剥夺它的自由。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: (9:40.) We will speak about health and illness more specifically later in the book. I would like to make one point here, however -- that often psychoanalysis is simply a game of hide-and-seek, in which you continue to relinquish responsibility for your actions and reality and assign the basic cause to some area of the psyche, hidden in a dark forest of the past. Then you give yourself the task of finding this secret. In so doing you never think of looking for it in the conscious mind, since you are convinced that all deep answers lie far beneath -- and, moreover, that your consciousness is not only unable to help you but will often send up camouflages instead. So you play that game.
quotes.translation: （九点四十分。）我们将在本书后面更具体地讨论健康与疾病。不过，我想在此先指出一点——精神分析往往只是一场捉迷藏游戏，你不断推卸对自己行为和现实的责任，将根本原因归咎于心灵中某个隐藏在过往黑暗森林里的领域。然后你给自己布置任务去寻找这个秘密。在此过程中，你从未想过在意识层面寻找答案，因为你深信所有深层答案都潜藏在更深处——而且，你的意识不仅无法帮助你，反而常常会释放出伪装。于是你便玩起了这个游戏。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality

quotes.text: If an inhabitant from another reality outside of your own physical system entirely were to visit it, and if "his" intelligence was roughly of the same degree as your own, he would still have to learn to focus his consciousness in the same way that you do, more or less, in order to perceive your world. He would have to alter his native focus and turn it in a direction that was foreign to him. In this way he could "pick up your station."1 There would be distortions, because even though he managed such manipulations he might not have the same kind of native physical structure as your own, of course, through which to receive and interpret those data his altered consciousness perceived.
quotes.translation: 如果有个完全来自你们物质系统之外另一实相的居民造访此地，而"他"的智力与你们大致相当，那么为了感知你们的世界，他仍必须学会以与你们大致相同的方式聚焦意识。他必须改变自己本来的聚焦方式，转向一个对他而言陌生的方向。这样他才能"接收你们的频道"。这会产生扭曲，因为即便他成功进行了这种操控，他可能没有你们这种天生的物质结构——当然，这种结构正是用来接收和诠释他改变后的意识所感知到的数据。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: The species is in a state of transition, one of many. This one began, generally speaking, when the species tried to step apart from nature in order to develop the unique kind of consciousness that is presently your own. That consciousness is not a finished product, however, but one meant to change, [to] evolve and develop." Certain artificial divisions were made along the way that must now be dispensed with.
quotes.translation: 这个物种正处于众多过渡阶段之一。大体而言，这个阶段始于物种试图脱离自然，以发展出你们目前这种独特的意识。然而，这种意识并非成品，而是注定要改变、进化和发展。在此过程中人为划分出的某些界限，现在必须予以摒弃。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: As he matures and learns, and studies and works, he will then be able to do this when he wishes to do so. There are no significant unhealthy elements connected in this rather innocent regard for fire. Now. The shift of his consciousness is unsteady. He has no discipline over it, and is sometimes in a state of consciousness that is between. Training will allow a strong disciplined focus within either reality.
quotes.translation: 当他成熟、学习、研究和工作时，他将能在愿意时做到这一点。这种对火的天真关注中并无重大不健康元素。现在。他的意识转换不稳定，对此缺乏自律，有时处于中间意识状态。训练将使他能在任一实相中保持强烈而专注的焦点。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)

quotes.text: (10:00.) Such endless creativity can seem so dazzling that the individual would appear lost within it,2 yet consciousness forms its own organizations and psychic interactions at all levels. Any consciousness automatically tries to express itself in all probable directions, and does so. In so doing it will experience All That Is through its own being, though interpreted, of course, through that familiar reality of its own. You grow probable selves as a flower grows petals. Each probable self, however, will follow through in its own reality -- that is, it will experience to the fullest those dimensions inherent to it. You pick and choose one birth and one death, in your terms.
quotes.translation: （十点整。）这种无尽的创造力可能令人目眩神迷，以至于个体仿佛迷失其中，然而意识在所有层面都形成自身的组织与心灵互动。任何意识都会自动尝试在所有可能方向表达自己，也确实如此。在此过程中，它将通过自身存在体验一切万有——当然，是通过它自身熟悉的实相来诠释。你生长出可能自我，如同花朵生长花瓣。然而每个可能自我都会在其自身实相中贯彻始终——也就是说，它将充分体验其内在固有的维度。以你们的说法，你们挑选并选择一次出生与一次死亡。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1

quotes.text: The precise stages are present beneath waking awareness, however, and with the same chemical, electromagnetic, and hormonal fluctuations. You simply are not aware of what your consciousness is doing. You cannot yourselves keep track of it for five full moments of your time. The dimensions of it can only be sensed by those determined enough to take the time and effort required to journey through their own subjective realities. Yet intuitively each individual knows that a part of his experience escapes from him all the time. When you suddenly cannot remember a name that you should know, you have in essence the same kind of feeling of which you are always subconsciously aware.
quotes.translation: 然而，精确的阶段存在于清醒意识之下，伴随着相同的化学、电磁与荷尔蒙波动。你根本不知道自己的意识在做什么。你们自己无法连续追踪它超过五个片刻。只有那些决心投入时间与精力探索自身主观实相的人，才能感知到它的维度。然而直觉上，每个人都知道自己经验的一部分始终在逃离。当你突然记不起本该知道的名字时，本质上你体验到的正是那种你始终潜意识感知到的感觉。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: At various times many people do catch themselves, the experience being so vivid that it leaps the gap, so to speak, with perception so intense that even normal waking consciousness is made aware of it. These intervals are quite necessary to physical consciousness. They are woven through the fabric of your awareness so cleverly and so intimately that they color your psychic and feeling atmosphere. (Pause.)
quotes.translation: 在不同时期，许多人确实会捕捉到自己的这种体验，其生动程度足以跨越意识的鸿沟，感知如此强烈，以至于连正常的清醒意识都能察觉到它。这些间歇对物质意识而言相当必要。它们如此巧妙而亲密地编织进你意识的织锦中，以至于它们为你的心灵和情感氛围染上了色彩。（停顿。）
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: It certainly seems that the best way to get specific answers is to ask specific questions, and the rational mind thinks first of all of something like a list of questions. In that regard, Ruburt's response before such a session is natural, and to an extent magical, because he knows that no matter what he has been taught, he must to some degree (underlined) forget the questions and the mood that accompanies them with one level of his consciousness, in order to create the proper kind of atmosphere at another level of consciousness -- an atmosphere that allows the answers to come even though they may be presented in a different way than that expected by the rational mind.
quotes.translation: 要获得具体答案，最佳方式显然是提出具体问题，而理性思维首先想到的便是类似问题清单之类的东西。就此而言，鲁柏在课程前的反应是自然的，且在某种程度上具有魔法性——因为他知道，无论受过何种教导，他必须在某种程度上（强调）用他意识的一个层面忘记那些问题及伴随的情绪，以便在意识的另一层面营造恰当的氛围——一种允许答案到来的氛围，即便这些答案可能以理性思维预期之外的方式呈现。
quotes.book: The Magical Approach

quotes.text: Consciousness is not made up of balances so much as it is made up of exquisite imbalances, and the focus of awareness is to some degree the result of this state of excitability. In this state all elements are never known because new ones are always being created. I am not speaking of physical elements, but of the psychological characteristics of consciousness, for even those continually merge and change.
quotes.translation: 意识并非由平衡构成，而更多是由精妙的不平衡构成，觉知的焦点在某种程度上正是这种兴奋状态的结果。在这种状态中，所有元素从未被完全知晓，因为新的元素总在被创造。我指的不是物理元素，而是意识的心理特性，因为就连这些特性也在不断融合与变化。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: (11:18.) Ruburt has been involved with what he calls the Sumari language (as referred to in the notes at break). This is an expression of the consciousness at a different focus. It is the native expression of a kind of experience that happens just outside of your official one-line focus of consciousness. First of all, it breaks up verbal patterning.9 It is composed, however, of sounds and syllables Ruburt has heard before, made up of jumbled Romance languages.10 These are "foreign" as far as he is concerned. At the same time those sounds are, in your terms, filled with the implications of antiquity, and bring up connotations of the species' and of the psyche's past.
quotes.translation: （11:18分）鲁柏一直沉浸在他所谓的苏马利语言中（如休息时笔记所述）。这是意识在不同焦点下的表达。它是一种发生在你们官方单线意识焦点之外的经验的本土表达。首先，它打破了语言模式。然而，它由鲁柏以前听过的声音和音节组成，由混杂的罗曼语系构成。就他而言，这些是“外来的”。同时，用你们的话说，这些声音充满了古老的意涵，并唤起物种与心灵过去的联想。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: (11:35.) The life of a star, the life of a flower, are entirely different in your terms of duration, size, and characteristics; yet each exists in a validity of experience that ultimately makes such comparisons meaningless. In the same way, it does not help to compare your own consciousness to one of starlike psychological or psychic properties. The psychological mobility of consciousness, however, allows for an inner kind of communication impossible to verbalize, an interlocking spiritual and biological language by which experience is directly transmuted. Many of your dreams therefore are translations of events occurring in other levels of the greater psyche.
quotes.translation: （11:35分。）一颗星辰的生命与一朵花的生命，在你们的时间长短、大小和特性上截然不同；然而，每一种都存在于经验的真实性中，最终使这样的比较失去意义。同样，将你们自己的意识与具有星辰般心理或灵性属性的意识相比，也无济于事。然而，意识的心理流动性允许一种内在的交流，这种交流无法用言语表达，它是一种交织的精神与生物语言，通过它，经验被直接转化。因此，你们的许多梦是对发生在更大心灵其他层面的事件的翻译。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche

quotes.text: Now. To you... (Long pause, eyes closed.) your paintings are your own, and I am no artist. If I read your inner intent correctly however, your prophet will dominate the background, and seem almost to come out of it. At the same time the background itself will be alive, so it is difficult to tell whether the living background propels him outward, or whether he himself, from his own power, seems to rise out apart from the background. Or whether he has been thrust outward from the background of which he is part, a living focus rising out of the background, a part of consciousness rising out of a larger, undifferentiated consciousness implied in the background.
quotes.translation: 现在。对你而言……（长久的停顿，闭着眼睛。）你的画作是你自己的，我不是艺术家。然而，如果我正确解读你内在的意图，你的先知将主导背景，并几乎像是从背景中浮现出来。同时，背景本身将是活生生的，因此很难分辨是活生生的背景将他推向前，还是他凭借自身的力量似乎从背景中独立升起。或者，他是否从他所属于的背景中被推向前，一个从背景中升起的活生生的焦点，一部分意识从背景中暗示的更大、未分化的意识中升起。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)

quotes.text: In trance states, there is a more or less stationary system waiting, so to speak. In trance states, consciousness itself brings about the chemical changes within it as it, consciousness, travels. Changes do occur within the body. When consciousness alters its direction it automatically alters the physical system. When it is ready to return the physical system is automatically prepared to receive it.
quotes.translation: 在出神状态中，可以说有一个或多或少静止的系统在等待。在出神状态中，意识本身在它——意识——旅行时，引发其内在的化学变化。身体内确实会发生改变。当意识改变其方向时，它自动改变物理系统。当它准备返回时，物理系统自动准备好接收它。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)

quotes.text: For example: There was no beginning, and there will be no end, yet parables have been given telling you of beginnings and endings simply because with your distorted ideas of time, beginnings and endings seem to be inseparable, valid events. As you learn to turn the focus of your attention away from physical reality and therefore experience some slight evidence of other realities, your consciousness will cling to old ideas that make true explanations impossible for you to understand. Multidimensional awareness is available to you in your dreams, however, in some trance states, and often even beneath ordinary consciousness as you go about your day.
quotes.translation: 例如：没有开始，也没有结束，然而你们被告知了关于开始和结束的寓言，仅仅因为以你们扭曲的时间观念，开始和结束似乎是不可分割的有效事件。当你们学会将注意力焦点从物质实相转移开，从而体验到其他实相的一些微弱证据时，你们的意识会紧抓旧观念，使真正的解释对你们而言无法理解。然而，多维觉知在你们的梦中、在某些出神状态中，甚至在你们日常活动时普通意识的底层，都是可及的。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: They can impress others with greater import through their communications. They can move from inner to outer reality with easy ability. They know instinctively how to use symbolism. They are highly creative on an unconscious level, constantly forming psychic frameworks beneath normal consciousness that can be used both by themselves and others in dream and trance states. They often appear to others in the dream condition, and they help dreamers in the manipulation of inner reality. They form images with which the dreamers can relate, images that can be used as bridges and then as gateways into kinds of consciousness more separated from your own.
quotes.translation: 他们能通过沟通传递更重要的讯息，轻松自如地在内在与外在实相之间转换。他们本能地懂得如何运用象征符号。他们在无意识层面极具创造力，不断在正常意识之下构建心灵架构，这些架构既可供他们自己使用，也可供他人在梦境与出神状态中运用。他们常在梦境中显现在他人面前，协助做梦者操控内在实相。他们形成做梦者能与之关联的意象，这些意象可先作为桥梁，再作为通道，通往与你自身意识更疏离的意识类型。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: Instead events are built up, so to speak, from significances. But let us forget that term for a moment and consider association, with which you are already familiar, since your stream of consciousness operates in that fashion. By its very nature each consciousness is a particular, peculiar, and unique focus of awareness which will experience any possible realities through its own characteristics.
quotes.translation: 可以说，事件是由意义累积而成的。但让我们暂时抛开这个术语，考虑一下你已熟悉的联想，因为你的意识流正是以这种方式运作。就其本质而言，每个意识都是特定、独特且唯一的觉知焦点，会通过自身特性体验任何可能的实相。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche

quotes.text: He [Darwin] spent his last years proving it, and yet it has no real validity. It has a validity within very limited perspectives only; for consciousness does, indeed, evolve form. Form does not evolve consciousness. It is according to when you come into the picture, and what you choose to observe ... Consciousness did not come from atoms and molecules scattered by chance through the universe....
quotes.translation: 他（达尔文）耗费晚年试图证明这一点，然而这并无真实有效性。它仅在极其有限的视角内具有有效性；因为意识确实演化出形态。形态并非演化出意识。这取决于你何时进入画面，以及你选择观察什么……意识并非来自偶然散布于宇宙中的原子与分子……
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: Various kinds of seemingly contradictory characteristics may appear, then. One Sumari may have many deeply rewarding personal relationships. Another might find friends a distraction. One Sumari might enjoy performing in front of an audience, while another might not even be able to bear the thought. Since each person is unique, the various Sumari characteristics will then appear quite differently. Some live in cities, basking in the emotional nearness of others, content with a few flowerpots for a reminder of nature's beauty. Another might have a farm. In most cases, however, the slant of consciousness is primarily creative. Period.
quotes.translation: 因此，各种看似矛盾的特质可能显现。某位苏马利可能拥有许多深刻有益的人际关系，另一位则可能视朋友为干扰。一位苏马利可能享受在观众面前表演，而另一位甚至无法忍受这种念头。由于每个人都是独特的，各种苏马利特质将以截然不同的方式呈现。有人居于城市，沐浴在他人情感的亲近中，满足于几盆花作为自然之美的提醒；另有人可能拥有农场。然而在大多数情况下，意识的倾向主要是创造性的。句号。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: Cats in the wild were, in those terms of time, exploring one kind of nature. In that kind of nature, with a natural population taken care of in the environment, there would be far fewer cats than there are now. Your cats would not exist. Why does it seem antinatural, even slightly perverse, for a household cat to, say, prefer fine cat food from a can, when it seems that he should be eating mice, perhaps, or dining upon grasshoppers? The household cat is exploring a different kind of nature, in which he has a certain relationship to human consciousness, a relationship that changes the reality of his particular kind of consciousness.
quotes.translation: 在那种时间尺度下，野猫探索着一种自然。在那种自然中，若环境能照料自然种群，猫的数量会比现在少得多。你们现在的家猫根本不会存在。当家猫似乎本该吃老鼠或蝗虫，却偏爱罐头精制猫粮时，为何这显得反自然甚至有些反常？家猫正在探索另一种自然，在这种自然中，它们与人类意识建立了特定关系——这种关系改变了它们自身意识类型的实相。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1

quotes.text: Vague yearnings toward certain accomplishments may be clues that the necessary characteristics are inherent but untrained in the self that you know. In its own way, the twenty-four hour period represents both an entire lifetime and many lives in one. In it, symbolically, you have "death" as your physically attuned consciousness comes to the end of the amount of stimuli it can comfortably handle without rest. So, at your normal physical death, you come to the point where your earth-attuned consciousness can no longer handle further data without a "longer rest," and organize it into a creative meaningful whole -- in terms of time.
quotes.translation: 对某些成就的模糊渴望，可能暗示着你所知的自我中潜藏着未经训练的必要特质。二十四小时周期以其独特方式，既代表完整的一生，也象征着一体多世。在其中，当你的身体调谐意识无需休息便能舒适处理的刺激量达到极限时，你便象征性地经历了"死亡"。因此，在正常的肉体死亡时，你的地球调谐意识会抵达一个临界点——它需要"更长的休息"才能继续处理新数据，并将其组织成时间维度上具有创造性的意义整体。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality

quotes.text: Ruburt has been working with alterations of consciousness (for Psychic Politics), and wondering about the basic validity of religion. He has been trying to reconcile intellectual and emotional knowledge. James is far from one of his favorite writers, yet Ruburt's interests, intent, and desire were close enough so that under certain conditions he could experience the world view held by James. The unknown reality is unknown only because you believe it must be hidden. Once that belief is annihilated, the other quite-as-legitimate views of reality can appear to your consciousness, and worlds just as valid as your own swim into view.
quotes.translation: 鲁柏一直在研究意识变化（为《心灵政治》一书），并质疑宗教的基本有效性。他试图调和理性知识与情感知识。詹姆斯远非他喜爱的作家之一，但鲁柏的兴趣、意图和渴望足够接近，以至于在特定条件下，他能体验詹姆斯所持的世界观。未知实相之所以未知，只因你相信它必然隐藏。一旦这个信念被摧毁，其他同样合理的实相视角便会向你的意识显现，与你的世界同样真实的世界便会涌入视野。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: For that matter, the body is naturally well-equipped to handle "projection of consciousness," or out-of-body travel, whatever you prefer to call it. Your biological makeup includes mechanisms that allow a certain portion of your consciousness to leave your body and return. These mechanisms are a part of the nature of animals as well. The body is equipped to perceive many other kinds of experiences that are not officially recognized as native to human experience. To one extent or another, then, you learn to constantly monitor your behavior, so that it conforms to the established criteria set up for sane or rational experience.
quotes.translation: 就这一点而言，身体天生就具备处理"意识投射"或出体旅行的能力，随你怎么称呼。你的生物构造包含某些机制，允许你的一部分意识离开身体再返回。这些机制也是动物天性的一部分。身体还具备感知许多其他体验的能力，这些体验并未被官方承认为人类经验的本能。因此，你在某种程度上学会不断监控自己的行为，使其符合为理智或理性经验所建立的既定标准。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche

quotes.text: It is equipped, as an animal is, to perform beautifully in its environment. You would call it mindless, since it would seem not to reason. For the purpose of this discussion alone, imagine a body with a fully operating body consciousness, not diseased for any reason or defective by birth, but one without the overriding ego-directed consciousness that you have. There have been species of such a nature. In your terms they would seem to be like sleepwalkers, yet their physical abilities surpassed yours. They were indeed as agile as animals -- nor were they unconscious.10 They simply dealt with a different kind of awareness.
quotes.translation: 它像动物一样，被设计成能在其环境中出色运作。你会称之为无意识，因为它似乎不会推理。仅就本次讨论而言，想象一个拥有完全运作的身体意识的身体——没有任何疾病或先天缺陷——但缺少你所拥有的那种主导性的自我导向意识。曾经存在过具有这种性质的物种。用你的说法，它们看起来像梦游者，然而它们的身体能力超越了你。它们确实像动物一样敏捷——而且它们并非无意识。它们只是处理着一种不同的觉知。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: The language or the method of communication can best be described perhaps as direct cognition. Direct cognition is dependent upon a lover's kind of identification, where what is known is known. At that stage no words or even images were needed. The wind outside and the breath were felt to be one and the same, so that the wind was the earth breathing out the breath that rose from the mouths of the living, spreading out through the earth's body. Part of a man went out with breath -- therefore, man's consciousness could go wherever the wind traveled. A man's consciousness, traveling with the wind, became part of all places.
quotes.translation: 这种语言或沟通方式或许最好被描述为直接认知。直接认知依赖于一种爱人般的认同，其中所知即被知。在那个阶段，甚至不需要语言或图像。外面的风和呼吸被感觉为一体，因此风就是大地呼出的气息，这气息从活物的口中升起，散布在大地的身体中。人的一部分随着呼吸外出——因此，人的意识可以随风所至。人的意识随风旅行，成为所有地方的一部分。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche

quotes.text: Applied to old age, the color black denotes a returning to those unconscious forces. Now all of this so far is from the standpoint of American and Western belief. It is simply the reality in which many of my readers are involved. In other "underground" systems of belief, however, black is seen as a symbol of great knowledge, power and strength. When this is carried to an extreme you wind up with devil cults, in which the poorly understood powers of creativity and exuberance rush out in distorted form; the undersides of consciousness are then glorified at the expense of the other, white, "conscious and objective" values.
quotes.translation: 应用于老年时，黑色象征着回归那些无意识的力量。到目前为止，这一切都是从美国与西方信仰的角度出发的。这仅仅是许多读者所涉入的现实。然而，在其他“地下”信仰体系中，黑色被视为伟大知识、力量与威能的象征。当这种观念走向极端时，便会导致恶魔崇拜，那些被误解的创造力与丰沛能量以扭曲的形式爆发出来；于是，意识的阴暗面被颂扬，而牺牲了另一面——白色、“有意识且客观”的价值。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality

quotes.text: In sleep your ordinary brain waves as you understand them register a chaotic jungle of experience not normally processed. Biologically or psychically, there is little need for such disorientation. The normal waking consciousness, with its characteristic patterns, can indeed follow [into sleep]. A mixture of brain waves would result. Consciousness as you think of it expands tremendously under such conditions. You would follow your own pattern of continuity and understanding, weaving this into the sleep and dream states, forming a "new" pattern that triumphantly combines all, as to some extent this occurs in our sessions.
quotes.translation: 在睡眠中，你所理解的普通脑波记录着通常未经处理的混乱经验丛林。从生物或心灵层面来看，这种迷失方向感几乎毫无必要。正常的清醒意识，带着其特有的模式，确实可以跟随进入睡眠。这会导致脑波的混合。你所认为的意识在此类条件下会大幅扩展。你将遵循自己的连续性与理解模式，将其编织进睡眠与梦境状态，形成一种“新”模式，胜利地结合了所有层面——某种程度上，这在我们课中已有所发生。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: The center of consciousness, that is the center of outward consciousness, the outer ego, is finally chosen by the inner ego after certain portions of the inner self show greater tendencies for objectification; these portions of course grouped around one of the subconscious subpersonalities which then wins out to become the outer ego, the manipulator for and the spokesman for the whole self.
quotes.translation: 意识的核心，即外在意识的核心、外在自我，最终由内在自我在内在自性的某些部分展现出更强的客体化倾向后所选择；这些部分自然围绕着某个潜意识次人格聚集，该次人格随后胜出，成为外在自我——即整个自性的操控者与代言人。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)

quotes.text: ("Right now I think I'm getting that everything on the face of the earth is related -- that your consciousness is in an ant, or a rock8 or a tree, but that we're not used to thinking that way. Not that one is superior to another -- just that we're all connected -- that there's some kind of weird familiarity, biologically and psychically, that we've never gotten consciously ... What I'm getting is that your father could do any of the things that you wrote about [in Note 4], without invading anything or anyone. It's just that our ideas of personhood and soul make it sound terrible, until you get used to those ideas....
quotes.translation: （"此刻我领悟到，地球上的一切都相互关联——你的意识存在于蚂蚁、岩石或树木之中，但我们不习惯这样思考。并非某个高于另一个——只是我们彼此相连——存在着某种生物学与心灵层面的奇异亲缘性，我们从未在意识层面察觉……我感受到的是，你父亲能做到你笔记4中描述的任何事，无需侵犯任何事物或任何人。只是我们对人格与灵魂的观念让这听起来可怕，直到你适应这些想法……"）
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: Now it can be used as the first of a series of steps, leading to "deeper" states of consciousness. It can also be used as the first of a series of adjacent steps. Each of the deeper layers of consciousness can also be used as first steps leading to other adjacent levels. A-l is simple to enter. When you listen to music that you like, when you are indulging in an enjoyable quiet pursuit, you can sense the different feeling. It may be accompanied by your own characteristic physical clues. You may tap your fingers in a certain way. There may be a particular gesture. You may stare or look dreamily to the left or right.
quotes.translation: 现在它可作为通往意识"更深"状态的一系列步骤中的第一步。它也可作为一系列相邻步骤中的第一步。意识的每个更深层次同样可作为通往其他相邻层次的第一步。A-1状态易于进入。当你聆听喜爱的音乐，或沉浸于愉悦的静心活动时，便能感知到这种不同感受。它可能伴随你特有的身体线索：你可能以特定方式轻叩手指，做出某个独特手势，或眼神迷离地凝视左方或右方。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: (Another one-minute pause.) Give us a moment ... The "unknown" reality sustains you and the web of life as you understand it. Your conscious concepts must enlarge so that the conscious self can understand its true nature. As you think of it, consciousness is barely -- barely -- half developed. It has learned to identify with one small group of neurologically accepted responses. Portions of the brain not used lie latent, waiting for the recognition that will trigger them into activity (intently). When this happens, the mind will become aware of the rich bed of probabilities upon which the ego now rides so blindly.
quotes.translation: （又停顿一分钟。）请稍等片刻……那个“未知”的现实支撑着你，也支撑着你所理解的生命之网。你的意识概念必须扩展，以便意识自我能够了解其真实本质。就你所认为的而言，意识几乎——几乎——只发展了一半。它学会了认同于一小群神经学上可接受的反应。大脑中未使用的部分处于潜伏状态，等待着能触发它们活动的认知（专注地）。当这种情况发生时，心智将意识到那丰富的可能性之床，而自我如今正盲目地骑乘其上。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1

quotes.text: These apparent inspirations from nowhere definitely come from somewhere, and this somewhere is that inner dreaming condition, which is a necessity on the part of every consciousness, human or otherwise. Here I am using the word consciousness to cover all organisms with awareness. This dream activity seems divorced from the conscious state itself, and came before the conscious state within your physical system. It cannot be turned off, and it will not cease. It will only be noticed, as a rule, when the aware portions of the personality are disconnected from their physical stimulations to some considerable degree.
quotes.translation: 这些看似凭空而来的灵感确实来自某处，而这个某处就是那种内在的做梦状态，这是每一个意识——无论是人类还是其他——所必需的。在此我使用“意识”一词来涵盖所有具有觉知力的有机体。这种做梦活动似乎与意识状态本身分离，并且在你们物理系统内的意识状态之前就已存在。它无法被关闭，也不会停止。通常，只有当人格中具有觉知的部分与它们的物理刺激相当程度地断开连接时，它才会被注意到。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)

quotes.text: In dreams, when you expect none, none appear. Out of body experiences indeed can be a health boon. Your system is automatically relaxed and free of pressure. Also, incidentally, free of time. It does not age during a projection; that is, the body does not age. It is in suspension. Any interpretations that are made are made by consciousness itself. The physical senses are not， underline not, utilized. At the moment of perception the traveling consciousness perceives through the inner senses, and by itself automatically makes the necessary adjustments so that the ego can perceive the data in its accustomed way.
quotes.translation: 在梦中，当你什么都不期待时，什么都不会出现。出体体验确实可以成为健康的恩赐。你的系统会自动放松，没有压力。顺便提一下，也没有时间。在投射期间它不会老化；也就是说，身体不会老化。它处于悬浮状态。任何诠释都是由意识本身做出的。物理感官并不——强调并不——被使用。在感知的那一刻，旅行中的意识通过内在感官进行感知，并自动做出必要的调整，以便自我能够以它习惯的方式接收数据。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)

quotes.text: "The insight that flashed into my consciousness was that human beings haven't changed either, really, that our more complicated mental processes only make it seem that we have. Coupled with this is the idea that magic, as we call it, reflects a basic part of our natural mental equipment and abilities, but that our present course of action, our focusing upon the material and the intellectual -- the 'reasonable' portions of our psyche -- has created artificial divisions, in which magic seems quite 'unreasonable' or unreal. Actually, our need for magic is a very real, vital, and integral portion of our psyches.
quotes.translation: 闪入我意识中的洞见是：人类其实也未曾改变，我们更复杂的心理过程只是让我们看似改变了。与此相伴的是，我们所谓的魔法反映了我们天生心智装备与能力的基本部分，但我们当前的行为方式——专注于物质与理智，即我们心灵中"合理"的部分——制造了人为的分割，使得魔法显得相当"不合理"或不真实。实际上，我们对魔法的需求是我们心灵中非常真实、充满活力且不可或缺的部分。
quotes.book: The Magical Approach

quotes.text: Nevertheless, the atoms and molecules within the nail do possess their own kind of consciousness. The atoms and molecules that make up the pages of this book are also, within their own level, aware. Nothing exists -- neither rock, mineral, plant, animal, or air -- that is not filled with consciousness of its own kind. So you stand amid a constant vital commotion, a gestalt of aware energy, and you are yourselves physically composed of conscious cells that carry within themselves the realization of their own identity, that cooperate willingly to form the corporeal structure that is your physical body.
quotes.translation: 然而，指甲内的原子和分子确实拥有它们自己的意识。构成这本书书页的原子和分子，在其自身层面上也是有觉知的。没有任何事物——无论是岩石、矿物、植物、动物还是空气——不充满其自身种类的意识。因此，你置身于持续的生命骚动之中，一个觉知能量的完形，而你们自身在肉体上由有意识的细胞组成，这些细胞内在携带着对自身身份的认识，并自愿合作以形成构成你们肉体的物质结构。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: Rest or sleep cures -- very extended sleep periods -- have been helpful for therapy in some cases, not because extended sleep is in itself beneficial, but because so many toxins had built up that such extended periods were required. Learning processes are definitely hampered through your present habits, for there are certain periods when consciousness is attuned to learning, and yet you try to force learning during unrecognized minimal periods. Creative and psychic abilities are thrust into the background simply because of this artificial division. Dualities result that affect all of your activities.
quotes.translation: 休息或睡眠疗法——非常延长的睡眠期——在某些案例中对治疗有帮助，并非因为延长睡眠本身有益，而是因为积累了太多毒素，需要如此长的睡眠期。通过你们目前的习惯，学习过程确实受到阻碍，因为存在某些意识与学习调谐的时期，而你们却试图在未被识别的最低时期强行学习。创造力和灵性能力被推到背景中，仅仅因为这种人为的划分。由此产生的二元性影响了你们所有的活动。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: Since they do, of course, you can discover them now and learn to use them. This will directly (dreactly) assist you in after-death (agter) experience. You will not be nearly so startled by the nature of your own reactions (reactone) if you understand beforehand for example that your consciousness not only is not imprisoned by your physical body, but (bud) can create other portions at will. Those who "overidentify" their consciousness with their body can suffer self-created torment (tortment) for no reason, lingering about the body. Indeed, quite the forlorn soul, thinking it has no other place to go.
quotes.translation: 既然它们确实如此，你们现在就可以发现它们并学会使用它们。这将直接帮助你们在死后的体验。如果你们事先了解，例如，你们的意识不仅不被肉体囚禁，而且可以随意创造其他部分，那么你们就不会对自己反应的性质感到如此震惊。那些将意识与身体“过度认同”的人，会无缘无故地遭受自我制造的折磨，在身体周围徘徊。确实，相当孤独的灵魂，认为自己无处可去。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: (10:22.) You are examining probable atoms. You are composed of probable atoms. (A one-minute pause.) Give us a moment ... (A one-minute pause.) Consciousness, to be fully free, had to be endowed with unpredictability. All That Is had to surprise himself, itself, herself, constantly, through freely granting itself its own freedom, or forever repeat itself. This basic unpredictability then follows through on all levels of consciousness and being. A certain cellular structure may seem inevitable within its own frame of reference only because opposing or contradictory probabilities do not appear therein.
quotes.translation: (10:22.) 你们正在审视可能的原子。你们由可能的原子构成。（停顿一分钟。）请稍等片刻……（停顿一分钟。）意识若要完全自由，就必须被赋予不可预测性。一切万有必须不断让自己惊讶——无论以男性、中性还是女性形态——通过自由地赋予自身自由，否则便会永远重复自身。这种基本的不可预测性随后贯穿所有层次的意识与存在。某个细胞结构在其自身参照系内看似必然，仅仅是因为对立或矛盾的或然性并未在其中显现。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1

quotes.text: I understand that this is somewhat difficult material to comprehend. However (pause), in its purest form a unit of consciousness can be in all places at the same time (forcefully). It becomes beside the point, then, to say that when it operates as a wave a unit of consciousness is precognitive, or clairvoyant, since it has the capacity to be in all places and all times simultaneously.
quotes.translation: 我明白这些材料有些难以理解。然而（停顿），纯粹形态的意识单位可以同时存在于所有地方（强调）。因此，说意识单位以波的形式运作时具有预知或遥视能力便显得无关紧要，因为它本就能同时存在于所有时空。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1

quotes.text: Consciousness is filled with content--any kind of consciousness. [The farmer's] animals understand that in a certain fashion he is a midwife, responsible for some of their births. Food comes from his hands. The animals understand, on their own, that life on any terms that are physical ends with death--that the physical properties must be returned to the earth from which they came....
quotes.translation: 意识充满内容——任何类型的意识皆然。[农夫的]动物们明白，在某种意义上他是助产士，负责它们部分个体的诞生。食物来自他的双手。动物们凭自身理解，任何以物质形式存在的生命终将死亡——物质属性必须回归它们所来自的大地……
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1

quotes.text: (9:31.) Give us a moment ... As you lay there you were aware of the fact just beneath consciousness -- usual consciousness -- that you had not brought in the paper before your nap, as is your habit, and almost at a dream level you idly wondered what stories it might contain. Your inclusion of the hospital mixup in the tale was, as, you suspected, connected with the medical ideas you have been dealing with of late (in extra notes for Mass Events, and the book by the physician) -- and here was an excellent fictional idea, you see, that could, among other things, bring those ideas into prominence.
quotes.translation: （9点31分。）请稍等片刻……当你躺下时，你隐约意识到在意识——通常意识——之下的事实：你没有像往常一样在午睡前把报纸拿进来，几乎在梦境层面，你漫不经心地想着报纸上可能有什么故事。你把医院混乱的情节融入故事中，正如你所怀疑的，这与最近你一直在处理的医学观念有关（在《群体事件》的补充笔记和那位医生写的书中）——你看，这是一个绝妙的小说构思，除了其他方面，还能让这些观念突显出来。
quotes.book: The Magical Approach

quotes.text: Consciousness cannot be separated from mobility. It is true that you focus within the physical system mainly, yet the inner self is highly mobile and embarks simultaneously upon a variety of projections. The closest example in your experience of this mobility is the dream situation. These dreams make little impression upon the waking consciousness unless you train it and take it with you as far as it can go. It is then able to translate the dream reality at least in part. In the psychedelic experience the ego can be trained to act in the same manner. Later it can serve as an interpreter.
quotes.translation: 意识无法与流动性分离。诚然，你们主要聚焦于物质系统之内，但内在自体具有高度流动性，并同时展开多种投射。在你们的经验中，这种流动性的最接近例子是梦境状态。除非你训练清醒意识并尽可能带它同行，否则这些梦境对清醒意识影响甚微。届时它至少能部分翻译梦境实相。在迷幻体验中，自我可被训练以相同方式运作。之后它能充当诠释者。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)

quotes.text: Cellular consciousness is a part of the consciousness of the inner ego or inner self. Very loosely speaking, cellular consciousness is to the inner self what the subconscious is to the outer ego. There are of course many differences however.
quotes.translation: 细胞意识是内在自我或内在自体意识的一部分。粗略来说，细胞意识之于内在自体，犹如潜意识之于外在自我。当然，两者存在诸多差异。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)

quotes.text: Now those beliefs separate man from his own nature.1 He cannot trust himself -- for who can rely upon the accidental bubblings of hormones and chemicals that somehow form a stew called consciousness (louder and quite ironic) -- an unsavory brew at best, so the field of science will forever escape opening up into any great vision of the meaning of life. (Long pause.) It cannot value life, and so in its search for the ideal it can indeed justify in its philosophy the possibility of an accident that might kill many many people through direct or indirect means, and kill the unborn as well.2
quotes.translation: 那些信念使人与其自身本质分离。他无法信任自己——因为谁能信赖那些偶然冒泡的荷尔蒙与化学物质，它们莫名其妙地熬成一锅名为意识（更大声且相当讽刺）的杂烩——充其量不过是令人作呕的混合物，因此科学领域将永远无法开启任何关于生命意义的伟大视野。（长停顿。）它无法珍视生命，因此在追求理想的过程中，其哲学确实能为一种可能性辩护：一场意外可能通过直接或间接手段杀死许多人，甚至杀死未出生的胎儿。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: You do exist therefore in both of these dimensions. The ego cannot participate directly in such experience. There is a compliance on the part of the ego, however, that allows it to step aside so that it does not block inner awareness of other-dimensional existence. It is difficult, but not impossible, for the ego to correlate the information gained. Dreams involve as you know several dimensions. The dream state itself is a very loose term, for there are several layers of consciousness within it, and there is no limit to the states of consciousness that can be achieved, ideally speaking.
quotes.translation: 因此你确实存在于这两个维度中。自我无法直接参与这类体验。然而，自我存在某种顺从，允许它退居一旁，以免阻碍对其它维度存在的内在觉知。自我要关联所获信息虽困难，但并非不可能。如你所知，梦境涉及多个维度。梦状态本身是个非常宽泛的术语，因为其中有数层意识，而理想而言，可达到的意识状态并无限制。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 6 of The Seth Material (Sessions 240-280)

quotes.text: In larger terms, the word reality and those characteristics that I will give you as attributed to it, will be seen as part and parcel of a unified psychic consciousness, from which all other consciousness emerges. These emerging consciousness form new moment points, create new value fulfillment experiences that constantly allow the overall reality to be created even as it creates.
quotes.translation: 从更广的角度看，“实相”一词及其我将赋予它的特性，将被视为统一心灵意识的一部分，所有其他意识皆由此而生。这些涌现的意识形成新的“当下点”，创造新的“价值完成”体验，不断让整体实相在创造的同时被创造出来。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)

quotes.text: Again, it is important that you realize the initial nonphysical reaction to stimuli that sparks off all physical reactions. There is constant interplay and communication between the areas of consciousness that are connected to the brain and those that are not. The "deeper" purposes of the consciousness involved "circulate," sometimes arising in the awareness that is joined with the brain. Information coming from those deeper sources of the self, reaching the areas connected to the brain, will be interpreted according to the beliefs of that most physically focused segment of the self.
quotes.translation: 再者，重要的是要认识到，引发所有物理反应的最初非物理性刺激。与大脑相连的意识领域和未相连的意识领域之间存在着持续的相互作用与交流。所涉及意识的"更深层"目的会"循环"，有时会出现在与大脑相连的觉知中。来自自我更深层源头的讯息，在抵达与大脑相连的领域时，将根据自我中最聚焦于物质的那部分信念被诠释。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality

quotes.text: Because you dwell in time, however, the god image will also reflect the state of your consciousness as it "is," as well as point toward the future state desired. The god concept will operate as a psychic and spiritual blueprint just like the architect's plan, only at a different level. Each species has within it such blueprints to varying degrees, and these are important, for they carry within them the idealized probabilities. They are valid, again, psychically and biologically. They will serve as biological patterns to the cells, as well as psychic stimuli in terms of consciousness.
quotes.translation: 然而，由于你居于时间之中，神祇形象不仅会指向未来渴望的状态，也会反映你意识"当下"的状态。神祇概念将像建筑师的蓝图一样运作，成为心灵与灵性的蓝图，只是层次不同。每个物种内在都不同程度地拥有此类蓝图，这些蓝图至关重要，因为它们承载着理想化的可能性。同样，它们在心灵与生物层面都是有效的。它们将作为细胞的生物模式，以及意识层面的心灵刺激。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1

quotes.text: These gaps exist quite literally in many systems. You encounter them on many levels. You may find yourself experiencing a state of consciousness, for example, in which nothing seems to happen, and no psychological landscape or recognizable symbols occur. These exist not only psychologically or psychically, but as blank areas in terms of space. The spaces may be filled finally with new symbols. If you are perceptive enough, you can sometimes catch yourselves encountering such states of reality in which nothing appears and no signs of any consciousness outside of your own is apparent.
quotes.translation: 这些空隙在许多系统中确实存在。你在多个层面都会遇到它们。例如，你可能会发现自己经历一种意识状态，其中似乎没有任何事情发生，也没有出现任何心理景观或可辨识的符号。这些空隙不仅存在于心理或心灵层面，也存在于空间中的空白区域。这些空间最终可能被新符号填满。如果你足够敏锐，有时能捕捉到自己正遭遇这样的实相状态——其中空无一物，且除了自身意识外，没有任何其他意识的迹象显现。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: Now. The drug allows for regulated periods of highly intensified consciousness, operating at peak levels, with all of the mental faculties accelerated. Between these periods however there are periods of unconsciousness. These are of a protective nature. During the periods of unconsciousness the drugs injected into the brain give increased nourishment to those areas of the physical brain that are involved in such ejections of consciousness. Therefore even though your probable self is within reaching distance, so to speak, he is sometimes involved in such blackout-nourishment periods.
quotes.translation: 现在。这种药物允许在高度强化的意识状态下进行有规律的运作，以巅峰水平运行，所有心智能力都加速运转。然而在这些时段之间，会出现无意识状态。这些状态具有保护性质。在无意识期间，注入大脑的药物会为参与此类意识投射的物理大脑区域提供更多滋养。因此，即使你的可能自我近在咫尺，可以说，他有时也会陷入这种意识中断的滋养期。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)

quotes.text: The concept of nirvana (see the 637th session in Chapter Nine) and the idea of heaven are two versions of the same picture, the former being one in which individuality is lost in the bliss of undifferentiated consciousness, and the latter one in which still-conscious individuals perform mindless adoration. Neither theory contains an understanding of the functions of the conscious mind, or the evolution of consciousness -- or, for that matter, certain aspects of greater physics. No energy is ever lost. The expanding universe theory* applies to the mind as well as to the universe.
quotes.translation: 涅槃的概念（见第九章第637节）与天堂的观念是同一画面的两个版本：前者是在无分别意识的极乐中丧失个体性，后者则是仍有意识的个体进行无心的崇拜。这两种理论都未能理解有意识心智的功能、意识的演化——或者就此而言，未能理解更宏大物理学的某些面向。能量永不消失。宇宙膨胀理论*既适用于宇宙，也适用于心智。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality

quotes.text: Christian theology sees the end of the world in certain terms, with a grand God coming to reward the good and to punish the wicked.6 That system of belief allows for no other probability. Some see the end of the world coming as a greater disaster, or envision man finally ruining his planet. Others see periods of peace and advance -- and each probability will happen "somewhere." However, many of my readers, or their offspring, will be involved in a new dimension of selfhood in which consciousness is fully explored and the potentials of the soul uncovered, at least to some extent.
quotes.translation: 基督教神学以特定方式描绘世界末日，一位伟大的上帝降临来奖赏善者、惩罚恶者。6 这种信仰体系不允许其他可能性存在。有些人认为世界末日将以更大的灾难降临，或设想人类最终毁灭自己的星球。另一些人则看到和平与进步的时期——而每种可能性都将在"某处"发生。然而，我的许多读者或其子孙后代，将参与一种全新的自我维度，在其中意识得到充分探索，灵魂的潜能至少在一定程度上被揭示。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: All directions taken by the flower of consciousness are good. The flower knows it is alive in the bulb, but it takes "time" for the bulb to let the stem and leaves and flower emerge. The flower is not better than the bulb. It is not even more progressed than the bulb. It is the bulb in one of its manifestations. So in your terms, it may seem as if there are progressions, or consecutive steps of development, in which more mature comprehensive selves will emerge. You are a part of those selves now, as the petals are of the bulb. Only in your system is that time period meaningful.
quotes.translation: 意识之花所采取的所有方向都是好的。花知道自己在球茎中活着，但球茎需要"时间"才能让茎、叶和花朵显现出来。花并不比球茎更好，甚至不比球茎更进步。它是球茎的一种显现形式。因此，用你们的说法，似乎存在着进展或连续的发展阶段，在其中更成熟、更全面的自我将会出现。你们现在就是那些自我的一部分，就像花瓣是球茎的一部分一样。只有在你们的系统中，那个时间段才有意义。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1

quotes.text: The cells are not inferior as far as you are concerned, even though they form part of the structure of your physical being. They are not even less conscious. (Emphatically:) They are conscious in a different fashion. There is no need to "romanticize" them, or to think of them as little people, but each of them possesses a highly focused consciousness, and a consciousness of self. You like to think -- again -- that only your own species possesses an awareness of its own selfhood. There are different kinds of selfhood, and an infinite variety of ways to experience self-awareness.
quotes.translation: 就你们而言，细胞并不低等，即使它们构成了你们物质身体结构的一部分。它们甚至不是意识较少的。（强调地：）它们以不同的方式具有意识。没有必要"浪漫化"它们，或者把它们想象成小人，但每一个细胞都拥有高度集中的意识，以及自我意识。你们喜欢再次认为——只有你们自己的物种才拥有对自身自我性的觉知。存在着不同种类的自我性，以及体验自我意识的无限多样的方式。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: In this manner of speaking, your thoughts then follow laws. Their behavior follows laws, and their activities, that you do not understand, though you call your thoughts your own. They are then manipulated, independently of you, by other kinds of consciousness as ever-changing natural phenomena. The native consciousness within such systems is not aware of the origin of this phenomena, nor of your own reality. They take the evidence that appears to their senses as reality, as most of you do. It would not occur to them that this phenomenon originated outside of their own system.
quotes.translation: 用这种说法，你们的思想随后遵循法则。它们的行为遵循法则，它们的活动也遵循法则，而你们并不理解这些法则，尽管你们称思想为你们自己的。它们随后被其他种类的意识独立于你们之外操纵，如同不断变化的自然现象。这类系统内的本然意识并不知晓这些现象的起源，也不知晓你们自己的实相。它们将感官所呈现的证据视为实相，就像你们大多数人一样。它们不会想到这种现象起源于它们自己的系统之外。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: Consciousness actually creates these perspectives, in which it can then manifest itself in form. Understand here that I speak in terms of psychological form and structure. In other words, individual consciousness operates in many different perspectives, in many various kinds of forms. In some stages it is unaware of the divisions or various manifestations of itself, due to the amount of focusing that is necessary for such form- [hyphen] projection.
quotes.translation: 意识实际上创造了这些视角，在其中它能够以形态展现自身。请理解，我在此讨论的是心理形态与结构。换言之，个体意识以多种不同视角运作，呈现为各种不同形态。在某些阶段，由于此类形态投射所需的专注程度，意识并未觉察到自身的分化或不同展现。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)

quotes.text: There are systems in which a moment,5 from your standpoint, is made to endure for the life of a universe. I do not mean that a moment is simply stretched, or that time is slowed down alone, but that all the experiences possible within a moment become realities within that framework. Such systems have little to do with you in any practical manner, nor is such information given to dwarf your idea of what your own consciousness is. It is important, however, that you realize the fact that there is more creativity and variety in an inner reality than you ever physically perceive.
quotes.translation: 存在某些系统，其中从你们的角度来看的一瞬间，被延续为整个宇宙的寿命。我并非指时间被简单拉长，或仅被减缓，而是指一瞬间内所有可能的经验都在该架构中成为实相。这类系统与你们在实用层面上关联甚少，提供这些信息也并非为了贬低你们对自身意识的认知。然而，重要的是你们必须认识到：内在实相中的创造力与多样性远超你们在物质层面所能感知的。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1

quotes.text: Again, your idea of personhood limits you when you think of these concepts. You imagine personhood to be a kind of mental particle that must have definite boundaries, or it will lose its identity. The identity of even the smallest consciousness is always maintained -- but not limited. If you can think of your present idea of identity as if it were but one shape or one motion of a moving particle, a shape or a motion that never loses its imprint or meaning, then you could also see how you could follow it forward or backward to the shape or motion taken "before or afterward."
quotes.translation: 再次强调，当你们思考这些概念时，你们对人格的认知会限制你们。你们将人格想象为一种必须具有明确边界的精神粒子，否则便会丧失其身份。即便最微小的意识，其身份也始终被维持——但不受限制。如果你们能将当前对身份的认知，视为如同运动粒子的一种形态或一次运动——这种形态或运动永不失去其印记或意义——那么你们也能明白，如何向前或向后追溯至"之前或之后"所呈现的形态或运动。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: Again, this is no reason to feel that the individual is nothing, simply because he is one of so many. Action develops according to value fulfillment, and value fulfillment has little to do with size or numbers; and action turns to consciousness. Action working on itself becomes consciousness, and in speaking of consciousness I do not necessarily mean what you mean by the word.
quotes.translation: 再次强调，这并非意味着个体毫无价值，仅仅因为他是众多存在之一。行动依据价值完成而发展，而价值完成与规模或数量几乎无关；行动转向意识。行动作用于自身便成为意识，而当我谈及意识时，并不必然指涉你们所理解的那个词义。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)

quotes.text: Using such significances as yardsticks, you accept or reject probable events. You imprint the universe with your own significance, and using that as a focus you draw from it, or attract, those events that fit your unique purposes and needs. In doing so, to some extent you multiply the creative possibilities of the universe, forming from it a personal reality that would otherwise be absent, in those terms; and in so doing you also add in an immeasurable fashion to the reality of all other consciousness by increasing the bank of reality from which all consciousness draws.
quotes.translation: 你们以这类意义为标尺，接受或拒绝可能事件。你们将自己的意义铭刻于宇宙，并以此为焦点，从中汲取或吸引那些契合你们独特目的与需求的事件。在此过程中，你们在某种程度上倍增了宇宙的创造可能性，从中构建出若非如此便不会存在的个人实相——就那些层面而言；同时，你们也以无可估量的方式，通过增加所有意识汲取的实相库藏，丰富了其他一切意识的实相。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche

quotes.text: Autohypnosis then will be utilized in an effort to discover what experiences are common in various levels or trance states, and these shall be examined in a search for primary and secondary conditions. These experiments however are for the future. Some of the data I will give you as a guide, but much you shall do on your own. There is no way to probe the realities of consciousness except that a personality travels through all levels of consciousness open to him, and do so in such a way that he can retain and apply the information that he receives in these inner travels.
quotes.translation: 随后将运用自我催眠法，以探索不同层次或出神状态中的共通经验，并检视这些经验以寻找主要与次要条件。然而这些实验属于未来之事。我会提供部分数据作为指引，但大部分仍需你们自行完成。除非人格遍历其可触及的所有意识层次，并能以保留并应用这些内在旅程中所得信息的方式完成探索，否则便无途径探究意识的实相。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 5 of The Seth Material (Sessions 199-239)

quotes.text: Man's consciousness, and to some extent that of the animals, is more specifically identified with form, however. In order to develop his own kind of individualized awareness, man had to consciously ignore for a while his own place within the structure of the earth. His experience of time would seem to be the experience of his identity. His consciousness would not seem to flow into his body before birth, and out of it after death. He would "forget" there was a time to die. He would forget that death meant new life. A natural message had to replace the old knowledge.
quotes.translation: 然而，人类的意识——某种程度上也包括动物的意识——与形态的关联更为具体。为了发展自身特有的个体化觉知，人类必须暂时有意识地忽略自己在地球结构中的位置。他对时间的体验似乎等同于对自我身份的体验。他的意识似乎不会在出生前流入身体，也不会在死亡后流出身体。他会"忘记"存在死亡时刻，忘记死亡意味着新生。一种自然的信息必须取代旧有的知识。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1

quotes.text: I am not speaking of you personally, Joseph, so much as I am emphasizing that the race at present identifies its individual being with highly limited concepts of the self. Those ideas are vigorously protected, and indeed must be understood and given honor even while attempts are made to expand them. Period. Certainly the quality of consciousness has changed through the centuries in many different ways, and sometimes in what would appear to be contradictory ones; but in your present you have nothing against which to compare your current consciousness of experience.
quotes.translation: 约瑟，我并非特指你个人，而是强调人类种族目前将其个体存在与高度受限的自我概念等同。这些观念受到激烈保护，即便在试图扩展它们时，也必须被理解并予以尊重。句号。数个世纪以来，意识品质确实以多种不同方式发生变化，有时甚至呈现矛盾的表象；但在你们当前的时点，你们没有任何参照物可以对比当前的意识体验。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1

quotes.text: For one thing, you are dealing with different kinds of consciousness than your own. They are focused consciousnesses, surely, each one feeling itself at life's center. While this is the case, however, these other forms of consciousness also identify then with the source of nature from which they emerge. In a way impossible to explain, the fly and the spider are connected, and aware of the connection. Not as hunter and prey, but as individual participants in deeper processes. Together they work toward a joint kind of value fulfillment, in which both are fulfilled.
quotes.translation: 首先，你面对的是与自身不同种类的意识。它们无疑是聚焦的意识，每个都感觉自己是生命中心。然而即便如此，这些其他形式的意识也会认同于它们所源自的自然本源。以一种难以解释的方式，苍蝇与蜘蛛彼此相连，并意识到这种关联——并非作为猎食者与猎物，而是作为更深层进程中的个体参与者。它们共同致力于一种联合的价值完成，使双方都得以圆满。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: I am speaking now of the consciousness within each physical particle regardless of its size; of molecular consciousness, cellular consciousness, as well as the larger gestalts of consciousness with which you are usually more familiar.
quotes.translation: 我现在谈论的是每个物理粒子内部的意识，无论其大小；包括分子意识、细胞意识，以及你通常更熟悉的那些较大的意识完形。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)

quotes.text: Now in moments of solitude you may become aware of some of these other streams of consciousness. You may at times for example, hear words, or see images that appear out of context with your own thoughts. According to your education, beliefs, and background you may interpret these in any number of ways. For that matter, they may originate from several sources. On many occasions, however, you have inadvertently tuned in on one of your other streams of consciousness, opened momentarily a channel to those other levels of reality in which other portions of you dwell.
quotes.translation: 在独处的时刻，你或许能觉察到这些其他意识流。例如，有时你会听到话语或看到图像，这些内容似乎与你自己的思绪无关。根据你的教育、信念和背景，你可能会以多种方式解读它们。就此而言，它们可能源自多个源头。然而在许多情况下，你只是无意中调频到你其他的意识流之一，短暂地打开了通往那些你其他部分所栖居的实相层次的通道。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: There is an inner knack, allowing for greater sensitivity to the feelings of others than you presently acknowledge. That knack will be activated. Again, the powers of the brain come from the mind, so while you learn to center your consciousness in your body -- and necessarily so -- nevertheless your inner perceptions roam a far greater range. Before sleep, then, imagine your consciousness traveling down a road, or across the world -- whatever you want. Forget your body. Do not try to leave it for this exercise. Tell yourself that you are imaginatively traveling.
quotes.translation: 存在一种内在诀窍，能让你对他人感受的敏感度远超当前认知。这个诀窍将被激活。再次强调，大脑的力量源自心智，因此当你学习将意识集中于身体——这确属必要——时，你的内在感知仍会驰骋于更广阔的领域。因此入睡前，不妨想象你的意识正沿着某条道路旅行，或穿越世界——随你所愿。忘却你的身体。此练习中切勿试图脱离躯体。告诉自己你正在想象中旅行。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche

quotes.text: I also believe, however, that generally speaking science still views our genetic systems in mechanical, deterministic, and reductionistic terms, and will continue to do so for a long time: So that evidence is being accumulated to support that overall view that at this time science has no need to seek for other, larger, and more unsettling frames of reference encompassing consciousness, intent, and genetics. Indeed, I seldom see consciousness mentioned in connection with genetics, except as its quality may relate to genetic "defects" like mental retardation, say.
quotes.translation: 然而我也相信，总体而言科学界仍以机械、决定论和还原论的视角看待基因系统，且这种状况将持续良久：因此积累的证据正在支持这种整体观点，使得科学目前无需寻求其他更宏大、更令人不安的参照系——这些参照系本应涵盖意识、意图与遗传学。事实上，我极少看到意识与遗传学被相提并论，除非其特质可能与智力障碍等"基因缺陷"有关。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2

quotes.text: Now: These stages of consciousness are all a part of your own reality. A knowledge of them can be most useful. You can learn to "shift gears," stand aside from your own experience, and examine it with much better perspective. You can prepare questions or problems, suggesting that they be solved for you in the sleep state. You can suggest that you will speak with distant friends, or convey important messages that you cannot convey verbally, perhaps. You can bring about reconciliations, for example, at another layer of reality though you cannot do so in this one.
quotes.translation: 听好：这些意识阶段皆是你自身实相的一部分。了解它们将大有裨益。你可以学会"切换档位"，抽离自身经验，以更佳视角审视之。你可以预设问题或难题，暗示它们在睡眠状态中为你解决。你可以暗示自己将与远方的朋友交谈，或传递某些无法用言语传达的重要讯息。举例而言，你可以在另一层实相中达成和解，即便在此层实相中无法做到。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: Before, the environment was effortlessly created and perceived by man and all other living things, knowing the nature of their inner unity. In order to begin this new venture, it was necessary to pretend that this inner unity did not exist. Otherwise the new kind of consciousness would always run back to its home for security and comfort. So it seemed that all bridges must be cut, while of course it was only a game because the inner reality always remained. The new kind of consciousness simply had to look away from it to maintain initially an independent focus.
quotes.translation: 在此之前，环境由人类及所有其他生物毫不费力地创造与感知，他们深知自身内在统一的本性。为了开启这场新的冒险，必须假装这种内在统一并不存在。否则，这种新型意识总会逃回它的家园寻求安全与慰藉。因此，所有桥梁似乎都必须被切断——当然这不过是一场游戏，因为内在实相始终存在。新型意识只需暂时移开目光，才能维持最初独立的焦点。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: Let me quickly recap a few more facts about the production of this work. Seth himself always referred to "Unknown" Reality as one unit until we reached the last session. He divided the manuscript into six sections of varying lengths. There are no chapters per se. As Seth explained in the 743rd session: "This book had no chapters [in order] to further disrupt your accepted notions of what a book should be. There are different kinds of organization present, however, and in any given section of the book, several levels of consciousness are appealed to at once."
quotes.translation: 让我快速补充关于这部作品创作的几个要点。赛斯本人始终将《未知的实相》视为一个整体单元，直到我们进行最后一节课。他将手稿分为六个长度不等的部分，没有传统意义上的章节。正如赛斯在第743节课中解释的："这本书没有章节（是为了）进一步打破你们对书籍应有形态的既定认知。然而书中存在不同形式的组织架构，在任何特定章节中，都会同时唤起多个意识层面的共鸣。"
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: As you understand the nature of natural hypnosis, you will no longer feel the need to generate new negative feelings. Your load of inhibitions will recede. As you trust yourself more you will naturally express your feelings, and their suppression will not bring about explosive reactions any more. They will come and go. The channel to power will be opened more clearly. Attention to your own stream of consciousness is highly important. This alone will help you see in what areas you are denying impulses or giving yourself directions that lead to powerlessness.
quotes.translation: 当你理解自然催眠的本质后，将不再需要制造新的负面情绪。你的压抑负担会逐渐消退。随着你更信任自己，自然会表达感受，而压抑不再引发爆炸性反应——它们会自然来去。通往力量的通道将更清晰地敞开。关注自己的意识流至关重要，仅此一点就能帮助你发现：在哪些领域你正在否定冲动，或正在给自己下达导致无力感的指令。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality

quotes.text: In your terms, the inner world does represent Idea Potential as yet unrealized -- but those ideas and those potentials do not exist outside of consciousness. They are ideals set in the heart of man,5 yet in other terms he is the one who also put them there, out of the deeper knowledge of his being that straddles physical time. Existence is wise and compassionate, so in certain terms consciousness, knowing itself as man, sent future extensions of itself out into the time scheme that man would know, and lovingly planted signposts for itself to follow "later."
quotes.translation: 用你们的话来说，内在世界确实代表了尚未实现的理念潜能——但这些理念和潜能并非存在于意识之外。它们是深植于人类心中的理想，然而从另一种角度看，正是人类自己将这些理想置于心中，源于其跨越物理时间的存有之更深层知识。存在是智慧而慈悲的，因此在某些层面上，意识——以人类身份认知自身——将自身的未来延伸投射到人类将知晓的时间架构中，并满怀爱意地为自己留下路标，以便“日后”循迹而行。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1

quotes.text: (With amusement:) The wrong kinds of questions are the right ones for you, however, in your civilization and with your beliefs, because you want to stay within that structure to that extent. Only now are you beginning to question your methods, and even your questions.5 The true physicist would be able to ask his questions from his usual state of consciousness, and then turn that consciousness in other directions where he himself would be led into adventures-with-reality, in which the questions would themselves be changed. And then the answers would be felt.
quotes.translation: （带着笑意：）然而，在你们的文明和信仰体系中，错误的问题对你们而言恰恰是正确的，因为你们希望在一定程度上固守那个结构。直到现在，你们才开始质疑自己的方法，甚至质疑你们提出的问题。真正的物理学家能够从自己惯常的意识状态中提出问题，然后将那意识转向其他方向，在那里他会被引领进入与现实的冒险之旅，而问题本身也会随之改变。届时，答案将被感知。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1

quotes.text: Science has seen man as an accidental product of an uncaring universe, a creature literally without a center of meaning, where consciousness was the result of a physical mechanism that only happened to come into existence, and that had no reality outside of that structure. Science has at least been consistent in that respect. Christianity, however, officially asks children of sorrow to be joyful and sinners to find a childlike purity; it asks them to love a God who one day will destroy the world, and who will condemn them to hell if they do not adore him.
quotes.translation: 科学将人类视为冷漠宇宙的偶然产物，一个本质上缺乏意义核心的生物，意识只是某种物理机制的结果，这种机制偶然产生，且在该结构之外毫无实相。科学至少在这方面保持了一致性。然而，基督教却正式要求悲伤之子保持喜乐，罪人寻回孩童般的纯净；它要求他们爱一位终将毁灭世界的神，若不爱他，便会被判入地狱。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: The symbols can come together or fly apart, can be perceived separately or as a unity. As each event has its own symbol for you, so you have your characteristic way of combining these. These symbols can be translated and perceived in many terms; as a series of notes for example, as a combination of senses, as a series of images. At various stages of consciousness you will perceive the symbols in different terms. The multidimensional symbol in its entirety, then, has a reality in other states of consciousness, but also at other levels of reality entirely.
quotes.translation: 象征可以聚合或离散，可被分别感知或视为一体。正如每个事件对你而言都有其独特象征，你也有自己组合这些象征的独特方式。这些象征能以多种术语被翻译和感知——例如作为一连串音符、感官组合或影像序列。在不同意识阶段，你会以不同方式感知这些象征。因此，多维象征的整体在其它意识状态中具有实相，同时也完全存在于其它实相层面。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: Though the past is actually quite as immediate, alive, and creative as the present is, man made certain adjustments, on several layers, that would focus definite distinctions and set past and present experience apart. While your particular kind of consciousness was developing, it began to intensify selectivity, to concentrate specifically in a small area of activity while blocking out other data. This was necessary because the particular kind of physical manipulation of corporal existence required instant physical response to immediately present stimuli.
quotes.translation: 尽管过去实际上与当下同样直接、鲜活且充满创造力，人类却在多个层面上做出了特定调整，以强化明确区分，将过去与当下的经验分隔开来。当你们特定类型的意识发展时，它开始强化选择性，专注于狭窄的活动领域，同时屏蔽其他信息。这是必要的，因为肉体存在的特定物理操作要求对即时刺激做出即时物理反应。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1

quotes.text: In the beginning, man's dreams were in certain terms of immediate physical survival. They gave man information--a kind that of necessity the new physical senses could not contain. Those senses could only perceive the immediate environment, but man's dreams compensated for that lack, and filled out his consciousness by giving it the benefit of that larger generalized information to which it had once had an easy access. When he was asleep man could take advantage of the information banks contained in the units of consciousness that composed his very flesh.
quotes.translation: 起初，人类的梦境直接关乎肉体的即时生存。它们传递信息——这类信息是全新的物质感官所无法容纳的。那些感官只能感知直接环境，但梦境弥补了这一缺失，通过赋予意识曾经轻易获取的、更广泛的概括性信息，充实了它的认知。当人类入睡时，便能利用构成其血肉之躯的意识单位所蕴含的信息库。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1

quotes.text: Now: Your consciousness leaves the physical organism in various ways, according to the conditions. In some cases the organism itself is still able to function to some degree, although without the leadership or organization that existed previously. The simple consciousness of atoms, cells, and organs continues to exist, after the main consciousness has left, for some time.
quotes.translation: 现在：根据条件不同，你的意识会以多种方式离开肉体组织。在某些情况下，肉体本身仍能维持一定程度的运作，尽管失去了先前存在的领导或组织。当主要意识离开后，原子、细胞和器官的简单意识仍会存续一段时间。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: On the other hand there are those who stress the great value of the inner self, the emotional being, at the expense of the conscious mind. These theories hold that the intellect and usual consciousness are far inferior to the inner "unconscious" portions of being, and that all the answers are hidden from view. (Pause.) The followers of this belief consider the conscious mind in such derogatory terms that it almost seems to be a supercilious cancer that sprouted like a growth upon man's psyche-impeding rather than aiding his progress and understanding.
quotes.translation: 另一方面，有些人则强调内在自我与情感存有的巨大价值，却牺牲了意识心。这些理论认为，智性与通常的意识远逊于存有内在的“无意识”部分，而所有答案都隐藏于视野之外。（停顿。）这种信念的追随者以如此贬抑的措辞看待意识心，以至于它几乎像是寄生在人类心灵上的一种傲慢的癌瘤——阻碍而非促进人类的进步与理解。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality

quotes.text: You must again realize that we speak of the self as being so divided only for simplicity's sake. While the self is whole, it is however compartmentalized for efficiency's sake, but beneath consciousness the doors are open. Again, the conscious self is most necessary. However it cannot be stressed too strongly that consciousness is merely a state of focus, and not a self.
quotes.translation: 你必须再次认识到，我们之所以将自我描述为如此分裂，仅仅是为了简化。虽然自我是完整的，但为了效率起见，它被划分成不同区块；然而在意识之下，这些门是敞开的。再次强调，意识自我是极为必要的。但无论如何强调都不为过：意识仅仅是一种聚焦状态，而非一个自我。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)

quotes.text: Your scientists consider themselves quite rational, yet many of them, at least, would be more honest when they tried to describe the beginning of the universe if they admitted that reason alone cannot provide any true insight. Each of you are as familiar with the so-called birth of the universe, as close to it or as distant [from it], as your own recognized consciousness is to your own physical birth, for the initiation of awareness and sensation in one infant really carries all of the same questions as those involved with the birth of the universe.
quotes.translation: 你们的科学家自诩理性，但至少其中许多人若承认单凭理性无法获得真知灼见，在试图描述宇宙起源时会更为诚实。你们每个人对所谓宇宙诞生的熟悉程度，或近或远，恰如你们自觉意识对自己肉身诞生的认知——因为一个婴儿觉知与感知的萌发，确实承载着与宇宙诞生相同的所有疑问。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: I never want any of my remarks to be construed in such a fashion that it seems I am in any way negating the fullness, validity, and magnificence of physical existence. I do want to point out, however, that a state you usually call dreaming is but a dim indication of an inner reality of events (intently), an inner order of events from which the physical world emerges. I hope to show how the nature of dreams has helped shape man's consciousness. I hope to show that consciousness forms the environment, and not the other way around (with many gestures).
quotes.translation: 我绝不愿任何言论被曲解为否定物质存在的圆满性、真实性与壮丽性。然而我确实要指出，你们通常称为梦境的状态，仅是内在事件实相（专注地）的模糊示现，是物质世界从中涌现的内在事件秩序。我希望揭示梦境本质如何塑造人类意识，更希望证明意识塑造环境，而非环境塑造意识（伴随大量手势）。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1

quotes.text: (Slowly:) Since you are not only a physically focused creature, however, other issues also operate. You have within yourself the condensed knowledge of your entire being. This information cannot appear in any complete fashion within a consciousness connected with a physical brain. The multidimensional reality simply cannot be expressed. In the dreaming state, when consciousness relates opaquely to physical concerns, glimpses of the multidimensional self can appear in dream imagery and fantasies that will symbolically express your greater existence.
quotes.translation: （缓慢地：）然而，既然你不仅是一个聚焦于肉体的存在，其他议题也在运作。你内在拥有你整个存有的浓缩知识。这些信息无法以任何完整的方式显现在与物质大脑相连的意识中。多维实相根本无法被表达。在梦境状态中，当意识与物质事务模糊关联时，多维自性的瞥见能以梦境意象和幻想的形式出现，这些将象征性地表达你更伟大的存在。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality

quotes.text: "It is essential that you realize that the dream world is a byproduct of your own existence. And because it is connected to you through chemical reactions, this leaves open the entryway of interactions, in animals as well as men. Since dreams are a by-product of any consciousness involved within matter, this leads us to the correct conclusion -- that trees have their dreams, that all physical matter, being formed about individualized units of consciousness of varying degrees, also participates in the involuntary construction of the dream universe."
quotes.translation: “至关重要的是，你必须认识到梦境世界是你自身存在的一个副产品。由于它通过化学反应与你相连，这为人类和动物都留下了互动的入口通道。既然梦是任何涉入物质的意识的副产品，这引导我们得出正确的结论——树木也有它们的梦，所有物质，既然是由不同程度个体化的意识单位所形成，也参与了梦宇宙的无意识构建。”
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1

quotes.text: The inner ego is the self who drives the wheel with purpose; at the same time there are many other wheels and many spokes... Our moment point analogy will also help you here. The sleeping self will of course be considered the primary self from the standpoint of its own reality. I cannot emphasize too strongly the fact that all of these portions are self-conscious. They may not be conscious of the other selves however. The inner senses connect all the selves, and the movements of consciousness are far more complicated than that of a Ferris wheel.
quotes.translation: 内在自我是带着目的驱动轮子的那个自己；同时还有许多其他轮子和辐条...我们的瞬间点类比在此也能帮助你们理解。沉睡的自我从自身实相的角度来看当然会被视为主要自我。我必须再三强调，所有这些部分都具有自我意识。然而它们可能对其他自我没有意识。内在感官连接着所有自我，而意识的运动远比摩天轮复杂得多。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 6 of The Seth Material (Sessions 240-280)

quotes.text: It was known, however -- and unconsciously written in the cells and mind and heart -- that this procedure would only go so far. When man's consciousness was sure of itself it would not need to be so narrowly focused. Then the true flowering of humanity's consciousness could begin. Then the ego could expand and become aware of realities it had "earlier" ignored. Period.
quotes.translation: 然而——这早已被无意识地铭刻在细胞、心智与心灵中——人们知道这个过程只能走到某一步。当人类的意识足够确信自身时，便无需如此狭隘地聚焦。那时人类意识的真正绽放才能开始。那时自我才能扩展，并觉察到它"早期"所忽视的实相。句号。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1

quotes.text: The tree is of course dissociated in one manner. In some ways its living forces and consciousness are kept to a minimum. It is in a state of drowsiness on the one hand, and on the other hand it focuses the usable portion of its energy into being a tree. The state of consciousness involved here is dull as compared to the highly differentiated human ability in many ways.
quotes.translation: 树木在某种程度上当然是解离的。在某些方面，它的生命力与意识被维持在最低限度。它一方面处于昏睡状态，另一方面将其可用能量聚焦于成为一棵树。与人类高度分化的能力相比，这里涉及的意识状态在许多方面都显得迟钝。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)

quotes.text: "There is no such thing as a cat consciousness, basically speaking, or a bird consciousness. In those terms, there are instead simply consciousnesses that choose to take certain focuses. We have not touched upon some of these matters, and some are, again, most difficult to explain, as we wish to avoid distortions. These would have nothing to do with Ruburt per se, but simply the way you put concepts together at this stage of development."
quotes.translation: “基本上来说，不存在所谓的猫意识或鸟意识。用那些术语来说，有的只是选择采取特定焦点的意识。我们尚未触及其中一些议题，而有些议题再次极难解释，因为我们希望避免扭曲。这些与鲁柏本身无关，而只是你们在这个发展阶段整合概念的方式。”
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: The impulse to be, in any terms that you can understand, is without beginning or end. What you have in your physical species are the manifestations of inner species of being, or creative groupings originated by consciousness as material patterns into which consciousness then flows. In those terms, the world came into being and the species appeared in a completely different framework of activity than is imagined, and one that cannot be scientifically established -- particularly within those boundaries with which science has protected itself.
quotes.translation: 以你们能理解的任何说法而言，存在的冲动无始无终。你们在物质物种中所拥有的，是内在存在物种的显现，或由意识作为物质模式所创造的创造性组合，意识随后流入这些模式。用那些术语来说，世界得以形成，物种出现在一个完全不同于想象的活动框架中——一个无法被科学证实的框架，尤其无法在科学用以自我保护的那些界限内被证实。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: To your way of thinking some lives are lived in a twinkling, and others last for centuries, as some huge trees. The perception of consciousness is not limited ... I have told you, for example, that the consciousness of the tree is not as specifically focused as your own. To all intents and purposes, however, the tree is conscious of 50 years before and 50 years hence.
quotes.translation: 按照你们的想法，有些生命如眨眼般短暂，有些则如巨树般延续数百年。意识的感知并不受限……例如我曾告诉你们，树木的意识不像你们那样高度聚焦。但就实际而言，树木能感知到五十年前与五十年后的存在。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: I want to mention that while form is not a characteristic of matter, it is one of the characteristics of consciousness. The form which consciousness takes is usually composed of matter on your plane. The form perceived by your outer senses is always composed of matter, of course. There are also however forms or structures that are what you may call psychological forms or structures, even within your own field, which you do not perceive with your outer senses, but which are nevertheless frameworks that underlie form as it appears in matter.
quotes.translation: 我想指出，虽然形态并非物质的特性，却是意识的特性之一。意识所采取的形态在你们层面通常由物质构成。你们外在感官所感知的形态当然始终由物质组成。然而，即便在你们自己的领域内，也存在你们可称之为心理形态或结构的东西——你们的外在感官无法感知它们，但它们却是物质形态显现时赖以存在的底层框架。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)

quotes.text: When you are only operating within physical reality, you have a fairly simple set of rules to serve you. Within dream reality you are much freer. The ego is not present. The waking consciousness, dear friends, is not the ego. The ego is merely a small portion of waking consciousness. The ego is the portion of waking consciousness that deals with physical manipulation.
quotes.translation: 当你们仅在物质实相中运作时，只需遵循一套相当简单的规则。在梦境实相中，你们则自由得多。自我并不在场。亲爱的朋友们，清醒意识并非自我。自我仅是清醒意识的一小部分，是清醒意识中处理物质操控的那部分。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 6 of The Seth Material (Sessions 240-280)

quotes.text: Various levels of consciousness are projected from the self in much the same manner. They are united however as interior and subsidiary identities that are a part of your own, and they are sent out by the inner self for various reasons. The thought is a psychological reality, a psychological and psychic identity but not a structured personalized identity. The self may send out fragments of itself in projections. These may or may not be structured personalized identities though they will be dependent for their existence upon the whole self.
quotes.translation: 各种层次的意识以大致相同的方式从自体投射出来。然而它们作为你自身的一部分的内在附属身份而统一，并由内在自体出于各种原因发送出去。思想是一种心理实相，一种心理与心灵的身份，但并非结构化的个性化身份。自体可能通过投射释放自身的碎片。这些碎片可能是也可能不是结构化的个性化身份，尽管它们的存在将依赖于整体自体。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)

quotes.text: If these are too vigorous, the individual may awaken. This is a vivid, intense, but usually brief stage. Another undifferentiated layer follows, this time marked quite definitely by voices, conversations, or images, as consciousness tunes in more firmly to other communications. Several of these may compete for the individual's attention. At this point the body is fairly quiet. The individual will follow one or another of these inner stimuli to a deeper level of consciousness, and form into light dreams the communications he is receiving.
quotes.translation: 若这些活动过于强烈，个体可能会醒来。这是一个生动、强烈但通常短暂的阶段。随后出现另一个未分化的层次，这次明显伴随着声音、对话或影像，因为意识更坚定地调频至其他通讯。其中几种可能竞相争夺个体的注意力。此时身体相当安静。个体将跟随这些内在刺激中的某一种进入更深的意识层次，并将接收到的通讯编织成浅层梦境。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: If you "read yourselves" sideways in such a manner, you would discover portions of your own consciousness stretching out across the entire fabric of the earth as you understand it -- becoming a part of the earth's material, even as those materials become part of the self that you recognize. Your consciousness would be far less hemmed in. Time would expand adjacently. You think of yourselves physically as "top dogs," however, separate from the other species and kinds of life, so that in effect you limit your own experience of your psyche.
quotes.translation: 若你们以这种方式"横向解读自身"，便会发现自身意识的片段延伸至你们所理解的整个地球织体——成为地球物质的一部分，正如那些物质成为你所认知的自体的一部分。你们的意识将远不受局限。时间会以相邻方式扩展。然而你们在物质层面视自己为"顶级掠食者"，与其他物种和生命形态相分离，这实际上限制了你们对自身心灵的经验。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche

quotes.text: (9:17.) Each species is endowed also, by virtue of the units of consciousness that compose it, with an overall inner picture of the condition of each other species (pause), and further characterized by basic impulses so that it is guided toward choices that best fulfill its own potentials for development while adding to the overall good of the entire world consciousness. This does not curtail free will any more than man's free will is curtailed because he must (underlined) grow from a fetus into an adult instead of the other way around.
quotes.translation: （9:17。）每个物种也因其构成的意识单元而被赋予了对其他物种状况的整体内在图像（停顿），并进一步以基本冲动为特征，从而被引导向最能实现自身发展潜能的选择，同时为整个世界的整体意识增添福祉。这并不会限制自由意志，就像人类的自由意志不会因为他必须（强调）从胎儿成长为成人而非相反而受到限制一样。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1

quotes.text: When you realize that the self has no boundaries, then you can begin to make progress. Since consciousness to begin with does not exist in space, then there is no reason why the consciousness cannot so expand beyond its set limitations, and theoretically continue to do so. Such an expansion would give excellent impetus and value experience to those basic components, the cells and molecules, experience that would be retained and utilized. When so-called space travel becomes truly popular, truly practical, it will come along these lines.
quotes.translation: 当你们认识到自我没有边界时，你们就能开始取得进步。既然意识最初并不存在于空间中，那么就没有理由认为意识不能超越其设定的限制而扩展，并且在理论上可以持续如此。这样的扩展将为那些基本组成部分——细胞和分子——提供极好的推动力和价值体验，这些体验将被保留和利用。当所谓的太空旅行真正普及、真正实用时，它将沿着这些路线实现。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)

quotes.text: As energy flows through your field and appears within it, it does so according to its own inherent nature. This nature of energy, believe it or not, includes individualization and consciousness. Therefore, if you will for a moment think of bits of energy, or consciousness, initially without definite form, entering your physical plane, then according to the innate strength and capacity of any given particle it will, on entering your plane, on its own subconscious level already know how small or large a physical pattern it can form.
quotes.translation: 当能量流经你的场域并在其中显现时，它依循自身固有本质运作。信不信由你，这种能量本质包含个体化与意识。因此，若你暂且设想那些最初无固定形态的能量微粒或意识进入物质层面，那么根据任何特定粒子的先天强度与能力，在进入物质层面时，它会在自身潜意识层面早已知晓自己能形成何种规模大小的物质模式。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)

quotes.text: That earth-god portion of yourself attempts to direct you through probabilities. Again, on deep biological levels beneath normal consciousness, and on psychic levels above normal consciousness, you are aware of the integrity of your being -- but also of your great connection, while living in flesh, with the natural environment of time and space. The earth-god concept can be consciously used, but only to your greatest advantage if you understand the purposes of your conscious mind and its relationship with your biological nature.
quotes.translation: 你内在的"大地之神"部分试图通过可能性引导你。同样，在正常意识之下的深层生物层面，以及正常意识之上的心灵层面，你既知晓自身存在的完整性——也明了在肉身存续期间与时空自然环境的深刻联结。"大地之神"概念可以有意识地运用，但唯有当你理解意识心智的目的及其与生物本质的关系时，才能获得最大裨益。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1

quotes.text: I have explained somewhat earlier how the gestalt ego consciousness was formed, and to some degree explained its psychic composition, but there is much more to be said here. There is a steady and unwavering cooperation that exists, and it is the basis of your physical universe. You do not see this cooperation. Your senses are rather more equipped to notice difference and divergence than sameness; but nevertheless the cooperation of all conscious entities provides physical objects with whatever appearance of permanence they have.
quotes.translation: 我先前已解释过完形自我意识是如何形成的，并在某种程度上说明了其心灵构成，但此处仍有更多可探讨之处。存在一种稳定而坚定的合作，它是你们物质宇宙的基础。你们看不见这种合作。你们的感官更擅长察觉差异与分歧而非相同之处；然而，所有有意识实体的合作，为物质对象提供了它们所拥有的任何表面上的持久性。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)

quotes.text: (10:26.) These people are particularly helpful because they are still involved with physical reality, and have a more immediate understanding of the feelings and emotions involved at your end. Such persons may or may not have a memory of their nightly activities. Experiences with projection of consciousness and knowledge of the mobility of consciousness, are therefore very helpful as preparations for death.
