You can experience the after-death environment beforehand, so to speak, and learn the conditions that will be encountered.
quotes.translation: （10:26）这些人特别有帮助，因为他们仍与物质实相有所牵连，并且对你们这一端的感受与情绪有更直接的理解。这些人可能记得也可能不记得他们的夜间活动。因此，意识投射的经验以及对意识流动性的认识，作为死亡的准备非常有益。可以说，你们可以预先体验死后的环境，并学习将会遇到的条件。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: Even when you lost sight -- as you knew you would -- of those deep connections, they would continue to operate until, in its own way, man's consciousness could rediscover the knowledge and put it to use -- deliberately and willfully, thereby bringing that consciousness to flower. In your terms this would represent a great leap, for the egotistically aware individual would fully comprehend unconscious knowledge and act on his own, out of choice. He would become a conscious co-creator. Obviously, this has not as yet occurred.
quotes.translation: 即使当你们——正如你们所知的——暂时遗忘那些深层联系时，它们仍会持续运作，直到人类意识能够以自己的方式重新发现这些知识并有意识地、主动地加以运用，从而使意识绽放。在你们的时间架构中，这代表一次巨大飞跃，因为具有自我觉察力的个体将完全理解无意识知识，并基于自由选择自主行动。他将成为有意识的共同创造者。显然，这尚未发生。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1

quotes.text: What gives life to chemicals now? That is the more proper question. All energy is (underlined) not only aware-ized but the source of all organizations of consciousness, and all physical forms. These represent frameworks of consciousness. (Long pause.) There was a day when the dreaming world, in your terms, suddenly awakened to full reality as far as physical materialization is concerned. The planet was visited by desire. There were ghost excursions there -- mental buildings, dream civilizations which then became actualized.
quotes.translation: 现在是什么赋予化学物质生命？这才是更恰当的问题。所有能量不仅（强调）具有觉知性，更是所有意识组织与所有物质形态的源头。这些代表意识的架构。（长停顿。）在你们的时间概念中，曾有一天，梦境世界就物质显化而言突然觉醒至完整实相。欲望造访了这颗行星。那里曾有过幽灵般的探索——心灵建筑、梦境文明——随后它们被具体化。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: (With emphasis:) I am not saying, for example, that the living consciousness of each individual returned to the earth literally, but that the physical material permeated and stamped with that consciousness did, and does. Again, even the cells retain knowledge of all of their affiliations. In physical terms the consciousness that you understand is based upon this.
quotes.translation: （强调地：）我并非说，每个个体的活生生的意识真的回到了地球，而是说被那种意识渗透并打上印记的物理物质确实如此，并且现在依然如此。再次强调，甚至细胞也保留着它们所有关联的知识。从物理角度来看，你所理解的意识正是基于此。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: There is no need to worry about your friend (Ruburt). We want you to realize that there is more than your human reality. We want you to realize that there is consciousness without form, that there is consciousness with will and vitality that comes to you from beyond even those places that your Seth knows. We want you to realize that though it is hard for us to communicate, we spoke with your race before your race learned language. We gave you mental images and upon these images you learned to form the world that you knew.
quotes.translation: 无需担心你的朋友（鲁柏）。我们希望你能认识到，除了你们的人类现实之外，还有更多存在。我们希望你能认识到，存在无形无相的意识，存在具有意志和活力的意识，它们来自甚至超越你的赛斯所知的那些领域。我们希望你能认识到，尽管我们难以沟通，但在你们的种族学会语言之前，我们就曾与你们交谈过。我们给予你们心灵意象，而你们在这些意象之上学会了构建你们所认知的世界。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)

quotes.text: For various reasons, such a personality has not learned to assimilate its own experience. The characteristics of such apparitions follow those of a disturbed personality -- with some exceptions, however. The whole consciousness is not present. The personality itself seems to be having a nightmare, or a series of recurring dreams, during which it returns to the physical environment. The personality itself is "safe and sound," but certain portions of it work out unresolved problems, and discharge energy in such a fashion.
quotes.translation: 由于各种原因，这样一个人格尚未学会同化自己的经验。这类幻影的特征遵循着困扰人格的模式——不过也有例外。整个意识并未在场。人格本身似乎正在经历一场噩梦，或一系列反复出现的梦境，在此期间它回到物理环境。人格本身“安然无恙”，但它的某些部分以这种方式解决未解决的问题，并释放能量。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: I will suggest many exercises throughout this book. Some of them will necessitate variations of normal consciousness. I may ask you to forget physical stimuli, or suggest that you amplify them, but I am nowhere stating that your mode of consciousness is wrong. It is limited, not by nature, but by your own beliefs and practice. You have not carried it far enough.
quotes.translation: 我将在本书中建议许多练习。其中一些练习需要改变正常意识状态。我可能会要求你忽略身体刺激，或建议你放大它们，但我从未说过你的意识模式是错误的。它受到限制，并非出于天性，而是源于你自己的信念与习惯。你尚未将其推进得足够深远。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche

quotes.text: Man is a part of that trans-species consciousness also, as are the plants and animals. Also, part of man's reality contributes to that trans-species organization, but he has not chosen to focus his practical daily consciousness in that direction, or to identify his individuality with it. As a result he does not understand the greater natural mobility he himself possesses, nor can he practically perceive the natural psychological gestalts of which he is a part, that form all of your natural -- meaning physical -- world.
quotes.translation: 人类也是那种跨物种意识的一部分，正如植物与动物一样。此外，人类实相的某些部分也贡献给那种跨物种组织，但他并未选择将日常实用意识聚焦于那个方向，也未将个体性与之认同。结果，他既不了解自身所拥有的更大自然流动性，也无法实际感知他所属的那些自然心理完形——正是这些完形构成了你们所有自然（即物质）世界。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche

quotes.text: If you then realize that every physical particle contains its own inner and initial consciousness, then you will see that we have come full circle. The individual or the self is all important. It operates to form as complicated a gestalt as possible, following the law of value fulfillment, and yet in so doing it does not either invade, deny or negate other individual consciousness. It is limitless because there are no limits to the possibilities of its value fulfillment, or to the number of gestalts which it can form.
quotes.translation: 如果你意识到每个物质粒子都包含其自身内在的初始意识，那么你会发现我们已回到原点。个体或自我至关重要。它遵循价值完成法则运作，尽可能形成复杂的完形，但在此过程中既不侵犯、否认也不否定其他个体意识。它是无限的，因为其价值完成的可能性和所能形成的完形数量都没有限制。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)

quotes.text: This does not mean that it ignores its earlier functions. In true expansion of consciousness then, you become more and more aware, more conscious, underlined, of inner realities that were previously never realized on a conscious level. Since the ego is presently your main vehicle of consciousness, it will not be obliterated. It will indeed be resurrected. It simply changes not only its form, but its inner core, accepting as a part of itself realities which it previously ignored out of fear, ignorance and insecurity.
quotes.translation: 这并不意味着它忽略早期的功能。在真正的意识扩展中，你会越来越觉察、越来越有意识地（着重强调）认识到那些先前从未在意识层面被认知的内在实相。由于自我目前是你主要的意识载体，它不会被抹除。它确实会重生。它不仅改变形式，更改变内在核心，接纳那些因恐惧、无知和不安全感而先前忽略的实相作为自身的一部分。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)

quotes.text: While your present conscious beliefs dictate your current experience, and while your physical body wears its solidity only in present time to your senses, beneath this both the ever-changing elements of your body and your consciousness are relatively free in time. They exist in a multidimensionality with which rational consciousness is not yet equipped to deal.
quotes.translation: 虽然你当前有意识的信念主宰着你的即时体验，虽然你的肉体只在当下时刻对感官显现其坚固性，但在这表象之下，你身体不断变化的元素与你的意识在时间中相对自由。它们存在于理性意识尚无法处理的多次元性中。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality

quotes.text: Many of the most powerful aspects of consciousness are at work precisely when it seems to you that you are relatively unconscious and asleep to physical reality. It would be impossible for you to handle the vast amount of material available, in the context of time as you presently experience it. To operate adequately in your highly specific field (pause), an almost infinite amount of information must be instantly assimilated, probabilities calculated, and certain balances maintained of which you are not even aware.
quotes.translation: 意识最强大的许多面向，恰恰在你看似相对无意识、对物质实相陷入沉睡时运作。若以你当前体验的时间脉络，你根本不可能处理如此庞大的可用信息。为了在你高度特定的场域中充分运作（停顿），必须瞬间吸收近乎无限的信息量、计算概率、维持某些你甚至未曾察觉的平衡。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality

quotes.text: Dictation: Usually you think in terms of a hypothetical whole self or consciousness, emerging at birth and disappearing at death. There are, however, learned arguments in which professors debate such questions. Some astrologers use the time of conception in their calculations, while others prefer the date of birth. Various religions have decided that the "soul" enters the fetus at its conception, while others argue that consciousness cannot be considered a human soul until some time later, just prior to birth.
quotes.translation: 口述：通常你们会设想一个假设性的整体自性或意识，在出生时浮现、死亡时消失。然而学术界确实存在教授们辩论这类问题的论证。有些占星家以受孕时刻计算星盘，另一些则偏好出生日期。各种宗教判定"灵魂"在受孕时进入胎儿，而另一些则认为意识要等到出生前某个较晚时刻才能被视为人类灵魂。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: They are the smallest imaginable "packages" of consciousness that you can imagine, and despite any ideas to the contrary, basically consciousness has nothing to do with size. If that were the case, it would take more than a world-sized globe to contain the consciousness of simply one cell.
quotes.translation: 它们是你能想象到的最小单位的意识"包裹"，且无论任何相反的观点，意识本质上与大小无关。若真与大小相关，那么仅一个细胞的意识就需要比地球还大的容器才能容纳。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2

quotes.text: It is not like a liquid that flows through a bowl. It becomes a part of that which contains it, and therefore a part of the consciousness, in your case, of the cells and all of your structures. Words on a printed page for example are not dead, nor are they merely inert symbols. They share the consciousness of the material upon which they are printed and the various individual letters themselves, by their position and reality, make each unique piece of paper upon which they are written original from all others.
quotes.translation: 它不像液体般流过容器，而是成为容器的一部分——就你们而言，即成为细胞及所有身体结构意识的一部分。例如印刷页面上的文字并非死物，亦非仅是无生命的符号。它们共享承载其物质的意识，而每个字母本身凭借其位置与实相，使每张承载文字的纸张都成为独一无二的存在。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)

quotes.text: These "period pieces," overall, have a particular purpose. By the very nature of consciousness, it seeks to materialize itself in as many dimensions as possible -- to create from itself new levels of awareness, new offshoots. In doing so it creates all reality. Reality, therefore, is always in a state of becoming. The thoughts that you think, for example, in your actor's roles, are still completely unique and lead to new creativity. Certain aspects of your own consciousness could be fulfilled in no other way.
quotes.translation: 整体而言，这些"时代产物"具有特定目的。基于意识的本质，它寻求在尽可能多的维度中具现化自身——从自身创造出新的觉知层次与新的分支。在此过程中它创造了一切实相。因此，实相始终处于"生成"状态。例如你作为演员所扮演的角色中产生的思想，依然完全独特，并导向新的创造力。你自身意识的某些面向，唯有通过这种方式才能得以实现。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: Your consciousness can seem to float. There are varying physical sensations, sometimes of growing large, sometimes of falling. Both sensations are characteristic of moments in which you almost catch yourself, almost become aware of this undifferentiated area, and then translate some of its experiences into physical terms. The sensation of largeness, for example, is a physical interpretation of the psychic expansion. The feeling of falling is an interpretation of a sudden return of consciousness to the body.
quotes.translation: 你的意识似乎可以漂浮。有各种身体感受，有时感觉变大，有时感觉坠落。这两种感受都是你几乎要抓住自己、几乎要意识到这个未分化领域，然后将其某些经验转化为物质术语时的典型时刻。例如，变大的感觉是心灵扩张的物质诠释，而坠落的感觉则是意识突然回归身体的诠释。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: Now this happens in every night's sleep. Two areas of activity are involved, one very passive and one acutely active. In one state this portion of consciousness is passive, receiving information. In the next stage it is active as it takes part through action -- the concepts given it are then vividly perceived through participation and examples. This is the most protected area of sleep. The rejuvenating characteristics enter in here, and it is during this period that the Speakers act as teachers and guides.
quotes.translation: 这种情况在每个夜晚的睡眠中都会发生。涉及两个活动区域，一个非常被动，一个极度活跃。在一个状态中，意识的这一部分是被动的，接收信息。在下一个阶段，它变得活跃，通过行动参与——所给予的概念随后通过参与和实例被生动地感知。这是睡眠中最受保护的领域。恢复活力的特质在此进入，而正是在此期间，言说者扮演着教师和向导的角色。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: You usually glide from wakefulness through to sleep without ever noticing the various conditions of consciousness through which you pass, yet there are several. First, of course, with various degrees of spontaneity, there is the inward turn of consciousness away from physical data, from worries and concerns of the day. Then there is an undifferentiated level between wakefulness and sleep where you act as a receiver -- passive but open, in which telepathic and clairvoyant messages come to you quite easily.
quotes.translation: 你通常从清醒滑入睡眠，却从未注意到你所经过的各种意识状态，然而这些状态确实存在。首先，当然，以不同程度的自发性，意识从物质资料、从白天的烦恼和担忧中向内转向。然后，在清醒与睡眠之间有一个未分化的层次，你在此充当接收者——被动但开放，在此层次中，心电感应和千里眼的讯息相当容易地来到你身边。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: Consciousness ejects signals, the consciousness within the cell, not the cell, you see. Now the consciousness is within the cell, all through the cell, no localized within it, and yet it is not the matter of the cell.
quotes.translation: 意识会发出信号，细胞内的意识，而非细胞本身，你明白吗。意识存在于细胞内，遍布整个细胞，并非局限于其中，但它并非细胞的物质本身。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)

quotes.text: So to rocks, say, you can be considered a portion of their environment, while you may consider them merely a portion of your environment. You simply do not tune into the range of rock consciousness. Actually (pause), many other kinds of consciousness, while focused in their own specific ways, are more aware than man is of earth's unified nature--but man, in following his own ways, also adds to the value fulfillment of all other consciousnesses in ways that are quite outside of usual systems of knowledge.
quotes.translation: 因此，比如说，对于岩石而言，你可以被视为它们环境的一部分，而你可能仅仅将它们视为你环境的一部分。你只是没有调准到岩石意识的频率范围。实际上（停顿），许多其他类型的意识，虽然以它们自己的特定方式聚焦，却比人类更了解地球的统一本质——但人类在遵循自己道路的同时，也以完全超出通常知识体系的方式，为所有其他意识的价值完成做出贡献。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1

quotes.text: And no memory, you see, would ever be lost, and nor is any memory lost. The personality that you have now is simply the flower of the moment, not realizing that it has the knowledge of its own past histories; and all of this would be but one cosmic flower. Energy constantly renews itself. The various flowers could then be compared to the various dimensions through which action and consciousness know their own reality. The expansion of consciousness automatically leads you to understanding and compassion.
quotes.translation: 没有任何记忆会丢失，你明白吗，而且确实没有任何记忆会丢失。你现在所拥有的人格只是当下的花朵，它不知道它拥有自己过去历史的知识；而这一切不过是一朵宇宙之花。能量不断更新自身。各种花朵可以比作行动与意识认识自身实相的各种维度。意识的扩展会自动引领你走向理解与慈悲。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)

quotes.text: Your arbitrary decision that consciousness ends with the change of physical properties at the arbitrary point you call death, does not preclude the fact that consciousness does not end here. There is a metamorphosis that occurs with the change of the fetus from a fetus into a fullblown, miniature human being at birth. This change is not much looked into, because it occurs beyond the reach of your outer senses. Nevertheless this metamorphosis involves, in appearance, such a change as to seem impossible.
quotes.translation: 你武断地认定意识在你称为死亡的任意点上随物理特性的改变而终结，但这并不排除意识并未在此终结的事实。在出生时，胎儿从胎儿形态转变为发育完整、微缩的人类形态，这一蜕变过程并未被深入研究，因为它发生在外在感官无法触及的领域。然而，这种蜕变在表象上涉及一种看似不可能的变化。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)

quotes.text: The consciousness exists however. The consciousness as it reasserts itself within physical reality has no memory of the interval in which it did not physically exist. The inner self however retains all memory. Dreams allow consciousness to disentangle itself from physical reality. For various reasons the intervals here, you see, are to some extent recalled.
quotes.translation: 然而，意识确实存在。当意识在物质实相中重新确立自身时，它并不记得自己曾不存在于物质形态的那段间隔。但内在自体保留了所有记忆。梦境让意识得以从物质实相中解脱出来。由于各种原因，这里的间隔在某种程度上会被忆起。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 6 of The Seth Material (Sessions 240-280)

quotes.text: Therefore, consciousness-of-self can appear with or without the existence of an ego. Consciousness-of-self is an attribute then of all physical species, regardless of their classification. Personality, human personality, is simply the name given to this class of self, as applied and seen within human beings. Personality changes and acts upon all other action. Personality, then, can be seen to operate as a field of action in identity; but identity that is conscious of its relation to action as a whole.
quotes.translation: 因此，自我意识可以在有或没有自我结构的情况下出现。自我意识是所有物种的属性，无论其分类如何。人格，人类人格，只是赋予这类自我的名称，在人类身上应用和显现。人格改变并作用于所有其他行动。人格可以被视为身份中的行动场域；但这是意识到自身与整体行动关系的身份。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)

quotes.text: (Slowly:) You have not learned to use your consciousness properly or fully, so that it seems that imagination, emotions and reasoning are separate faculties, or sometimes set against each other. The mature conscious mind, once more, accepts data from the exterior world and from the interior one. It is only when you believe that consciousness must be attuned only to exterior conditions that you force it to cut itself off from inner knowledge, intuitional "voices," and the depths from which it springs.
quotes.translation: （缓慢地：）你们尚未学会正确或充分地运用自己的意识，以至于想象、情感和理性看似是分离的能力，有时甚至相互对立。成熟的意识心智再次接纳来自外在世界和内在世界的资料。只有当你们相信意识必须只调准于外在条件时，你们才迫使它切断与内在知识、直觉性"声音"及其所源起的深层之间的联系。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality

quotes.text: Do not place the words of gurus, ministers, priests, scientists, psychologists, friends -- or my words -- higher than the feelings of your own being. You can learn much from others, but the deepest knowledge must come from within yourself. Your own consciousness is embarked upon a reality that basically can be experienced by no other, that is unique and untranslatable, with its own meaning, following its own paths of becoming.
quotes.translation: 不要将大师、牧师、祭司、科学家、心理学家、朋友的话语——或我的话语——置于你们自身存在的感受之上。你们可以从他人那里学到很多，但最深刻的知识必须来自你们自己。你们的意识正投身于一种本质上无法被他人体验的现实，它是独特且不可翻译的，拥有自己的意义，遵循自己成为的道路。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality

quotes.text: Your consciousness is far divorced from the physical organism, and it would be dangerous to stay away for any extended period of physical time. It would for example be quite possible to return to the physical body from this form, and not recognize it as your own. We would not want you to have such an experience. There is confusion and disorientation that can occur, using this third form. You need have no worries however, since as a rule your excursions will be along the lines of your own development.
quotes.translation: 你的意识远非与肉体组织密不可分，若离开肉体时间过长，在物理时间内将十分危险。举例来说，从这种形态返回肉体时，极有可能认不出那是你自己的躯体。我们不希望你经历这种体验。使用这第三种形态时，可能会产生困惑与迷失感。不过你无需担忧，因为按照惯例，你的出游将遵循自身发展的轨迹。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 6 of The Seth Material (Sessions 240-280)

quotes.text: Beta was not meant to carry the full weight of conscious activity, however, although its accelerating qualities can lead to initiations into "higher" realms of consciousness, where indeed the brain waves quicken. The other patterns (delta, theta, and alpha) are highly important to physical and mental stability, being very interwound with cellular consciousness. In cases usually called schizophrenic, the beta acceleration is not supported by the stabilizing attributes of the other known frequencies.
quotes.translation: 贝塔波本非设计来承载意识活动的全部重量，尽管其加速特性可引领进入"更高"意识领域——彼时脑波确实会加快。其他脑波模式（德尔塔、西塔、阿尔法）对身心稳定至关重要，与细胞意识深度交织。在通常称为精神分裂症的案例中，贝塔波的加速作用缺乏其他已知频率稳定属性的支撑。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: In more general terms, how do the members of each family operate through the mechanics of reincarnation? Or of probabilities or counterparts? It's also important to keep in mind what Seth told us in his first delivery for the 735th session: "Each personality carries traces of other characteristics besides those of the family of consciousness to which he or she might belong ... A book would be needed to explain the dimensions of the psyche in relationship to the various families of consciousness."
quotes.translation: 更概括地说，每个家族的成员如何通过转世机制运作？或通过概率机制与对等人物机制？同样重要的是要记住赛斯在第735节首次传讯时告诉我们的话："每个人格都带有其可能所属的意识家族特征之外的其他特征痕迹……需要一本书才能解释心灵维度与各个意识家族之间的关系。"
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: You do not have completely empty shells of matter about to be filled, in that the new personality hovers in and about, particularly after conception and with greater frequency and intensity thereafter. The shock of birth has several consequences, however, that usually draw the personality full blast, so to speak, into physical reality. Before this, the conditions are fairly uniform. The body consciousness is nurtured almost automatically, reacting strongly but under highly controlled conditions.
quotes.translation: 你们并非拥有等待被填充的完全空壳物质，因为新人格在受孕后尤其会盘旋于物质内外，此后频率与强度逐渐增加。然而出生的冲击会带来若干后果，通常会使人格以全速（可谓如此）投入物质实相。在此之前，状态相当均匀。身体意识几乎自动获得滋养，在高度受控条件下强烈反应。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: The first journey from one home station to another, unfamiliar one may bring you in contact with various kinds of bleed-throughs, distortions, or static. These can be expected. They are simply the result of not yet learning how to tune your own consciousness clearly in to other kinds of focus. Before you can pick up the "next" station, for example, you may see ghost images in your mind, or pick up distorted versions from your own home station. You have momentarily dispensed with the usual, habitual organizational process by which you unite regular physical sense perceptions, so while you are "between stations," you may well encounter mixed signals from each. When you alter your conscious focus in such a fashion, you are also moving away from the part of your psyche that you consider its center. You are journeying through your own psyche, in other words, for different realities are different states of the psyche -- materialized, projected outward and experienced. That applies to your home station or physical world as well.
quotes.translation: 从一个本家站台前往另一个陌生站台的首次旅程，可能会让你遭遇各种渗透、扭曲或静电干扰。这些现象皆属正常，仅因尚未学会如何清晰调谐自身意识至其他焦点所致。例如在接收"下一个"站台前，你可能会在脑海中看到幻影图像，或接收到来自本家站台的扭曲版本。你暂时摒弃了整合常规物理感官知觉的惯常组织过程，因此在"站台间隙"期间，很可能遭遇来自各方的混合信号。当你以这种方式改变意识焦点时，也在远离你视为心灵中心的部分。换言之，你正在穿越自己的心灵——因为不同实相就是心灵的不同状态：物质化、向外投射并被体验。这同样适用于你的本家站台或物质世界。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: Your physical life and your dreaming life are so intimately connected that it can be misleading to say what I am about to say, colon: that waking experience springs from the unknown dream reality. On the one hand the statement is indeed true. On the other hand, the intricate inner workings make it impossible to separate one from the other. "Reality" operates basically, however, in a way that is perceived more clearly in the dream state. Freedom from time and place, the wider kind of communication, the great mobility of consciousness -- all of these experiences under dreaming conditions are characteristic of the basic nature of reality -- whereas your waking experience provides limitations that are indicators of certain conditions only. Period.
quotes.translation: 你的物质生活与梦境生活如此紧密相连，以至于我接下来要说的话可能产生误导：清醒体验源自未知的梦境实相。一方面，这个说法确实成立；另一方面，内在运作的错综复杂使得两者无法分离。然而，"实相"的基本运作方式在梦境状态中能被更清晰地感知。不受时空限制的交流、更广泛的沟通方式、意识的高度流动性——所有这些梦境中的体验都是实相基本本质的特征，而清醒体验所提供的限制仅能反映特定条件。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: This artificial aggressiveness has nothing to do either, basically, with the struggle for survival. It is the direct result of the fact that the male has been taught to deny the existence within himself of certain basic emotions. This means that he denies a certain portion of his own humanity, and then is forced to overreact in expressing those emotions left open to him. The reasons for such a lopsided focus have been discussed at various times in my works. The male, however, chose to take upon himself a kind of specialization of consciousness that, carried too far, leads to a hard over-objectivity. Only in dreams in your time, in your society, is the male free to cry unabashedly, to admit any kind of dependency, and only at certain occasions and usually in relative privacy is he allowed to express feelings of love.
quotes.translation: 这种人为的侵略性本质上与生存竞争毫无关联。它直接源于男性被教导否认自身某些基本情感的存在。这意味着他否认了自身人性的某一部分，进而被迫过度反应以表达那些被允许流露的情感。我在著作中多次讨论过这种失衡聚焦的原因。然而，男性选择承担某种意识专门化的角色，这种专门化若过度发展，会导致僵化的过度客观性。在你们的社会中，唯有在梦境里，男性才能毫无羞耻地哭泣、承认任何形式的依赖；只有在特定场合且通常相对私密时，他才被允许表达爱的情感。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche

quotes.text: Ruburt glimpsed some of the principles involved when you were at [your downtown apartments] on several occasions--once when he tried to write a poem about the comprehensions that simply would not be verbalized.2 I do not know how to explain some of this, but in your terms there is (underlined) light within (underlined) darkness. Light has more manifestations than its physical version (intently), so that even when it may not be physically manifested there is light everywhere, and that light is the source of your physical version and its physical laws. In a manner of speaking, light itself forms darkness. Each unit of consciousness, whatever its degree, is, again, composed of energy--and that energy manifests itself with a kind of light that is not physically perceived: a light that is basically, now, far more intense than any physical variety, and a light from which all colors emerge.
quotes.translation: 鲁柏在你们住市中心公寓时曾数次瞥见其中某些原理——有一次他试图写一首诗来传达那些根本无法言说的领悟。² 我不知道如何解释其中某些部分，但用你们的话说，黑暗之中存在着（着重号）光（着重号）。光的显现有比物质版本更多的形态（专注地），因此即便在物质层面可能不可见时，光依然无处不在，而这光正是你们物质版本及其物理法则的源头。从某种意义上说，光本身形成了黑暗。每个意识单元，无论其程度如何，同样由能量构成——这种能量以某种非物理感知的光显现自身：这种光本质上比任何物质形态的光都强烈得多，是所有色彩的源头。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1

quotes.text: Any consciousness is therefore innately aware of its basic identity. The inner self knows what is behind the stars and planets that the eyes views, but the ego would be swept aside in panic. This system spoken of earlier, the sun and original 9 planets, in your terms, have long ago passed into and formed other universal systems. The whole cosmic structure however was the materialization of one original thought, for the thought, the real reality, must always exist before the representation of it.
quotes.translation: 因此，任何意识都天生知晓其基本身份。内在自我知道眼睛所见的星辰与行星背后是什么，但自我会被恐慌所席卷。之前提到的这个系统，即太阳和最初的九颗行星，用你们的话说，早已消逝并形成了其他宇宙系统。然而，整个宇宙结构是一个原始思想的物质化，因为思想、真实的实相，必须始终存在于其表象之前。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)

quotes.text: Let us begin with the normal waking consciousness that you know. But one step away from this is another level of consciousness into which you all slip without knowing. We will call it "A-l." It is adjacent to your normal consciousness, separated from it very slightly; and yet in it very definite effects can appear that are not present in your usual state.
quotes.translation: 让我们从你所熟知的正常清醒意识开始。但仅一步之遥，便是另一种意识层次，你们所有人都会不知不觉地滑入其中。我们称之为"A-1"。它紧邻你的正常意识，与之仅有细微的间隔；然而在其中，某些明确的效果可能出现，而这些效果在你通常的状态下并不存在。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: The intertwining of consciousness and matter is most intricate and highly complicated. In all cases consciousness is first, and it forms its physical constructions according to its abilities, first of all forming its own primary construction, and then branching outward, constructing secondary images of other consciousnesses with whom it comes in contact.
quotes.translation: 意识与物质的交织极为错综复杂。在所有情况下，意识都是首要的，它根据自身能力形成其物质构造，首先形成自己的主要构造，然后向外延伸，构建它所接触的其他意识的次要影像。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)

quotes.text: Up until then, the main communications had followed the characteristic patterns of units of consciousness (long pause), each unit knowing its relationship to all others upon the planet. Creatures relied upon inner senses while learning to operate the new, highly specific physical ones that pinpointed perception in time and place. This pinpointing of perception was of vital importance, for with the full arousal of consciousness in flesh, intersections with space and time [had to be] impeccable.
quotes.translation: 在此之前，主要的沟通遵循意识单元的特征模式（长停顿），每个单元都知道自己与地球上所有其他单元的关系。生物在学会运用高度具体的新物质感官——这些感官精准定位时空中的感知——的同时，仍依赖内在感官。这种感知的精准定位至关重要，因为随着意识在肉身中的完全觉醒，与时空的交汇必须分毫不差。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1

quotes.text: It is precisely in the low points of fluctuation that such experiences originate, for normal consciousness is momentarily at a weak state and in a period of rest. The whole physical organism undergoes such normal fluctuations, again, that are usually quite unnoticed. These periods also fluctuate, following rhythms that have to do with the characteristic personality. In some the waves of motion are comparatively long and slow, the valleys within being sloped; with others, the reverse is true.
quotes.translation: 这类体验恰恰起源于波动的低点，因为正常意识此刻处于短暂虚弱状态与休息期。整个物质有机体都会经历这类通常不被察觉的正常波动。这些周期同样遵循与人格特质相关的节奏而波动。有些人的波动幅度相对长而缓慢，波谷呈缓坡状；另一些人则恰恰相反。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: Almost all experiences from this level will be symbolically represented, for otherwise they would have no meaning to you. The experiences will all have to do in one way or another with nonphysical life, noncorporeal consciousness and forms, and the independence of consciousness from matter. These experiences will always be supportive. Out-of-body experiences will often be involved here, in which the projectionist finds himself in an unearthly environment or one of great beauty and grandeur.
quotes.translation: 来自这一层面的几乎所有经验都会以象征性方式呈现，否则它们对你而言将毫无意义。这些经验都将以某种方式涉及非物质生命、非肉体意识与形态，以及意识独立于物质的事实。这些经验始终具有支持性。此处常涉及出体经验，投射者会发现自己身处非尘世的环境，或处于极为壮丽宏伟的境地。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: As I have mentioned many times, at present you focus your attentions and consciousness within the physical system. This alone can be compared to what Leary calls imprinting. Existence within any system will necessitate some imprinting. The imprinting simply involves an adjustment whereby consciousness is attuned to a particular station, so to speak.
quotes.translation: 正如我曾多次提及，目前你们将注意力与意识聚焦于物质系统内。仅此一点便可类比于利里所称的印记作用。存在于任何系统内都必然涉及某种印记作用。这种印记作用仅涉及一种调整，使意识得以调频至特定"站点"——姑且如此比喻。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)

quotes.text: The purpose is the expansion of consciousness itself, and this automatically leads to the knowledge that every consciousness is connected to every other, and that any harm to one is harm to all. Time simply does not exist as a series of moments. This is three-dimensional illusion. Therefore in actuality no one life is lived before nor after another.
quotes.translation: 其目的在于意识本身的扩展，这自然导向一个认知：每个意识都与所有其他意识相连，对任一意识的伤害即是对所有意识的伤害。时间根本不存在于连续片刻的序列中。这是三维空间的幻相。因此实际上，没有任何生命存在于其他生命之前或之后。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)

quotes.text: In many ways history is your built-in past, the obvious events that are significant. All of the different variations that can be played upon human consciousness, all of the racial probabilities, are in one way occurring in ages past -- but they are also happening in what you think of as your present. As mentioned earlier (in sessions 680-82), your consciousness seizes upon certain events over others and brings these into significance, and therefore into the official reality that you know.
quotes.translation: 从许多方面来说，历史是你们内置的过去，是那些显而易见且具有重大意义的事件。所有能在人类意识上演奏的不同变奏，所有种族可能性，在某种程度上都发生在过去的年代——但它们也同时发生在你们所谓的当下。如先前（第680-82节）所提，你们的意识会从众多事件中抓住某些特定事件，赋予其重要性，从而将其纳入你们所知的官方实相。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1

quotes.text: Reality means consciousness, and through the very physical apparatus developed by inwardness, which was aware from the beginning, further embellishments became possible for consciousness itself. The inwardness not only formed physical matter then, but formed new dimensions of inwardness itself, new dynamics from which consciousness could originate.
quotes.translation: 实相即意识，而透过由内在性所发展的物质机制——内在性从一开始就具有觉知——意识本身得以获得进一步的修饰。内在性不仅形成了物质，更形成了内在性本身的新维度，以及意识得以从中起源的新动力。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)

quotes.text: Instead, you have put love into very definite categories, so that its existence is right only under the most limited conditions. Love goes underground, but springs up in distorted forms and exaggerated tendencies. You have followed this course for different reasons at different times. Neither sex is to blame. Instead your sexual situation is simply another reflection of the state of your consciousness. As a species, presently at least in the Western world, you equate sex and love. You imagine that sexual expression is the only one natural to love. Love, in other words, must it seems express itself exclusively through the exploration (humorously and deeper), in one way or another, of the beloved's sexual portions.
quotes.translation: 相反地，你们将爱归入非常明确的范畴，使其存在仅在极其有限的条件下才被视为正当。爱转入地下，却以扭曲的形式和夸大的倾向涌现。你们在不同时期因不同理由遵循此道。两性皆非罪魁祸首。你们的性状况不过是你们意识状态的另一面反映。作为一个物种，至少在西方世界，你们将性与爱等同。你们想象性表达是爱唯一自然的表现方式。换言之，爱似乎必须通过探索（幽默且更深入地）所爱之人的性部位，以某种方式才能表达自身。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche

quotes.text: (10:05.) As I said, there are all ranges of suffering, and I am beginning this discussion, which I will continue now and then in between regular book dictation, in a very general manner. In times past in particular, though the custom is not dead, men purged themselves, wore ashes and beat themselves with chains, or went hungry or otherwise deprived themselves. They suffered, in other words, for religion's sake. It was not just that they believed suffering was good for the soul--a statement which can or cannot be true, incidentally, and I will go into that later--but they understood something else: The body will only take so much suffering when it releases consciousness. So they hoped to achieve religious ecstasy.
quotes.translation: （十点零五分。）正如我所说，痛苦有各种层次。我现在以非常概括的方式开始这个讨论，之后会不时在常规书籍口述之间继续。尤其在往昔——尽管这种习俗并未完全消亡——人们净化自身、披麻蒙灰、用锁链鞭打自己，或忍饥挨饿或以其他方式自我剥夺。换言之，他们为宗教的缘故受苦。这不仅仅是因为他们相信痛苦对灵魂有益——顺便说，这个说法可能正确也可能不正确，我稍后会探讨——而是他们领悟了另一件事：当身体承受足够多的痛苦时，它会释放意识。因此他们希望借此达到宗教狂喜。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1

quotes.text: The mechanics of consciousness remain the same. They do not change for animals or men. Therefore there are no limitations set upon the development of any individual consciousness, or growth of any identity. Consciousness both in the body and without finds its own range, its own level. A dog, then, is not limited to being a dog in other existences.
quotes.translation: 意识的运作机制始终如一。无论对动物还是人类，它都不会改变。因此，任何个体意识的发展或任何身份认同的成长都不存在限制。意识无论处于身体之内还是之外，都会找到自身的范围与层次。所以，一只狗在其他存在中并不局限于只能当狗。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: So if you want to know what my environment is like, you will have to understand what I am. In order to explain, I shall have to speak about the nature of consciousness in general. In doing so I shall end up telling you much about yourself. The inner portions of your identity are already aware of much that I will tell you. Part of my purpose is to acquaint your egotistical self with knowledge that is already known to a larger portion of your own consciousness, that you have long ignored.
quotes.translation: 因此，若你想了解我的环境是什么样子，就必须先理解我是什么。为了解释这一点，我将不得不谈论意识的一般本质。在此过程中，我最终会告诉你许多关于你自己的事。你身份的内在部分早已知晓我将要讲述的许多内容。我的部分目的是让你那自我本位的自己，重新认识那些早已被你自身更大意识部分所知晓、却长期被你忽视的知识。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: You run into many contradictions. God is supposed to be male. The soul is sometimes considered female. The angels are male. Now let us look at the Garden of Eden. The story says that Eve tempted the male, having him eat of the tree of good and evil, or the tree of knowledge. (Pause.) This represented a state of consciousness, the point at which the species began to think and feel for itself, when it approached a certain state of consciousness in which it dared exert its own creativity.
quotes.translation: 你会遇到许多矛盾。上帝被认为是男性。灵魂有时被视为女性。天使是男性。现在让我们看看伊甸园。故事说夏娃引诱男性，让他吃下善恶知识树的果实。（停顿。）这代表了一种意识状态，即该物种开始为自己思考和感受的时刻，当它接近某种敢于发挥自身创造力的意识状态时。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: As I finish this note--and Dreams--I believe it quite safe to predict that whenever this book is published the reader will find that the general situation in the Middle East is essentially the same: The consciousnesses of the countries involved, then, will continue to resist all outside overtures to "sanity" that do not help perpetuate the exploration of their long-term goals. In the Middle East (as anywhere else on our planet) there's plenty of "time" for peace later! There are endless variations of peace to be ultimately explored, to be held in delicious abeyance, to be savored by each consciousness there, no matter how great or small, like the knowledge of a fine wine or a rich dessert still to come....
quotes.translation: 当我写完这篇笔记——以及《梦》——我相信可以稳妥地预测：无论这本书何时出版，读者都会发现中东的总体局势本质上依然如故：相关国家的意识将继续抵制所有外部提出的"理智"倡议，除非这些倡议有助于延续其长期目标的探索。在中东（如同地球任何地方）有的是"时间"留给未来的和平！那里有无数种和平的变体等待最终探索，被美妙地悬置，被那里的每个意识细细品味——无论其大小——就像等待品尝的美酒或丰盛甜点的知识……
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2

quotes.text: Now they are like time capsules, cutting down stimuli for certain intervals, and then injecting stimulants as destination points are reached. The process is highly involved. The injections are made into the physical being, affecting therefore the physical brain. Consciousness projects in an out of body experience. The physical brain is cushioned against shock because in this case consciousness travels at such a fast pace that ordinarily contact between it and the body would be severed.
quotes.translation: 现在它们如同时间胶囊，在特定间隔削减刺激，然后在到达目的地时注入兴奋剂。这个过程极为复杂。注入作用于物质体，进而影响物质大脑。意识在出体体验中投射。物质大脑受到缓冲保护，因为在这种情况下意识以极快速度移动，通常与身体的联系会被切断。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)

quotes.text: According to the situation, you can do the same thing to find out the effect of this decision on others specifically. You then return to normal consciousness, going through the A-l state that you used as a preliminary. After a period of rest, return and make the second decision, and again the third, following through in the same manner. Then in your normal state of consciousness, of course, you make the decision that you want from the information and experience that you have received.
quotes.translation: 根据情况，你可以用同样的方法找出这个决定对他人具体产生的影响。然后你通过之前作为预备的A-1状态，回到正常意识。休息一段时间后，再回来做出第二个决定，接着以同样的方式做出第三个决定。当然，之后在你正常的意识状态下，你会根据所获得的信息和经验做出你想要的抉择。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: So I prefer, here at least, to speak of these units of consciousness instead. (Long pause.) Their nature is the vitalizing force behind everything in your physical universe, and others as well. These units can indeed appear in several places at once, and without going through space, in your terms. Literally now, these basic units of consciousness can be in all places at once. They are in all places at once. They will not be recognized because they will always appear as something else.
quotes.translation: 因此我宁愿在此处谈论这些意识单位。（长 pause。）它们的本质是你物质宇宙乃至其他宇宙中一切事物背后的活化力量。这些单位确实能同时出现在多个地方，而且以你们的时间观念而言，无需穿越空间。严格来说，这些基本意识单位能同时存在于所有地方。它们存在于所有地方。它们不会被辨认出来，因为它们总是以其他形态显现。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1

quotes.text: "The whole thing had been going on for some time before I finally woke up with my objections. Several times earlier I'd awakened also, sat up, and smoked a cigarette. Each time as I lay down again, the material started coming. So the last time I said: 'Now, look, Seth, if you want to take me to some of these probabilities, great; with you leading the way; but my consciousness is having a hell of a time handling whatever it is we're doing.' Then I fell asleep and the material stopped."
quotes.translation: "整个过程持续了一段时间，最后我才带着反对意见醒来。之前我也醒过几次，坐起来抽了根烟。每次我重新躺下时，资料又开始涌现。所以最后一次我说：'听着，赛斯，如果你想带我去看某些可能性，没问题，由你带路；但我的意识在处理我们正在做的事情时简直要疯了。' 然后我睡着了，资料也停止了。"
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1

quotes.text: I will not endeavor to go into this in any detail at this time. We will work within these limitations and see what we can do. The human is not innately limited... the waking state, as I have said often, is as much a trance state as any other state. Here we merely switch the focus of attention to other channels. All that is required is that we switch the attention... consider merely that all types of awareness are trance states... consciousness is the direction in which the self looks.
quotes.translation: 此刻我不打算深入探讨这个问题。我们将在这些限制内工作，看看能做些什么。人类并非天生受限……清醒状态，正如我常说的，与其他任何状态一样，也是一种出神状态。在此我们只是将注意力焦点切换到其他通道。所需要的只是转移注意力……只需考虑所有觉知类型都是出神状态……意识是自我注视的方向。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)

quotes.text: (11:59.) Because that state is also connected with waking life, you also take into it many of the elements of your daily existence, so that your recalled dreams are often cast in fairly conventionalized terms. As a rule you remember the dream's outer veneer, or what it turns into as you approach your usual level of consciousness. In a dream you are basically aware of so many facets of an event that many of them must necessarily escape your waking memory. Yet any real education must take into consideration the learning processes within dreams, and no one can hope to glimpse the nature of the psyche without encouraging dream experience, recall, and the creative use of dream education in waking life.
quotes.translation: （11:59。）由于该状态也与清醒生活相连，你也会将日常存在的许多元素带入其中，因此你回忆起的梦境往往以相当常规化的形式呈现。通常你记得的是梦境的外层表象，或是当你接近惯常意识水平时梦境所转化成的样子。在梦中你基本能意识到一个事件的众多面向，其中许多必然逃过你的清醒记忆。然而任何真正的教育都必须考虑梦中的学习过程，没有人能在不鼓励梦境体验、回忆以及在清醒生活中创造性运用梦境教育的情况下，窥见心灵的本质。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche

quotes.text: The creative impulses are behind your languages, yet often you use the languages to silence rather than free inner communication. There have always been rhythms in consciousness that are not historically obvious. At certain times some behavior has been primarily expressed in the waking state, and sometimes in the dream state. The emphasis is never static, but ever-changing In some periods, then, the normal behavior was "more dreamlike," while more specific developments occurred in the dream state, which was then the more clear or specified of the two. Men went to sleep to do their work, in other words, and the realm of dreams was considered more real than waking reality. Now the opposite is true.
quotes.translation: 创造性的冲动存在于你们的语言背后，然而你们常常用语言来压制而非解放内在沟通。意识中始终存在着历史上并不明显的节奏。在某些时期，某些行为主要表达于清醒状态，有时则表达于梦境状态。重点从未静止，而是不断变化。因此，在某些时期，正常行为"更像梦境"，而更具体的发展则发生在梦境状态中，那时梦境是两者中更清晰或更明确的状态。换句话说，人们入睡去完成工作，梦境领域被认为比清醒现实更真实。现在情况正好相反。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche

quotes.text: It may be a rushing upward. Whichever motion you experience there will be a moment where you feel yourself, your identity and consciousness, definitely withdrawing from the physical organism. Before you attempt the projection, however, the suggestion should be given that the physical organism will be well protected and comfortable. Now when you feel the consciousness withdrawing, there are two things you may do. I suggest the first step I shall give you in preference to the second.
quotes.translation: 它可能是一种向上的急冲。无论你体验到何种运动，都会有一个时刻，你感觉到自己、你的身份和意识，明确地从肉体组织中撤离。然而，在你尝试投射之前，应该给予暗示：肉体组织将得到妥善保护并保持舒适。现在，当你感觉到意识撤离时，你可以做两件事。我建议你优先采用我将给你的第一个步骤，而非第二个。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 6 of The Seth Material (Sessions 240-280)

quotes.text: This makes it highly difficult in a discussion, however, for there is no particular point at which life was inserted into nonliving matter. There is no point at which consciousness emerged. Consciousness is within the tiniest particle, whatever its life conditions seem to be, or however it might seem to lack those conditions you call living.
quotes.translation: 然而，这使得讨论变得极为困难，因为并没有一个特定的点，生命被注入无生命的物质中。也没有一个点，意识突然出现。意识存在于最微小的粒子中，无论其生命状态看似如何，或者它似乎多么缺乏你所称为生命的那些条件。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche

quotes.text: If possible, you should make an effort to conquer the fear involved with levitations, for the fear prevents you from recalling such experiences. Projections of a kind do also occur while the normal waking consciousness is up and about its normal chores. These are rarely remembered: that is, the waking consciousness retains no memory of them.
quotes.translation: 你应尽力克服与漂浮相关的恐惧，因为这种恐惧会阻碍你回忆此类经历。当正常清醒意识忙于日常事务时，某种投射也会发生。这些经历极少被记住：也就是说，清醒意识对此毫无记忆。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)

quotes.text: (9:15.) The earth-tuned consciousness must deal within the space-time context, for only inside this framework can it clearly perceive events. In the dream state consciousness ignores space-time relationships to a large degree, and yet it is still firmly based upon the body's corporeal mechanism. Dreams then are physically experienced. You perceive yourself running, talking, eating, in quite physical activities -- except that they are not performed by the body that lies on the bed.
quotes.translation: （9:15）调频至地球的意识必须在时空框架内运作，唯有在此架构中它才能清晰感知事件。在梦境状态中，意识很大程度上忽略了时空关系，却仍牢固扎根于身体的物质机制。因此梦是身体层面的体验。你感知自己在奔跑、交谈、进食，进行着完全物理性的活动——只是这些活动并非由躺在床上的身体所执行。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality

quotes.text: Now: In one way or another religions have always followed, again, the development of your consciousness, and so they have served its purposes and yours; and they have always reflected, though distorted, those greater inner realities of your being. In historic terms, as you understand them, the "progression" of religion gives you a perfect picture of the development of human consciousness, the differentiation of peoples and nations, and the growth of the ideas of the "individual."
quotes.translation: 现在：无论以何种方式，宗教始终追随着你们意识的发展轨迹，因此它们既服务于意识的目的，也服务于你们的目的；它们始终反映着（尽管经过扭曲）你们存有那些更伟大的内在实相。按你们所理解的历史术语而言，宗教的"演进"为你们呈现了人类意识发展的完整图景、族群与国家的分化过程，以及"个体"概念的成长脉络。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1

quotes.text: Seth's material largely opposes science's mechanistic model of the body wearing down within certain age limits, abetted as that model is by the power of the beliefs that say it will. He had much to say about how the out-of-place stresses we impose upon ourselves through our fearful projections into the future adversely affect our body consciousnesses, which are focused in the present. Telepathy, "molecular mentality," and cellular consciousness are deeply involved in all of this.
quotes.translation: 赛斯资料在很大程度上反对科学界那种认为身体会在特定年龄范围内磨损的机械论模型，而该模型又因那些声称身体会磨损的信念力量而得到助长。他详细阐述了：我们因对未来恐惧投射而施加于自身的、不合时宜的压力，如何对我们聚焦于当下的身体意识产生不利影响。心灵感应、"分子心智"和细胞意识都与此密切相关。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1

quotes.text: This does not mean that an alienation results in those realities -- simply a relationship in which the body and consciousness relate to other events. Only your beliefs, training, and neurological indoctrination prevent you from recognizing the true nature of your consciousness while you sleep. You close out those data. In that period, however, at an inner order of events, you are highly active and do much of the interior mental work that will later appear as physical experience.
quotes.translation: 这并不意味着这些实相会导致疏离——只是身体和意识与其他事件建立了一种关系。唯有你的信念、训练和神经系统的灌输，才阻止你在睡眠时认识到意识的真实本质。你屏蔽了那些数据。然而，在那个时段，在内在事件秩序中，你高度活跃，进行着大量日后将显现为物质体验的内在心智工作。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: (10:26.) You are the multidimensional self who has these existences, who creates and takes part in these cosmic passion plays, so to speak. It is only because you focus in this particular role now that you identify your entire being with it. You have set these rules for yourself for a reason. And consciousness is in a state of becoming, and so this multidimensional self of which I speak is not a psychological structure completed and done with. It is also in a state of becoming.
quotes.translation: （10点26分）你就是那个拥有这些存在形式的多维自性，可以说，你创造并参与了这些宇宙激情剧。只因为你此刻聚焦于这个特定角色，才将整个存有与之等同。你为自己设定这些规则是有原因的。而意识处于"生成"状态，因此我所说的这个多维自性并非一个已完成、已终结的心理结构——它也处于"生成"状态。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: In those terms of which I am speaking, man's identification with nature allowed him to utilize those inner channels. He could send his own consciousness swimming, so to speak, through many currents, in which other kinds of consciousness merged. I said that the language of love was the one basic language, and I mean that quite literally. Man loved nature, identified with its many parts, and added to his own sense of being by joining into its power and identifying with its force.
quotes.translation: 在我所谈论的这些层面中，人类与自然的认同使他能够运用那些内在通道。可以说，他可以让自己的意识游弋于众多潮流之中，而其他种类的意识也融入其中。我曾说过爱的语言是唯一的基本语言，我这话是字面意思。人类热爱自然，认同自然的各个部分，并通过融入自然的力量、认同自然的能量来增强自身的存在感。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche

quotes.text: It is not that physical reality is false. It is that the physical picture is simply one of an infinite number of ways of perceiving the various guises through which consciousness expresses itself. The physical senses force you to translate experience into physical perceptions. The inner senses open your range of perception, allow you to interpret experience in a far freer manner and to create new forms and new channels through which you, or any consciousness, can know itself.
quotes.translation: 并非物质实相是虚假的。而是物质画面只是意识表达自身的无限种感知方式之一。物质感官迫使你将经验转化为物质感知。内在感官则拓展你的感知范围，让你能以更自由的方式诠释经验，并创造新的形式与新的通道，使你自己或任何意识得以认识自身。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: Its own stability and awareness can be vastly deepened and strengthened. In times of seemingly calamitous encounters with nature, individuals may find themselves amazed at their capacity to relate with other people, but in the artificially induced psychic disaster area of massive LSD therapy, the situation is reversed. Consciousness finds itself in a crisis situation; not [because of one coming] from the exterior world, but because it is forced to fight on a battleground for which it was never designed and cannot understand, where basically counted-upon allies of association, memory and organization, and all the powers of the inner self, are suddenly turned into enemies.
quotes.translation: 其自身的稳定性和觉知力可以被大幅深化与强化。在看似与自然灾难性遭遇的时刻，个体可能会惊讶于自己与他人建立联系的能力；但在大规模LSD治疗所人为引发的心理灾难区域中，情况则完全相反。意识发现自己陷入危机状态——并非因为来自外在世界的冲击，而是因为它被迫在一个从未被设计去应对、也无法理解的战场上作战，在那里，原本赖以依靠的联想、记忆、组织能力以及内在自我的所有力量，突然都变成了敌人。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality

quotes.text: Some of these may involve the thoughts of what you would call a reincarnational self, focused in another period of history as you know it. You may instead, "pick up" an event in which a probable self is involved, according to your inclination, your psychic suppleness, your curiosity, your desire for knowledge. In other words, you may become aware of a far greater reality than you now know, use abilities that you do not realize you possess, know beyond all doubt that your own consciousness and identity is independent of the world in which you now focus your primary attention. If all of that were not true, I would not be writing this book and you would not be reading it.
quotes.translation: 其中某些可能涉及你们所谓的转世自我的思想，聚焦在你们所知历史中的另一个时期。你反而可能根据自身倾向、心灵柔韧度、好奇心与求知欲，"捕捉"到涉及某个可能自我的事件。换言之，你可能会意识到比现有所知更宏大的实相，运用你尚未察觉自身拥有的能力，确凿无疑地知晓自己的意识与身份独立于你目前主要聚焦的世界。若非如此，我便不会撰写此书，你也不会阅读它。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: (9:04.) Again, the environment as you think of it is composed of living consciousness. Ancient religions, for example, speak of nature's spirits, and such terms represent memories dating from prehistory. Part of consciousness, then, transformed itself into what you think of as nature--the vast sweep of the continents, the oceans and the rivers, the mountains and the valleys, the body of the land. The creative thrust of the physical world must rise from that living structure.
quotes.translation: （九点零四分。）再者，你们所认为的环境是由活生生的意识所构成。例如，古老宗教谈及自然精灵，这类术语代表着源自史前时期的记忆。因此，部分意识将自己转化为你们所认为的自然——大陆的辽阔延伸、海洋与河流、山脉与峡谷、大地的躯体。物质世界的创造性驱动力必然源自那活生生的结构。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1

quotes.text: The concept, structure, can be used by you in many ways--as a vehicle to carry many differing ideas. A personality communicating with your system, who was once a part of it, can make use of this trace image. The trace image is not his consciousness however. His consciousness is aware of the trace image, and on occasion can utilize it.
quotes.translation: “结构”这个概念可以被你们以多种方式运用——作为一个载体来承载许多不同的理念。一个曾属于你们系统、如今与你们系统沟通的人格，可以利用这个痕迹影像。然而，痕迹影像并非他的意识。他的意识觉察到这个痕迹影像，有时也能运用它。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)

quotes.text: In your terms they did not have [an overall] purpose, yet their purpose was simply to be. Their main points of consciousness were elsewhere, in another kind of reality, while their physical manifestations were separate. Their primary focuses of consciousness were scarcely aware of the bodies they had created. Yet even those bodies learned, in quotes now, "through experience," and began to "awaken," to become aware of themselves, to discover time, or to create it. Period.
quotes.translation: 用你们的说法，他们并没有[整体]目的，然而他们的目的仅仅是“存在”。他们的意识主要焦点在别处，在另一种实相中，而他们的物质显化则是分离的。他们意识的主要焦点几乎不知道他们创造的身体。然而，即使是那些身体，也在引号中“通过经验”学习，并开始“觉醒”，开始意识到自身，发现时间，或者说创造时间。句号。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: If you believe firmly that your consciousness is locked up somewhere inside your skull and is powerless to escape it, if you feel that your consciousness ends at the boundary of your body, then you sell yourself short, and you will think that I am a delusion. I am no more a delusion than you are, and that may be a loaded sentence.
quotes.translation: 如果你坚信自己的意识被锁在头骨里且无力逃脱，如果你觉得意识止于身体的边界，那么你便低估了自己，并会认为我是个幻象。我与你们一样真实不虚，这句话或许意味深长。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: This is a cornerstone for consciousness and for personality development. It is only a first step, however. Without it, no further development of consciousness can occur. This particular step is not attained by all within your system. You are at this point now. This state has been called cosmic consciousness, but it is hardly that.
quotes.translation: 这是意识与人格发展的基石，却仅是第一步。没有它，意识的进一步进化便无从发生。并非你们系统中的所有人都能达到这一步。你们此刻正处在此阶段。这种状态曾被称作宇宙意识，但它远非如此。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)

quotes.text: We must use analogies. It can be thought of as the most exterior projection of the inner ego. When you find yourself, so to speak, watching what you think of as the ego, then you are in contact with this portion of the self. It is aware of both conscious and subconscious motivations and realities, and it is also aware of projections into other fields of actuality. I have hinted before of these matters, for when I say that you will use this other portion of the self to examine waking consciousness and probe beneath it, I already presuppose a you that uses this self. In other words, you are already magnifying the limitations of the self and extending them.
quotes.translation: 我们必须借助类比。它可被视为内在自我最外层的投射。当你仿佛在旁观自己所谓的"自我"时，你便与自我的这一部分建立了联系。它既觉察意识与潜意识的动机与实相，也觉察向其他实相领域的投射。我此前已暗示过这些事——当我说你将运用自我的这一其他部分来检视清醒意识并深入其下时，我已预设存在一个运用此自我的"你"。换言之，你正在放大自我的局限性并拓展其边界。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)

quotes.text: A-5 opens up a dimension in which the vital consciousness of any personality can at least theoretically be contacted. This involves communicating not only with past personalities in your terms, but future ones. It is a level of consciousness very seldom reached. It is not, for example, the layer used by most mediums. It is a meeting ground in which personalities from any time or place or probable system can communicate with each other in clear terms understood by all.
quotes.translation: A-5开启了一个维度，在其中任何人格的生命意识至少理论上可以被接触。这不仅涉及与你们所谓的过去人格沟通，也包括未来人格。这是一个极少达到的意识层次。例如，它并非大多数灵媒所使用的层面。它是一个交汇场域，任何时间、地点或可能系统中的所有人格都能以彼此清晰理解的明确语言相互沟通。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: I am not saying that the universe is the result of some "psychological machine," either, but that each portion of consciousness is a part of All That Is, and that the universe falls together in a spontaneous, divine order (intently)--and that each portion of consciousness carries within it indelibly the knowledge of the whole.
quotes.translation: 我也并非说宇宙是某种"心理机器"的结果，而是说每个意识片段都是"一切万有"的一部分，宇宙以自发、神圣的秩序（专注地）聚合而成——并且每个意识片段内在都不可磨灭地携带着整体的知识。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1

quotes.text: Now. Some of Fox's suggestions are excellent. They will allow you to take the waking consciousness along with you. As your abilities develop and as you become more accustomed to the experience, the waking consciousness will recall more and more, and not become frightened. When it becomes frightened you see it forces the whole experience to end. If it were not taken along the same experience might have continued, and greater benefits been achieved for the whole self.
quotes.translation: 现在。福克斯的一些建议非常出色。它们将允许你带着清醒意识一同前行。随着你能力的发展，以及你越来越习惯于这种体验，清醒意识将回忆起越来越多的事物，并且不会感到恐惧。当它感到恐惧时，你看，它会迫使整个体验终止。如果它没有被带着同行，同样的体验本可以继续下去，并为整个自我带来更大的益处。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 6 of The Seth Material (Sessions 240-280)

quotes.text: (10:28.) Because of your habits of an extended sleep period, followed by an extended waking period, you do not take advantage of these rhythms of consciousness. The high peaks are to some extent smothered, or even go unnoticed. The sharp contrasts and the high efficiency of the natural waking consciousness is barely utilized.
quotes.translation: （十点二十八分。）由于你们习惯长时间睡眠后再长时间清醒，你们并未善用这些意识节奏。意识的高峰期在某种程度上被压抑，甚至未被察觉。自然清醒意识所具有的鲜明对比与高效能几乎未被充分利用。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: You are aware, alert, and participating in many more realities than you know as your soul expresses itself through you. That consciousness of your usual daylight hours, the ego consciousness, rises up like a flower from the ground of the "underneath," the unconscious bed of your own reality. Though you are not aware of it, this ego itself emerges, then falls back again into the unconscious, from which another ego then rises as a new bloom from the springtime earth.
quotes.translation: 当你的灵魂通过你表达自身时，你正觉察、警觉并参与着远比你认知中更多的实相。你日常白昼的意识——即自我意识——如同花朵般从"底层"的土壤中升起，这底层正是你自身实相的无意识基底。尽管你未曾察觉，这个自我本身会浮现，随后又沉回无意识之中，接着另一个自我会如春日大地绽放的新花般再度升起。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality

quotes.text: It is quite as true to say that the planets behave in a certain way because you are what you are, as it is to turn the statement around, as is generally done. The very positions of the planets and the stars are effects of the senses -- perceptions that would have no meaning were it not for your own kind of consciousness. Those perceptions, then, cannot cause you to behave in any given way because of conditions that have no meaning outside of your own consciousness.
quotes.translation: 说行星以某种方式运行是因为你之所是，与通常所说的反向陈述同样真实。行星与恒星的特定位置本身就是感官的效应——若非因你这类意识的存在，这些感知将毫无意义。因此，这些感知无法因你自身意识之外毫无意义的条件，而迫使你以任何特定方式行事。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: You are not attempting a projection here. You are following levels of your own consciousness until you discover yourself in an environment of which the ordinary waking consciousness had been previously unaware. This is a relatively difficult feat, for the waking consciousness must not be shut off, or you defeat your purpose.
quotes.translation: 你在此并非试图进行投射。你是在追随自己意识的层次，直到你发现自己置身于一个先前普通清醒意识未曾察觉的环境中。这是一项相对困难的任务，因为清醒意识不能被关闭，否则你就违背了自己的目的。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)

quotes.text: First of all, to me the term "unconscious" is a poor one, barely hinting at an actual open psychic system, with deep intertwining roots uniting all kinds of consciousness; a network in which we are all connected. Our individuality rises out of it, but also helps form it. This source contains past, present, and future information; only the ego experiencing time as we know it. I also believe that this open system contains other kinds of consciousness beside our own.
quotes.translation: 首先，对我来说，“无意识”这个术语很糟糕，它几乎无法暗示一个实际开放的心灵系统，这个系统有着深深交织的根，连接着各种意识；这是一个我们都相互连接的网络。我们的个体性从中升起，但也帮助形成了它。这个源头包含过去、现在和未来的信息；只有自我在经历我们所知的时间。我也相信，这个开放系统除了我们自己的意识之外，还包含其他种类的意识。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: You see physical bodies and suppose that any personality must appear in physical terms. None of you are physical; that which is enduring is not physical. In that respect at least you are no different than I. Consciousness is the force behind matter, and it forms many other realities beside the physical one. It is only because your own viewpoint is presently so limited that it seems to you that physical reality is the rule and the mode and the purpose of existence.
quotes.translation: 你看到物质身体，便以为任何人格都必须以物质形式显现。你们中没有一个人是物质的；那持久不变的并非物质。至少在这方面，你与我没有不同。意识是物质背后的力量，它除了物质实相之外，还形成了许多其他实相。只是因为你们目前的观点如此受限，才让你们觉得物质实相是存在的规则、模式与目的。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)

quotes.text: Ruburt instinctively likes your tree painting. It represents a certain state of consciousness -- an in-between threshold dimension of awareness, in which the imagination and the senses are almost caught in the act of putting an object together, or of bringing the world into a sensed reality, brand-new, from the realm of the inner mind: a very evocative state of consciousness, and one that as I believe Ruburt mentioned, you could also use in connection with faces.
quotes.translation: 鲁柏本能地喜欢你那幅树的画作。它代表某种意识状态——一种介于中间的阈限觉知维度，在此维度中，想象力与感官几乎被捕捉到正在将物体组合成形，或正将世界从内在心灵的领域带入崭新的感官实相：这是一种极具唤起力的意识状态，我相信鲁柏也提过，你同样可以将其运用在面容的描绘上。
quotes.book: The Magical Approach

quotes.text: Now: It is well known that fluctuations of consciousness and alertness exist in the sleep state. Some periods of dream activity do indeed supersede those of some waking states. But there are also fluctuations in normal waking consciousness, rhythms of intense activity followed by a much less active period of consciousness.
quotes.translation: 现在：众所周知，睡眠状态中存在意识与警觉性的波动。某些梦境活动的强度确实超越了某些清醒状态。但在正常的清醒意识中同样存在波动，即强烈活动期之后紧跟着意识活动较弱的时期。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: It does not fear destruction however, as the ego does. It still fights for survival, of course; but the consciousness of plant life involves a consciousness of self as it operates within action. It sees or feels itself as a part of continuing action, and because of this inner atomic knowledge it does not fear destruction, basically, knowing that it will be changed into other kinds of action.
quotes.translation: 然而，它不像自我那样惧怕毁灭。它当然仍为生存而战；但植物生命的意识包含一种在行动中运作的自我意识。它将自己视为持续行动的一部分，并因这内在的原子知识而基本不惧怕毁灭，深知自身将转化为其他形式的行动。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)

quotes.text: Self-consciousness entered in very shortly after, but not what you are pleased to call human self-consciousness. I certainly do not like to wound your egos in this manner. However the fact remains, and I can hear you all yell foul, that there is no actual differentiation between the various types of self-consciousness.
quotes.translation: 自我意识随后很快出现，但并非你们所谓的人类自我意识。我当然不愿以这种方式伤害你们的自尊。然而事实是——我能听到你们都在大喊不公——各种自我意识之间其实并无实质区别。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)

quotes.text: Give us a moment ... The universe is seeded with various kinds of consciousnesses. Some of these appear to you as planets or stars,8 as they "intrude" into your field of actuality. As such they appear to behave in a certain fashion, to take a certain form, to have certain effects. You and the stars are simultaneous events, each conscious and aware but in different "scales" of actuality -- as your scale of consciousness is different from that of the violets.
quotes.translation: 请稍等片刻……宇宙中播撒着各种类型的意识。其中一些以行星或恒星的形式呈现在你们面前，因为它们"侵入"了你们的实相场域。它们以特定方式运作、呈现特定形态、产生特定效应。你们与星辰是同时发生的事件，各自具有觉知与意识，只是处于不同的实相"尺度"——正如你们意识的尺度与紫罗兰的尺度不同。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: This point is extremely important. As you know, animals dream. What you do not know is that all consciousness dream. We have said that to some degree even atoms and molecules have consciousness, and this minute consciousness nevertheless forms its own dreams, even as on the other hand it forms its own physical image.
quotes.translation: 这一点极为重要。如你所知，动物会做梦。但你所不知道的是，所有意识都会做梦。我们曾说过，甚至原子和分子在某种程度上也拥有意识，而这种微小的意识同样会形成自己的梦境，正如它另一方面也形成自己的物质形象。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)

quotes.text: Normal waking consciousness weaves in and out of this infinite supportive webwork. Your inner experience is so intricate that verbally it is almost impossible to describe. Normal waking consciousness, while having memory of itself, obviously does not retain all memory all of the time. It is said that memory of past events drops back into the subconscious. It is still intensely alive, and by alive I mean living and active, although you do not focus upon it.
quotes.translation: 正常的清醒意识在这张无限支撑的网络中穿梭往来。你的内在体验如此复杂精妙，以至于几乎无法用语言描述。正常的清醒意识虽然拥有对自身的记忆，但显然并非始终保留全部记忆。据说对过往事件的记忆会沉入潜意识中，它们依然充满活力——我所说的"活力"是指鲜活且活跃的状态，尽管你并未聚焦于它。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: This human self-consciousness existed in psychological time and in inner time long before you as a species constructed it in terms of your particular camouflage patterns. For your friend's sake I will simplify this, saying that human consciousness was inherent and latent from the beginning of your physical universe.
quotes.translation: 这种人类自我意识存在于心理时间和内在时间中，远早于你们这个物种以特定伪装模式构建它之前。为了你朋友的理解，我将简化说明：人类意识从你们物质宇宙诞生之初就已存在并潜藏其中。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)

quotes.text: Beneath these, so to speak, the CU's (or units of consciousness) are aware of the different kinds of consciousness of which they are part. By their nature certain kinds of organization, behavior, and experimentation exclude other quite-as-valid but different approaches. The CU's, in their freewheeling nature beneath all matter, are acquainted with all such organizations, so that some of the lessons learned by one species are indeed transferred to another.
quotes.translation: 在这些之下，可以说，CU（或意识单位）意识到它们所属的不同种类的意识。由于其本质，某些组织形式、行为方式和实验方法会排除其他同样有效但不同的途径。CU在其自由流动的本质中，存在于所有物质之下，熟悉所有这些组织，因此一个物种学到的一些教训确实会传递给另一个物种。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1

quotes.text: In those early times, then, consciousness was more mobile. Identity was more democratic. In a strange fashion this does not mean that individuality was weaker. Instead it was strong enough to accept within its confines many divergent kinds of experience. A person, then, looking out into the world of trees, waters and rock, wildlife and vegetation, literally felt that he or she was looking at the larger, materialized, subjective areas of personal selfhood.
quotes.translation: 在那些早期时代，意识更具流动性。身份认同更具民主性。奇怪的是，这并不意味着个体性更弱。相反，它足够强大，能够在其范围内接纳许多不同种类的经验。因此，一个人望向树木、水域和岩石、野生动物和植被的世界时，会真切地感到他或她正在看着个人自我更宏大、物质化的主观领域。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche

quotes.text: Remember this is an analogy, so that the word "instant" should not be taken too literally. There is, then, what we can call an underside of consciousness. Now, in the same way, atoms and molecules exist so that they are "dead," or inactive within your system, then alive or active, but you cannot perceive the instant in which they do not exist. Since your bodies and your entire physical universe are composed of atoms and molecules, then I am telling you that the entire structure exists in the same manner. It flickers off and on, in other words, and in a certain rhythm, as, say, the rhythm of breath.
quotes.translation: 记住这只是一个类比，所以“瞬间”这个词不应被过于字面地理解。那么，存在一个我们可以称之为意识底层的部分。同样地，原子和分子以这样的方式存在：它们在你们的系统中是“死的”或不活跃的，然后又是活的或活跃的，但你们无法感知它们不存在的那个瞬间。由于你们的身体和整个物质宇宙都由原子和分子组成，那么我告诉你们，整个结构也以同样的方式存在。换句话说，它以一种特定的节奏闪烁明灭，就像呼吸的节奏一样。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: The inner senses, though I have in the past described them by separating their functions and characteristics, basically operate together in such a way that in your terms it would be highly difficult to separate one from the others. They function with a perfect spontaneous order, aware of all synchronicities. In that psychological universe, then, it is possible for entities "to be everywhere at once," aware of everything at once. Your world is composed of such "entities"--the units of consciousness that form your body. The kinds of conscious minds that you have cannot hold that kind of information.
quotes.translation: 内在感官虽然我过去曾通过区分其功能与特性来描述它们，但基本上它们是共同运作的，以你们的时间观念而言，极难将其中之一与其他感官完全分离。它们以完美的自发秩序运作，觉察所有同时性事件。因此，在那个心理宇宙中，存有可以"同时存在于各处"，同时觉察一切。你们的世界正是由这样的"存有"——构成你们身体的意识单位——所组成。你们所拥有的那种意识心智无法容纳这类信息。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1

quotes.text: In a situation such as your father's or Miss Callahan's, the overall consciousness is not less, but less of it expresses itself in physical terms. It either has less and less control over the organism because it has not solved its problems well enough, because it has already largely decided to leave the system, but does so gradually; or because in some instances there is a psychic block, which prevents full utilization of energy in this important regard.
quotes.translation: 在如你父亲或卡拉汉小姐这类情况下，整体意识并未减少，只是以物质形式表达的部分减少了。它要么因未能妥善解决自身问题而对有机体的控制力越来越弱，要么因已大体决定离开这个系统却采取渐进方式；要么在某些案例中存在心理障碍，阻止了能量在此重要层面的充分运用。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)

quotes.text: Verbalization is not a basic method of communication for in quotes "higher" forms of consciousness, nor is it for lower forms of consciousness. It is only at your particular intermediate state that verbalization as such is so important. It is a main basis for your species. Because of its importance at your particular stage it is an excellent method for our purposes. In many other systems of reality it is never adopted, for it is in many ways restrictive.
quotes.translation: 语言表达并非所谓"较高"意识形态的基本沟通方式，对较低意识形态而言亦然。唯有在你们特定的中间阶段，语言表达才显得如此重要。它是你们物种的主要基础。由于它在你们特定阶段的重要性，它成为我们达成目的之绝佳方法。在许多其他实相系统中，语言表达从未被采用，因为它在许多方面具有限制性。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)

quotes.text: 3. Seth packed a lot of information into the short 689th session for Volume 1. He discussed the innumerable experiments of consciousness with animal-man and man-animal forms; the great communication between man and animal in ancient times, and the deep rapport of both with their natural heritage; psychic and biological blueprints and cellular precognition; the growth of man's ego consciousness; the beginnings of our god concepts and mythology; and more.
quotes.translation: 3. 赛斯在第一册第689节简短课程中传递了大量信息。他探讨了意识在动物-人类与人类-动物形态中的无数实验；远古时期人类与动物间的伟大沟通，以及两者与自然传承的深刻共鸣；心灵与生物蓝图及细胞预知能力；人类自我意识的发展；我们神祇概念与神话的起源；以及更多内容。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: I have a few comments to make concerning the article on dreams, that you read in the paper. Maturity has nothing to do with the meaning of the reptiles and mammals mentioned as dream images. There is generic imprint stamped within the cells, and at various levels of cell consciousness. These are reactivated. The reptilian images do not represent maturity nor immaturity, but are simply designations natural to a particular level of cellular consciousness.
quotes.translation: 关于你在报上读到的那篇论梦的文章，我有几点评论。成熟与文中提到的爬虫类和哺乳类作为梦境意象的意义毫无关联。细胞内部及细胞意识的不同层次都印刻着物种的印记。这些印记会被重新激活。爬虫类意象并不代表成熟或不成熟，而只是细胞意识特定层次的自然标识。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)

quotes.text: Animals have varying degrees of self-consciousness, as indeed people do. The consciousness that is within them is as valid and eternal as your own, however. There is nothing to prevent a personality from investing a portion of his own energy into an animal form. This is not transmigration of souls. It does not mean that a man can be reincarnated in an animal. It does mean that personalities can send a portion of their energy into various kinds of form.
quotes.translation: 动物拥有不同程度的自我意识，正如人类一样。然而，它们内在的意识与你的意识同样真实且永恒。没有任何事物能阻止一个人格将其部分能量注入动物形态。这不是灵魂转世。这并不意味着人能够转生为动物。它确实意味着人格能够将其部分能量送入各种形态之中。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: Consciousness is not basically built upon those precepts of good and evil that so presently concern you. By inference, neither is a soul. This does not mean that in your system, and in some others, these problems do not exist and that good is not preferable to the evil. It simply means that the soul knows that good and evil are but different manifestations of a far greater reality.
quotes.translation: 意识本质上并非建立在你们当前所关注的善恶教条之上。由此推论，灵魂亦非如此。这并不意味着在你们的系统及其他某些系统中，这些问题不存在，也不意味着善不比恶更可取。这只是意味着灵魂知晓善与恶不过是某个更伟大实相的不同显现。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: (9:50.) Now, there are no closed systems in reality. In your physical system the nature of your perceptions limits your idea of reality to some extent, because you purposely decide to focus within a given "locale." But basically speaking, consciousness can never be a closed system, and all barriers of such a nature are illusion. Therefore the soul itself is not a closed system. When you consider the soul, however, you usually think of it in such a light -- unchanging, a psychic or spiritual citadel. But citadels not only keep out invaders, they also prevent expansion and development.
quotes.translation: （九点五十分。）现在，实相中不存在封闭的系统。在你们的物质系统中，感知的本质在某种程度上限制了你们对实相的观念，因为你们刻意决定聚焦于某个特定的"区域"。但基本上来说，意识永远不可能是一个封闭的系统，所有这类性质的障碍都是幻相。因此，灵魂本身也不是一个封闭的系统。然而，当你们思考灵魂时，通常都会以这样的角度来理解它——不变、一个心灵或精神的堡垒。但堡垒不仅阻挡入侵者，也阻碍了扩展与发展。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: Everything that you experience has consciousness, and each consciousness is endowed with its own feeling-tone. There is great cooperation involved in the formation of the earth as you think of it, and so the individual living structures of the planet rise up from the feeling-tone within each atom and molecule.
quotes.translation: 你们所体验的一切都具有意识，每个意识都被赋予了它自己的感觉基调。在你们所认为的地球形成过程中，存在着极大的合作，因此这个星球上各个活生生的结构，都是从每个原子和分子内的感觉基调中升起的。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality

quotes.text: (She hasn't undergone a classical religious conversion of the kind William James describes in his The Varieties of Religious Experience,4 yet more than once she's known her own forms of ecstasy, or deep alteration of consciousness, or illumination -- whatever one chooses to call such states. From two perspectives she rather briefly describes one such episode, which actually lasted several hours, in her Dialogues, and in Adventures in Consciousness.5
quotes.translation: （她并没有经历过威廉·詹姆斯在《宗教经验之种种》中所描述的那种古典宗教皈依，但她不止一次体验过自己的狂喜形式、意识的深度改变或灵光乍现——无论人们如何称呼这类状态。她在《对话录》和《意识探险》中，从两个角度相当简短地描述了其中一次持续数小时的经历。）
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1

quotes.text: Nothing is seen directly. You are always forming such constructions, and the methods by which this is accomplished are the same regardless of the nature of the consciousness which is to be perceived in physical terms. In all cases, if the ego consciousness is to be reached, some sense imagery is necessary.
quotes.translation: 没有任何事物是被直接看见的。你总是在构建这样的结构，而完成这些构建的方法是一致的，无论要以物质方式被感知的意识本质为何。在所有情况下，若要触及自我意识，某种感官意象是必要的。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 6 of The Seth Material (Sessions 240-280)

quotes.text: If you find severe errors of judgment, you may then correct them. You may perfect, in other words, but you cannot again enter into that frame of reference as a completely participating consciousness following, say, the historic trends of the time, joining into the mass-hallucinated existence that resulted from the applied consciousness of your self and your "contemporaries."
quotes.translation: 若你发现严重的判断错误，便可加以修正。换言之，你可以完善，但无法再次以完全参与的意识进入那个参考架构——比如追随当时的历史潮流，加入由你自身及"同时代人"的应用意识所引发的群体幻觉性存在。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: Hypnosis is one method of examining dreams. Our method is perhaps the best one. The inner self constantly changes its focus. I have said that consciousness is merely the direction in which the inner self focuses at any particular time. In order to examine the reality of dreams it is necessary to change the focus of the inner self to those directions in which the inner self moves, when the ego does not limit its scope to camouflage reality only.
quotes.translation: 催眠是检视梦境的方法之一。我们的方法或许是最佳途径。内在自我不断改变其焦点。我曾说过，意识不过是内在自我在特定时刻聚焦的方向。若要检视梦境的实相，必须将内在自我的焦点转向那些当自我不再将其范围局限于伪装实相时，内在自我所移动的方向。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)

quotes.text: When dream elements combine to cloud a projection, this is the work of a certain level of consciousness, trying to interpret the strange conditions. This usually occurs at certain levels where consciousness is not yet completely free of the physical body. Some of the dream elements will be thought forms.
quotes.translation: 当梦境元素结合成遮蔽投射的云雾时，这是特定意识层次试图解读陌生状况的运作。这通常发生在意识尚未完全脱离肉体的某些层次，部分梦境元素会是思想形相。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)

quotes.text: In other words, the environment, conditions, and methods of perception will not be alien. You are not suddenly thrust into an unknown; that unknown is a part of you now. It was a part of you before this physical birth, and will be after physical death. These conditions, however, have been blotted out of your consciousness in the main, throughout physical history. Mankind has had various conceptions of his own reality but he has purposely, it seems, turned away from it in the last century. There are many reasons for this, and we will try to cover some of them.
quotes.translation: 换言之，环境、状况与感知方式并不会显得陌生。你并非突然被抛入未知领域——那未知本就是你现在的一部分，在本次肉身诞生前已是如此，在肉身死亡后仍将如是。然而这些状况在整个人类历史中，大体上已被你的意识抹除。人类对自身实相有过各种认知，但上个世纪却刻意回避了它。这背后有许多原因，我们将尝试探讨其中几点。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: Certain kinds of pain automatically eject consciousness from the body. Such pain cannot be verbalized, for it is a mixture of pain and pleasure, a tearing free, and it automatically brings about an almost exhilarating release of consciousness. Such pain is also very brief. Under your present system, however, drugs are usually administered, in which case pain is somewhat minimized, but prolonged -- not triggering the natural release mechanisms.
quotes.translation: 某些类型的痛苦会自动将意识逐出身体。这种痛苦无法用语言描述，因为它是痛苦与愉悦的混合体，一种撕裂般的释放，并会自然引发近乎狂喜的意识解脱。这类痛苦也极为短暂。然而在你们现有的体系下，通常会被施以药物，此时痛苦虽稍有减轻，却会延长——无法触发自然的释放机制。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche

quotes.text: While this may sound quite sacrilegious scientifically, it is possible to understand the electron's nature and greater reality by using certain focuses of consciousness: by probing the electron, for example, with a "laser" [beam] of consciousness finely focused and attuned -- and more will be said about this later in the book. So far in any of your investigations, you have been probing exterior conditions, searching for their interior nature.
quotes.translation: 虽然这在科学上听起来颇为亵渎，但通过运用特定的意识焦点——例如以高度聚焦调谐的"意识激光"探入电子——确实可能理解电子的本质与更广大的实相。本书后文将对此详述。在你们迄今的任何研究中，你们始终在探查外在条件，追寻其内在本质。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1

quotes.text: There may or may not be disorientation on your part, according to your beliefs and development. Now I do not necessarily mean intellectual development.
