The intellect should go hand in hand with the emotions and intuitions, but if it pulls against these too strongly, difficulties can arise when the newly freed consciousness seizes upon its ideas about reality after death, rather than facing the particular reality in which it finds itself. It can deny feeling, in other words, and even attempt to argue itself out of its present independence from the body.
quotes.translation: 根据你们的信念与进展，你可能会经历或不会经历迷失感。此处我并非特指智力发展。智力应与情感及直觉并驾齐驱，但若它过于强烈地对抗后两者，当新获自由的意识执着于死后实相的观念，而非直面自身所处的特定实相时，困难便可能产生。换言之，它可能否认感受，甚至试图通过争辩来否定自己当前脱离肉身的独立状态。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: Your visitor would then be forced to translate that information as best he could through his own native structure, if it were to make any sense to his consciousness in its usual orientation. All realities are the result of certain unique focuses taken by consciousness, therefore. In those terms, there is no outside. The effects of objectivity are caused as the psyche projects its experience into inner dimensions that it has itself created.
quotes.translation: 若要让访客的意识在其惯常取向中理解这些信息，他必须尽可能透过自身原生结构来转译。因此，所有实相都是意识采取特定焦点后的产物。就这些术语而言，并不存在"外在"。客观性的效应，源于心灵将其经验投射到自身创造的内在维度中。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: Science, however, seeing the body as a mechanism, has promoted the idea that consciousness is trapped within a mechanical model, that man's suffering is mechanically caused in that regard: You simply give the machine some better parts and all will be well (amused). Science also operates as magic, of course, so on some occasions the belief in science itself will seemingly work miracles: The new heart will give a man new heart, for example.
quotes.translation: 然而，科学将身体视为机械装置，因而助长了"意识被困在机械模型中"的认知——人类的痛苦在机械层面被归因于此：只要给机器换些更好的零件，一切就会好转（带着戏谑）。当然，科学本身也如魔法般运作，因此某些时候对科学的信仰本身似乎能创造奇迹：例如，新心脏确实能赋予人新的心力。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1

quotes.text: The awareness is focused along certain lines. The energy is likewise focused. Much of the ability again is suspended as for a subject in a trance, but consciousness is present. Your hybrid plants merely demonstrate this susceptibility to new suggestion which your plant, like your susceptible trance subject, will gladly follow. I will have more to say along this road of thought, but am detoured for just a moment as to which fork to follow.
quotes.translation: 觉知沿着特定路线集中，能量也同样集中。许多能力再次被悬置，如同处于恍惚状态的受试者，但意识依然存在。你们的杂交植物只是展示了这种对新暗示的易感性——你们的植物，就像容易接受暗示的恍惚受试者一样，会欣然遵从。我将在这一思路中进一步阐述，但此刻我稍作迂回，以决定该追随哪条岔路。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)

quotes.text: Your ego is now focused within this reality. You have other egos focused in other realities. The inner self is aware of what is being done in all of these realities. There is some compensation, and the race knows this. Ruburt wrote that there were clumps of consciousness, and of course he is correct. You are a part of many such clumps of consciousness. The abilities and potentials are not only being developed in this system but in others.
quotes.translation: 你们的人格自我如今聚焦于这个实相。你们还有其他的人格自我聚焦于其他实相。内在自己知晓所有这些实相中所进行的一切。存在某种补偿，而种族明白这一点。鲁柏曾写道存在意识团块，他当然是对的。你们是许多此类意识团块的一部分。能力与潜能不仅在此系统中发展，也在其他系统中发展。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)

quotes.text: Inherent, and I repeat inherent in the spider as in man, is the complete comprehension, or rather comprehension through direct experience, of the universe as a whole. In its particular existence the spider is not aware of all this knowledge, but it uses what is necessary of it to construct its web. It experiences directly. There is of course no "I" consciousness, but there is direct consciousness, nevertheless, of the most intimate kind.
quotes.translation: 蜘蛛与人类一样，天生——我重复一遍，天生——就具备对宇宙整体的完全理解，或者更确切地说，是通过直接经验获得的理解。蜘蛛在其特定存在中并未意识到所有这些知识，但它会运用其中必要的部分来编织蛛网。它直接体验着一切。当然，这里没有"我"的意识，但确实存在着最亲密层次的直接意识。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)

quotes.text: Earlier I said that you are only familiar with those characteristics of your own consciousness that you use through the mediumship of the body. You rely upon the body to express the perceptions of your consciousness. You tend, again, to identify the expression of your consciousness with the body.
quotes.translation: 我先前说过，你只熟悉自己意识中那些透过身体媒介所运用的特质。你依赖身体来表达意识的感知。你往往再次将意识的表达等同于身体本身。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: The same applies to your eating habits. By turn you overstuff and then starve the tissues. This has definite effects upon the nature of your consciousness, your creativity, your degree of concentration. Along these lines, for example, you do literally starve your bodies at night, and add to the aging of your bodies by denying them food throughout those long hours. All of this reflects upon the strength and nature of your consciousness.
quotes.translation: 同样的道理也适用于你的饮食习惯。你时而暴饮暴食，时而让组织挨饿。这对你意识的性质、创造力、专注程度都有明确影响。例如，你确实在夜间让身体挨饿，通过整夜不进食来加速身体老化。这一切都反映在你意识的力量与性质上。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: There may seem to you to be no logic to these inner pictures, and certainly no connection between them and what you were thinking a moment before, or even an hour before. To some extent they seem disconnected from you and not of your doing. Often, however, they represent the characteristics shown by consciousness when it is somewhat turned away from physical stimuli. The form of symbols is changed as the states of consciousness change.
quotes.translation: 这些内在画面在你看来可能毫无逻辑可言，显然与你片刻前甚至一小时前的想法毫无关联。某种程度上它们似乎与你脱节，并非出自你的作为。然而，这些画面往往展现了意识在略微脱离物质刺激时所呈现的特质。随着意识状态的改变，象征的形式也随之变化。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: (9:25.) The states of consciousness merge also one into the other, and it is obvious that I am using the terms of depth to make the discussion easier. Starting with the ego or waking consciousness as the outer self focused toward exterior reality, these states are broad, more like plains to be explored. Each one, therefore, opens in great adjacent areas also, and there are many "paths" to be taken according to your interest and desire.
quotes.translation: （九点二十五分）意识的各种状态也彼此交融，显然我使用“深度”这类术语是为了让讨论更易懂。从作为聚焦外在现实的外向自我的自我意识或清醒意识开始，这些状态是广阔的，更像是待探索的平原。因此，每个状态也向邻近的广大区域敞开，根据你的兴趣和渴望，有许多“路径”可供选择。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: Consciousness is far more mobile than you realize. Operationally, you have focused yours primarily with the body. You cannot experience subjective behavior "from outside," so this natural mobility of consciousness, which for example the animals have retained, is psychologically invisible to you.
quotes.translation: 意识远比你所认知的更具流动性。在运作上，你主要将意识聚焦于身体。你无法“从外部”体验主观行为，因此这种意识的自然流动性——例如动物仍保留着——在心理层面对你而言是不可见的。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche

quotes.text: Basically there are no real divisions to the self, but for the sake of explanation we must speak of them in those terms. First of all you had the inner self, the creative dreaming self--composed, again, of units of consciousness, awareized energy that forms your identity, and that formed the identities of the earliest earth inhabitants. These inner selves formed their own dream bodies about them, as previously explained, but the dream bodies did not have to have physical reactions. They were free of gravity and space, and of time.
quotes.translation: 基本上，自我并无真正的分割，但为了解释之便，我们必须用这些术语来谈论。首先，你有内在自我、创造性的做梦自我——同样由意识单位组成，即形成你身份的意识化能量，也形成了最早地球居民的身份。这些内在自我围绕自身形成了它们的梦体，如先前所解释的，但梦体无需有物理反应。它们不受重力、空间和时间的束缚。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1

quotes.text: The consciousness will expand on your planet, just as it expands for those who go beyond your plane. The scope of consciousness will be so broadened in the future that all personality fragments, split personality images, and even individual fragments in succeeding incarnations, will be held in clear focus without strain. It is toward this that evolution on the earthly plane is headed, though of course with its usual donkey-slow rate.
quotes.translation: 意识将在你们星球上扩展，正如它为那些超越你们层面者所扩展的一样。未来意识的范畴将如此宽广，以至于所有的人格片段、分裂人格意象，甚至后续转世中的个体片段，都将被清晰聚焦而毫不费力。地球层面的进化正是朝着这个方向前进，尽管自然是以其惯常的驴步缓慢速度。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)

quotes.text: Again, the term God is used simply because it is an accepted term for the reality we are discussing. That which knows itself, which experiences itself within many forms and yet knows itself as something apart from the total of its sums, that left-over, unexplainable remnant you see, can be thought of as original action, original consciousness, prime mover, or consciousness as distinct from its own creations, of which it is also part.
quotes.translation: 再次说明，使用"神"这个术语，仅仅因为它是我们讨论之实相的一个公认称谓。那个认识自身、在众多形式中体验自身、却又知晓自身是超越其总和之物——你们看，那剩余的、无法解释的残余——可被视为原初行动、原初意识、原动者，或与其自身创造物（它也是其中一部分）有所区别的意识。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)

quotes.text: (10:00.) Give us a moment ... Cells compose natural forms. An identity is not a thing of a certain size or shape that must always appear in one given way. It is a unit of consciousness ever itself and inviolate while still free to form other organizations, enter other combinations in which all other units also decide to play a part. As there are different shapes to physical objects, then, so identity can take different shapes -- and basically those forms are far more rich and diverse than the variety of physical objects.
quotes.translation: （10:00。）请稍等片刻……细胞构成自然形态。身份并非某种固定大小或形状、必须始终以特定方式呈现的事物。它是一个永远保持自身完整且不可侵犯的意识单位，同时仍能自由形成其他组织，进入其他组合——在这些组合中，所有其他单位也决定参与其中。正如物质对象有不同形状，身份也能呈现不同形态——而从根本上说，这些形态远比物质对象的多样性更为丰富多元。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: Now, my dear friends, your self-consciousness is the self-consciousness of the ego which you know, and which you consider . But where this self -conscious self ends, another self-conscious self begins. The two selves, being self-conscious selves, cannot be aware of any reality but their own.
quotes.translation: 现在，我亲爱的朋友们，你们的自我意识是你们所熟知并视为理所当然的自我意识。但在这个自我意识结束之处，另一个自我意识开始了。这两个自我意识作为自我意识，除了自身之外无法感知任何其他实相。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 5 of The Seth Material (Sessions 199-239)

quotes.text: It is very difficult to try to assign anything like human motivation to All That Is. I can only say that it is possessed by "the need" to lovingly create from its own being; to lovingly transform its own reality in such a way that each most slight probable consciousness can come to be (long pause); and with the need to see that any and all possible orchestrations of consciousness have the chance to emerge, to perceive and to love.
quotes.translation: 试图将类似人类动机的东西赋予"一切万有"是非常困难的。我只能说，它被"需要"所占据——需要从自身存在中充满爱意地创造；需要以如此方式充满爱意地转化自身实相，使每个最微小的可能意识都能得以存在（长停顿）；并且需要确保任何及所有可能的意识编排都有机会涌现、感知与去爱。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1

quotes.text: Imagine if you can the figures or inhabitants in the painting having psychological reality, all within the set limits prescribed by the given space. Imagine in other words consciousness, growth, reality and expansion, having nothing to do with expansion of space in your terms, but an almost complete freedom of psychological realities, and you will come at least within the realm of understanding what I mean by an expanding universe that has nothing to do with the expanding universe of which your scientists speak.
quotes.translation: 请试想，画中的人物或居民具有心理实相，全数存在于特定空间所设定的界限之内。换言之，请想象意识、成长、实相与扩张，皆与你们所谓的空间扩张无关，而是心理实相近乎完全的自由。如此，你至少能稍稍理解我所说的"扩张宇宙"——这与你们科学家所谈论的扩张宇宙毫无关联。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)

quotes.text: The ego may assimilate only a part of a given experience. Sometimes it will not assimilate or accept an experience at all. Remember here again that there is a difference between the ego and consciousness of self. It is not necessary that the ego assimilate all experiences that are open to consciousness of self. ego must have at hand, however, those experiences that are significant for manipulation within the physical environment.
quotes.translation: 自我可能仅同化某一特定经验的一部分。有时它根本不会同化或接受某个经验。在此请再次记住：自我与自体意识之间存在差异。自我无需同化自体意识所能触及的所有经验。然而，自我必须随时掌握那些对在物质环境中的操控具有重要意义的经验。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)

quotes.text: At the other end, so to speak, there is also a change which you do not perceive, and you do not perceive it because as the consciousness constructs the fetus from the inner plane, so then the consciousness is now beginning to enter another plane, where it is equally beyond your perception.
quotes.translation: 在另一端，可以说，也存在着一种你无法感知的变化，你之所以无法感知它，是因为当意识从内在层面构建胎儿时，意识此刻正开始进入另一个同样超出你感知范围的层面。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)

quotes.text: Any creative work involves you in a cooperative process in which you learn to dip into these other streams of consciousness, and come up with a perception that has far more dimensions than one arising from the one narrow, usual stream of consciousness that you know. Great creativity is then multidimensional for this reason. Its origin is not from one reality, but from many, and it is tinged with the multiplicity of that origin.
quotes.translation: 任何创造性工作都让你参与一个合作过程，在其中你学会潜入这些其他意识流，并产生一种远比源于你所知的单一狭窄常规意识流的感知更具维度的认知。伟大的创造力因此具有多维性。其起源并非来自单一实相，而是来自多重实相，并带有那种起源的多元性。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: They of course have their results or effects on psychological levels, representing what we may call psychological personality thrust. This does not apply only to the reality of human personality, but to that basic inner and smallest unit, individual prime consciousness that is at the core of any psychic gestalt. That is, the smallest unit of individual consciousness which finds physical fulfillment in the formation of matter.
quotes.translation: 它们当然在心理层面产生结果或影响，代表我们可称之为心理人格推力。这不仅适用于人类人格的实相，也适用于那个基本内在的最小单位——任何心灵完形核心处的个体原初意识。也就是说，在物质形成中寻求物质实现的个体意识的最小单位。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)

quotes.text: Though objects upon the table be dearly familiar to him, in his trance state he will not recall them. Any sentiment involved with the objects on the table, such sentiment will disappear and have no meaning. The ordinary state of consciousness is no different from that trance state. You have merely turned the focus of your attention into different realities. My attention, and my reality, is mainly focused in another direction.
quotes.translation: 虽然桌上的物品对他而言极为熟悉，但在出神状态中，他并不会记起它们。任何与桌上物品相关的情感都会消失，变得毫无意义。普通的意识状态与那种出神状态并无不同。你只是将注意力的焦点转向了不同的实相。而我的注意力与实相，则主要聚焦在另一个方向。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)

quotes.text: The consciousness so attuned however is only a small portion of the individual's total consciousness. In your system it is now fashionable to refer to this as the ego. The psychological structures are indeed so imprinted. However they are also aware and conscious of huge portions of themselves that are not so imprinted. They are aware of themselves simultaneously as individuals imprinted so as to react within several systems.
quotes.translation: 然而，如此调准的意识只是个体整体意识的一小部分。在你们的系统中，现在流行称之为“自我”。心理结构确实被如此铭印。但它们也觉察并意识到自身那些未被如此铭印的庞大部分。它们同时意识到自己是作为个体被铭印，以便在数个系统内做出反应。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)

quotes.text: I told you that we do not experience your time sequence. We travel through various intensities. Our work, development, and experience all takes place within what I term the "moment point." Here, within the moment point, the smallest thought is brought to fruition, the slightest possibility explored, the probabilities thoroughly examined, the least or the most forceful feeling entertained. It is difficult to explain this clearly, and yet the moment point is the framework within which we have our psychological experience. Within it, simultaneous actions follow "freely" through associative patterns. For example, pretend that I think of you, Joseph. In so doing I immediately experience -- and fully -- your past, present, and future (in your terms), and all of those strong or determining emotions and motivations that have ruled you. I can travel through those experiences with you, if I choose. We can follow a consciousness through all of its forms, for example, and in your terms, within the flicker of an eye.
quotes.translation: 我告诉过你们，我们并不经历你们的时间序列。我们穿越各种强度。我们的工作、发展与体验，都发生在我所谓的“瞬间点”内。在此，在瞬间点内，最微小的念头得以实现，最细微的可能性被探索，或然率被彻底检视，最微弱或最强烈的情感被体验。这很难解释清楚，然而瞬间点正是我们拥有心理体验的架构。在其中，同时性的行动通过联想模式“自由地”相继发生。例如，假设我想到了你，约瑟夫。如此一来，我立即且完全地体验到——以你们的说法——你的过去、现在与未来，以及所有那些支配你的强烈或决定性的情感与动机。如果我选择，我可以与你一同穿越那些体验。举例来说，我们可以跟随一个意识穿越它所有的形态，而按你们的说法，这只需一眨眼的工夫。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: Within him there are methods of perception that allow him to see through the camouflage settings, to see beyond the stage. He uses these inner senses constantly, though the actor part of himself is so intent upon the play that this escapes him. In a large manner, the physical senses actually form the physical reality they seem to only perceive. They are themselves part of the camouflage, but they are like lenses over your natural inner perceptions that force you to "see" an available field of activity as physical matter; and so they can be relied upon only to tell you what is happening in a superficial manner. You can tell the position of the other actors for example, or time by clock, but these physical senses will not tell you that time is itself a camouflage, or that consciousness forms the other actors, or that realities that you cannot see exist over and beyond the physical matter that is so apparent.
quotes.translation: 在他之内存在着感知方法，使他能看穿伪装设定，看到舞台之外。他不断运用这些内在感官，尽管他作为演员的那部分如此专注于戏剧，以至于忽略了这一点。在很大程度上，物质感官实际上形成了它们似乎只是感知到的物质实相。它们本身就是伪装的一部分，但就像覆盖在你自然内在感知上的透镜，迫使你将可用的活动场域“视为”物质；因此，它们只能告诉你表面发生的事情。例如，你可以知道其他演员的位置，或时钟显示的时间，但这些物质感官不会告诉你时间本身就是一种伪装，或意识形成了其他演员，或你无法看到的实相存在于如此明显的物质之上和之外。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: (9:53.) If you have any intuitive understanding as yet concerning the nature of the entity or whole self, you will see that it has placed you in a position in which certain abilities, insights, and experience can be realized, and in which your unique kind of consciousness can be nurtured. Your slightest experience has far more repercussions within this multidimensional environment than the physical brain can conceive. For if you are intensely preoccupied with what may seem to be one infinitesimally minute aspect of reality, and while you seem to be completely embedded within it, only the most "surface" elements of the self are so entranced. I do not like the term "surface" in this regard, though I have used it to suggest the multitudinous portions of the self that are otherwise engaged -- some of them as entranced in their reality as you are in yours.
quotes.translation: （9:53）如果你对存有或整体自性的本质有任何直觉理解，你会看到它已将你置于一个位置，使某些能力、洞见和体验得以实现，并使你独特的意识得以滋养。你最微小的体验在这个多维环境中的回响，远超出物质大脑所能想象。因为如果你全神贯注于实相中看似微不足道的一个方面，而当你似乎完全沉浸其中时，只有自性最“表面”的元素如此着迷。我不喜欢用“表面”这个词，尽管我用它来暗示自性中其他忙碌的部分——其中一些部分在其自己的实相中，就像你在你的实相中一样着迷。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: Dictation then: Probabilities are an ever-present portion of your invisible psychological environment. You exist in the middle of the probable system of reality. It is not something apart from you. To some extent it is like a sea in which you have your present being. You are in it, and it is in you. Occasionally at surface levels of consciousness, you might wonder what might have happened had you made other decisions than those you have; chosen different mates, for example, or taken up residence in other portions of the country. You might wonder what would have happened had you mailed an important letter that you subsequently decided not to mail; and in such small wonderings only, have you ever questioned the nature of probabilities. But there are deep connections between yourself and all those individuals with whom you have had relationships, and with whom you were involved in deep decisions.
quotes.translation: 口述开始：可能性是你无形心理环境中始终存在的一部分。你存在于可能性系统的中央。它并非与你分离之物。某种程度上它如同你当下存有所在的海洋。你在其中，它也在你之内。偶尔在意识的表层，你或许会好奇：若当初做出不同抉择——比如选择不同的伴侣，或定居在国家的另一区域——又会发生什么。你或许会思忖：若寄出那封后来决定不寄的重要信件，结果将如何。唯有在这些细微的遐想中，你才曾质疑可能性的本质。但你和所有曾与你建立关系、共同经历重大抉择的人之间，存在着深层的联结。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: Since consciousness forms matter, and not the other way around, then thought exists before the brain and after it. A child can think coherently before he learns vocabulary -- but he cannot impress the physical universe in its terms. So this inner knowledge has always been available, but is to become physically manifest -- literally made flesh. The Speakers were the first to impress this inner knowledge upon the physical system, to make it physically known. Sometimes only one or two Speakers were alive in several centuries. Sometimes there were many. They looked around them and knew that the world sprang from their interior reality. They told others. They knew (pause) that the seemingly solid natural objects about them were composed of many minute consciousnesses.
quotes.translation: 既然意识形成物质，而非物质形成意识，那么思想便存在于大脑之前与之后。孩童在学会词汇前就能连贯思考——但他无法以物质世界的语言影响它。因此这种内在知识始终可得，却需化为物质显化——真正成为血肉。传讯者首次将内在知识铭刻于物质系统，使其为物质世界所知。有时数个世纪仅有一两位传讯者存世，有时则有许多。他们环顾四周，深知世界源于自身的内在实相。他们告知他人。他们知晓（停顿）周遭看似坚实的自然物体，实由无数微小的意识构成。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: After death, for example, an individual may continue to create these -- masses of individuals may -- until they realize that the frameworks are no longer necessary. The Speakers were not confined in their activities, therefore, to waking consciousness. In all periods of your time they went about their duties both in the waking and sleep state. Much of the most pertinent information, in fact, was memorized by trainees during the dream condition, and passed on in the same manner. These unwritten manuscripts therefore were also illustrated, so to speak, by dream journeys or field trips into other kinds of reality. Such training still goes on. The particular psychic or story framework may vary. For example, conventional images of the Christian God and the saints may be utilized by the Speakers, with all of this highly vivid. The dreamer may find himself then in a magnificent harem, or instead in a brilliantly illuminated field or sky. Some Speakers confine their abilities to the dream state; and, waking, are largely unconscious of their own abilities or experience.
quotes.translation: 例如，死后个体可能继续创造这些——群体亦可能——直至他们意识到这些框架不再必要。因此传讯者的活动不受清醒意识局限。在你们时间的任何时期，他们都在清醒与睡眠状态中履行使命。事实上，最关键的资讯多由受训者在梦境中记忆，并以同样方式传递。这些未成文的手稿，可谓通过梦境之旅或实地考察其他实相领域而得以具象化。此类训练至今仍在进行。特定的心灵或故事框架或有不同。例如，传讯者可能运用基督教上帝与圣徒的传统意象，且一切栩栩如生。做梦者可能发现自己置身华美后宫，或璀璨原野与天际。有些传讯者将能力局限于梦境状态；清醒时，他们对自己的能力或体验几乎毫无察觉。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: Now the Speakers are familiar with the rules within many systems. Still, however, most of these systems in larger terms are somewhat connected with your own kind of reality. There are an infinite number of inner universes. Only the very highest, most developed gestalt consciousness can be aware of anything like their totality. In this larger context, then, the Speakers must be called local. There is something like a chart mapping many of the nearby systems of reality, and I hope some day in your terms to make this available. In order to do so, Ruburt must be trained somewhat more intensely. There are points of coincidence where under certain conditions entry may be made from one of these systems to the other. They need not exist separately in space as you know it, of course.
quotes.translation: 现在，演讲者们熟悉许多系统内的规则。然而，从更宏观的角度来看，这些系统大多与你们这类实相有所关联。存在着无限数量的内在宇宙。只有最高度发展、最完整的完形意识才能感知到类似其整体性的任何事物。因此，在这个更大的背景下，演讲者们必须被称为局部的存在。有一张类似地图的东西描绘了许多邻近的实相系统，我希望在你们的时间概念里，有一天能让这地图可用。为了做到这一点，鲁柏必须接受更密集的训练。存在一些巧合点，在这些点上，特定条件下可以从一个系统进入另一个系统。当然，它们不必像你们所认知的那样在空间中独立存在。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: I am not saying that, nor implying it in the case just mentioned. In your system you have a direct hand in the formation of physical reality. Your natural given data is the result of individual, mass, and collective thoughts, feelings, and emotions, materialized. Your system in this respect is more creative than the systems just mentioned. On the other hand, within these other systems there is a strong innovative group consciousness developing, in which identity is retained but greater inner play allowed between individuals, a large creative interchange of symbol-pools, a drawing upon mental and psychic symbols with greater facility. Because of this, these individuals recognize more clearly the connection between creative images and given sense data. They purposely alter and change their given sense data, and experiment with it.
quotes.translation: 我并非在说，也并非暗示刚才提到的情况。在你们的系统中，你们直接参与了物质实相的形成。你们自然接收到的感官资料，是个体、群体和集体的思想、感受与情绪物质化的结果。在这方面，你们的系统比刚才提到的那些系统更具创造力。另一方面，在这些其他系统中，正在发展一种强大的创新群体意识，其中身份得以保留，但个体之间允许更大的内在互动，符号库之间进行大规模创造性交流，更自如地运用心智与心灵符号。正因为如此，这些个体更清晰地认识到创造性意象与给定感官资料之间的联系。他们有意改变和调整自己的给定感官资料，并对其进行实验。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: When I come here to speak I focus my energy, but not toward this room as a destination, for in your terms this room does not exist to me. In your terms, this room does not even exist to you. You pretend to agree that it does; we meet in no place of space or time. The true meetings that take place here have nothing to do with the room or the people that you think you are. You know that you hallucinate the room, that you are as much in trance here as you ever are when you are in psychological time. I simply want you to realize that if this life is a trance, then you can turn the direction of your consciousness to perceive greater realities that presently exist. You can be aware of your own greater identity, even as I am. You sit within the miracle of yourselves and ask for signs. It is your inner eyes I would open.
quotes.translation: 当我来到这里讲话时，我会集中我的能量，但并非以这个房间为目的地，因为在你们的时间概念里，这个房间对我而言并不存在。在你们的时间概念里，这个房间甚至对你们也不存在。你们假装同意它存在；我们在任何空间或时间的地点都不相遇。这里发生的真正会面与房间或你们自以为是的那些人毫无关系。你们知道自己幻化出了这个房间，你们在这里进入的出神状态，与你们在心理时间中时一样深。我只是想让你们认识到，如果这一生是一场出神，那么你们可以转变意识的方向，去感知当下存在的更大实相。你们可以觉察到自己更大的身份，就像我一样。你们坐在自身的奇迹之中，却寻求征兆。我想要开启的是你们的内在之眼。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: (10:10.) The dream state provides you with a preliminary stage in which working hypotheses can be creatively formed and tried out in a context of playfulness. Still, the dreams that you have and recall, and the resulting solution of many problems, represent only the surface layer of dream activity. To follow yourself into your own dreams is a fascinating endeavor, and there in the dream context you can become aware of the working of your own consciousness. To do so you must believe in the integrity of your own being. If you do not trust your waking self you will not trust your dreaming self, and the landscape of your dreams will appear threatening. Your belief that dreams are unpleasant can make them so, or at best you will only remember frightening dream events.
quotes.translation: （十点十分。）梦境状态为你提供了一个初步阶段，在此阶段中，工作假设得以在游戏性的脉络中被创造性地形成与测试。尽管如此，你所拥有并忆起的梦境，以及由此解决许多问题的结果，仅代表梦境活动的表层。跟随自己进入梦境是一项迷人的努力，在梦境脉络中，你能觉察自身意识的运作。要做到这一点，你必须相信自身存有的完整性。若你不信任清醒的自己，便不会信任做梦的自己，而梦境的景观将显得充满威胁。你相信梦境不愉快的信念会使其成真，或至多你只会忆起可怕的梦境事件。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality

quotes.text: (10:45.) A certain kind of affirmation of self allows the brain to tune into these more spacious methods of perception that are the natural characteristics of the mind. There are very good reasons why this type of assertion must first occur. The brain (and the entire physical system) is meant to insure your bodily survival and to follow your conscious beliefs about reality. There is always a harmonious unifying connection between your beliefs and activities. Some people feel utterly confident in certain areas and are timorous in others. Some aspects of life may be ignored or even refuted for a time while others are focused upon. The individual will very cleverly and shrewdly go ahead in those areas in which he or she feels safe, often when in the process of altering beliefs. You will not use your spacious mind until you affirm its reality within yourself, and until you are ready to handle the additional data which will then become consciously available to one extent or another. But the spacious mind operates through your creaturehood; in your terms it represents latent abilities of consciousness that can be more or less normal functions.
quotes.translation: （10:45分。）某种对自我的肯定，能让大脑调频至这些更广阔的感知方法，这些方法本是心智的自然特质。这种断言必须首先发生，这其中有非常充分的理由。大脑（以及整个物质系统）旨在确保你身体的生存，并遵循你对实相的有意识信念。你的信念与活动之间，始终存在着和谐统一的联结。有些人在某些领域充满自信，在其他领域却畏首畏尾。生命的某些方面可能暂时被忽视甚至否定，而另一些方面则被聚焦。个体在感到安全的领域，会非常巧妙而精明地前进，这在改变信念的过程中尤为常见。除非你在自己内心肯定广阔心智的实相，除非你准备好处理那些随后会在某种程度上变得有意识可用的额外资料，否则你不会运用你的广阔心智。但广阔心智通过你的生物性运作；用你们的话来说，它代表了意识的潜在能力，这些能力或多或少可以成为正常功能。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality

quotes.text: (Slowly:) I told you once that there were pulses of activity in which you blinked off and on -- this applying even to atomic and subatomic particles.1 "You" assign as real -- present here and now -- only that activity that is your signal. "You" are not aware of the others. When people think in terms of one self, they of course identify with one body. You know that the cellular structure of it changes constantly. The body is at any given moment, however, a mass conglomeration of energy formed from that rich bank of probable activity. The body is not stable in the terms usually thought of. On deeper biological levels the cells straddle probabilities, and trigger responses. Consciousness rides upon and within the pulses mentioned earlier, and forms its own organizations of identity. Each probability -- probable only in relation to and from the standpoint of another probability -- is inviolate, however, in that it is not destroyed. Once formed, the pattern will follow its own nature.
quotes.translation: （缓慢地：）我曾告诉过你，存在着活动的脉冲，在其中你忽隐忽现地闪烁——这甚至适用于原子和亚原子粒子。你"将"只有那种活动视为真实——存在于此时此地——那是你的信号。你"并未"意识到其他活动。当人们以单一自我的概念思考时，他们当然会认同一个身体。你知道它的细胞结构在不断变化。然而，在任何特定时刻，身体都是由那个丰富的可能活动库形成的能量大杂烩。身体并非通常所认为的那样稳定。在更深的生物层面上，细胞跨越可能性，并触发反应。意识乘着并存在于前面提到的脉冲之中，形成其自身的身份组织。每种可能性——仅相对于另一种可能性并从其立场而言才是可能的——都是不可侵犯的，因为它不会被摧毁。一旦形成，模式将遵循其自身的本性。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1

quotes.text: (Faster at 10:45:) The god concept then was an aid, and an important one, to man's emerging ego. To develop its sense of specialization, the ego forgot the great cooperative venture of the earth. If a hunter literally knows his relationship with an animal, he cannot kill it. On deeper levels both animal and man understand the connections. Biologically the man knows he has come from the earth. Some of his cells have been the cells of animals, and the animal knows he will look out through a man's eyes.3 The earth venture is cooperative. The slain beast is tomorrow's hunter. In terms of ego consciousness, however, there were stages of growth; and the god concepts that spoke of oneness with nature were not those that served the ego's purposes in the line of development as you understand it (deliberately).
quotes.translation: （10:45加快速度：）神的概念在当时是一种助力，而且是人类新兴自我意识的重要助力。为了发展其专业化的感知，自我意识遗忘了地球这场伟大的合作冒险。如果猎人真正理解他与动物的关系，他就无法杀害它。在更深层次上，动物和人类都明白这种联结。从生物学角度，人类知道自己来自大地，他的一些细胞曾是动物的细胞，而动物也知道自己将通过人类的眼睛向外看。地球冒险是合作性的，被宰杀的野兽就是明天的猎人。然而，就自我意识的层面而言，存在成长阶段；那些讲述与自然合一的神的概念，并不符合你们所理解的自我意识发展路线上的目的。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1

quotes.text: (Intently:) I am saying that the individual self must become consciously aware of far more reality; that it must allow its recognition of identity to expand so that it includes previously unconscious knowledge. To do this you must understand, again, that man must move beyond the concepts of one god, one self, one body, one world, as these ideas are currently understood.5 You are now poised, in your terms, upon a threshold from which the race can go many ways. There are species of consciousness. Your species is in a time of change. There are potentials within the body's mechanisms, in your terms, not as yet used. Developed, they can immeasurably enrich the race, and bring it to levels of spiritual and psychic and physical fulfillment. If some changes are not made, the race as such will not endure.
quotes.translation: （专注地：）我要说的是，个体自我必须有意识地觉察到远为更多的实相；它必须允许其身份认同的扩展，以包含先前无意识的知识。要做到这一点，你们必须再次理解，人类必须超越"一个神、一个自我、一个身体、一个世界"的概念——正如这些观念目前被理解的那样。用你们的话来说，你们此刻正站在一个门槛上，人类可以从这里走向许多方向。存在多种意识物种。你们的物种正处于变革时期。用你们的话来说，身体机制内存在尚未被利用的潜能。一旦发展这些潜能，它们将无可估量地丰富人类，并将其带向灵性、心灵与肉体圆满的层次。如果不做出某些改变，人类这个物种将无法存续。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1

quotes.text: Because of the ego's particular line of development, you have experimented with artificial drugs and chemicals, both in foods and for medicinal purposes, as well as for "religious" enlightenment. Some of the effects of LSD7 and other artificial psychedelic drugs give you a hint of other probable directions your consciousness might have followed, or might still follow. As the experiments are conducted, however, and in the ignorance of the framework, the conscious mind takes a subordinate position. Instead, using methods other than drugs, it could be taught to expand its knowledge far more safely, to organize it in ways that could be most advantageous. Still, some of the experiments do give hints of certain aspects of one of the species' probable developments.
quotes.translation: 由于自我意识特定的发展路线，你们在食物、医药以及"宗教"启蒙方面，都实验过人工药物和化学制品。LSD及其他人工迷幻药的部分效果，暗示了你们的意识可能已经遵循或仍可能遵循的其他可能方向。然而，在这些实验进行的过程中，由于对框架的无知，意识心处于从属地位。相反，若能采用药物以外的方法，意识心可以被教导以更安全的方式扩展其知识，并以最有利的方式组织这些知识。不过，某些实验确实暗示了该物种可能发展的某些面向。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1

quotes.text: Give us a moment ... In the body certain cells "kill" others, and in so doing the body's living integrity is maintained. The cells do each other that service (with gestures). In the exterior world certain animals "kill" others. You had for centuries, then, speaking in your limited terms, a situation in which men and animals were both hunters and prey. In those misty eras7 -- from your standpoint -- these activities were carried on with the deepest, most sacred comprehension. Again, the slain animal knew that it would "later" look out through its slayer's eyes8 -- attaining a newer, different kind of consciousness. The man, the slayer, understood the great sense of harmony that existed even in the slaying, and knew that in turn the physical material of his body would be used by the earth to replenish the vegetable and animal kingdoms.
quotes.translation: 请稍等片刻……在身体中，某些细胞会"杀死"其他细胞，借此维持身体活生生的完整性。细胞们彼此提供这种服务（以手势示意）。在外部世界中，某些动物会"杀死"其他动物。因此，用你们有限的术语来说，几个世纪以来，人类与动物既是猎手也是猎物。从你们的角度来看，在那些朦胧的远古时期——这些活动是在最深沉、最神圣的理解中进行的。再次强调，被杀的动物知道它"稍后"会透过猎杀者的眼睛向外看——获得一种更新、不同的意识。而作为猎杀者的人类，理解到即使在杀戮中也存在着伟大的和谐感，并且知道他的身体物质终将被大地利用，以滋养植物与动物王国。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1

quotes.text: (A one-minute pause at 10:28.) In one way or another all mythology contains descriptions of other species existing on the earth in various forms. This includes stories of fairies and giants, for example. Mythology tells you about the archaeology of your race psychically as well as physically. There were, then, smaller and larger species of men,4 with varying conscious connections with the rest of nature. The larger experiments involved the production of a species that would be a part of the earth, and yet become aware co-creators of it. There were innumerable considerations, innumerable experiments, with size, brain capacity, neurological structure, and with a kind of consciousness flexible enough to change with its environment, and also vigorous enough to explore and alter that environment. Do you have that?
quotes.translation: （10:28停顿一分钟）所有神话都以某种方式描述了其他物种以各种形态存在于地球上的情景。例如，这包括精灵与巨人的故事。神话不仅告诉你们种族在生理上的考古学，也揭示了心灵上的考古学。因此，曾经存在过体型较小与较大的人类物种，它们与自然界的其他部分有着不同的意识连结。更宏大的实验涉及创造一种既属于地球一部分，又能成为其有意识的共同创造者的物种。这其中有无数的考量与实验，涉及体型、脑容量、神经结构，以及一种足够灵活以随环境改变、又足够强健以探索并改变环境的意识。你们明白了吗？
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1

quotes.text: The CU's, forming the structure later in its entirety, form all the atoms, molecules, cells, and organs that make up your world. Land changes and alterations of species are conditions brought about in line with overall patterns that involve all species, or land and water masses, at any given "time." There is a great organization of consciousness involved on such occasions -- sometimes creative cataclysms, in which, again from its own precognitive information, nature brings about those situations best suited to its needs. Such biological precognition is firmly based in the chromosomes and genes, and reflected in the cells. As mentioned earlier (in the 684tb session), the present corporal structure of any physical body of any kind is maintained only because of the cells' innate precognitive abilities. To the self the future, of course, is not experienced as future. It is simply one of the emerging conditions of an experienced Now (you had better capitalize that). The cells' practically felt "Now" includes, then, what you would think of as past and future, as simple conditions of Nowness. They maintain the body's structure in your poised time only by manipulating themselves in a rich medium of probabilities. There is a constant give-and-take of communication between the cell as you know it in present time, and the cell as it "was" in the past, or "will be."
quotes.translation: CU（意识单位）后来完整地形成了结构，构成了组成你们世界的所有原子、分子、细胞与器官。陆地的变迁与物种的改变，是在任何特定"时间"内，依据涉及所有物种或陆块与水体的整体模式所引发的状况。在这样的场合中，存在着一个庞大的意识组织——有时是创造性的剧变，在其中，自然再次根据自身的预知信息，带来最适合其需求的情境。这种生物预知能力牢固地建立在染色体与基因中，并反映在细胞里。如先前所述（在第684节），任何种类物质身体的当前肉体结构之所以得以维持，完全是因为细胞与生俱来的预知能力。对自体而言，未来当然不会被体验为未来。它只是被体验的"当下"（你最好大写）的涌现条件之一。因此，细胞实际感受到的"当下"包含了你们视为过去与未来的事物，作为"当下性"的简单条件。它们仅在你们所认为的稳定时间中，通过在一个丰富的或然率媒介中操纵自身，来维持身体的结构。在你们所知的当下细胞与"过去"或"将来"的细胞之间，存在着持续不断的交流与互动。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1

quotes.text: At certain levels the brain can handle simultaneous material, of course, even though you may be conscious of only a smattering of it. The body is aware of multitudinous simultaneous stimuli that consciously escape you, and is able to act on the information. This includes all kinds of sense data that are not consciously pertinent. (Intently:) Because of the particular kind of ego-orientation that the race decided upon, however, many probabilities of development inherent in the species have been latent. Inherently the physical brain is capable of dealing with more than one main line of consciousness. This does not mean the development of dual personality, by the way. It means the further expansion of the concept of identity: "You" would not only be aware of the you that you have always known, in the same way that you are now, but a deeper sense of identity would also arise.
quotes.translation: 在特定层次上，大脑当然能处理同步发生的素材，尽管你或许只意识到其中零星片段。身体觉察到无数同步刺激——这些刺激在意识层面被你忽略——并能根据这些信息采取行动。这包含所有在意识层面不相关的感官数据。（专注地：）但由于人类种族决定采用的特定自我导向模式，物种内在的许多发展潜能始终处于潜伏状态。物理大脑本质上具备处理多条意识主线的能力。这并非指发展出双重人格，而是身份概念的进一步扩展：“你”不仅会以现有方式觉察到那个你一直熟悉的自己，更会升起更深层的身份感。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1

quotes.text: "As I walk into the kitchen the head of my dream self fills with vivid scenes, like other dreams, interpretations of each cell of this new awareness. I project all of this outward around me into literally hundreds of brilliant scenes; expressions, I knew, of probabilities, 'past' and 'future' events, sideways events I can't even understand ... all happening at once, with perfect comprehension of that by the 'anchor' dream self. I feel that while all of this is still coming from this anchor self, the selves in these dreams are equally as focused -- each of them being dream selves, existing in their universes, and with each of their own connections expanding outwards in much the same way that mine do. I literally become the experience of being myself contained in all of these selves, while being these selves contained by me. In at least one of these selves, the knowledge of this entire event comes to consciousness like a half-recalled dream of its own, and the experience of recalling and being recalled is like liquid electricity in me, the anchor self.
quotes.translation: “当我走进厨房时，梦境自我的头部充满生动场景，如同其他梦境般，诠释着这新觉知的每个细胞。我将这一切向外投射，形成数百个璀璨场景——我明白，这些是概率的表达，是‘过去’与‘未来’事件，甚至是我无法理解的横向事件……所有事件同时发生，而‘锚定’梦境自我对此全然理解。我感觉到，当这一切仍源自这个锚定自我时，这些梦境中的自我同样专注——每个都是存在于各自宇宙中的梦境自我，其连接向外扩展的方式与我如出一辙。我真正成为包含在所有自我中的那个自己，同时这些自我又为我所包含。在至少其中一个自我中，对这整个事件的认知如同半忆起的梦境般浮现，而回忆与被回忆的体验，在我这个锚定自我中如同液态电流般流淌。”
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1

quotes.text: In many cases your scientists seem to have the strange idea that you can understand a reality by destroying it; that you can perceive the life mechanism of an animal by killing it; or that you can examine a phenomenon best by separating yourself from it. So, often, you attempt to examine the nature of the brain in man by destroying the brains of animals, by separating portions of the animal brain from its components, isolating them, and tampering with the overall integrity of both the animal in question and of your own spiritual processes. By this I mean that each such attempt puts you more out of context, so to speak, with yourself and your environment, and other species. Period. While you may "learn" certain so-called facts, you are driven still further away from any great knowledge, because the so-called facts stand in your way. You do not as yet understand the uniqueness of consciousness.
quotes.translation: 在许多情况下，你们的科学家似乎持有一种奇怪的观念：认为可以通过摧毁现实来理解它；认为可以通过杀死动物来感知其生命机制；或者认为可以通过与现象分离来最佳地研究它。因此，你们常常试图通过破坏动物的大脑、将动物大脑的各个部分从其整体中分离出来、孤立它们，并干扰所涉动物的整体完整性以及你们自身灵性过程的完整性，来研究人类大脑的本质。我的意思是，每一次这样的尝试，可以说都让你们更加脱离自身、环境以及其他物种的脉络。句号。虽然你们可能“学到”某些所谓的事实，但这些事实却阻碍了你们，使你们离任何伟大的知识越来越远。你们尚未理解意识的独特性。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1

quotes.text: In the entire gestalt from cellular to "self" consciousness, there is a vast field of knowledge -- much of it now "unconsciously" available -- used to maintain the body's integrity in space and time. With the conscious mind as director, there is no reason why much of this knowledge cannot become normally and naturally available. There is, therefore, a quite valid, vital, real and vastly creative inner reality, and an inward sequence of events from which your present universe and life emerges. Any true scientist will ultimately have to learn to enter that realm of reality. So-called objective approaches will only work at all when you are dealing with so-called objective effects -- and your physicists are learning that even in that framework many "facts" are facts only within certain frequencies,2 or under certain conditions. You are left with "workable facts" that help you manipulate in your own backyard, but such facts become prejudice when you try to venture beyond your own cosmic neighborhood and find that your preconceived, native ideas do not apply outside of their context.
quotes.translation: 在从细胞意识到“自我”意识的整个完形中，存在一个广阔的知识领域——其中许多现在“无意识地”可用——用于维持身体在时空中的完整性。以意识心作为指导者，没有理由不让这些知识中的大部分变得正常且自然地可用。因此，存在一个相当有效、充满活力、真实且极具创造性的内在实相，以及一个内在的事件序列，你们的当前宇宙和生命从中涌现。任何真正的科学家最终都必须学会进入那个实相领域。所谓的客观方法只有在处理所谓的客观效果时才会有效——而你们的物理学家正在学习，即使在那框架内，许多“事实”也只是在某些频率或特定条件下才成立。你们剩下的是“可操作的事实”，这些事实帮助你们在自己的后院中操控，但当你们试图冒险超越自己的宇宙邻里，发现你们先入为主的本地观念在其脉络之外并不适用时，这些事实就变成了偏见。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1

quotes.text: (10:36.) Your medical technology may help you "conquer" one disease after another -- some in fact caused by that same technology -- and you will feel very efficient as you do heart transplants, as you fight one virus after another. But all of this will do nothing except to allow people to die, perhaps, of other diseases still "unconquered." People will die when they are ready to, following inner dictates and dynamics. A person ready to die will, despite any medication. (Emphatically:) A person who wants to live will seize upon the tiniest hope, and respond. The dynamics of health have nothing to do with inoculations. They reside in the consciousness of each being. In your terms they are regulated by emotions, desires, and thoughts. A true doctor cannot be scientifically objective. He cannot divorce himself from the reality of his patient. Instead, usually, the doctor's words and very methods literally separate the patient from himself or herself. The malady is seen almost as a thing apart from the patient's person -- but thrust upon it -- over which the patient has little control.3
quotes.translation: （10:36。）你们的医疗技术可能帮助你们“征服”一个又一个疾病——其中一些实际上是由同一技术引起的——当你们进行心脏移植、与一个又一个病毒作斗争时，你们会感到非常高效。但这一切除了让人们可能死于其他“尚未征服”的疾病之外，别无他用。人们会在他们准备好时死去，遵循内在的指令和动力。一个准备好死去的人，无论用什么药物都会死去。（强调地：）一个想要活下去的人会抓住最微小的希望，并做出回应。健康的动力与接种无关。它们存在于每个存有的意识中。用你们的术语来说，它们由情绪、欲望和思想所调节。一个真正的医生不能是科学客观的。他不能将自己与病人的实相分离。相反，通常医生的言语和治疗方法实际上将病人与自身分离开来。疾病几乎被视为与病人人格分离的东西——但强加于其上——病人对此几乎无法控制。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1

quotes.text: "The growth of ego consciousness by itself set up both challenges and limitations. This automatically meant that emerging man, in that framework, must let go of a certain kind of animal comprehension that was extremely valuable overall, but could inhibit ego growth ... For many centuries there was no clear-cut differentiation between various species of man and animal ... There were also, of course, parallel developments in the emergence of physical man. Again, for many centuries, there were innumerable species of man-in-the-making, in your terms; various postures, and even types of manipulation, as well as alterations in brain size and activity. In some, different kinds of senses predominated. At the same time a great give-and-take was occurring at all levels -- including vegetation, for example -- so that together the creatures and the earth worked out the kind of stability best suited for the particular kind of developments that were to emerge.
quotes.translation: "自我意识的发展本身就带来了挑战与局限。这自然意味着，在那个框架中，正在崛起的人类必须放弃某种极为宝贵的动物性理解，但这种理解可能会阻碍自我的成长……许多世纪以来，人类与动物的不同物种之间并没有明确的区分……当然，在物质人类的崛起过程中也有平行的发展。同样，用你们的话说，许多世纪以来存在着无数种正在形成中的人类：不同的姿态，甚至不同的操作方式，以及大脑大小和活动的变化。在某些物种中，不同的感官占主导地位。与此同时，在各个层面——例如包括植被——都发生着巨大的交换，因此生物与地球共同发展出最适合即将出现的特定发展的那种稳定性。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1

quotes.text: Your sexuality is a point of focus, and that is all. For those of you who need it said, I say it: A woman is as intellectual as a man. A man is as intuitive as a woman. You chose your sexual focus for a reason. The reason has more to do with the flexibility of consciousness than you presently understand. It has to do with the real nature of aggression and passivity,1 which you have allowed yourselves to forget ... Birth is an aggressive experience. Passivity is based upon the joyful recognition of natural aggression. To be carried along, each of you must be very sure of yourself. To allow yourself what is now, in your terms, a luxury of passivity, you must have confidence in the nature of your own reality and strength. Otherwise passivity frightens you to the core.
quotes.translation: 你们的性是一个焦点，仅此而已。对于那些需要听到这句话的人，我说：女人和男人一样理智。男人和女人一样直觉。你们选择自己的性焦点是有原因的。这个原因与意识的灵活性有关，远超你们目前的理解。它与攻击性和被动性的真实本质有关，1 而你们允许自己忘记了这一点……出生是一种攻击性体验。被动性建立在对自然攻击性的喜悦认知之上。要随波逐流，你们每个人都必须对自己非常确信。要允许自己拥有——用你们的话说——被动性的奢侈，你们必须对自己实相和力量的本性有信心。否则，被动性会让你们恐惧到骨子里。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1

quotes.text: Any discussion of the unknown reality must necessarily involve certain usually dismissed hypotheses about the characteristics of consciousness itself. The world as you know it is the result of a complicated set of "codes" (as given at the beginning of the last session), each locked in one to the other, each one in those terms dependent upon the others. Your precise perceived universe in all of its parts, then, results from coded patterns, each one fitting perfectly into the next. Alter one of these and to some extent you step out of that context (underlined). Any event of any kind that does not directly, immaculately intersect with your space-time continuum, does not happen, in your terms, but falls away. It becomes probable in your system but seeks its own "level," and becomes actualized as it falls into place in another reality whose "coded sequence" fits its own. Period.
quotes.translation: 任何对未知实相的探讨，必然涉及某些通常被忽视的关于意识本质的假设。你们所知的世界，是复杂"编码"系统（如上节课程所述）运作的结果——每套编码环环相扣，彼此依存。你们精确感知的宇宙及其所有组成部分，皆源于这些完美衔接的编码模式。改变其中任一编码，便会在某种程度上脱离该脉络（下划线标注）。任何未与你们时空连续体直接、纯粹相交的事件，在你们的认知中便不会发生，而是消隐无踪。这类事件在你们系统中成为可能性，转而寻求自身"层级"，并在落入另一套"编码序列"与之匹配的实相时得以显化。句号。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: New paragraph. While all of your own thoughts and feelings are "somewhere" materialized, only some of them become physical in your terms. They are then accepted as physical reality. They provide the basis for the physical events, objects, and phenomena upon which you all agree. Therefore your world has a stability that you accept, a certain order and predictability3 that works well enough for daily concerns. At that point you are tuned in precisely on your "home station." You ignore the ghost symbols or voices, the probable actions that also occur, but that are muffled in the clear tones of your accepted reality. When you begin to travel away from that home station, you become more aware of the other frequencies4 that are buried within it. You move through other frequencies, but to do this you must alter your own consciousness.*
quotes.translation: 新段落。虽然你所有的思想和情感都在"某处"物质化了，但只有其中一部分在你们的说法中变成具体物质。它们随后被接受为物质实相，为你们共同认同的物质事件、物体和现象提供基础。因此，你们的世界具有你们所接受的稳定性、某种秩序和可预测性，足以应付日常事务。在这一点上，你恰好调准了你的"主台"。你忽略了那些幽灵符号或声音，那些同样发生但被你们所接受的实相的清晰音调所掩盖的可能行动。当你开始远离那个主台时，你会更加意识到埋藏其中的其他频率。你穿越其他频率，但要做到这一点，你必须改变自己的意识。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: (Smile:) The supernatural radio that is your entire psyche contains many such stations, however. These are all playing at the same time. It would be highly confusing in this analogy to experience or hear all of these at once, however, so different portions of the psyche tune in to different stations, concentrate upon them, and tune out the others for immediate practical purposes. Because these stations all operate within the same psyche or supernatural radio, the overall quality of the programs will have much to do with the nature of the psyche itself. Radios are wired and contain transformers and transistors. The overall reception is dependent upon the wiring and the inner workings of the radio -- and (intently) those workings exist apart from the stations they are meant to pick up. In the same way, the "supernatural psyche" exists apart from the stations of consciousness that it contains. In this case indeed the psyche itself makes the radio, adding ever-new connections and stations.
quotes.translation: （微笑：）然而，作为你整个心灵的超自然收音机包含许多这样的电台。所有这些电台同时播放。在这个类比中，同时体验或听到所有这些会非常混乱，因此心灵的不同部分调谐到不同的电台，专注于它们，并为即时实用目的而屏蔽其他电台。因为这些电台都在同一个心灵或超自然收音机内运作，节目的整体质量将与心灵本身的本质有很大关系。收音机有线路，包含变压器和晶体管。整体接收效果取决于收音机的线路和内部运作——（专注地）这些运作独立于它们旨在接收的电台而存在。同样，"超自然心灵"独立于它所包含的意识电台而存在。在这种情况下，确实是心灵本身制造了收音机，不断添加新的连接和电台。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: Your experience of yourself marks the seeming boundaries of yourself. In a manner of speaking, I am one personality and one program or station. Ruburt is another. We have learned to be aware of each other* to communicate between stations, to affect each other's programs and to change each other's worlds. I do not speak alone to Ruburt and Joseph, for example, but my words go out to the world that you know. Still within your framework, Ruburt tunes in to another station, translates it and broadcasts the information. To do this, however, he has to alter his own consciousness, withdraw momentarily from the official station to bring in this one. That means tuning in to other portions of the psyche, as well as another kind of reality. The final translation of my material has to come through his organism, however, or it would be meaningless to you.
quotes.translation: 你对自己的体验标志着你自己的表面界限。可以说，我是一个人格和一个节目或电台。鲁柏是另一个。我们学会了彼此觉察，在电台之间沟通，影响彼此的节目，改变彼此的世界。例如，我不仅对鲁柏和约瑟夫说话，而且我的话传向你们所知的世界。仍在你们的框架内，鲁柏调谐到另一个电台，翻译它并广播信息。然而，要做到这一点，他必须改变自己的意识，暂时离开官方电台以接入这个电台。这意味着调谐到心灵的其他部分，以及另一种实相。然而，我材料的最终翻译必须通过他的有机体传达，否则对你们来说将毫无意义。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: Seth's material in those early sessions, given well over a decade ago for the most part, reflected of course his reactions to current astronomical theory about the state -- and fate -- of our physical (camouflage) universe. The idea of an infinitely expanding universe, with all of its stars ultimately burned out and all life extinct, is still the view largely accepted today; it's based on the red shift measurements of some of the supposedly receding galaxies, their apparent brightnesses, the "missing mass" of the universe, and other very technical data. Yet I find it most interesting to note that now some astrophysicists and mathematicians believe our universe may be destined to contract -- indeed, to collapse in upon itself -- after all. But again, these ideas aren't based on the kind of thinking Seth espouses (that consciousness comes first, that its creations are continuous), but upon other quite complicated camouflage observations and measurements. One of these is the discovery of at least some of that missing mass, thus indicating that gravitational fields may exist among the galaxies, and galactic clusters, strong enough not only to halt the expansion of the universe but to pull all matter back together again.
quotes.translation: 那些早期课程中赛斯提供的资料（大部分是十多年前给出的）自然反映了他对当时天文学理论关于我们物理（伪装）宇宙状态与命运的回应。无限膨胀的宇宙——所有恒星最终熄灭、所有生命灭绝——的观念至今仍是主流观点；它基于对某些所谓退行星系的红移测量、它们的视亮度、宇宙"缺失质量"以及其他非常技术性的数据。然而我发现最有趣的是，如今一些天体物理学家和数学家认为我们的宇宙可能终将收缩——甚至最终自我坍缩。但这些想法并非基于赛斯所主张的思维方式（意识优先，其创造是连续的），而是基于其他相当复杂的伪装观测与测量。其中之一是至少发现了部分缺失质量，表明星系与星系团之间可能存在足够强大的引力场，不仅能阻止宇宙膨胀，还能将所有物质重新拉回一起。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: There are good reasons for conventions. Generally, they help organize experience. If they are lightly held to and accepted, they can serve well as guidelines. Applied with a heavy hand they become unnecessary dogma, rigidly limiting experience. This applies to inner and outer activity. Conventions are the results of stratified and rigid "spontaneity." At one time, in your terms, each custom had a meaning. Each represented a spontaneous gesture, an individual reaction. When these become a system of order, however, the original spontaneity is lost, and you project an artificial order that serves to stratify behavior rather than to express it. So there are psychic customs as there are physical ones, religious and psychic dogmas, guided tours of consciousness in which you are told to follow a certain line or a certain program. You become afraid of your private interpretation of whatever reality you find yourself experiencing.
quotes.translation: 惯例自有其存在的充分理由。一般而言，它们有助于组织经验。若能轻松地持守并接纳，它们便能成为良好的指引。若以强硬手段应用，它们就会变成不必要的教条，僵化地限制经验。这适用于内在与外在活动。惯例是分层化且僵化的"自发性"的结果。在你们的时间观念里，每个习俗都曾有其意义。每个都代表一种自发姿态、一种个体反应。然而，当这些成为一套秩序系统时，原本的自发性便丧失了，你们投射出一种人为秩序，用以固化行为而非表达行为。因此，有心灵习俗如同有物质习俗，有宗教与心灵教条，有意识的导览行程，在其中你们被告知要遵循特定路线或特定程序。你们开始害怕对自己所经历的任何实相进行私人诠释。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: (At a slower pace:) He is consciously entering into another room of the psyche, and also entering into the reality that corresponds to it. This brings the two experiences together so that they coincide. They are held, however, both separately and in joint focus. As a rule you use one particular level of awareness, and this correlates all of your conscious activities. I told you that the physical body itself was able to pick up other neurological messages beside those to which you usually react.8 Now let me add that when a certain proficiency is reached in alterations of consciousness, this allows you to become practically familiar with some of these other neurological messages. In such a way Ruburt is able to physically perceive what he is doing in his "library."
quotes.translation: （以较慢的速度：）他有意识地进入心灵的另一个房间，同时也进入与之对应的实相。这使两种经验汇聚在一起，从而相互重合。然而，它们既被分别聚焦，也被共同聚焦。通常你们使用一个特定的意识层面，这协调了你们所有有意识的活动。我曾告诉你们，物质身体本身能够接收除了你们通常反应的那些神经讯息之外的其他神经讯息。现在让我补充：当在意识转换中达到某种熟练度时，这能使你实际熟悉其中一些其他神经讯息。以这种方式，鲁柏能够从物质层面感知他在"图书馆"中所做的事。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: Each person will have his or her own private experience here, but gradually certain kinds of physical data will seem to disappear while others may take prominence. For example, you might mentally hear sounds, while knowing they have no physical origin. You may see nothing in your mind, or you may see images that seem to have no exterior correlation, but you may hear nothing. For a while ordinary physical data may continue to intrude. When it does, recognize it as your home station, and mentally let yourself drift further away from it. What is important is your own sensation as you experience the mobility of your consciousness. If ever you grow concerned simply return to your home station, back to the left or right according to the direction you have chosen. I do not suggest that you use "higher" or "lower" as directions, because of the interpretations that you may have placed upon them through your beliefs.
quotes.translation: 每个人在此都会有各自私密的体验，但渐渐地某些特定类型的物质资料会看似消失，而其他资料则可能突显出来。例如，你可能会在心灵中听见声音，同时知道它们没有物质来源。你可能在脑海中什么也看不见，也可能看见看似与外界无关的影像，但可能什么也听不见。有一段时间，普通的物质资料可能会持续侵入。当这种情况发生时，请认出那是你的本台，并在心灵中让自己逐渐远离它。重要的是你体验自身意识可动性时的感受。如果你感到担忧，只需回到你的本台，根据你选择的方向向左或向右返回。我不建议你使用"较高"或"较低"作为方向，因为根据你的信念，你可能已经赋予它们特定的诠释。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: In your sleep, however, your consciousness slips out of your body and returns to it frequently. You dream when you are out of your body, even as you dream inside it. You may therefore form dream stories about your own out-of-body travel, while your physical image rests soundly in bed. The unknown reality, you see, is not really that mysterious to you. You only pretend that it is. Sometimes you have quite clear perceptions of your journeys, but the actual native territories that you visit are so different from your own world that you try to interpret them as best you can in the light of usual conditions. If you remember such an episode at all it may well seem very confusing, for you will have superimposed your own world view where it does not belong.
quotes.translation: 然而在睡眠中，你的意识会滑出身体，又频繁地返回。你在出体时做梦，正如你在体内时也会做梦。因此，当你的肉身安卧床上时，你可能会围绕自己的出体之旅编造梦境故事。你看，未知的实相对你而言其实并不那么神秘，你只是假装它很神秘而已。有时你对旅程有相当清晰的感知，但你实际造访的本土领域与你的世界差异如此之大，以至于你只能尽力以惯常条件来诠释它们。若你竟能记住这样的片段，它很可能显得非常混乱，因为你已将自身世界观强加于本不属于它的地方。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: When you awaken with a dream photograph in mind, it may appear meaningless because it does not seem to correlate with the official order of activities you recognize. You may make one particular decision in physical and waking consciousness, and that decision may bring forth certain events. Using your dream camera, you can with practice discover the history of your own psyche, and find the many probable decisions experienced in dreams. These served as a basis from which you made your physical decision. There is some finesse required as you learn to interpret the individual pictures within your dream album. This should be easy to grasp, for if you tried to understand physical life having only a group of snapshots taken at different places and in different times, then it would be rather difficult to form a clear idea of the nature of the physical world.
quotes.translation: 当你带着梦中快照醒来时，它可能看似毫无意义，因为它似乎与你所认知的官方活动秩序毫无关联。你或许在物质与清醒意识中做出某个特定决定，而这个决定可能引发某些事件。运用你的梦境相机，通过练习你能发现自身心灵的历史，并找到在梦中体验过的诸多可能决定。这些决定构成了你做出物质决定的基础。当你学习解读梦境相册中的单张照片时，需要一些技巧。这应该不难掌握，因为若你试图仅凭在不同时空拍摄的一组快照来理解物质生命，那么要形成对物质世界本质的清晰概念就会相当困难。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: The Roman soldier dreams of the black woman, and of Joseph. There is a reminiscence that appears even in the knowledge of the cells, and a certain correspondence.14 There are connections then as far as cellular recollection is concerned, and dreams. Now the Roman soldier and Nebene and the woman went their separate ways after death, colon: They contributed to the world as it existed, in those terms, and then followed their own lines of development, elsewhere, in other realities. So each of you exists in many times and places, and versions of yourselves exist in the world and time that you recognize. As you are part of a physical species, so you are a part of a species of consciousness. That species forms the races of mankind that you recognize.
quotes.translation: 罗马士兵梦见了黑女人和约瑟。即使在细胞的知识中也存在一种回忆，以及某种对应关系。14 就细胞记忆和梦境而言，存在着种种关联。现在，罗马士兵、内贝内和那女人死后各奔前程：他们以那些术语为当时存在的世界做出贡献，随后在别处、在其他实相中遵循各自的发展路线。因此，你们每个人都存在于许多时空之中，而你们自身的版本也存在于你们所认知的世界与时间里。正如你们是物质物种的一部分，你们也是意识物种的一部分。那个物种形成了你们所认知的人类种族。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: The play of sunlight or shadow upon any given rock may utterly escape you.5 You will simply pass it by under the category of "rock." In the dream state you might find yourself sleeping on a sunwarmed rock, or climbing on icy ones. You might feel yourself encased in a rock, with your consciousness dispersed. You might have any number of different experiences involving rocks, all quite liberating. After such an experience you might look at rocks in an entirely different fashion, and see them in ways that escape your language. Rocks give forth sounds that you do not hear, for example, yet your language automatically limits your perception of what any rock is. To some extent words come between you and your direct expression. They should and can express that experience instead.
quotes.translation: 阳光或阴影在岩石上的嬉戏，你可能会完全视而不见。你只会简单地把它归类为"岩石"而忽略过去。在梦境状态中，你可能会发现自己躺在被阳光晒暖的岩石上，或攀爬在冰冷的岩石上。你可能会感觉自己被包裹在岩石中，意识分散。你可能会经历无数种与岩石相关的不同体验，全都相当解放。经过这样的体验后，你可能会以完全不同的方式看待岩石，并以你的语言无法描述的方式感知它们。例如，岩石会发出你听不到的声音，然而你的语言自动限制了你对岩石本质的感知。在某种程度上，文字横亘在你和你的直接表达之间。它们应该且能够表达那种体验。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: Give us a moment ... Selfhood overspills with great luxurious outcroppings, yet you jealously guard against such creativity. To a certain extent you do carry the knowledge of your forefathers within your [cells'] chromosomes,1 which present a pattern that is not rigid but flexible -- one that in codified fashion endows you with the subjective living experience of those who, in your terms, have gone before. As Ruburt recently suspected, some very old cultures have been aware of this.2 Period. While being independent individuals their members also identified with their ancestors to some extent, accepting them as portions of their selfhoods. This does not mean that the individual self was less, but was more aware of its own reality. A completely different kind of focus was presented, in which the ancestors were understood to contribute to the "new" experience of the living; one in which the physically focused consciousness clearly saw itself as perceiving the world for itself, but also for all of those who had gone before -- (gradually louder for emphasis:) while realizing that in those terms he or she would contribute as well as the generations past.
quotes.translation: 请稍等……自体性以丰盛的外显形式满溢而出，然而你们却小心翼翼地防范这种创造力。在某种程度上，你们确实将祖先的知识携带在[细胞]的染色体中，这呈现出一个并非僵化而是灵活的模式——一个以编码方式赋予你们那些，按你们说法，已逝者主观生活经验的模式。正如鲁柏最近所怀疑的，一些非常古老的文化早已意识到这一点。句号。虽然其成员是独立的个体，但他们也在某种程度上与祖先认同，将祖先视为自体性的一部分。这并不意味着个体自我更少，而是更意识到自己的实相。一种完全不同的焦点被呈现出来，在这种焦点中，祖先被理解为对生者的"新"经验有所贡献；在这种焦点中，聚焦于物质世界的意识清楚地看到自己不仅为自己感知世界，也为所有已逝者感知世界——（逐渐提高音量以强调：）同时认识到，按那些说法，他或她也将像过去的世代一样做出贡献。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: (I want to add here that our real challenge in knowing our own species, and others, may lie in our cultivating the ability to understand the interacting consciousnesses involved, rather than to search only for physical relationships supposedly created through evolutionary processes. The challenge is profound. The consciousnesses of numerous other species may be so different from ours that we only approximately grasp the meanings inherent in some of them, and miss the essences of others entirely. To give just two examples, at this time we are surely opaque to the seemingly endless search for value fulfillment that consciousness displays through the "lowly" lung fish and the "unattractive" cockroach. Yet those entities are quite immune to our notions of evolution, and they explore time contexts in ways far beyond our current human comprehension. As far as science knows, both have existed with very little change for over 300 million years.
quotes.translation: （我想在此补充说明，我们认识自身物种及其他物种的真正挑战，或许在于培养理解其中所涉及的各种互动意识的能力，而非仅仅追寻据称通过进化过程产生的物理关系。这个挑战意义深远。众多其他物种的意识可能与我们的意识截然不同，以至于我们只能大致把握其中某些物种所蕴含的意义，而完全错失其他物种的本质。仅举两个例子，目前我们显然无法理解意识通过"低等"的肺鱼和"不起眼"的蟑螂所展现的那种看似永无止境的价值实现追求。然而这些实体对我们的进化概念完全免疫，它们探索时间脉络的方式远超我们当前人类的理解范畴。就科学所知，两者在三亿多年间几乎未发生任何变化。）
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: If we must speak in terms of continuity, which I regret, then in those terms you could say that life in the physical universe, on your planet, "began" spontaneously in a given number of species at the same time. Words do nearly forsake me, the semantic differences are so vast. In those terms there was a point where consciousness, through intent, impressed itself into matter. That "breakthrough" cannot be logically explained, but only compared to, say, an illumination -- that is, a light occurring everywhere at once, that became a medium for life as you define it. It had nothing to do with the propensity of certain kinds of cells to reproduce -- [all cells are] imbued with the "drive" for value fulfillment -- but with an overall illumination that set the conditions in which life was possible as you think of it; and at that imaginary, hypothetical point, all species became latent. The inner pulsations of the invisible universe reached certain intensities that "impregnated" the entire physical system simultaneously. That illumination was everywhere then at every point aware of itself, and of the conditions formed by its presence.
quotes.translation: （若必须用连续性来表述——我对此深感遗憾——那么用这些术语来说，你们可以认为物理宇宙中你们星球上的生命，是在特定数量的物种中同时"自发开始"的。语言几乎让我束手无策，语义差异实在太大。用这些术语来说，曾有一个时刻，意识通过意图将自己烙印于物质之中。这种"突破"无法用逻辑解释，只能比喻为一种照明——即同时发生在所有地方的光，成为你们所定义的生命媒介。这与某些细胞繁殖的倾向无关——所有细胞都充满价值实现的"驱力"——而是与一种整体性的照明有关，它设定了你们所认为的生命得以存在的条件；在那个虚构的假设性时刻，所有物种都成为潜在状态。无形宇宙的内在脉动达到某种强度，同时"孕育"了整个物理系统。那时那光无处不在，在每一点上都意识到自身，以及由其存在所形成的条件。）
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: It's often been claimed that Darwin's natural selection, while ruling out any question of design or a planner -- God, say -- behind living matter, leaves unexplained the same question relative to the structure of nonliving matter, which in those terms obviously preceded life. I'd rather approach that argument through another statement Seth made in Chapter 20 of Seth Speaks (in the 582nd session): "You are biologically connected, chemically connected with the Earth that you know...." How is it that as living creatures we're made up of ingredients -- atoms of iron, molecules of water, for instance -- from a supposedly dead world? In the scientific view we're utterly dependent upon that contradictory situation. No one denies the amazing structure or design of our physical universe, from the scale of subatomic particles on "up" (regardless of what cosmological theory is used to explain the universe's beginning). The study of design as one of the links between "living" and "nonliving" systems would certainly be a difficult challenge -- but a most rewarding one, I think -- for science. I have little idea of how the work would be carried out. Evidently it would lead from biology through microbiology to physics with, ultimately, a search that at least approached Seth's electromagnetic energy (EE) units and units of consciousness (CU's). Yet according to Seth, both classes of "particles" are in actuality nonphysical; as best words can note, they have their realities on scales so minute that we cannot hope to detect them through our present technology....
quotes.translation: 人们常声称达尔文的自然选择虽排除了生命物质背后存在设计或规划者（比如上帝）的可能性，却未能解释非生命物质结构中的相同问题——按此理论，非生命物质显然先于生命存在。我更愿通过赛斯在《赛斯书》第20章（第582节）中的另一段论述来探讨这个论点："你们在生物学上与你们所知的地球相连，在化学上与它相连……"作为生物，我们如何由来自所谓死寂世界的成分——例如铁原子、水分子——构成？在科学视角下，我们完全依赖于这种矛盾状态。无论用何种宇宙学理论解释宇宙起源，从亚原子粒子尺度"向上"延伸，无人否认我们物质宇宙的惊人结构或设计。将设计作为"生命"与"非生命"系统之间的纽带进行研究，对科学而言无疑是艰巨挑战——但我认为这是最有价值的探索。我几乎不知如何开展这项工作，显然它将从生物学经由微生物学通向物理学，最终至少会触及赛斯的电磁能量单元（EE单位）与意识单元（CU）。然而据赛斯所言，这两类"粒子"实际上是非物质的；用最贴切的文字描述，它们存在于如此微小的尺度，以至于我们无法通过现有技术探测到……
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: Somehow the twice-yearly, north-and-south migrations of the geese have become symbols for me of the known and unknown qualities of life -- sublime and indecipherable at the same time, enduring yet fleeting, and almost outside of the range of human events. For me, those migrations have become portents of the seasons and of the earth itself as it swings around "our" sun in great rhythms. The one consciousness (mine) stands in its body on the ground and looks up at the strange variations of itself represented by the geese. And wonders. In their own ways, do the geese wonder also? What kind of hidden interchanges between species take place at such times? If the question could he answered, would all of reality in its unending mystery lie revealed before us?
quotes.translation: 不知何故，大雁每年两次的南北迁徙，对我而言已成为生命已知与未知特质的象征——既崇高又不可解读，既持久又短暂，几乎超乎人类事件的范畴。对我来说，这些迁徙已成为季节和地球本身的预兆，地球以宏大韵律围绕"我们的"太阳旋转。那唯一的意识（我的意识）立于地面上的身体中，仰望由大雁所代表的自身奇异变体。并感到惊奇。以它们自己的方式，大雁是否也感到惊奇？在这样的时刻，物种之间隐藏着怎样的交流？如果这个问题能得到解答，所有现实那无尽的奥秘是否就会展现在我们面前？
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: Consciousness forms symbols. It is not the other way around. Symbols are great exuberant playthings. You can build with them as you can with children's blocks. You can learn from them, as once you piled alphabet blocks together in a stack at school. Symbols are as natural to your minds as trees are to the earth. There is a difference, however, between a story told to children about forests, and a real child in a real woods. Both the story and the woods are "real." But in your terms the child entering the real woods becomes involved in its life cycle, treads upon leaves that fell yesterday, rests beneath trees far older than his or her memory, and looks up at night to see a moon that will soon disappear. Looking at an illustration of the woods may give a child some excellent imaginative experiences, but they will be of a different kind, and the child knows the difference.
quotes.translation: 意识形成符号，而非相反。符号是极尽丰盛的玩物。你可以像搭积木般用它们构建，如同当年在学校将字母积木堆叠成塔。符号之于心灵，犹如树木之于大地。然而，给孩子讲述森林的故事，与让孩子真正走进森林，两者截然不同。故事与森林都是"真实的"，但以你们的说法，走进真实森林的孩子会参与其生命循环：踏过昨日飘落的树叶，在比记忆更古老的树下休憩，仰望夜空中即将隐没的月亮。观看森林插图或许能给孩子绝妙的想象体验，但那将是另一种体验——孩子自己明白其中的差异。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche

quotes.text: As I have mentioned many times, cellular comprehension deals with probabilities and encompasses future and past, so at that level of activity time as you understand it does not exist. You are not consciously aware of such data, however. The psyche -- at the other end of the scale, so to speak -- is also free of time. Often, however, your own stream of consciousness leads you to think of events outside of their usual order. You may receive a letter from your Aunt Bessie, for example. In a matter of moments it may trigger you to think of events in your childhood, so that many mental images fly through your mind. You might wonder if your aunt will take an anticipated journey to Europe next year, and that thought might give birth to images of an imagined future. All of these thoughts and images will be colored by the emotions that are connected to the letter, and to all of the events with which you and your aunt have been involved.
quotes.translation: 如我多次提及，细胞认知处理着可能性，涵盖未来与过去，因此在那个活动层面，你们所理解的时间并不存在。然而你们并未有意识地觉察这类信息。可以说，在光谱另一端的心理同样不受时间束缚。但你们意识流的运作常使事件脱离惯常顺序。例如收到贝茜姑妈的来信，瞬间可能触发童年回忆，无数心灵影像掠过脑海。你或许会猜想姑妈明年是否按计划前往欧洲，这个念头又衍生出对想象未来的影像。所有这些思绪与影像，都将被与信件相关的情绪，以及你与姑妈共同经历的所有事件所浸染。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche

quotes.text: (11:16.) This unlimited world constantly replenished itself. Children came from women's wombs. Man was acquainted with death, and many children were stillborn, or were naturally aborted. This also, however, was in the natural order of things, and was done far more easily then than now. All flower seeds do not fall on fertile ground and bring forth other flowers. The seeds that do not grow go [back] into the ground, forming the basis for other life. Biologically speaking, fetuses grow and develop -- I am going slowly here because I am being tricky -- and when innate consciousness merges with proper form, the conditions are right for the birth of a healthy child. When the conditions are not right, the child does not develop properly. Nature aborts it. The physical elements return to the earth to become the basis for other life.
quotes.translation: （11:16）这个无限世界不断自我更新。孩童从母体子宫诞生。人类熟悉死亡，许多婴儿胎死腹中或自然流产。然而这同样属于自然秩序，且在当时比现在容易得多。并非所有花种都能落入沃土绽放新花。未能生长的种子回归大地，成为其他生命的基础。从生物学角度而言——此处我将放慢语速，因涉及微妙之处——当先天意识与适当形体融合，健康婴儿诞生的条件便已成熟。若条件不符，胎儿便无法正常发育。自然法则会终止其生长，物质元素回归大地，成为其他生命的基础。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche

quotes.text: The more able the child is to force such an artificial identification, the greater its feelings of inner rebellion. The lack of a "suitable" father or mother image has "saved" more children than it has hurt. The psyche, with its great gifts, always feels thwarted and attempts to take countering measures. Your schools further continue the process, however, so that the areas of curiosity and learning become separated for males and females. The "she" within the male does indeed represent portions of his personality that are being unexpressed -- not because of any natural predominance of mental or emotional characteristics over others, but because of artificial specializations. The same applies to the "male" within the female. You have accepted this version of personhood, again, in line with your ideas about the nature of consciousness. Those ideas are changing, and as they do the species must accept its true personhood. As this happens, your understanding will allow you to glimpse the nature of the reality of the gods you have recognized through the ages. You will no longer need to clothe them in limited sexual guises.
quotes.translation: 孩子越是能够强行进行这种人为的认同，其内在的叛逆感就越强烈。缺乏"合适"的父亲或母亲形象，实际上拯救的孩子比伤害的更多。拥有伟大天赋的心灵总是感到受挫，并试图采取对抗措施。然而你们的学校进一步延续了这一过程，使得好奇心和学习的领域在男女之间被割裂开来。男性内在的"她"确实代表了他个性中未被表达的部分——这并非因为心智或情感特征天然优于其他，而是由于人为的专门化。女性内在的"他"也是如此。你们接受了这种人格版本，再次与你们关于意识本质的观念相符。这些观念正在改变，随着改变的发生，物种必须接纳其真实的人格。当这发生时，你们的理解将使你们得以瞥见历代以来所认知的神祇实相的本质。你们将不再需要用有限的性别伪装来包裹他们。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche

quotes.text: Those experienced events, however, are also the result of a screening process. They attain their focus, brilliance, and physical validity because they rise into prominence on the backs of other seemingly unperceived events. In the dream state you work intimately with the "inner grammar" of events. In dreams you find the unspoken sentence and the physically unexperienced act. The skeletons of the inner workings of events are there more obvious. Actions are not yet fully fleshed out. The mechanics of your waking psychological behavior are brilliantly delineated. That state can be explored and utilized far more fully than it is, and should be. Yet there will always be a veil between the waking and sleeping consciousness, for while you are physical, the waking mind can only deal with so much information. It would simply forget what it cannot hold.
quotes.translation: 那些被体验的事件，同样也是筛选过程的结果。它们之所以能获得焦点、鲜明度与物质有效性，是因为它们借由其他看似未被感知的事件作为支撑而凸显出来。在梦境状态中，你与事件的"内在语法"紧密合作。在梦中，你发现未被言说的句子与未被物理体验的行为。事件内在运作的骨架在那里更为明显。行动尚未完全成形。你清醒时心理行为的机制被鲜明地勾勒出来。那种状态可以被探索并更充分地利用——远比现在更充分，也应当如此。然而，清醒意识与梦境意识之间始终存在一层帷幕，因为当你处于物质形态时，清醒心智只能处理有限的信息。它只会忘记自己无法承载的内容。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche

quotes.text: Again, I do not mean to deny the validity of that experience, but to point out its specialized nature. By its nature, however, that precise specialization and tuning of consciousness in to space and time largely precludes other less-specialized encounters with realities. Dreams often present you with what seems to be an ambiguity, an opaqueness, since they lack the immediate impact of psychological activity with space and time. From your viewpoint it seems often that dreams are not events, or that they happen but do not happen. The lack of normal time and space intersections means that you cannot share your dreams with others in the way that you can share waking events. Nor can you remember dream events -- or so it seems -- as you do your normal conscious experience. In actual fact you remember consciously only certain highlighted events of your lives, and ordinary details of your days vanish as dreams seem to.
quotes.translation: 我并非要否认那种体验的有效性，而是要指出其专门化的本质。就其本质而言，意识对时空的精准专门化与调谐，在很大程度上排除了其他较不专门化的现实接触。梦境常常呈现给你看似模糊不清、晦涩难懂的内容，因为它们缺乏心理活动在时空中的直接冲击力。从你的视角来看，梦境往往不像事件，或者它们发生了却又像没发生。缺乏正常的时空交汇点意味着你无法像分享清醒事件那样与他人分享梦境。你似乎也无法像记住正常意识体验那样记住梦境事件。事实上，你清醒时也只记得生命中某些突出的事件，而日常的琐碎细节就像梦境一样消失无踪。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche

quotes.text: I am what I am, interpreted through your reality. I have personality characteristics by which those who have come to sessions can identify me. I have a peculiarity of voice and accent that is, if I may say so myself, unique and individualistic. Yet I come to your reality by a strange route -- one that does not involve roads or highways but psychological dramas that wind backward like paths into the "psychological history" of your species. To some extent, I am like a particularly vivid, persistent, recurring dream image, visiting the mass psyche, only with a reality that is not confined to dreams -- a dream image that attains a psychological fullness that can seem to make ordinary consciousness a weak apparition by contrast, psychologically speaking.
quotes.translation: 我是我所是，经由你们的现实被诠释。我拥有那些参加课程的人能辨识我的人格特征。我有独特的声音与口音——恕我直言，这独一无二且充满个性。然而，我通过一条奇特的路径来到你们的现实——这条路径不涉及道路或高速公路，而是如同蜿蜒小径般回溯至你们物种的"心理历史"的心理剧。在某种程度上，我就像一个特别鲜明、持久、反复出现的梦境意象，造访集体心灵，只是拥有不受限于梦境的现实——一个获得心理圆满性的梦境意象，从心理学角度而言，这种圆满性反而让普通意识显得像虚弱的幻影。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche

quotes.text: (11:23.) On the one hand, dreaming on the part of animals -- and men in particular -- involves not only information processing, but information gathering. Dreaming prevents life from becoming closed-ended by opening sources of information not practically available in the waking state, and by providing feedback from other than the conventional world. Data gained through waking learning endeavor and experience are checked in dreaming, not only against physical experience, but are also processed according to those "biological" and "spiritual" data, colon: Again, that information is acquired as the sleeping consciousness disperses itself, in a manner of speaking, and merges with other consciousnesses of its own and other species while still retaining its overall identity. These [other consciousnesses] are dispersed in like manner.
quotes.translation: （11点23分。）一方面，动物——尤其是人类——的做梦不仅涉及信息处理，还涉及信息收集。做梦通过开启清醒状态下无法实际获得的信息来源，并提供来自常规世界之外的反馈，防止生命变得封闭。通过清醒时的学习努力和经验获得的数据，在梦中不仅会对照物质经验进行检验，还会根据那些“生物性”和“灵性”数据进行处理：再说一次，当睡眠中的意识以某种方式分散自身，并与自身及其他物种的其他意识融合，同时仍保持其整体身份时，这些信息就被获取了。这些[其他意识]也以类似方式分散。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche

quotes.text: While the large proportion remain relatively hidden, however, the average person often meets with dream fragments just below the normal threshold of consciousness -- not recognizing them as what they are -- experiencing instead the impulse to do this or that on a given day; to eat this or that, or to refrain from something else. An easy enough example is the case where an individual with no memory [of such a dream] decides to cancel a plane trip on a given day, and later discovers that the plane crashed. The impulse to cancel may or may not seem to have an acceptable, rational explanation; that is, for no seeming reason, the individual may simply, impulsively, feel a premonition. On the other hand the impulse might appear as a normal, logical change of plan.
