quotes.translation: 然而，虽然大部分仍相对隐藏，但普通人常常在正常意识阈限之下遇到梦的片段——不认出它们是什么——反而在特定一天体验到做这做那的冲动；吃这个或那个，或避免做别的事。一个简单的例子是，当一个人[对此梦]毫无记忆，却决定取消某天的飞机行程，后来发现那架飞机坠毁了。取消的冲动可能看似有合理的理性解释，也可能没有；也就是说，似乎毫无理由地，这个人可能只是冲动地感到一种预感。另一方面，这种冲动也可能表现为一个正常、合乎逻辑的计划变更。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche

quotes.text: Such events occur as a result of individual beliefs, desires, and intents. There is no such thing as a chance encounter. No death occurs by chance, nor any birth. In the creative atmosphere of Framework 2, intents are known. In a manner of speaking, no act is private. Your communication systems bring to your living room notices of events that occur throughout the world. Yet that larger inner system of communications is far more powerful in scope, and each mental act is imprinted in the multidimensional screen of Framework 2. That screen is available to all, and in other levels of consciousness, particularly in the sleep and dreaming stages, the events of that inner reality are as ever-present and easily accessible as physical events are when you are awake.
quotes.translation: 此类事件源于个体信念、欲望和意图的结果。不存在偶然的相遇。没有死亡是偶然发生的，也没有出生是偶然的。在架构二的创造性氛围中，意图是可知的。可以说，没有任何行为是私密的。你们的通讯系统将世界各地发生的事件信息传送到客厅。然而那个更宏大的内在通讯系统在范围上要强大得多，每个心智行为都烙印在架构二的多维屏幕上。这个屏幕对所有人开放，在其他意识层面，特别是在睡眠和梦境阶段，内在实相的事件就像你们清醒时物理事件一样随时可得、易于触及。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: Much of this is difficult to explain, again, for information and knowledge is constantly transformed -- almost completely reborn, so to speak, through characteristics that are inherently a part of thought itself. Knowledge is changed automatically through the auspices of each consciousness who perceives it. It is magnified and yet refined. It is a constant language, yet one that transforms itself. When I "attend these sessions," or "speak," then I exchange with others a more complicated system of reality than any computer could handle. You do not understand or perceive the ways in which your reality contributes to the foundation of the mass-world reality that you experience. Unconsciously, each individual participates in forming that world. In my case, however, I am aware of the same kind of activities, only in regard to many realities rather than one.2
quotes.translation: 这些内容大多难以解释，因为信息和知识不断被转化——可以说，通过思想本身固有的特性，它们几乎被完全重塑。知识经由感知它的每个意识的引导而自动改变，被放大却又被精炼。它是一种恒常的语言，却又是一种自我转化的语言。当我“出席这些课程”或“发言”时，我与他人交流的是一种比任何计算机都能处理的更复杂的现实系统。你们并不理解或感知到你们的现实如何为你们所经历的大众世界现实奠定基础。无意识中，每个人都在参与形成那个世界。然而就我而言，我意识到同样的活动，只是涉及许多现实而非单一现实。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: Once scientists theorized the ether as the medium in which the physical universe existed.1 Framework 2 is the psychological medium in which the consciousness of the world exists. The word "ego" is much bandied about, and in many circles it has a poor reputation. It is, however, as I use it, a term meant to express the ordinarily conscious directive portion of the self. It is your conscious version of what you are -- an excellent description, if I do say so myself (with amusement). It is directed outward into the physical world. It is also aware, however, of some of your "unconscious" activities. It is the you you identify with, so it is as aware of your dreams, for example, as you are, and it is quite conscious of the fact that its existence rests upon knowledge that it does not itself possess.
quotes.translation: 科学家曾假设以太是物理宇宙存在的介质。框架二是世界意识存在的心理介质。“自我”这个词被广泛使用，在许多圈子里名声不佳。然而，正如我所使用的，它是一个术语，意在表达自我通常有意识的指导部分。它是你对自身的有意识版本——如果我可以这么说的话（带着幽默），这是一个极好的描述。它向外指向物理世界，但也意识到你的一些“无意识”活动。它是你认同的那个“你”，因此它同样意识到你的梦境，例如，就像你意识到它们一样，并且它很清楚它的存在依赖于它自身并不拥有的知识。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: Obviously there are objects of all sizes, durability, and weight. There are private objects and public ones. There are also "vast psychological objects," then, sweeping mass events, for example, in which whole countries might be involved. There are also mass natural events of varying degrees, as say, the flooding of large areas. Such events involve psychological configurations on the part of all those involved, so that the inner individual patterns of those lives touched by each such event have in one way or another a common purpose that at the same time serves the overall reality on a natural planetary basis. In order to endure, the planet itself must be involved in constant change and instability. I know it is difficult to comprehend, but every object that you perceive -- grass or rock or stone -- even ocean waves or clouds -- any physical phenomenon -- has its own invisible consciousness, its own intent and emotional coloration. Each is also endowed with patterns toward growth and fulfillment -- not at the expense of the rest of nature, but to the contrary, so that every other element of nature may also be completed (all with much emphasis).
quotes.translation: 显然，存在各种大小、耐久度和重量的物体。有私人物体和公共物体。那么也存在“巨大的心理物体”，例如席卷整个国家可能卷入的广泛大众事件。还有不同程度的自然事件，比如大面积洪水。这类事件涉及所有相关者的心理构型，因此每个事件所触及的那些生命的内在个体模式，都以某种方式拥有一个共同目的，同时服务于行星自然基础上的整体现实。为了存续，行星本身必须卷入持续的变化和不稳定。我知道这难以理解，但你所感知的每一个物体——草、岩石或石头——甚至海浪或云朵——任何物理现象——都有其自身的无形意识、自身意图和情感色彩。每一个也被赋予了朝向成长和圆满的模式——不是以牺牲自然其余部分为代价，恰恰相反，以便自然的每一个其他元素也能得以完成（全部着重强调）。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: You cannot begin to have a true psychology, again, unless you see the living self in a greater context, with greater motives, purposes and meanings than you now assign to it, or for that matter than you assign to nature and its creatures. You have denied many impulses, or programmed others so that they are allowed expression in only certain forms of action. If any of you do (underlined) still believe in the Freudian or Darwinian selves, then you will be leery about impulses to examine your own consciousness, afraid of what murderous debris might be uncovered. I am not speaking merely in hypothetical terms. For example, a well-intentioned woman was here recently. She worried about her overweight condition, and [was] depressed at what she thought of as her lack of discipline in following diets. In her dismay, she visited a psychologist, who told her that her marriage might somehow be part of the problem. The woman said she never went back. She was afraid that she might discover within herself the buried impulse to kill her husband, or to break up the marriage, but she was sure that her overweight condition hid some unfortunate impulse.
quotes.translation: 再次强调，除非你在更大的背景下看待活生生的自己，赋予它比你现在所赋予的——或者就此事而言，比你所赋予自然及其生物的——更伟大的动机、目的和意义，否则你无法开始拥有真正的心理学。你否认了许多冲动，或者将其他冲动编程，使它们只允许以某些特定形式的行动表达。如果你们中任何人（确实）仍然相信弗洛伊德或达尔文式的自我，那么你们会对审视自己意识的冲动保持警惕，害怕可能会发现什么凶残的碎片。我并非仅仅在假设性地说。例如，最近有一位善意的女士来到这里。她担心自己的超重状况，并因认为自己缺乏节食纪律而感到沮丧。在沮丧中，她拜访了一位心理学家，后者告诉她，她的婚姻可能部分地是问题所在。这位女士说她再也没有回去。她害怕自己可能会发现内心深处埋藏着杀死丈夫或破坏婚姻的冲动，但她确信自己的超重状况隐藏着某种不幸的冲动。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: I keep wondering about the results of an individual's choosing not to call upon any of his or her bank of reincarnational lives, though, whether from the past or the future. This approach would very nicely eliminate having to deal with one's "karma" this time around--should there really be a system of consciousness embodying that ancient concept. Think of the fun a person could have who decided at an early age--or even before physical birth--to experience a life unencumbered by other psychic relationships; wherein it had little or nothing to "work out." What freedoms might lie ahead--and yes, what challenges, too! Buddhism and Hinduism would banish the very thought: How dare one even think of escaping, or just simply ignoring, his or her "fate or destiny" (to put it loosely)! Yet our mass reality obviously is large enough to allow me room to generate such fanatical thoughts....
quotes.translation: 我一直在思考，如果一个人选择不调用他或她的任何一世转世生命库——无论是来自过去还是未来——结果会怎样。这种方法可以巧妙地避免今生处理自己的“业力”——如果确实存在一个体现这一古老概念的意识体系的话。想想看，一个人如果在早年——甚至在肉身出生之前——就决定体验一段不受其他心灵关系牵绊的人生，其中几乎没有什么需要“解决”的，那该多有趣。前方会有怎样的自由——是的，还有怎样的挑战！佛教和印度教可能会摒弃这种想法：一个人怎敢想着逃避，或者干脆无视他或她的“命运或天命”（粗略地说）！然而，我们的集体实相显然足够广阔，足以让我有空间产生这些狂热的念头……
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1

quotes.text: It would be impossible at this time, I'm certain, for a researcher to find any evidence that reincarnational heritages are coded for among the approximately 100,000 genes lined up on the 46 chromosomes we carry in the nucleus of each of our cells. We say that a certain gene contains the instructions for the manufacture of a certain protein the body uses in the construction or function of an eye, for instance, and that in expressing that code the gene passes on characteristics inherited from physical ancestors--but is that endowment influenced or directed in any fashion by reincarnational attributes as well? Might those factors be just as potent as those inherited from a grandfather, say? The genes in each cell have their individual jobs to do in furnishing the quivering templates for the manufacture (via the nucleic acids DNA and messenger RNA) of all of our bodily proteins. But if we think of our genetic endowment as first being a system of consciousness as our reincarnational history is, we can see how the two nonphysical systems could be intermixed, as Seth put it, with one influencing the other. Conceivably, each of us could be a mixed bag of ancestral and reincarnational heritages, then--more "mongrelized" than we may care to admit. Interesting.... What we choose to do with those possibilities that we present ourselves with at each temporal birth may be another matter entirely.
quotes.translation: 我确信，在目前这个时代，研究人员不可能找到任何证据表明转世遗产被编码在我们每个细胞核中46条染色体上排列的大约10万个基因里。我们说某个基因包含制造某种蛋白质的指令，比如身体用于构建或运作眼睛的蛋白质，并且在表达该密码时，基因传递了从肉身祖先那里继承的特征——但这种禀赋是否也以某种方式受到转世属性的影响或引导？那些因素是否可能和从祖父那里继承的因素一样强大？每个细胞中的基因各有其职责，通过核酸DNA和信使RNA为制造我们所有身体蛋白质提供颤动的模板。但如果我们把基因禀赋首先视为一个意识系统——就像我们的转世历史那样——我们就能看到这两个非物理系统如何相互混合，正如赛斯所说，一个影响另一个。可以想象，我们每个人可能都是祖先遗产和转世遗产的混合体——比我们愿意承认的更加“混杂”。有趣的是……我们选择如何利用每次时间性出生时呈现给自己的那些可能性，可能完全是另一回事。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1

quotes.text: Within the idea of probable realities, then, there are innumerable opportunities for redemption to take place, between or among creatures--or even between or among ideas--and in all manner of ways. In how many ways? Seth remarked a long time ago that we humans can at least approach the notion of infinity by considering the ramifications inherent within probabilities. For my own amusement, in recent years I've often tried to objectify that statement by equating the possible number of probable realities with the current scientific estimate of the number of atoms in the universe: 1079, or a 1 followed by 79 zeroes. But even if that rather simple number is inconceivable to us it still won't do, of course, for it represents only a limit of measurement inside the "physical" universe we think we know. Within the limitless realms of consciousness, 1079 is still but a doorway to vastly greater imaginative quantities and qualities of either numbers or probable realities. Fascinating! There are multitudinous possibilities for a redemption--or equalization or love or forgiveness, say--to take place amid such a dazzling array of probable realities. As far as our understanding can go, such a redemptive quality can be psychic, physical, both, or simply based on explorations of feeling and accomplishment we have yet to know.
quotes.translation: 因此，在可能实相的概念中，存在着无数救赎的机会，发生在生物之间——甚至观念之间——并以各种方式实现。有多少种方式？赛斯很久以前说过，我们人类至少可以通过考虑概率中固有的分支来接近无限的概念。为了自娱自乐，近年来我经常试图将这一说法具体化，将可能实相的可能数量等同于当前科学对宇宙中原子数量的估计：10的79次方，即1后面跟79个零。但即使这个相当简单的数字对我们来说难以想象，它当然仍然不够，因为它只代表了我们认为自己知道的“物理”宇宙内的测量极限。在意识的无限领域中，10的79次方仍然只是通往更宏大想象数量和质量的数字或可能实相的大门。迷人！在如此令人眼花缭乱的可能实相中，存在着无数救赎——或者说平衡、爱或宽恕——的可能性。就我们所能理解的范围而言，这种救赎性质可以是心灵的、物理的、两者兼有，或者仅仅基于我们尚未知晓的感受和成就的探索。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1

quotes.text: After six months, then, Three Mile Island is still "a closed enigma," as I wrote in finishing Mass Events--only now the costs for the repair and cleanup of its damaged reactor have been projected as being well over $1 billion instead of the $40 million to $400 million of just a month ago, and into many years of "time" instead of just four. TMI has become the unfortunate symbol of our unprepared experimentation with a nature that contains all sorts of surprises for us; especially when, as Seth maintains, each of those "surprises," once created, becomes conscious in its own way. [I do believe that this kind of thinking is totally unacceptable to most businessmen, as well as generally to the public they serve, the irony here being that neither businessman or scientist can explain what that fantastic nuclear energy--or any energy, for that matter--really is. In the frontmatter, see the first of the four quotations from Seth; the one taken from a private session given just two months ago: "All energy contains consciousness (underlined). ... A recognition of that simple statement would indeed change your world. "]
quotes.translation: 六个月后，三里岛仍然是"一个封闭的谜团"，正如我在完成《群体事件》时所写——只是现在，修复和清理受损反应堆的成本预计已超过10亿美元，而一个月前还只是4000万到4亿美元；所需"时间"也从仅仅四年延长到许多年。三里岛已成为我们未经准备就进行实验的不幸象征，这个实验涉及一种充满各种惊喜的自然；尤其是，正如赛斯所主张的，这些"惊喜"一旦被创造出来，就会以各自的方式变得有意识。[我确实相信，这种想法对大多数商人以及他们服务的大众来说是完全不可接受的，讽刺的是，商人和科学家都无法解释那奇妙的核能——或者就此而言，任何能量——究竟是什么。在前言部分，请参见赛斯四段引文中的第一段；它摘自两个月前的一次私人课："所有能量都包含意识（下划线）。……认识到这个简单的陈述确实会改变你的世界。"]
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1

quotes.text: Mountains rose and tumbled. Oceans filled. Tidal waves thundered. Islands appeared. The seasons themselves were not stable. In your terms the magnetic fields themselves fluctuated--but all of the species were there at the beginning, though in the same fashion, for as the dream world broke through into physical reality there was all of the tumultuous excitement and confusion with which a mass creative event is achieved. There was much greater plasticity, motion, variety, give-and-take, as consciousness experimented with its own forms. The species and environment together formed themselves in concert, in glorious combination, so that each fulfilled the requirements of its own existence while adding to the fulfillment of all other portions of physical reality (all very intently, and with many gestures).
quotes.translation: 山峦隆起又崩落。海洋盈满。海啸轰鸣。岛屿浮现。季节本身也不稳定。以你们的时间尺度而言，连磁场本身都在波动——但所有物种在最初就已存在，尽管以同样的方式，因为当梦境世界突破进入物质实相时，伴随着一场集体创造事件得以实现的所有骚动兴奋与混乱。当时有更大的可塑性、运动、多样性、互动交流，意识以其自身形态进行实验。物种与环境共同协调地形成自身，以辉煌的组合方式，使得每个物种既满足自身存在的需求，又增添了物质实相所有其他部分的圆满（这一切都极为专注，且伴随着许多姿态）。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1

quotes.text: These EE units also operate as fields, as waves, or as particles, as the units of consciousness do--but in your terms they are closer to physical orientation. Their die is cast, so to speak: They have already begun the special kind of screening process necessary that will bring about physical form. They begin to deal with the kinds of information that will help form your world. There are literally numberless steps taken before EE units combine in their own fashion to form the most microscopic physical particles, and even here the greatest, gentlest sorting-out process takes place as these units disentangle themselves at certain operational levels (underlined) from their own greater fields of "information," to specialize in the various elements that will allow for the production of atoms and molecules impeccably suited to your kind of world.
quotes.translation: 这些EE单位也像意识单位一样，以场、波或粒子的方式运作——但以你们的时间尺度而言，它们更接近物质取向。可以说，它们的命运已定：它们已开始进行必要的特殊筛选过程，这将带来物质形态。它们开始处理有助于形成你们世界的信息。在EE单位以自身方式结合形成最微观的物质粒子之前，实际上经历了无数步骤，即便在此处，当这些单位在特定运作层面（下划线强调）从自身更广阔的"信息"场中解脱出来，专精于各种元素——这些元素将允许产生完美适合你们这类世界的原子和分子——时，最伟大、最温和的筛选过程仍在发生。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1

quotes.text: You could not afford to identify too completely with such bodies until you learned how to survive within them, so in the dream state (pause) the true processes of life began as these new bodies and earth-tuned consciousnesses saw themselves mentally exercising all portions of the body. Behind all that was the brilliant comprehension and cooperation of all of the units of consciousness that go to compose the body, each adding its own information and specific knowledge to the overall bodily organizations, and each involved in the most intricate fields of relationships, for the miracle of the body's efficiency is the result of relationships that exist among all of its parts, connecting it to other levels of existence that do not physically appear.
quotes.translation: 在你们学会如何在这些身体中生存之前，你们无法承受与这些身体过于完全地认同，因此在梦境状态（停顿）中，生命的真正过程开始了，因为这些新身体和调谐于地球的意识看到自己在心智中锻炼身体的各个部分。在这一切背后，是构成身体的所有意识单位的辉煌理解与合作，每个单位都将其自身信息和特定知识添加到整体身体组织中，每个单位都参与最复杂的关系场域，因为身体效率的奇迹是其所有部分之间存在的相互关系的结果，这些关系将其连接到其他不具物理显现的存在层面。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1

quotes.text: The body learned to maintain its stability, its strength and agility, to achieve a state of balance in complementary response to the weather and elements, to dream computations that the conscious mind alone could not hold. The body learned to heal itself in sleep in its dreams--and at certain levels in that state even now each portion of consciousness contributes to the health and stability of all other portions. Far from the claw-and-dagger universe, you have one whose very foundation is based upon the loving cooperation of all of its parts. That is given--the gift of life brings along with it the actualization of that cooperation, for the body's parts exist as a unit because of inner relationships of a cooperative nature; and those exist at your birth (most emphatically), when you are innocent of any cultural beliefs that may be to the contrary.
quotes.translation: 身体学会了维持自身的稳定、力量与敏捷，在应对天气和元素时达到互补的平衡状态，并完成意识心无法单独承载的梦境运算。身体学会了在睡眠与梦境中自我疗愈——即使在那个状态的某些层面，此刻每一部分意识仍为其他所有部分的健康与稳定做出贡献。远非利爪匕首的宇宙，你拥有的宇宙其根基建立于所有部分充满爱的合作之上。这是天赋——生命的礼物伴随着这种合作的实现而来，因为身体各部分之所以能作为整体存在，正是源于合作本质的内在关系；而这些关系在你出生时（极其强调地）就已存在，那时你尚未受到任何可能与之相悖的文化信念影响。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1

quotes.text: The entities, or units of consciousness--those ancient fragments that burst into objectivity from the vast and infinite psychological realms of All That Is--dared all, for they joyfully abandoned themselves in space and time. They created new psychological entities, opened up an area of divine creativity that "until then" had been closed, and therefore to that [degree] extended the experience and immense existence of All That Is. For in so abandoning themselves they were not of course abandoned, since they contained within themselves their inherent relationship with All That Is. In those terms All That Is became physical also, aroused at its divine depth by the thrusting of each grass blade through the soil into the air, aroused by each birth and by each moment of each creature's existence.
quotes.translation: 这些存有或意识单元——那些从"一切万有"浩瀚无垠的心理领域迸发进入客观性的古老碎片——敢于承担一切，因为它们欢欣地投身于时空之中。它们创造了新的心理实体，开启了"此前"一直封闭的神圣创造力领域，并因此（在某种程度上）扩展了"一切万有"的经验与浩瀚存在。因为在如此投身的过程中，它们当然没有被遗弃，因为它们内在包含着与"一切万有"固有的联系。就那些术语而言，"一切万有"也变成了物质性的，在其神圣深处被每一片草叶破土而出刺入空气的冲劲所唤醒，被每一个诞生以及每个生灵存在的每一刻所唤醒。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1

quotes.text: Give us a moment.... You have taught yourselves to be aware of and to follow only certain portions of your own consciousnesses, so that mentally you consider certain subjects taboo. Thoughts of death and suffering are among those. In a species geared above all to the survival of the fittest, and the competition among species, then any touch of suffering or pain, or thoughts of death, become dishonorable, biologically shameful, cowardly, nearly insane. Life is to be pursued at all costs--not because it is innately meaningful, but because it is the only game going, and it is a game of chance at best. One life is all you have, and that one is everywhere beset by the threat of illness, disaster, and war--and if you escape such drastic circumstances, then you are still left with a life that is the result of no more than lifeless elements briefly coming into a consciousness and vitality that is bound to end.
quotes.translation: 请稍等片刻……你们教导自己只觉察并追随自身意识的特定部分，因此在心智上视某些主题为禁忌。死亡与苦难的思绪便在其中。在一个以适者生存和物种竞争为至高准则的族类里，任何对痛苦或折磨的触碰，或死亡的念头，都变得不光彩、在生物学上可耻、懦弱、近乎疯狂。生命要不计代价地追求——并非因其天生有意义，而是因为这是唯一进行的游戏，且充其量不过是场机会游戏。你只有一次生命，而这一次生命处处被疾病、灾难和战争的威胁所困扰——即便你逃脱了这些极端境遇，你仍会面对一个生命，它不过是无生命的元素短暂地进入注定终结的意识与活力之中。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1

quotes.text: Man does have an instinct and a desire to live, and he has an instinct and a desire to die. The same applies to other creatures. In his life [each] man is embarked upon a cooperative venture with his own species, and with the other species, and dying he also in that regard acts in a cooperative manner, returning his physical substance to the earth. (Pause.) Physically speaking, man's "purpose" is to help enrich the quality of existence in all of its dimensions. Spiritually speaking, his "purpose" is to understand the qualities of love and creativity, to intellectually and psychically understand the sources of his being, and to lovingly create other dimensions of reality of which he is presently unaware. (Pause.) In his thinking, in the quality of his thoughts, in their motion, he is indeed experimenting with a unique and a new kind of reality, forming other subjective worlds which will in their turn grow into consciousness and song, which will in their turn flower from a dream dimension into other ones. Man is learning to create new worlds. In order to do so he has taken on many challenges.
quotes.translation: 人类确实有生存的本能与欲望，也有死亡的本能与欲望。这同样适用于其他生物。在生命中，每个人都与自己的物种及其他物种展开合作性冒险，而在死亡时，他也以合作方式行事，将物质身体归还大地。（停顿。）从物质层面来说，人类的“目的”是帮助丰富存在在所有维度上的品质。从灵性层面来说，他的“目的”是理解爱与创造力的品质，在智识与心灵上理解其存在的源头，并充满爱意地创造他目前尚未意识到的其他实相维度。（停顿。）在他的思维中，在其思想的品质与运动中，他确实在实验一种独特而崭新的实相，形成其他主观世界，这些世界将依次成长为意识与歌声，将从梦境维度绽放为其他维度。人类正在学习创造新世界。为此，他承担了许多挑战。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1

quotes.text: (9:42.) The large classifications of life give you the patterns into which consciousness forms itself, and because those patterns seem relatively stable it is easy to miss the fact that they are filled out, so to speak, in each moment with new energy. Man does not in his physical development pass through the stages supposedly followed by the hypothetical creature who left the water for the land to become a mammal--but each species does indeed have written within it the knowledge of "its past." Part of this, again, is most difficult to express, and I must try to fill out old words with new meanings. (Pause.) The reincarnational aspects of physical life, however, serve a very important purpose, providing an inner subjective background. Such a background is needed by every species.
quotes.translation: （9点42分。）生命的大类为你提供了意识塑造自身的模式，由于这些模式看似相对稳定，你很容易忽略一个事实：可以说，每个瞬间都有新的能量在填充这些模式。人类在身体发展过程中，并未经历那个假想生物从水中登陆成为哺乳动物所遵循的阶段——但每个物种确实在其内在铭刻着"自身过去"的知识。再次强调，这部分内容极难表述，我必须尝试用旧词赋予新意。（停顿。）然而，物质生命的转世层面确实具有非常重要的目的，它提供了内在的主观背景。每个物种都需要这样的背景。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1

quotes.text: Reincarnation exists, then, on the part of all species. Once a consciousness, however, has chosen the larger classification of its physical existences, it stays within that framework in its "reincarnational" existences. Mammals return as mammals, for example, but the species can change within that classification.1 This provides great genetic strength, and consciousnesses in those classifications have chosen them because of their own propensities and purposes. The animals, for example, seem to have a limited range of physical activity in conscious terms, as you think of them. An animal cannot decide to read a newspaper. Newspapers are outside of its reality. Animals have a much wider range, practically speaking, in certain other areas. They are much more intimately aware of their environment, of themselves as separate from it, but also of themselves as a part of it (intently). In that regard, their experience deals with relationships of another kind.
quotes.translation: 因此，所有物种都存在转世。一旦某个意识选择了其物质存在的大类，它就会在"转世"存在中保持在该框架内。例如，哺乳动物会转世为哺乳动物，但该分类中的物种可以改变。这提供了强大的遗传力量，而选择这些分类的意识正是基于其自身的倾向与目的。例如，动物在你们所理解的意识层面，其身体活动范围似乎有限。动物无法决定阅读报纸。报纸在其现实之外。实际上，动物在其他某些领域拥有更广阔的范围。它们对环境、对自身与环境分离、以及对自身作为环境一部分的感知都更为亲密（专注地）。在这方面，它们的经验涉及另一种关系。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1

quotes.text: Now in a manner of speaking--though I see that little time has passed in this living room where I speak with Ruburt's permission--we have transcended time to some extent this evening, for in what I have said there are indeed hints and illusions--cadences--that can, if you are ready, give you a feeling for existence as it is outside of time's context. Even to try and verbally present such material necessitates alterations involving perception, for while that gridwork appears quite stable to your senses, giving you a reliable picture of reality, this is also because you have trained yourselves to pick up certain signals only. Others at other levels are (underlined) available. You can (underlined) tune into cellular consciousness, for example.
quotes.translation: 现在，从某种意义上说——尽管我看到在这间我与鲁柏交谈的客厅里时间几乎没有流逝——今晚我们在某种程度上已经超越了时间，因为在我所说的话中，确实存在着暗示与幻象——韵律——如果你准备好了，这些能让你感受到时间脉络之外的存在本质。即便试图用语言呈现这样的材料，也必然涉及感知的调整，因为虽然那网格结构在你的感官中显得相当稳定，为你提供了可靠的现实图景，但这同样是因为你只训练自己接收特定信号。其他层面的信号是（强调）可获取的。例如，你（强调）可以调频进入细胞意识。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1

quotes.text: Your overreliance upon physical norms, and your distorted concepts concerning survival of the fittest, help exaggerate the existence of any genetic defects, of course. Many religious dogmas consider such conditions, again, the result of a god's punishment. The survival of the species is far more dependent upon your subjective activities than your physical ones--for it is your subjective behavior that is responsible for your physical acts. Science of course looks at it the other way around, as if your physical acts are the result of a robot's mechanical, formalized behavior--a robot miraculously programmed by the blind elements of an accidental universe formed by chance. The robot is programmed only to survive at anyone's or anything's expense. It has no real consciousness of its own. Its thoughts are merely mental mirages, so if one of its parts is defective then obviously it is in deep trouble. But man is no robot, and each so-called genetic defect has an internal part to play in the entire picture of genetic reality. The principle of uncertainty must operate genetically, or you would have been locked into overspecializations as a species.2
quotes.translation: 你对物理常态的过度依赖，以及关于适者生存的扭曲观念，自然会夸大任何遗传缺陷的存在。许多宗教教义同样将此类状况视为神罚的结果。物种的生存远更依赖于你的主观活动而非物理活动——因为正是你的主观行为决定了你的物理行为。科学当然持相反观点，仿佛你的物理行为是机器人机械、形式化行为的结果——一个被偶然形成的随机宇宙中盲目元素奇迹般编程的机器人。这个机器人被编程为不惜任何代价求生存。它没有真正的自我意识。它的思想只是精神海市蜃楼，所以如果它的某个部件有缺陷，显然就会陷入大麻烦。但人类不是机器人，每个所谓的遗传缺陷在遗传现实的整体图景中都有其内在作用。不确定性原理必须在遗传层面运作，否则作为一个物种，你们早已陷入过度专门化的困境。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2

quotes.text: (9:32. With many pauses:) All That Is, as the source of all realities and experience, is so psychologically complex, so multidimensionally creative, that it constantly surprises itself. It is, itself, the invisible universe that is everywhere implied within your world, but that becomes manifest to your perception only through historic time. All That Is disperses itself, therefore, so that it is on the one hand "a massive" subjective entity, a psychological structure--and on the other hand, it also disperses itself into the phenomenal world. It is, in all meanings of the word, divine, yet it disperses even that divinity so that in your terms (long pause), each unit of consciousness contains within itself those properties of divinity. All That Is has no one image, but is within all images--and in parentheses: (whether or not they are manifest). Your thoughts are the invisible partners of your words, and the vast unstated subjectivity of All That Is is in the same way behind all stated or manifest phenomena.
quotes.translation: （9点32分。多次停顿：）一切万有，作为所有实相与经验的源头，其心理结构如此复杂，多维创造力如此丰沛，以至于它不断令自身惊喜。它本身即是无形宇宙，处处隐含于你们的世界之中，却唯有通过历史时间才能向你们的感知显化。因此，一切万有分散自身——一方面作为"庞大的"主观实体、心理结构；另一方面又分散自身进入现象世界。从所有字面意义而言，它神圣无比，却连这份神性也加以分散——用你们的话说（长停顿），每个意识单位内在本就包含神性的特质。一切万有无固定形象，却存在于所有形象之中——括号说明：（无论这些形象是否显化）。你们的思想是话语的无形伴侣，一切万有那浩瀚未言明的主观性，同样存在于所有已言明或显化的现象背后。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2

quotes.text: Religion was hampered--and is--by its own interpretation of good and evil, but it did not deny the existence of other versions of consciousness, or differing kinds of psychological activity and life. (Long pause.) Reincarnation suggests, of course, the extension of personal existence beyond one time period, independently of one bodily form, the translation or transmission of intelligence through nonphysical frameworks, and implies psychological behavior, memory and desire as purposeful action without the substance of any physical mechanism--propositions that science at its present stage of development simply could not buy, and for which it could find no evidence, for its methods would automatically preclude the type of experience that such evidence would require.
quotes.translation: 宗教一直受到——现在仍然受到——自身对善恶诠释的阻碍，但它并未否认其他意识版本的存在，或不同种类的心理活动与生命。（长停顿。）轮回当然暗示了个人存在超越一个时间段的延伸，独立于一个身体形态，通过非物理框架进行智能的翻译或传递，并暗示心理行为、记忆和欲望作为有目的的行动，无需任何物理机制的实质——这些命题在科学目前的发展阶段根本无法接受，也找不到证据，因为科学的方法会自动排除这类证据所需的经验类型。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2

quotes.text: "The dream representing his grandfather symbolically allowed him to go back to the past in this life, to a time of severe shock--his grandfather's death--which occurred when (Ruburt, at age 19) was beginning to substitute scientific belief for religious belief, wondering if his grandfather's consciousness then fell back into a mindless state of being, into chaos, as science would certainly seem to suggest. In the dream his grandfather survives. His grandfather survived in a suit that was too large, which means that there was still room for him to grow. Ruburt had a small experience of hearing a voice speak in his mind (yesterday)--a voice of comfort, all he remembered of quite legitimate assistance he received from other personalities connected with the French life, that came as a result of the French dream.
quotes.translation: “这个象征性地代表他祖父的梦，让他得以回到今生的过去，回到一个遭受严重冲击的时刻——他祖父的去世——那发生在（鲁柏19岁时）开始用科学信仰取代宗教信仰的时期，他当时怀疑祖父的意识是否如科学似乎暗示的那样，坠入了一种无意识的存在状态，陷入混沌。在梦中，他的祖父幸存了下来。他的祖父穿着一件过大的西装幸存下来，这意味着他仍有成长的空间。鲁柏有过一次小小的体验，听到一个声音在他脑海中说话（昨天）——一个安慰的声音，这是他记得的全部，那是他从与法国生活相关的其他人格那里获得的完全正当的援助，这是法国梦带来的结果。”
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2

quotes.text: There are plenty of more immediate challenges. For example: The staff of the Nuclear Regulatory Commission has asked the operators of more than 40 nuclear plants to check for cracks in the walls of the vessels encasing their pressurized-water reactors (which are the kind installed at TMI). Evidence is accumulating that the vessels are becoming embrittled by neutron radiation from the reactors much more quickly than their designers had anticipated. Small cracks have been found, but not all areas are reachable for testing. A rupture of a typical pressure vessel could result in an uncontrollable release of radiation into a containment building not designed to handle such a situation. If the building itself was breached, the escaping radiation could cause some 48,000 deaths, 250,000 nonfatal cancers and injuries, 5,000 first-generation birth defects, render 200 square miles uninhabitable, require decontamination of another 3,200 square miles, and damage other properties worth many billions of dollars. No protection against that kind of accident has ever been required by the NRC. The forces of consciousness at work would seem to be incredible--beyond our grasp.
quotes.translation: 还有更直接的挑战。例如：核管理委员会的工作人员已要求40多座核电站的运营方检查包裹其压水反应堆（即三哩岛安装的那种）的容器壁是否存在裂缝。越来越多的证据表明，这些容器因反应堆中子辐射而变脆的速度远超设计者的预期。已发现细小裂缝，但并非所有区域都能触及检测。典型压力容器破裂可能导致放射性物质不受控制地释放到并非为此设计的安全壳建筑中。若建筑本身被突破，逸出的辐射可能导致约4.8万人死亡、25万人罹患非致命癌症和受伤、5000例第一代出生缺陷、200平方英里区域无法居住、另需清理3200平方英里土地，并造成价值数十亿美元的其他财产损失。核管理委员会从未要求对此类事故采取防护措施。意识运作的力量似乎令人难以置信——超乎我们的理解。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2

quotes.text: (Much slower after a long pause at 9:10:) Such considerations should naturally spark within you far vaster and yet far more intimate insights--insights in whose light the hazy rhetoric of prepackaged knowledge begins to disappear. As it does, so the speakers within each of you can rise to the surface of ordinary consciousness without being considered blabbermouths or mad men and women, or fools, without having to distort their information simply to bring it to your attention. The speakers are those inner voices that first taught you physical languages. You could be equally correct in calling them the voices of electrons or the voices of the gods, for each is a representation of All That Is, overflowing like a fountain both with knowledge and with love.
quotes.translation: （9:10分长时间停顿后，语速明显放缓：）这些思考自然会在你们心中激发更宏大却也更亲密的洞见——在那些洞见的光芒中，预装知识的模糊修辞开始消散。当它消散时，你们每个人内在的言说者便能浮上普通意识的表层，而不必被视为胡言乱语者、疯癫男女或愚人，也不必扭曲它们的信息只为引起你的注意。这些言说者正是最初教会你们物质语言的内在声音。你们同样可以称它们为电子的声音或神祇的声音，因为每个声音都是万有之全然的显现，如喷泉般满溢着知识与爱。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2

quotes.text: One of the intellect's main purposes is to give you a conscious choice in a world of probabilities. To do that properly the intellect is to make clear, concise decisions, on its level, of matters that are its concern, and therefore to present its own picture of reality to add to the entire construct. (Long pause.) On the one hand you have been told to identify yourselves almost completely with your intellects. On the other hand, you have been taught that the intellect, the "flower of consciousness," is a frail, vulnerable adjunct -- again, a chance creation, without meaning and without support -- without support because you believe that "beneath it" lie "primitive, animalistic, bloody instincts," against which reason must exert what strength it has.
quotes.translation: 智性主要目的之一，是在或然世界中赋予你有意识的选择。为妥善达成此目的，智性须在其层面，对属于其范畴的事务做出清晰、明确的决定，从而将其自身的实相图像贡献给整体建构。（长停顿。）一方面，你被教导几乎完全认同于你的智性。另一方面，你又被教导智性——这"意识之花"——是脆弱、易受伤害的附属品——再次，是偶然的产物，毫无意义且缺乏支持——缺乏支持，因为你相信"在其之下"潜藏着"原始、兽性、血腥的本能"，理性必须竭尽全力与之抗衡。
quotes.book: The Magical Approach

quotes.text: However, as we know the cell is a part of the body, giving nourishment and receiving nourishment from it. Its outer rim, more correctly, connects it to the body. Parts of it literally travel throughout the body. It is yet an individual. It possesses condensed consciousness and comprehension, it partakes of value fulfillments through the gestalt of which it would not otherwise be capable. If you considered the body as a closed system, which it is not, then you could say that the self of the cell had as its limitations only the limits of the whole closed system. But the system is not closed, and through the participation of the cell in the activities of the body, which is an open system, then you could truly say that the cell itself had no limitations.
quotes.translation: 然而，如我们所知，细胞是身体的一部分，从身体获取滋养并回馈滋养。其外缘更确切地说，是将其与身体相连。它的某些部分确实游走于全身。它仍是一个独立的个体。它拥有浓缩的意识与理解力，通过整体形态参与价值完成，否则它无法做到这一点。若你将身体视为一个封闭系统（实则不然），那么你可以说细胞的自我之局限仅限于整个封闭系统的边界。但系统并非封闭，通过细胞参与身体这一开放系统的活动，你才能真正地说细胞本身毫无局限。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)

quotes.text: It is the ego or directive consciousness behind all personified aspects of the subconscious; in dormant fashion however, and contrary to Jung's propositions, within the subconscious and in those personified aspects of it will be found remnant memory personalities of past reincarnated selves. They may be called shadows and yet they are not powerless. The inner ego, the directive organizer of the subconscious, also is the part of the self which is familiar with activities and methods of which the outer ego is ignorant. It is this organizer who directs not only the movements of the physical body from within, but directs from within those intimate survival mechanisms, without which the physical body could not exist, and upon which the existence of the outer ego is so dependent.
quotes.translation: 它是潜意识所有拟人化面向背后的自我或指导性意识；然而以休眠的方式，且与荣格的命题相反，在潜意识及其拟人化面向中，将发现过去转世自我的残余记忆人格。它们可被称为阴影，却并非无力。内在自我，即潜意识的指导性组织者，也是自我中熟悉外在自我所不知的活动与方法的部分。正是这位组织者，不仅从内在指导身体的动作，还从内在指导那些亲密的生存机制——没有它们，身体无法存在，而外在自我的存在如此依赖于此。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)

quotes.text: The entity should therefore not be thought of as completed. The electric universe, of which you know so little, is a reality, and yet it is also a symbol of another reality. For behind even this electrical universe there is a reality which cannot be explored in terms of speech; for all consciousness, while having an electric reality, has a reality beyond even this. It is the vitality of the universe, and all universes, which causes the electrical reality, and the vitality of the universe, composing everything that is, cannot be touched. It is touch, but touch not as it is conceived of in your terms. This vitality is the most immediate and intimate aspect that composes the camouflage appearance within all fields. And yet it is by far more than the camouflage.
quotes.translation: 因此，不应将存有视为已完成。你们知之甚少的电性宇宙是一个实相，却也是另一实相的象征。因为即便在这电性宇宙之后，还有一个无法用言语探索的实相；因为所有意识，虽具有电性实相，却拥有超越此层的实相。正是宇宙及所有宇宙的生命力引发了电性实相，而构成万物的宇宙生命力无法被触及。它是触觉，但非你们所理解的触觉。这生命力是构成所有场域中伪装外观的最直接、最亲密的面向。然而它远不止是伪装。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)

quotes.text: （Seth's remark above, "turn into that dream", involves the fourth inner sense: the Conceptual Sense. He discussed this to some extent in the 37th and 38th sessions. See Volume 1: "The fourth inner sense involves cognition of a concept in much more than your usual intellectual terms. It involves experiencing a concept completely, to the extent of being a concept completely. You do not leave what you call yourself behind; you merely change what you are into a different pattern. Concepts have what we call electrical and chemical composition. The molecules and ions of the consciousness change into the concept, which is thereby directly experienced. You cannot truly understand or appreciate any other thing unless you can become that thing. You can best achieve some approximation of an idea by using psychological time..."）
quotes.translation: （赛斯上述"变成那个梦"的评论涉及第四种内在感官：概念感官。他在第37和38节中对此进行了部分讨论。参见第一卷："第四种内在感官对概念的认知远不止于你们通常的智力层面。它涉及完全体验一个概念，乃至完全成为那个概念。你并未抛下所谓的自我，只是将你所是的形态转变为另一种模式。概念具有我们所谓的电性与化学构成。意识的分子与离子转变为概念，从而被直接体验。除非你能成为某物，否则你无法真正理解或欣赏它。通过使用心理时间，你最能接近某个理念的近似体验……"）
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 5 of The Seth Material (Sessions 199-239)

quotes.text: Then, rereading some material I was struck by the massive intellect behind it, the real beauty of the material, and sad that I did not really let myself realize it before; indeed that I had allowed myself to be affected by the lesser writings of others; even to the extent that in some late sessions it affected the material. Told my class last night the whole bit, and said that from now on classes were to be focused about the material; we were going to study it from scratch; this was my life work, my direction, what I was meant to do. Realized yesterday also, going over old notes, that my original 'cosmic consciousness' (borrowed phrases again) experience was with The Physical Universe as Idea Construction which turned into and developed into the sessions and Seth Material; the natural, intuitive, and logical development; which to a large extent I relegated to the background and sometimes even distrusted. (Largely because I feared setting myself up as some sort of a false prophet' or something; or distrusted myself, rather than it, actually.)
quotes.translation: 随后重读某些资料时，我被其背后宏大的智慧与真正的美感所震撼，遗憾自己之前未能真正领悟；甚至允许自己被他人较次等的作品影响，以至于在后期某些课程中影响了资料本身。昨晚向全班坦陈一切，宣布今后课程将围绕这些资料展开；我们要从头研读；这是我毕生的事业、方向与使命。昨日整理旧笔记时也意识到，我最初"宇宙意识"（再次借用短语）的体验正是《物质宇宙即意念建构》——它演变为课程与赛斯资料；这是自然、直觉且合乎逻辑的发展，却在很大程度上被我搁置甚至怀疑。（主要是因为害怕自己成为某种"假先知"之类；实际上是不信任自己，而非不信任资料本身。）
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)

quotes.text: Now. As I have told you, there is duration of a kind. What would correspond, in any case, to your idea of duration within the spacious present ; it is a matter of intensities however. Now the entity is in itself composed of such intensities. Simply for the sake of analogy, imagine the image, a humanoid one, of an entity giant-sized, spread out anywhere in your physical universe. And if the image were projected against a midnight sky, within its apparent boundaries then you would see a multitude of planets and stars. Let these represent moment points. On one hand they are a part of the entity, as your cells are a portion of your body. On the other hand the entity's consciousness can travel through these. They are doorways within his own psychological makeup, into experiences.
quotes.translation: 现在。正如我曾告诉你们的，存在某种持续性。无论如何，这相当于你们对"广袤当下"中持续性的概念；然而这关乎强度。存有本身即由这些强度构成。仅作类比，想象一个类人形态的存有影像，如巨人般巨大，散布在你们物质宇宙的任何角落。若将此影像投射于子夜苍穹，在其显见轮廓内，你们将看见无数行星与星辰。让这些代表"片刻点"。一方面，它们是存有的一部分，如同你们的细胞是身体的一部分；另一方面，存有的意识能穿行其间。这些是他自身心理结构中的门户，通往各种体验。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)

quotes.text: Now there is a reason why these lessons must be learned in just this way. Elementally there is only creativity. Destruction is merely the changing of form. A cloudburst or a tornado knows nothing of destruction. This same energy encased within a human form is something else. There are different kinds of creativity, then, to learn, and a specialization in energy is focus and feelings that emerge; elemental energy becoming conscious of itself, and aware of issues that did not exist for it earlier; millions of molecules momentarily united with the living consciousness (Pause), filled with primal energy, now learning love, and forming highly sensitive psychic patterns, electrical charges that now form emotions instead of clouds; the innocent chaos of undifferentiated personality that exists behind the highly specified and truly sophisticated mechanism of one thought. And all of this before an individual is born within your system. In terms of time this is behind us all.
quotes.translation: 这些课程必须以这种方式学习是有原因的。在元素层面，只有创造力。毁灭仅是形式的改变。暴雨或龙卷风不知毁灭为何物。这同一种能量被包裹在人类形态中则另当别论。因此有不同种类的创造力需要学习，能量的专门化产生焦点与涌现的情感；元素能量开始觉察自身，意识到先前不存在的议题；数百万分子瞬间与活生生的意识结合（停顿），充满原初能量，此刻正在学习爱，并形成高度敏感的心灵模式——那些形成情感而非云朵的电荷；在你们系统中个体诞生之前，那存在于一个思想高度精微且真正复杂机制背后的、未分化人格的纯真混沌。就时间而言，这已是我们所有人的过往。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)

quotes.text: First of all, let us consider the fact just mentioned. There is no separate, indivisible, specific point of death. Life is a state of becoming, and death is a part of this process of becoming. You are alive now, a consciousness knowing itself, sparkling with cognition amid a debris of dead and dying cells; alive while the atoms and molecules of your body die and are reborn. You are alive, therefore, in the midst of small deaths; portions of your own image crumble away moment by moment and are replaced, and you scarcely give the matter a thought. So you are to some extent now alive in the midst of the death of yourself -- alive despite, and yet because of, the multitudinous deaths and rebirths that occur within your body in physical terms.
quotes.translation: 首先，让我们考虑刚才提到的事实。并不存在独立、不可分割、特定的死亡点。生命是一种"成为"的状态，死亡是这"成为"过程的一部分。你们此刻活着，是一个认知自身的意识，在死亡与垂死细胞的残骸中闪烁着认知之光；当你们身体的原子与分子死亡又重生时，你们依然活着。因此，你们活在微小死亡之中——你们自身影像的局部时刻崩解又更替，而你们几乎不曾思及此事。所以从某种程度上，你们此刻正活在自己死亡的进程中——尽管有，甚至正因为，你们身体在物质层面发生的无数死亡与重生，你们依然活着。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: And when you realize that you form the events of your life in the same way, you will learn to take hold of your entire consciousness in whatever aspect it shows itself in this life. Through all of this you must realize that you are not powerless. Remember, also, that this life is a dimension of experience and reality even if it is, in contrast, a dream in a higher level of reality in which you have your larger consciousness.
quotes.translation: 当你意识到你以同样的方式形成自己生活中的事件时，你就会学会掌握你的整个意识，无论它在此生中以何种面貌显现。通过这一切，你必须认识到你并非无能为力。同时记住，此生是经验与实相的一个维度，即便相比之下，它只是你拥有更大意识的那个更高实相层面中的一个梦。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: You should try willing yourself into another room. When you awaken in the middle of the night, try such an experiment. Your projections do involve you in extensive levitations from the dream state, but you recall only a few. The most extensive traveling is done in nightly excursions, but it is easiest to remember those dream projections that occur during naps in the day, simply because the waking consciousness is more alert.
quotes.translation: 你应该尝试用意念将自己送入另一个房间。当你在半夜醒来时，试试这样的实验。你的投射确实会涉及从梦境状态进行广泛的飘升，但你只记得其中少数。最广泛的旅行是在夜间出游中完成的，但最容易记住的是白天小睡时发生的那些梦境投射，仅仅是因为醒时意识更为警觉。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)

quotes.text: New paragraph (and more rapidly): As egotistical consciousness expands to include hereto largely neglected data, then it will experience, practically speaking, a new kind of identity; knowing itself differently. Its concepts of godhood will significantly alter, as will the dimensions of emotion. Your heritage includes vastly richer veins of love, yet your concepts of self and godhood have severely limited these. You often seem to hate those with different beliefs than your own, for example, and you have perpetrated cruelties upon others in the name of religion and in the name of science, because your limited ideas about the nature of the self led you to fear your emotions. Often you are afraid that love will overwhelm you, for instance.
quotes.translation: 新段落（更快速地）：当自我意识扩展以包含迄今被严重忽略的数据时，它实际上将体验到一种新的身份；以不同的方式认识自己。它对神性的概念将显著改变，情感的维度也将如此。你的传承包含远为丰富的爱之矿脉，然而你对自我和神性的概念严重限制了这些。例如，你常常似乎憎恨那些信仰与你不同的人，并且以宗教和科学的名义对他人施加残酷行为，因为你关于自我本质的有限观念导致你恐惧自己的情感。例如，你常常害怕爱会淹没你。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1

quotes.text: In your terms, the person at birth is affected by multidimensional conditions, and the collective position of the planets is but one very minute indication of the other realities involved. Ruburt is correct: Even in conventional terms a true horoscope would have to involve the time of death in your temporal reality, as well [as that of birth]. Your focus of attention forms boundaries that predispose you to believe in a point at which your consciousness emerges, as you understand it, and a point when it is no longer effective, or dies. Your beliefs in such concepts limit your perception, for by altering the focus of your attention you can to some extent become aware of perception before and after the recognized points of birth and death.
quotes.translation: 就你们而言，出生时的人受到多重维度条件的影响，而行星的集体位置只是所涉及的其他实相的一个极其微小的指示。鲁柏是正确的：即使按传统说法，真正的星盘也必须涉及你们时间实相中的死亡时刻，以及出生时刻。你们注意力的焦点形成了界限，使你们倾向于相信，在你们所理解的意识出现的那一刻，以及它不再有效或死亡的那一刻。你们对这些概念的信念限制了你们的感知，因为通过改变注意力的焦点，你们可以在某种程度上意识到公认的出生和死亡点之前和之后的感知。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: The consciousness forms its own materialization. The physical body is truly a more wondrous phenomenon than is supposed, but here I hope I do not push you too far, for this combination of consciousness continues, and its results can be seen in the consciousness of the physical brain.
quotes.translation: 意识形成其自身的物质化。肉体确实是一种比人们想象的更奇妙的现象，但我希望这里不会让你感到过于超前，因为这种意识的组合仍在继续，其成果可见于物质大脑的意识中。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)

quotes.text: The imagination is highly involved with event-forming. Children's imaginations prevent them from being too limited by their parents' world. Waking or dreaming, children "pretend." In their pretending they exercise their consciousness in a particularly advantageous way. While accepting a given reality for themselves, they nevertheless reserve the right, so to speak, to experiment with other "secondary" states of being. To some extent they become what they are pretending to be, and in so doing they also increase their own knowledge and experience. Left alone, children would learn how to cope with animals by pretending to be animals, for example. Through experiencing the animals' reactions, they would understand how to react themselves.
quotes.translation: 想象力与事件的形成高度相关。儿童的想象力使他们免于被父母的世界过度限制。无论清醒或做梦，孩子们都会"假装"。在假装中，他们以一种特别有利的方式锻炼自己的意识。在为自己接受既定现实的同时，他们仍保留着——可以这么说——尝试其他"次级"存在状态的权利。在某种程度上，他们变成了自己假装的样子，并在此过程中也增加了自身的知识与经验。例如，若不加干预，孩子们会通过假装成动物来学习如何与动物相处。通过体验动物的反应，他们会明白自己该如何反应。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche

quotes.text: (From the 775th session for May 10, 1976:) Bits of your consciousnesses,34 Joseph and Ruburt, go out through these books. I am not speaking symbolically. Those portions will mix with the consciousness of others. Portions of your intent and purpose become theirs. My own psychological reality is not particleized. My identity includes the identities of many others, and they each operate in their own fashion. In those terms I am a wave formation. More specifically, however, and to a lesser degree, each physical person operates partially as a particleized being, and partially in terms of a wave.35 But identity, being itself inviolate, is on the other hand everchanging -- and there is, in the larger framework of reality, no contradiction.
quotes.translation: （摘自1976年5月10日第775节：）约瑟和鲁柏，你们意识的片段34会通过这些书籍向外发散。我并非在象征性地说话。那些部分将与他人的意识混合。你们意图与目的的部分会成为他们的。我自己的心理实相并非粒子化的。我的身份包含了许多其他身份，而它们各自以自身的方式运作。用那些术语来说，我是一种波状结构。然而更具体地说，在较低程度上，每个物质人部分地以粒子化存在运作，部分地以波的形式运作35。但身份本身虽不可侵犯，另一方面却永远在变化——而在实相的更大架构中，这并无矛盾。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: As you and your brothers or sisters might belong to the same physical family, so generally are you and your counterparts part of the same psychic group of consciousness.1 Remember, however, that these psychic groups are like natural formations into which consciousness seems to flow. Your own interests, desires, and abilities are not predetermined by your membership in a given psychic family. Underline that entire sentence.
quotes.translation: 正如你与兄弟姐妹可能属于同一物质家庭，一般而言，你与你的对应者也同属某个心灵意识群体。但请记住，这些心灵群体如同意识自然汇聚的形态。你自身的兴趣、欲望和能力，并不因你属于某个特定心灵家族而预先注定。请将整句话划线强调。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: (9:35.) Because of the energy he is given by others, he will have a certain consciousness of his own, but such a mock devil has no power or reality to those who do not believe in his existence, and who do not give him energy through their belief. He is, in other words, a superlative hallucination As mentioned earlier, those who believe in a hell and assign themselves to it through their belief can indeed experience one, but certainly in nothing like eternal terms. No soul is forever ignorant.
quotes.translation: （9:35。）由于他人给予他的能量，他会有某种自我意识，但这种虚假的恶魔对那些不相信其存在、也不通过信念赋予其能量的人而言，既无力量也无实相。换言之，它是一种极致的幻觉。如前所述，那些相信地狱存在并通过信念将自己投入其中的人，确实能体验到地狱，但绝非永恒之境。没有灵魂会永远无知。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: Projections involve many more aspects of the whole self, and are a mark that the personality is progressing in important ways. The inner senses are allowed their greatest freedom in projection states, and the whole self retains experience that it would not otherwise. When this knowledge becomes part of the usual waking consciousness, that is when you realize what you have done, then you have taken a gigantic step forward.
quotes.translation: 投射涉及整体自性的更多面向，是人格在重要层面取得进展的标志。在投射状态中，内在感官得以获得最大程度的自由，整体自性会保留原本无法获得的经验。当这些知识融入日常清醒意识——即你意识到自己已完成之事时——你便迈出了巨大的一步。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 6 of The Seth Material (Sessions 240-280)

quotes.text: (9:50.) Now: Ruburt is that kind of courier. There are many in all areas of life, and this involves not only an excitement on the part of your own species, but the same kind of curiosity and excitement on the part of other species as well. Again, most difficult to explain--but those connections that exist between all species and the environment are themselves affected. The horizontal communications stretch and expand to allow for later developments in terms of probabilities, for consciousness always knows itself in more than one context, and it is possible for nature to experience itself in ways that would seem to be most improbable when the properties of conservation and learning are at their (underlined) strongest spring.
quotes.translation: （9:50。）现在：鲁柏就是那种信使。生命各个领域都有许多这样的信使，这不仅涉及你们物种自身的兴奋，也涉及其他物种同样类型的好奇与兴奋。再次强调，这极难解释——但所有物种与环境之间存在的那些联系本身也受到了影响。横向沟通得以扩展延伸，为概率层面的后续发展预留空间，因为意识总是在多重情境中认知自身，当守恒与学习的特性处于（着重强调的）最强盛之春时，自然有可能以看似最不可能的方式体验自身。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2

quotes.text: Now: This extended period, given to waking consciousness without rest periods, builds up chemicals in the blood that are discharged in sleep. But in the meantime they make the body sluggish and retard conscious concentration. The long sleep period to which you are accustomed then does become necessary. A vicious circle then is formed. This forces overstimulations during the night, increasing the body's work, making it perform continuously over an extended time physical purifications that ideally would be taken care of in briefer periods of rest. The ego feels threatened by the extended "leave of absence" it must take, becomes wary of sleep, and sets up barriers against the dream state. Many of these are highly artificial.
quotes.translation: 现在：这种延长清醒意识活动时间、不给予休息期的做法，会在血液中积累本应在睡眠中排出的化学物质。但与此同时，这些物质会使身体变得迟钝，阻碍意识的专注。于是，你所习惯的长时间睡眠就变得必要了。这就形成了一个恶性循环。它迫使夜间过度刺激，增加身体负担，使其在延长时间内持续进行本可在较短休息期完成的物理净化。自我因必须经历这种延长"休假"而感到威胁，变得对睡眠警惕，并设置障碍对抗梦境状态。其中许多障碍都是高度人为的。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: (A one-minute pause at 9:40.) Give us a moment.... Generally speaking, the states of waking and sleeping are the only levels of consciousness with which you have been primarily concerned. It seems to you that this is the result of your evolutionary progress--but there have been civilizations upon the earth that specialized in the use of many focuses of consciousness, as for example you are focused upon the use of tools.
quotes.translation: （9:40停顿一分钟。）请稍等片刻……一般来说，清醒与睡眠状态是你们主要关注的仅有的意识层次。在你们看来，这似乎是进化进程的结果——但地球上曾有过专精于运用多重意识焦点的文明，正如你们专注于使用工具一般。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1

quotes.text: (10:39.) Simply as an analogy, look at it this way: Your present universe is a mass-shared dream, quite valid -- a dream that presents reality in a certain light; a dream that is above all meaningful, creative, based not upon chaos (with a knowing look), but upon spontaneous order. To understand it, however, you must go to another level of consciousness -- one where, perhaps, the dream momentarily does not seem so real. There, from another viewpoint, you can see it even more clearly, holding it like a photograph in your hands; at the same time you can see from that broader perspective that you do indeed also stand outside of the dream context, but in a "within" that cannot show in the snapshot because of its limitations.
quotes.translation: （10:39。）仅作比喻，不妨这样看：你们当前的宇宙是一场集体共享的梦，相当真实——这场梦以特定视角呈现实相；它首要的是充满意义与创造力，并非基于混沌（意味深长地看了一眼），而是基于自发秩序。然而要理解它，你必须进入另一层意识——在那里，或许这场梦暂时显得不那么真实。从另一视角，你能更清晰地审视它，如同将照片握在手中；同时从那个更广阔的视角，你能看见自己确实也站在梦境脉络之外，却处于一个因照片局限而无法显现的"内在"之中。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1

quotes.text: "The relaxation involves a curious sense of dropping down inwardly, of going slowly beneath the realities we usually recognize. It's a smooth transition in which perception is slowed down topwise, but deepened so that usually unperceived stimuli seem to rise from an underside of consciousness and bodily sensation. In that kind of relaxation the body itself perceives differently; that's what I'm trying to emphasize. Looking at a leaf while in that state, I easily feel myself as part of the leaf, and I think this is a biological as well as a psychic perception. At certain levels the body feels that way itself, although ordinarily we aren't aware of it. Such a relaxation, then, is almost an extension of biological insight."
quotes.translation: 这种放松包含一种奇特的内在下沉感，仿佛缓缓潜入我们通常认知的现实之下。这是一种平滑的过渡，其中感知在表层上放缓，却深化到让通常未被察觉的刺激从意识与身体感受的底层浮现。在这种放松状态中，身体本身的感知方式截然不同——这正是我想强调的重点。处于那种状态凝视一片叶子时，我轻易便感觉自己成为叶子的一部分，我认为这既是生物性感知也是心灵性感知。在特定层次上，身体本身就持有这种感受，尽管我们通常并未察觉。因此，这种放松几乎可视为生物性洞见的延伸。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: But would our time traveler ever want to give up his or her present mental and physical focus to enter completely into an earlier personality? I think not, in the overwhelming majority of cases--and perhaps never--for in those terms it would mean surrendering a portion of the whole self or entity that had, through a projection into our scheme of "present" time, attained a certain consciousness and physical form of a unique degree. Yet, on second thought I wouldn't dare rule out completely such bizarre developments. Perhaps transfers like that can and do take place within the vast arena of probable realities (which I'll also be discussing in this essay). If so, then, they would be strange only from our limited viewpoints.
quotes.translation: 但我们的时间旅行者会愿意放弃当前的心智与身体焦点，完全进入某个早期人格吗？我认为在绝大多数情况下不会——或许永远都不会——因为从那些角度而言，这意味着放弃通过投射进入我们"当下"时间架构而获得独特意识与物质形态的整个自性或存有的一部分。然而转念一想，我也不敢完全排除这种奇特发展的可能性。或许这类转移确实能在可能性实相（我将在本文中继续探讨）的广阔领域内发生。若果真如此，它们仅在我们有限的视角下显得怪异罢了。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1

quotes.text: No one atom of air is like another. Each in its own way is aware and capable of entering into greater transformations and organizations, filled with infinite potential. As your breath leaves you and becomes part of the world, free, so do your lives leave you and continue to exist in your terms. You cannot confine a personality that you "were" to a particular century that is finished and deny it other fulfillments, for even now it exists and has fresh experience. As your moment of reflection gave birth to consciousness as you think of it -- for both really came together -- so then can another phenomenon and kind of reflection give birth to at least some dim conscious awareness of the vast dimensions of your own reality.
quotes.translation: 空气中没有两个原子是相同的。每个原子都以自身方式觉察着，并能够参与更伟大的转化与组织，充满无限潜能。正如你的气息离开你成为世界的一部分而获得自由，你的生命同样离开你，在你认知的维度中继续存在。你无法将某个"曾是"你的人格禁锢在已完结的特定世纪，并否认它获得其他成就的可能性——因为即便此刻它仍存在并经历着新鲜体验。正如你反思的瞬间孕育出你所认知的意识（两者实则同时诞生），另一种现象与反思方式也能催生出对你自身实相浩瀚维度的至少某些模糊觉察。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality

quotes.text: Even with so-called mental disorders, however, orientation with the body is very important, as are the individual's beliefs about his own form and its relationship with others and with time and space. (Pause.) There will often be chemical imbalances in such a situation, unconsciously produced by the individual, sometimes in order to allow him to work out a series of hallucinatory events. Such sustained "objectified dreaming" necessitates a change, chemically, from the normal state of waking consciousness. It is important to note that regardless of the mental or physical illness adopted, it is chosen for a reason, and is a natural method that the individual himself knows he is physically and mentally equipped to handle.
quotes.translation: 即使所谓的心理疾病，与身体的协调也非常重要，正如个人对自己形态及其与他人、时间、空间关系的信念一样重要。（停顿）在这种情况下，通常会出现化学失衡，由个体无意识地产生，有时是为了让他能够经历一系列幻觉事件。这种持续的"客体化梦境"需要从正常的清醒意识状态发生化学变化。值得注意的是，无论采用何种心理或生理疾病，它都是出于某种原因被选择的，并且是个体自知其身心有能力应对的自然方法。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality

quotes.text: 6. I'd say that in a context like the one he uses here, Seth automatically refers to Albert Einstein's special and general theories of relativity. Within the overriding constancy of the speed of light, all phenomena in our camouflage reality -- motion, velocity, mass, matter, time, space, gravitation, and so forth -- are seen as relative to each other. Space and time, for instance, are not separate or uniform entities, but closely related intuitive "constructs" of consciousness; mass is a form of energy; motion is not absolute, but relative to the motion of something else; two observers, each moving at a different velocity relative to a common sequence of events, will perceive those events in different courses of time.
quotes.translation: 6. 我想说，在他此处使用的语境中，赛斯自动引用了阿尔伯特·爱因斯坦的狭义和广义相对论。在光速的绝对恒常性之下，我们伪装实相中的所有现象——运动、速度、质量、物质、时间、空间、引力等——都被视为相互关联的。例如，空间和时间并非独立或统一的实体，而是意识密切相关的直觉"建构"；质量是能量的一种形式；运动并非绝对，而是相对于其他事物的运动；两个观察者，各自以不同速度相对于共同的事件序列运动，将在不同的时间进程中感知这些事件。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: The experience came within a framework that he could accept -- that of highly accelerated inspiration. His consciousness left his body only after he was in the throes of what seemed to him to be inspiration of almost unbearable intensity. Had his habits instead led him, say, to regular prayer, then that framework could also have been used. In all such cases several qualities are apparent: an ability to look inward, to concentrate deeply, to lose the sharp edges of the physically oriented self in contemplation, and an intense desire to learn. These must be coupled with the inner confidence that pertinent knowledge can be directly received. To those who believe that all answers are known, there is little need to search.
quotes.translation: 这种体验发生在他能够接受的框架内——即高度加速的灵感。他的意识只有在陷入在他看来强度几乎难以承受的灵感阵痛之后才离开身体。如果他的习惯引导他进行定期祈祷，那么那个框架也可以被使用。在所有此类案例中，有几个特质显而易见：向内审视的能力、深度专注的能力、在沉思中磨去以物质为导向的自我尖锐边缘的能力，以及强烈的求知欲。这些必须与内在信心相结合，即相信相关知识可以直接接收。对于那些相信所有答案都已为人所知的人来说，几乎没有必要去探索。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: So your physical life is the result of a spectacular spontaneous order--the order of the body spontaneously formed by the units of consciousness. Your experience of the world is largely determined by your imaginations and your reasoning abilities. These did not develop through time, as per usual evolutionary beliefs. Both imagination and reason belonged to the species from the beginning, but the species has used these qualities in different ways throughout what you think of as historic time. There is great leeway in that direction, so that the two can be combined in many many alternate fashions, each particular combination giving you its own unique picture of reality, and determining your experience in the world.
quotes.translation: 因此，你们的物质生活是壮观自发秩序的结果——由意识单位自发形成的身体秩序。你们对世界的体验很大程度上取决于你们的想象力和推理能力。这些能力并非如通常的进化信仰所认为的那样随时间发展而来。想象力和理性从一开始就属于这个物种，但该物种在你们所谓的历史时期中以不同方式运用了这些特质。在这方面存在极大的余地，因此这两者可以以许多许多交替的方式结合，每一种特定的结合都会赋予你们自己独特的现实图景，并决定你们在世界的体验。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2

quotes.text: Psychically, your world is composed of the contents of its consciousness. You have maps of continents and oceans, and in the entire view each portion is like a piece of a jigsaw puzzle, all fitting together perfectly, smoothly flowing into the natural structure of the world. So at any given time there is a world consciousness, a perfect jigsaw of awareness in which each identity, however large or small, has its part.
quotes.translation: 在心灵层面，你们的世界由意识的全部内容构成。你们拥有大陆和海洋的地图，从整体视角看，每个部分都像拼图的一块，完美契合，流畅地融入世界的自然结构。因此，在任何特定时刻，都存在一个世界意识，一个完美的意识拼图，其中每个身份，无论大小，都有其位置。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: Ruburt once wrote a poem about a nail on a window sill. He endowed the nail with consciousness and self-awareness. Now every nail is indeed in its own way responsive to stimuli. It acts and reacts. A nail may not choose to jump down from a window still and dance about the room, but a nail is indeed aware of the room, of the window sill, and aware of the temperature on both sides of the window. The atoms and molecules that compose the nail possess their own lively consciousnesses. Their motion is directed by electrons, so that within itself the nail actually experiences constant motion. Indeed, a dance is executed of great symmetry and rhythm. The nail, then, is indeed filled with its own sense of self-approval.
quotes.translation: 鲁柏曾写过一首关于窗台上钉子的诗。他赋予了钉子意识和自我觉知。现在，每颗钉子确实以其自身方式对刺激做出反应。它行动并回应。一颗钉子或许不会选择从窗台跳下并在房间里跳舞，但一颗钉子确实意识到房间、窗台，并意识到窗户两侧的温度。构成钉子的原子和分子拥有它们自己活泼的意识。它们的运动由电子引导，因此在钉子内部，它实际上经历着持续的运动。确实，一场具有极大对称性和节奏的舞蹈正在上演。那么，这颗钉子确实充满了它自己的自我认可感。
quotes.book: The Way Toward Health

quotes.text: Yet the units of consciousness, being independent of space and time, form your cellular structure, and that structure deals in a most basic manner with the nature of probabilities. Although the body appears permanent and in existence from one moment to the next, basically it constantly rises out of the bed of probabilities, hovering at your now-point of perception and experience, and its apparent stability is dependent upon the knowledge of "future" probabilities as well as "past" ones.
quotes.translation: 然而，意识单位独立于时空，构成你们的细胞结构，而该结构以最基本的方式处理或然性的本质。虽然身体看似永恒存在，从一刻延续到下一刻，但基本上它不断从或然性的基底中升起，悬浮在你们感知与体验的当下点，其表面稳定性既依赖于"未来"或然性的知识，也依赖于"过去"的。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1

quotes.text: (With much animation:) As an example, it appears to you that animals do not reflect upon their own reality. Certainly it seems that a cell has no "objective" knowledge of its own being, colon: as if it is without knowing what it is, or without appreciation of its own isness. You are quite wrong in such deductions. Nor are there necessarily gradations in which one kind of consciousness progresses in rigid terms from a lower to a higher state. Any cell has practical use of precognitive abilities,4 for example, that quite escape you, yet many of you assign such abilities to "higher" souls. Each kind of life has its own qualities that cannot be compared with those of others, and that often cannot be communicated.
quotes.translation: （充满活力地：）举例来说，在你们看来，动物并不反思自身的实相。当然，细胞似乎对其自身存在没有"客观"认知——仿佛它不知自己为何物，也不欣赏自身的存在性。你们这样的推论大错特错。意识也未必存在严格的等级，从低级向高级线性进化。例如，任何细胞都实际运用着预知能力，而你们对此浑然不觉，却常将这种能力归于"高等"灵魂。每种生命形态都有其无法与其他形态比较的特质，这些特质往往无法被传达。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: Man desired in one way to step out of himself, out of the framework in which he had his psychological existence, to try new challenges, to step out of a mode of consciousness into another. He wanted to study the process of his own consciousness. In one way this meant a giant separation from the inner spontaneity that had given him both peace and security. On the other hand, it offered a new creativity, in his terms.
quotes.translation: 人类一方面渴望跳出自我，跳出其心理存在的框架，尝试新的挑战，从一种意识模式跃入另一种。他想研究自身意识的过程。从某方面而言，这意味着与曾赋予他平静与安全的内在自发性产生巨大分离。另一方面，这又提供了他认知中的新创造力。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: Not only that, it is simply a fact that your scientists will indeed discover, and no misty, magical superstition, that consciousness, of itself and because of its nature, forms physical matter. Because I once inhabited physical matter, as you all do now, does not mean that I am now some esoteric, occult creature of dim spiritualistic rather doubtful origins, who manages to invade gullible and neurotic consciousness.
quotes.translation: 不仅如此，这纯粹是事实——你们的科学家终将发现，意识因其本质而自行形成物质实体，这绝非模糊的魔法迷信。我曾栖身于物质形体（正如你们现在所做），但这并不意味着我如今是某种源自可疑灵媒、侵入轻信者与神经质意识的玄秘幽暗存在。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)

quotes.text: He was looking for a state of higher consciousness that would represent a unique and yet universal source of information and revelation. Such a source does exist for each individual, regardless of how it is interpreted. The white light is characteristically a symbol in such cases. He could not assimilate the information, and became frightened, to some extent at least, at the vastness of the experience involved, as if the ancient yet new knowledge that he sought for his individual reasons was so encompassing that his own individuality would have trouble handling it while retaining its own necessary frame of reference. A natural-enough reaction, simply because of the usual unfamiliarity of such experiences.
quotes.translation: 他在寻找一种更高的意识状态，这种状态将代表一个独特而普遍的信息与启示之源。这样的源头确实存在于每个人之中，无论人们如何诠释它。白光在此类情境中典型地是一种象征。他无法吸收这些信息，并在某种程度上被所涉体验的浩瀚所惊吓，仿佛他因个人原因所追寻的古老而崭新的知识如此包罗万象，以至于他的个体性在保留自身必要参照系的同时，难以处理它。这是一种相当自然的反应，仅仅因为这类体验通常不为人熟悉。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: Now it is easier perhaps for some of you to understand the simple stories and parables of beginnings of which I have spoken. But the time has come for mankind to take several steps further, to expand the nature of his own consciousness by trying to comprehend a more profound version of reality. You have outgrown the time of children's tales. When your own thoughts have a form and reality, when they have validity even in other systems of reality of which you are unaware, then it is not difficult to understand why other systems of probabilities are also affected by your own thoughts and emotions -- nor why the actions of the probable gods are not affected by what happens in other dimensions of existence.
quotes.translation: 现在，你们中有些人或许更容易理解我所讲述的那些简单的起源故事和寓言。但人类已到了需要再迈出几步的时刻，通过尝试理解一个更深刻的实相版本，来扩展自身意识的本质。你们已经超越了孩童故事的时代。当你们自己的思想具有形态和实相，甚至在你们所不知的其他实相系统中也具备有效性时，那么就不难理解为什么其他概率系统也会受到你们自己思想和情感的影响——也不难理解为什么那些可能神祇的行为不受其他存在维度中所发生之事的影响。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: You might feel as if you are crossing your wires, so to speak, or stretching vaguely sensed psychic muscles. The purpose is not so much the perfect execution of such exercises as it is to involve you in a different mode of experience and of awareness that comes into being as you perform in the ways suggested. You have been taught not to mix, say, waking and dreaming conditions, not to daydream. You have been taught to focus all of your attention clearly, ambitiously, energetically in a particular way -- so daydreaming, or mixing and matching modes of consciousness, appears passive in a derogatory fashion, or nonactive, or idle. (Louder:) "The devil finds work for idle hands" -- an old Christian dictum.
quotes.translation: 你们可能会感觉，打个比方说，像是接错了线路，或是在伸展隐约感知到的灵性肌肉。其目的与其说是完美执行这类练习，不如说是让你们在按建议方式行动时，进入一种不同的体验与觉知模式。你们被教导不要混淆清醒与梦境状态，不要做白日梦。你们被教导要以特定方式清晰、专注、充满活力地集中所有注意力——因此，白日梦，或混合与匹配意识模式，便显得被动而带有贬义，或被视为不活跃、无所事事。（更大声地：）“魔鬼为游手好闲者找活干”——一句古老的基督教格言。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche

quotes.text: And I want you both to know if you do not already know that the creativity and the joy and the wonder that is inherent in each consciousness is present not only in your minds and in your consciousness as you think of it, but also in the smallest cell within your fingertips.
quotes.translation: 我要你们俩知道——若你们尚不知晓——每个意识中固有的创造力、喜悦与惊奇，不仅存在于你们所认知的头脑与意识中，也存在于你们指尖最微小的细胞里。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)

quotes.text: Ego consciousness must now be familiarized with its roots, or it will turn into something else. You are in a position where your private experience of yourself does not correlate with what you are told by your societies, churches, sciences, archaeologies, or other disciplines. Man's "unconscious" knowledge is becoming more and more consciously apparent. This will be done under and with the direction of an enlightened and expanding egotistical awareness (much louder), that can organize the hereto neglected knowledge -- or it will be done at the expense of the reasoning intellect (again louder), leading to a rebirth of superstition, chaos, and the unnecessary war between reason and intuitive knowledge.
quotes.translation: 自我意识如今必须熟悉其根源，否则它将蜕变为其他形态。你们正处在这样的处境：你们对自身的私人体验，与社会、教会、科学、考古学或其他学科告诉你们的内容并不相符。人类"无意识"的知识正越来越清晰地显现在意识层面。这将在开明且扩展的自我觉知（声音更响亮）的指引下完成——它能组织起迄今被忽视的知识——否则将以牺牲理性智性为代价（再次提高音量），导致迷信重生、混乱蔓延，以及理性与直觉知识之间不必要的战争。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1

quotes.text: Give us a moment ... Those intimate realizations, however, had to be counterbalanced in line with certain purposes set by your species, and even for that matter momentarily set aside so that other abilities and characteristics could emerge. The species' sense of curiosity would not allow it to stay in any home territory for long,6 and so the sense of intimacy was purposely broken. It would become highly important again, however, when the planet was populated extensively, as it is now -- only the original feeling of home area has to be extended over the face of the earth. The "absent" portions of the self are ready to emerge. The other, to you probable, lines of consciousness can now come into play.
quotes.translation: 请稍等片刻……然而，那些亲密的领悟必须根据你们物种设定的某些目的而加以平衡，甚至暂时搁置，以便其他能力与特性得以浮现。物种的好奇心不允许它长久停留在任何家园领域，因此亲密感被刻意打破。但当星球像现在这样广泛人口定居时，这种亲密感将再次变得极为重要——只是最初的家园感必须扩展到整个地球表面。自我中"缺席"的部分已准备好浮现。那些对你们而言属于可能性的意识脉络，现在可以开始发挥作用了。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: If you are normally capable of dealing with physical reality, you will encounter no difficulties in alterations of consciousness, or leaving your home station. Be reasonable, however: If you have difficulties in New York City, you are most apt to encounter them in a different form no matter where else you might travel. A change of environment might help clear your head by altering your usual orientation, so that you can see yourself more clearly, and benefit. The same applies when you leave your home station. Here the possible benefits are far greater than in usual life and travel, but you are still yourself. It is impossible not to structure reality in some fashion. Reality implies a structuring.
quotes.translation: 若你通常能妥善处理物质实相，那么在意识转换或离开家园站台时便不会遭遇困难。但请保持理性：若你在纽约市遇到困难，无论旅行至何处，这些困难都可能以不同形式重现。环境改变或许能通过调整惯常取向来理清思绪，让你更清晰地看见自己并获益。离开家园站台时亦是如此——此处可能的益处远超寻常生活与旅行，但你仍是自己。以某种方式构建实相是不可避免的，因为实相本身就意味着一种构建。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: So you look back through the historical past. All of the counterparts alive as contemporaries then form, together, a musical composition in what you think of as a present; and once that multidimensional song is struck then its past ripples out behind it, so to speak, and its future sings "ahead." But the song is being created from its beginning and its end simultaneously. In this case, however, it is as if each note has its own consciousness and is free to change its portion of the melody. Yet all are in the same overall composition, in "time," so that time itself serves as the scale (gesturing) in which the [musical] number is written -- chosen as a matter of organization, focus, and framework.
quotes.translation: 因此你回顾历史过往。所有当时活着的同期人物共同构成了一首乐曲，存在于你所认为的当下；一旦那首多维度的歌曲被奏响，它的过去便在其后荡漾开来，而它的未来则在"前方"歌唱。但这首歌同时从其开端和结尾被创造出来。然而在此情况下，仿佛每个音符都拥有自己的意识，并自由改变其在旋律中的部分。但所有音符都处于同一整体乐章中，在"时间"之内，使得时间本身成为谱写这首乐曲的音阶——它被选择作为组织、聚焦和框架的媒介。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: The division between the two aspects of experience begins to take on the characteristics of completely diverse behavior. The unconscious becomes more and more unfamiliar to consciousness. Those beliefs build up about it, and the symbolisms involved are exaggerated. The unknown seems to be threatening and degenerate. The color black assumes stronger tendencies in its connection with evil -- something to be avoided. Self-annihilation seems to be a threat ever-present in the dream or sleep state. At the same time all of those flamboyant, creative, spontaneous, emotional surges that emerge normally from the unconscious become feared and projected outward, then, upon enemies, other races and creeds.
quotes.translation: 经验的两个面向之间的分野开始呈现出截然不同行为的特征。无意识对意识而言变得越来越陌生。关于它的那些信念逐渐累积，相关的象征意义被夸大。未知似乎变得具有威胁性且堕落。黑色在与邪恶的关联中呈现出更强烈的倾向——成为需要回避的事物。自我毁灭似乎成为梦境或睡眠状态中始终存在的威胁。与此同时，所有那些从无意识中自然涌现的华丽、创造、自发、情感澎湃的冲动，都变得令人恐惧，并被投射到外部，转而投射到敌人、其他种族和信仰之上。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality

quotes.text: I am more aware of my reality than you are of yours, but the terms of being are the same in every place and every time. They bring forth the greater comprehension of each self, of itself. (Long pause.) Ruburt experiences now what he calls a massive quality, a physical and psychic expansion of consciousness in which the dear familiar world seems small -- yet twice precious. So does it appear to my consciousness.7
quotes.translation: 我对自身实相的意识比你对自身实相的意识更为清晰，但存在的法则在每一个时空点都是相同的。这些法则促使每个自我更深入地理解自身。（长停顿。）鲁柏此刻正经历他所谓的“巨大特质”——一种身体与心灵的意识扩张，使原本熟悉的世界显得渺小，却倍加珍贵。我的意识所见亦是如此。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: This psychological construction takes place in what you call the subconscious. I have earlier explained that no real division exists between consciousness and sub-consciousness, both equally being conscious, and both being part of the same consciousness of the whole self.
quotes.translation: 这种心理建构发生在你们所谓的潜意识中。我先前解释过，意识与潜意识之间并无真正的分野，两者同样具有意识，且同属整体自性的同一意识。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)

quotes.text: (9:21.) This body prediction is then assessed, and on more levels than it is possible for me to explain. Briefly, the picture is "shown" in the invisible arena where flesh and spirit meet. This arena is not a place, of course, but an inner state of gestalt consciousness. The state is brought about through certain interactions that occur deep within the body. Magnetic structures are formed. They are created on a physical level through certain activations of the nerves in which the normal patterns are jumped, so to speak, and images are formed. The nerves and the cellular structures at their tips take pictures. These are all assembled and used to form the larger picture of the body's condition.
quotes.translation: （9点21分。）随后，这具身体的预测会被评估，其评估层面之多远超我所能解释的范围。简言之，这幅“图像”会在血肉与灵性交汇的无形场域中“显现”。这个场域当然并非一个地点，而是一种完形意识的内在状态。此状态通过身体深处发生的特定交互作用而形成。磁力结构由此产生——它们借由神经的特定激活在物质层面被创造出来，在此过程中，正常模式被“跳跃”，图像随之成形。神经及其末梢的细胞结构会拍摄影像。所有这些影像被整合，用以构建身体状态的完整图像。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality

quotes.text: We prefer instead Seth's -- as well as our own -- concepts of the inviolate nature of the individual consciousness, before, during, and after physical existence, in ordinary terms, and whether or not any theory of reincarnation is involved. It may be natural enough for us in the West not to enjoy the idea of surrendering our individual natures upon physical death, even if intellectually we can understand, for instance, the Buddhist teaching that "perfect" joy can be found in the eventual, blissful surrender of the self to a supreme spirit -- although I note with some humor that personally I've yet to determine how the self who surrenders knows it's done so if it's been so thoroughly absorbed.
quotes.translation: 我们更倾向采用赛斯——以及我们自己——关于个体意识在肉身存在之前、期间与之后（以通俗说法而言）不可侵犯本质的概念，无论是否涉及任何转世理论。对西方人而言，抗拒在肉体死亡时放弃个体本性或许很自然，即便理智上我们能理解例如佛教教导——在最终将自我喜乐地交付给至高灵性时能获得"圆满"喜悦——不过我略带幽默地注意到，我始终无法确定那个交付自我的存在，若已被彻底吸收，又如何知晓自己已完成交付。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1

quotes.text: Now, many diseases are simply caused by this division of yours and this long period of bodily inactivity, and this extended focus of attention in either waking or dreaming reality. Your normal consciousness can benefit by excursions and rest in those other fields of actuality that are entered when you sleep, and the so-called sleeping consciousness will also benefit by frequent excursions into the waking state.
quotes.translation: 如今许多疾病正是源于这种分裂状态、长期的身体不活动，以及清醒或梦境实相中注意力的过度聚焦。你的正常意识能通过睡眠时进入的那些实相领域的游历与休憩获益，而所谓的睡眠意识也将因频繁游历清醒状态而受益。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: These chemical excesses are a natural byproduct of consciousness that is bound up in physical materialization. The more intense the characteristic experience of reality, the greater the chemical excess that is built up. Consciousness itself, when physically oriented, burns up chemicals. The more intense the individual the hotter the fire, so to speak, and the greater the chemical excesses that must be released.
quotes.translation: 这些化学物质过剩是固着于物质显化中的意识自然副产品。对实相的特征体验越强烈，累积的化学过剩就越多。当意识以物质为导向时，本身就会燃烧化学物质。可以说，个体越强烈，火焰就越炽热，必须释放的化学过剩也就越多。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 6 of The Seth Material (Sessions 240-280)

quotes.text: Not being, in other terms, is impossible. It is being without the means of expressing being. Now, every portion of consciousness is imbued with innate knowledge towards the means of expression and creativity.
