If, and this is impossible, all portions but the most minute last unit of All That Is were destroyed, All That Is could still continue, for within the smallest portion is the innate knowledge of the whole.
quotes.translation: 换言之，不存在是不可能的。存在是没有表达存在的手段。现在，意识的每个部分都充满了对表达和创造手段的内在知识。如果，尽管这不可能，所有部分除了"一切万有"最微小的最后一个单位都被摧毁了，"一切万有"仍能继续存在，因为最微小的部分也蕴含着整体的内在知识。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)

quotes.text: Now in the drug situation the state of consciousness desired is brought about by altering chemical properties. Consciousness, travelling, cannot automatically manipulate the physical station. Consciousness must stay out, you see, until the chemical situation has changed.
quotes.translation: 在药物情境中，所渴望的意识状态是通过改变化学性质来实现的。意识在旅行时无法自动操控物质站。你看，意识必须保持在外，直到化学状况改变。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)

quotes.text: In that picture consciousness has little part to play. In man's very early history, however, and in your terms for centuries after the "awakening," as described in our book, people lived in good health for much longer periods of time--and in certain cases they lived for several centuries.1 No one had yet told them that this was impossible, for one thing. Their sense of wonder in the world, their sense of curiosity, creativity, and the vast areas of fresh mental and physical exploration, kept them alive and strong. For another thing, however, elders were highly necessary and respected for the information they had acquired about the world. They were needed. They taught the other generations.
quotes.translation: 在那幅画面中，意识几乎不起作用。然而，在人类非常早期的历史中，用你们的话说，在我们书中描述的"觉醒"之后的几个世纪里，人们健康地生活了更长的时间——在某些情况下，他们活了好几个世纪。首先，还没有人告诉他们这是不可能的。他们对世界的好奇感、好奇心、创造力，以及广阔的精神和物质探索领域，使他们保持活力和强壮。然而，另一方面，长者因其积累的世界知识而非常必要且受尊敬。他们被需要。他们教导其他世代。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1

quotes.text: Ruburt was correct in his introductory notes (for Mass Events) today--about the poet's long-forgotten abilities, and his role. Ruburt has been a poet all of the time in the most profound meaning of that term. For the poet did not simply string words together, but sent out a syntax of consciousness, using rhythm and the voice, rhyme and refrain as methods to form steps up which his own consciousness could rush.
quotes.translation: 鲁柏今天（为《群体事件》）所作的引言笔记是正确的——关于那位诗人久被遗忘的能力及其角色。鲁柏始终是诗人，以该词最深刻的意义而言。因为诗人并非简单堆砌文字，而是发出意识的句法，运用节奏与声音、韵律与叠句作为方法，构筑出能让自身意识奔涌而上的阶梯。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1

quotes.text: The form that consciousness takes in the field of matter is determined by its own strength and capacity. Consciousness forces its way into matter. You should see, then, that though you call a particular portion of grass one blade of grass, the matter that composes it is not static or permanent, and it is not a particular physical object growing, since the matter that composes it is neither static or permanent.
quotes.translation: 意识在物质领域呈现的形态，取决于其自身的力量与容量。意识强行进入物质之中。因此你应当明白，当你将某一特定部分的草称为一株草时，构成它的物质既非静止也非永恒，它并非一个正在生长的固定物理对象，因为构成它的物质既非静止也非永恒。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)

quotes.text: Creation, therefore, constantly continues, and not always along the lines of old patterns, but by means of completely different patterns. And another small but interesting point: On your own plane, there is a subconscious storehouse of knowledge, whereby it is known in a condensed fashion, by all molecules and atoms, exactly which variant or evolutionary attempts have been made, with what results--and always with an eye out, so to speak, for circumstances that might fit forms once adopted with failure, or to attempt other forms for which present circumstances may not be right. I have said that, first of all, molecules and atoms and even smaller particles, have a condensed consciousness.
quotes.translation: 因此，创造持续不断地进行，且并非总是沿袭旧有模式，而是通过完全不同的模式进行。另一个有趣的小细节是：在你们自己的层面上，存在一个潜意识的知识库，所有分子和原子都以浓缩的方式知晓，究竟哪些变异或进化尝试已被进行过，结果如何——而且可以说，它们始终在留意那些可能适合曾被尝试却失败的形式的环境，或尝试那些当前环境可能尚不适合的其他形式。我曾说过，首先，分子、原子乃至更微小的粒子，都具有浓缩的意识。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)

quotes.text: (11:40.) Give us a moment ... Other minute creatures might well mark portions of their lives with your coming and going, and imagine that your position at their birth regulated their activity. Imagine them making up charts correlating their lives with your own. Are you in the habit of pacing the floor? In another scale of time, how many ages might it seem to take for your shadow to cross from one side of the room to another? The analogy is not as farfetched as it may seem, for certainly your shadow will affect the temperature of the room minutely, and alter other conditions there in ways you would never comprehend, often causing gigantic variations to a consciousness on another scale.
quotes.translation: （11:40）稍等片刻……其他微小的生物可能会用你们的来去标记它们生命的某些部分，并想象你们在它们诞生时的位置决定了它们的活动。想象它们绘制图表，将它们的生命与你们的生命相关联。你是否习惯在地板上踱步？在另一个时间尺度上，你的影子从房间一侧移到另一侧，可能需要多少个纪元？这个类比并不像看起来那么牵强，因为你的影子确实会细微地影响房间的温度，并以你永远无法理解的方式改变那里的其他条件，常常对另一个尺度的意识造成巨大的变化。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: Once again, however, these are of course blueprints of probabilities. As you know, the past itself is constantly changed as your own attitude toward it changes. Therefore you see, even dealing with the past, cellular consciousness does not involve knowledge of a closed and finished time of existence. Obviously then, the future is also constantly changing. Cellular consciousness mirrors these changes, you see.
quotes.translation: 然而，这些当然又是或然性的蓝图。如你所知，过去本身会随着你对其态度的改变而不断变化。因此你看，即便在处理过去时，细胞意识所涉及的也并非一个封闭且已完成的存在时间。显然，未来同样在不断变化。细胞意识映照着这些变化，你明白吗。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)

quotes.text: I do not mean to imply, however, that only the great share in this communication of consciousness. (Pause.) A great simplicity is necessary, and out of this, many of the most lowly in men's terms also share in these communications. There is an unending conversation going on throughout the universe, and a most meaningful one. (Long pause.) Those from both your past and your future have a hand in your present world, and at this level the problems that have been met and will be encountered are being discussed. This is the heart of communication. It is most usually encountered either in a protected deep level of sleep or in a sudden spontaneous trance state. Great energy is generated.
quotes.translation: 但我并非暗示只有伟大者才能参与这种意识交流。（停顿）一种极大的单纯性是必要的，由此，按人类标准而言最卑微的许多人也参与其中。整个宇宙中持续进行着一场永无止境的对话，一场意义深远的对话。（长停顿）来自你过去与未来的存在都参与塑造着你的当下世界，在这个层面上，已被解决和将要遭遇的问题正在被探讨。这是交流的核心。它最常在受保护的深层睡眠状态或突发的自发恍惚状态中被触及。巨大的能量由此生成。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: "Now I'm getting ideas from so many places at once, so fast, that I can't express them all. I need you to coach me, to ask, 'What's happening now?' to keep me focused on one channel.... Because our mental habits automatically block out such material, we only recognize one series of neurological happenings -- it takes time for the message to leap the nerve endings [the synapses]. We just recognize one speed. Other messages leap too fast or too slow for us to focus upon them. By altering our consciousness in the way I'm learning to do now, though, we can line up our focuses with these other 'ghostly' messages, that are quite as real as the neurological validity we usually accept."
quotes.translation: "此刻我同时从如此多源头接收信息，速度快到无法全部表达。我需要你引导我，问'现在发生了什么？'来让我聚焦于单一通道……因为我们的心智习惯会自动屏蔽这类材料，我们只识别一连串神经事件——信息需要时间跨越神经末梢（突触）。我们只识别一种速度。其他信息要么太快要么太慢，以致我们无法聚焦。但通过我正在学习的方式改变意识，我们可以将焦点对准这些'幽灵般'的信息——它们与我们通常接受的神经有效性同样真实。"
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1

quotes.text: (10:00.) The Arab was a very interesting character, by the way, and to illustrate some of the difficulties involved, I will tell you about him. He hated the Jews, but somehow he was obsessed with the idea that Moses was more powerful than Allah, and for years this was the secret sin upon his conscience. He spent some time in Constantinople at the time of the Crusades. He was captured, and ended up with a group of Turks, all to be executed by the Christians, in this case very horribly so. They forced his mouth open and stuffed it with burning coals, as a starter. He cried to Allah, and then in greater desperation to Moses, and as his consciousness left his body, Moses was there.
quotes.translation: （10:00。）顺便说一句，那个阿拉伯人是个非常有趣的角色。为了说明其中涉及的一些困难，我要讲讲他的故事。他憎恨犹太人，但不知为何，他执著地认为摩西比安拉更强大，多年来这成了他良心上隐秘的罪孽。他在十字军东征时期曾在君士坦丁堡待过一段时间。他被俘后，最终与一群土耳其人一起被基督徒处决，这次处决极其残忍。他们先撬开他的嘴，塞满燃烧的煤块。他向安拉呼救，随后在更绝望中向摩西呼救，当他的意识离开身体时，摩西就在那里。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: Such a consciousness is at a state of being in which its reality generates more energy and power than it can physically express in its brilliant intersection with physical reality. The "sparks" generated by each instant of its existence cause additional experiences, perceptions, that will not fit in the known moment of the present -- for by then in your terms that present has already vanished into the past.
quotes.translation: 这样的意识处于一种存在状态，其现实所生成的能量与力量，远超它在与物质现实辉煌交汇时所能物理表达的程度。它每瞬间存在所激发的“火花”，会引发额外的经验与感知，这些无法被纳入已知的当下时刻——因为按你们的说法，那个当下早已消逝于过去之中。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche

quotes.text: (9:19.) Remember that cells have consciousness, so while I say these leanings are biologically entwined, they are also mental properties. Drawing in its simplest form is, again, an extension of those inclinations, and in a fashion serves two purposes. Particularly on the part of children, it allows them to express forms and shapes that they see mentally first of all. When they draw circles or squares, they are trying to reproduce those inner shapes, transposing those images outward into the environment--a creative act, highly significant, for it gives children experience in translating inner perceived events of a personal nature into a shared physical reality apparent to all.
quotes.translation: （9:19。）记住，细胞拥有意识，因此虽然我说这些倾向与生物学交织，但它们同时也是心智属性。以最简单的形式而言，绘画再次是那些倾向的延伸，并且以某种方式服务于双重目的。尤其在儿童身上，它允许他们首先表达出内心所见的形态与形状。当他们画圆圈或方块时，他们是在试图重现那些内在形状，将这些影像向外投射到环境中——这是一种创造性行为，意义重大，因为它让儿童体验到将个人性质的内在感知事件，转化为众人皆可见的共享物质现实。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2

quotes.text: Yet you are more limited than this by far. It is important for the race of men now to begin to use and experiment with the inner senses, since for the potentialities of humanity to be fully realized, new concepts must arise which cannot arise in the limited scope he now permits himself. Because the cells and molecules in general have consciousness, because they contain within themselves a capsule comprehension of the universe as a whole, and because they contain the ability to form into an almost infinite variety of form, there is a kinship between every atom and molecule, a basic enduring connection, regardless of the separate appearance which is seen using the outer senses.
quotes.translation: 然而你们远比这更受限制。对人类种族而言，现在开始使用并实验内在感官至关重要，因为若要充分实现人类的潜能，就必须产生新的概念——而这些概念无法在他目前允许自己的有限范围内产生。由于细胞和分子普遍具有意识，由于它们自身包含对宇宙整体的胶囊式理解，由于它们具备形成几乎无限多样形态的能力，每个原子与分子之间都存在着亲缘关系，一种基本而持久的联结，无论用外在感官观察到的表象多么分离。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)

quotes.text: We have been speaking of ascending selves in your terms. In your (underlined) terms, there are also, of course, descending selves, in that each atom and molecule has its own consciousness and contains all the characteristics inherent in consciousness itself. It is individualistic, it contains qualities you term personality qualities. It adopts form, gains experience, and develops through value fulfillment.
quotes.translation: 我们一直在用你们的说法讨论上升的自我。在你们（强调）的用语中，当然也存在下降的自我，因为每个原子和分子都拥有自己的意识，并包含意识本身固有的所有特性。它是个体化的，包含你们称之为个性品质的特质。它采取形态，获取经验，并通过价值完成来发展。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)

quotes.text: Some personalities are drawn to enter at conception as a result of seemingly less worthy motives -- greed, for example, or an obsessional desire that is partially composed of unresolved problems. Other personalities who never completely take to earthly existence may hold off full entry for some time, and even then always remain at a certain distance from the body. At the other end of the scale, before death the same applies, where some individuals remove their focus from physical life, leaving the body consciousness alone. Others stay with the body until the last moment. In the early days of infancy, there is not a steady focus of the personality in the body in any case.
quotes.translation: 有些人格因看似不那么高尚的动机——例如贪婪，或部分由未解问题构成的执念——而选择在受孕时进入。另一些从未完全适应尘世存在的人格，可能会推迟全面进入一段时间，甚至始终与身体保持一定距离。在生命另一端，死亡前也是如此：有些人将焦点从物质生活中抽离，让身体意识独自存在；另一些人则与身体相伴至最后一刻。在婴儿早期，人格在身体中本就没有稳定的焦点。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: Again Ruburt "tuned in," altered his state of consciousness, and was told not to take them. Joseph was not to have them either. The unconscious knowledge was given (pause), and statements of each body's condition. Both were safe as long as shots were not taken. In this case Ruburt and Joseph acted in direct contradiction to authoritative radio statements, and held their own despite the fact that others in the immediate environment rushed off to the medical centers. They placed their lives on the line. Only an hour later the radio announcements completely changed; people were told that they did not need shots, and that indeed the inoculations could cause severe reactions.
quotes.translation: 鲁柏再次“调频”，改变了他的意识状态，并被告知不要接种疫苗。约瑟也不应接种。无意识的知识被传递（停顿），并陈述了每具身体的状况。只要不接种疫苗，两人都是安全的。这次，鲁柏和约瑟直接违背了权威的广播声明，坚持己见，尽管周围其他人纷纷涌向医疗中心。他们将自己的生命置于险境。仅一小时后，广播公告完全改变；人们被告知无需接种疫苗，且接种确实可能引发严重反应。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality

quotes.text: Cells retain their shape and integrity, and their position more or less within your organs, although the atoms and molecules within them change. The overall pattern continues, however, so that your body retains its familiarity even as, in other terms, the mountain maintains its form. The cells serve as patterns of development on the one hand, through which atoms and molecules express their being. Each category is dependent upon the others. So your own consciousness follows a certain line of development that is its own, and that recognizes its own "seasons." Other offshoots of yourself, in your terms, operate following their own orders in times quite apart from your own.
quotes.translation: 细胞保持其形状与完整性，并在你的器官内大致维持其位置，尽管其中的原子和分子不断变化。然而，整体模式持续存在，因此你的身体保持其熟悉感——正如从其他角度而言，山脉维持其形态。细胞一方面作为发展的模式，原子和分子通过它们表达自身的存在。每个类别都依赖于其他类别。因此，你自己的意识遵循一条属于它自己的特定发展路线，并识别其自身的“季节”。在你看来，你的其他分支自我，则在与你自身时间截然不同的时段中，遵循各自的秩序运作。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: The number one time cannot contain other times but the consciousness, with help, can to some extent perceive these other times. And this perception then allows consciousness to escape some of the confinements of that one time. Our spacious present of which I have spoken contains all times, but it is not a thing apart from them, nor precisely their sum. It is ever unfolding and mobile, and changing itself.
quotes.translation: 第一时间无法包含其他时间，但意识在协助下能在某种程度上感知这些其他时间。这种感知随后使意识得以摆脱那单一时间的某些束缚。我曾提及的我们那广阔当下包含所有时间，但它既非独立于这些时间之外之物，亦非它们的精确总和。它始终在展开、流动，并不断改变自身。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 5 of The Seth Material (Sessions 199-239)

quotes.text: We gave you the pattern by which your physical selves are formed. We gave you the pattern by which you learned to form your physical reality. We gave you the patterns intricate, involved, and blessed from which you form the reality of each physical thing you know. The most minute cell within your brain has been made from the patterns of consciousness, which we have given you. We gave you the pattern upon which you formed your entire physical universe... and the comprehension that exists within each cell, the knowledge that each cell has, the desire for organization was given by us. The entire webwork was initiated by us. We taught you to form the reality that you know.
quotes.translation: 我们曾给予你们形成物质自我的模式，给予你们学习构建物质实相的模式，给予你们那些精妙、复杂且受祝福的模式——你们借此构建所知每件物质事物的实相。你们大脑中最微小的细胞，皆由我们给予的意识模式所造。我们给予你们构建整个物质宇宙的模式……每个细胞内存在的理解力、每个细胞拥有的知识、对组织的渴望，皆由我们所赐。整个网络架构由我们启动。我们教导你们构建所知的实相。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)

quotes.text: The conscious mind can, for instance, see a rose as a symbol of life or death, or joy or sadness, and under certain conditions its interpretation of a simple flower can trigger deep experiences that call up power and strength from the inner resources of being. Since the attributes of egotistical consciousness have been so misinterpreted, you usually consider it only in its analytical breaking-down functions. These are very important as it separates larger fields of perception into smaller ones that can be physically understood. But the conscious mind is also a great synthesizer. It brings together diverse elements from your experience and unites them in new patterns.
quotes.translation: 例如，意识心可以将玫瑰视为生命或死亡、喜悦或悲伤的象征，在某些条件下，它对一朵简单花朵的诠释能触发深层体验，从存有的内在资源中唤起力量与强度。由于自我意识的特质被严重误解，你们通常只考虑其分析拆解的功能——这固然重要，因为它将较大的感知领域分解为可被物质性理解的小单元。但意识心同样是一位伟大的综合者，它汇集你经验中的多元元素，并将其重组为新的模式。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality

quotes.text: (10:14.) These affect entirely different systems of existence than any closely connected with your own The experience of such kinds of consciousness is highly alien to you. One such fluctuation might take several thousand of your years, for example. These several thousand years would be experienced, say, as a second of your time, with the events occurring within it perceived simply as a "present period."
quotes.translation: （10:14）这些波动影响的存有系统与你们自身紧密相关的系统截然不同。此类意识形态的经验对你们而言极为陌生。例如，某次波动可能持续你们数千年之久。而这数千年会被体验为你们时间中的一秒钟，其中发生的事件仅被感知为"当下时段"。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: All psychological structures then are composed of such organizations, however long-or short-lived in your terms. They are innately endowed with the desire or propensity for growth and creative organization. They are not found alone, then, in isolation. Since these units of consciousness exist at once, they are aware of all the organized self-structures of which they are a part. To this extent, all probable realities are connected in that basic manner. These units grow out of themselves. Since I have told you that in your terms your past, present, and future exist at once, these units are constantly emerging out of your now-point from both the future and the past.
quotes.translation: 所有心理结构皆由这类组织构成，无论以你们的标准衡量其寿命长短。它们天生具备成长与创造性组织的渴望或倾向。因此它们不会孤立存在。由于这些意识单位同时存在，它们能觉察自身所属的所有组织化自体结构。就此而言，所有可能实相都以这种基本方式相连。这些单位从自身中生长而出。既然我曾告诉你们，在你们的时间概念中过去、现在与未来同时存在，那么这些单位便不断从你们"当下点"的未来与过去两端同时涌现。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1

quotes.text: Consciousness is not dependent upon form, as I have said, and yet it always seeks to create form. We do not exist in any time framework as you know it. Minutes, hours, or years have lost both their meaning and their fascination. We are quite aware of the time situations within other systems, however, and we must take them into account in our communications. Otherwise what we say would not be understood.
quotes.translation: 如我曾说过的，意识并不依赖形态，但它始终寻求创造形态。我们并不存在于你们所知的时间框架中。分钟、小时或年都已失去其意义与魅力。然而，我们相当清楚其他系统内的时间状况，并且在沟通时必须将其纳入考量。否则我们所说的话将无法被理解。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: Quite practically speaking, there is no one answer, for each of you is an individual. Generally speaking, of course, there is an answer that will serve to cover main issues of this experience, but the kinds of deaths have much to do with the experience that consciousness undergoes. Also involved is the development of the consciousness itself, and its overall characteristic method of handling experience.
quotes.translation: 实际来说，并没有单一的答案，因为你们每个人都是独立的个体。当然，一般而言，存在一个足以涵盖此经验主要议题的答案，但死亡的类型与意识所经历的体验密切相关。此外，还涉及意识本身的发展程度，以及它处理经验的整体特性模式。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: Young people are urged to tackle life aggressively, but in the usage of the term this means competitively. It also implies, and of course, promotes, the direction of individual consciousness in an exterior fashion only. Not only is consciousness to be focused to the external reality, but within those limits it is still further harnessed toward certain specific goals. Other inclinations are frowned upon.
quotes.translation: 年轻人被敦促要积极进取地面对人生，但在此术语的使用中，这意味著竞争。它同时也暗示并促进个体意识仅朝向外在方向的发展。意识不仅被导向外在实相，更在此限制内被进一步束缚于特定目标。其他倾向则遭到排斥。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality

quotes.text: Each self, as a part of All That Is, therefore also retains memory of that state. It is for this reason that each portion of All That Is, each most minute consciousness, is endowed with the impetus toward survival, change, development and creativity. It is not enough that All That Is, as a primary consciousness- gestalt, desires further being, but, that every portion of it also carry this determination.
quotes.translation: 每个自我作为一切万有的一部分，因此也保留了对那种状态的记忆。正因如此，一切万有的每一部分，每一个最微小的意识，都被赋予了朝向生存、变化、发展与创造的动力。一切万有作为一个主要意识完形，不仅渴望进一步的存在，而且它的每一部分也都承载着这种决心。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)

quotes.text: It was composed of actual matter. As you should know, like all other atoms and molecules, its atoms and molecules had capsule comprehension, generalized consciousness. They formed themselves at Mark's instigation into a gestalt of physical construction; but the gestalt could not operate consistently or endure for any amount of physical time, in your field, because of the absence here of the greater data which is the property of the whole self, in its construction of a completed human form. This will take some reading over. Certain conditions, which we will discuss at length later, are necessary for a completely valid or effective materialization into human form.
quotes.translation: 它是由实际物质构成的。正如你应该知道的，像所有其他原子和分子一样，它的原子和分子具有胶囊般的理解力和普遍化的意识。它们在马克的促动下，将自己形成为一个物质结构的完形；但这个完形无法在你们的场域中持续运作或维持任何物理时间，因为这里缺少了整体自我在构建完整人类形态时所拥有的更大数据。这需要多读几遍才能理解。某些条件——我们稍后会详细讨论——对于完全有效或成功地物质化为人形是必要的。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)

quotes.text: Each reader, being presently ensconced within a physical form, I presume (humorously), knows only a small portion of himself -- as I mentioned earlier. The entity is the overall identity of which his personality is one manifestation -- an independent and eternally valid portion. In these communications, therefore, Ruburt's consciousness expands, and yet focuses in a different dimension, a dimension between his reality and mine, a field relatively free of distraction. Here I impress certain concepts upon him, with his permission and assent. They are not neutral, in that all knowledge or information bears the stamp of the personality who holds it or passes it on.
quotes.translation: 每位读者目前都安坐于肉身之中，我（幽默地）假定你们只认识自己的一小部分——正如我之前提到的。存是整体身份，而人格是其一种显现——一个独立且永恒有效的部分。因此，在这些通讯中，鲁柏的意识扩展了，却聚焦于另一个维度，一个介于他的实相与我的实相之间的维度，一个相对不受干扰的场域。在此，我经他允许与同意，将某些概念烙印于他。这些概念并非中立，因为所有知识或信息都带有持有或传递它的人格的印记。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: It deals with a different kind of experience than the one with which you are physically familiar, although that experience is also a part of the psyche. Daily life is a focus taken on the part of that portion of the psyche you call you, and there are many other such focuses. The psyche is never destroyed. Nor is that unique individuality of your own ever minimized. The psyche's experiences straddle your ideas of time, however. It seems to you quite definitely that you come alive and die. At your particular focus of consciousness, no arguments will suffice to convince you otherwise, for you are everywhere presented with the physical evidence of "fact." Period.
quotes.translation: 它处理的是与你肉体熟悉的那种经验不同的一种经验，尽管那种经验也是心灵的一部分。日常生活是心灵那部分——你称之为“你”——所采取的一个焦点，而还有许多其他这样的焦点。心灵永不毁灭。你自身那独特的个体性也绝不会被削弱。然而，心灵的经验跨越了你对时间的观念。对你而言，你确实似乎有生有死。在你特定的意识焦点上，任何论证都不足以说服你相信相反的情况，因为你处处都看到“事实”的物理证据。句号。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche

quotes.text: They are a dimension, as I mentioned. Consciousness is not one thing, therefore consciousness is not of itself limiting. Boundaries may be set up in terms of a self. A self is a gestalt of action perception patterns, which are formed together through attraction.
quotes.translation: 它们是一个维度，正如我提过的。意识并非单一之物，因此意识本身并不设限。界限可能以自我的形式被建立。自我是行动感知模式的完形，这些模式通过吸引力共同形成。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)

quotes.text: (9:26.) They built your world from the inside out. As physical creatures, they focused upon what you think of as physical identities: separate, individual differences, endowing each physical consciousness with its own original variations and creative potentials, its own opportunity for completely original experience, and a viewpoint or platform from which to participate in reality--one that at that level could not be experienced in the same way by any other individual (all very intently). This is [the] privileged, always new, private and immediate, direct experience of any individual of any species, or of any degree, as it encounters the objective universe.
quotes.translation: （9点26分）他们从内向外构建了你们的世界。作为物质生物，他们聚焦于你们所谓的物质身份：分离的、个别的差异，赋予每个物质意识其独特的原创变化和创造潜能，使其拥有完全原创体验的机会，以及参与实相的观点或平台——在那个层面上，任何其他个体都无法以相同方式体验（极其专注地）。这是任何物种、任何程度的个体在遭遇客观宇宙时，所享有的特权性、常新、私密且直接的体验。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1

quotes.text: As you read this book, now and then look about you at the room in which you sit. Chairs and tables, the ceilings and the floors, may seem very real and solid -- quite permanent -- while you by contrast may feel yourself to be highly vulnerable, caught in a moment between birth and extinction. You may even feel jealous when you think of it, imagining that the physical universe will continue to exist long after you are gone. By the end of our book, however, I hope you will realize the eternal validity of your own consciousness, and the impermanence of those physical aspects of your environment, and of your universe, that now seem so secure. Do you have that?
quotes.translation: 当你阅读本书时，请不时环顾你所在的房间。椅子、桌子、天花板和地板，看似非常真实而坚固——相当持久——而相比之下，你可能会觉得自己极度脆弱，被困在出生与消亡之间的一瞬间。你甚至可能对此感到嫉妒，想象物质宇宙在你离世后仍将长久存在。然而，到本书结束时，我希望你能认识到自己意识的永恒有效性，以及你环境与宇宙中那些看似稳固的物质面向的无常性。你明白吗？
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: The idea of counterparts1 somewhat shatters that old concept, yet you still want definitions for the self so that you know where you "stand." You are so taken with the idea of labels that many follow astrology blindly. You are born at a certain time, at a certain place, under certain conditions -- but consciousness always forms the conditions. If it is to some extent affected by those conditions, then, it is because the effects follow in the same way that a painter is affected by the landscape that he has himself created. So you decide to be born, say, in a certain month when the planets are thus-and-so. Ahead of time, you choose the seasons of your birth.
quotes.translation: 对应体的概念多少打破了那个旧观念，但你们仍想要为自我下定义，以便知道自己的"立场"。你们如此执着于标签的概念，以至于许多人盲目追随占星术。你们在特定时间、特定地点、特定条件下出生——但意识总是形成这些条件。如果说它在某种程度上受这些条件影响，那是因为这种影响就像画家被他亲手创造的风景所影响一样。因此，你决定在某个行星排列如此这般月份出生。你事先选择了你出生的季节。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: The official line of consciousness is a certain mental stance, a kind of convention. (Long pause.) When you were a child you thought in a freer fashion, but little by little you were educated to use words in a certain way. You discovered that your needs were met more quickly, and you received approval more often, when you thought and spoke in that particular manner. Finally it seemed to be the only --
quotes.translation: 意识的主流路线是一种特定的心理立场，一种惯例。（长停顿）当你还是个孩子时，你以更自由的方式思考，但渐渐地你被教育要以特定方式使用语言。你发现当你以那种特定方式思考和说话时，你的需求能更快得到满足，也更容易获得认可。最终这似乎成了唯一的——
quotes.book: The Way Toward Health

quotes.text: Everything that is, is a materialization of aware, individualized, inquiring consciousness; and to love this is a personal, almost all-encompassing discipline and devotion. I say discipline because whenever, individualized inquiring consciousness expresses itself in form, it not only expresses its spontaneity; and this is not contradictory: But even while expressing its spontaneity, it disciplines it.
quotes.translation: 一切万有，都是有觉知、个体化、探索中的意识的物质化；而热爱这一点是一种个人性的、几乎包罗万象的纪律与奉献。我说纪律是因为，每当个体化的探索中的意识以形态表达自己时，它不仅在表达其自发性；这并不矛盾：即使在表达其自发性时，它也在约束它。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)

quotes.text: In some cases you literally force yourselves to sleep when your consciousness could be at one of its maximum points. This is, incidentally, in the predawn period. In certain afternoon hours consciousness is lowered, and needs refreshment that is instead denied.
quotes.translation: 在某些情况下，当你们的意识可能处于巅峰状态时，你们反而强迫自己入睡——这通常发生在黎明前的时段。而在某些午后时分，意识会降低，需要本应被拒绝的休憩。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: While it (ego consciousness) recognized its deep oneness with the earth and all creatures, it could not at the same time develop those abilities of specialization and its own particular unique focus. The growth of separate tribal cultures, for example, and later of nations, could emerge only through a sense of separation, and a certain kind of alienation. This, however, allowed for a diversity that could not otherwise be achieved under the accepted conditions. (Pause.) The seemingly local Jewish god (Yahweh/Jehovah) ended up in one way or another by destroying the Roman Empire, and in so doing brought about a complete reorganization of planetary culture.
quotes.translation: 当（自我意识）认识到自身与大地及所有生灵的深层一体性时，它便无法同时发展那些专门化的能力及其独特焦点。例如，独立部落文化及后来民族国家的成长，只能通过分离感与某种疏离感才能出现。然而，这却促成了在既有条件下无法实现的多样性。（停顿）看似地方性的犹太神祇（耶和华/雅威）最终以某种方式摧毁了罗马帝国，并由此带来了行星文化的彻底重组。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1

quotes.text: It has been truthfully said that the "unconscious" is intimately aware of the most minute details of your health, state of mind, and relationships. "It" is also aware of the state of the earth's health -- even of the environmental conditions on the other side of the planet. It is familiar with the cultural climate also. Your recognized consciousness operates as it does because of immense information-gathering procedures -- procedures that unite all species. Biologically such information is coded, but that physical information, such as in the genes and chromosomes, can be altered through experience and mental activity in other species as well as your own.
quotes.translation: 诚然，"无意识"密切知晓你们健康、心境与关系中最细微的细节。"它"也知晓地球的健康状态——甚至包括行星另一侧的环境状况。它同样熟悉文化气候。你们所认知的意识之所以如此运作，是因为庞大的信息收集程序——这些程序联合了所有物种。生物学上，此类信息被编码，但基因与染色体中的这种物理信息，可以通过经验及包括人类在内的其他物种的心智活动而改变。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche

quotes.text: 17. These excerpts from Seth's material in the 690th session, for Volume 1, furnish a close analogy to the sort of "time" available to molecular consciousness: "... biological precognition is firmly based in the chromosomes and genes, and reflected in the cells ... The cells' practically felt 'Now' includes, then, what you think of as past and future, as simple conditions of Nowness. They maintain the body's structure in your poised time only by manipulating themselves in a rich medium of probabilities. There is a constant give-and-take of communication between the cell as you know it in present time, and the cell as it 'was' in the past, or 'will be.'"
quotes.translation: 第十七节：赛斯资料第690节（第一卷）的这段摘录，为分子意识可及的"时间"类型提供了精确类比："……生物预知能力牢固建立在染色体与基因中，并反映在细胞层面……细胞实际感知的'当下'，包含你们视为过去与未来的状态，皆属当下性的简单条件。它们仅在概率的丰饶媒介中自我运作，方能维持你们时间坐标中的身体结构。你们在当下所认知的细胞，与它'过去'或'将来'的形态之间，存在着持续不断的沟通交流。"
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: Projecting your consciousness out of your body, therefore, provides at the same time an inner probing of consciousness itself, as well as experience of its manifestations. There are then inner lands of the mind, and other worlds quite as legitimate as your own. They are intimately connected, however, with mental states which are then materialized, and so your own mental processes are highly involved.
quotes.translation: 因此，将意识投射出身体的同时，既是对意识本身的内在探询，也是对其显化形态的体验。由此存在心灵的内在疆域，以及与你们世界同样真实的其他世界。这些世界与随后物质化的心灵状态紧密相连，因此你们自身的心智过程深度参与其中。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: Consciousness is not limited. Identities can mix and merge while retaining their inviolate nature and memories. This is all for now, but again, later, you will see where it relates, and how you can disperse your own characteristics into another and they can disperse theirs into you, with your consent and theirs, to form new aspects of reality and to cast new light on combined purposes and challenges.
quotes.translation: 意识不受限制。身份可以交融与合并，同时保持其不可侵犯的本质与记忆。本次暂且至此，但后续你们将明白其关联所在，以及如何经双方同意，将自己的特质分散融入他人，他人特质亦可融入你们，从而形成实相的新面向，为共同目标与挑战投射全新光芒。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: Your spirit joined itself with flesh, and in flesh, to experience a world of incredible richness, to help create a dimension of reality of colors and of form. Your spirit was born in flesh to enrich a marvelous area of sense awareness, to feel energy made into corporeal form. You are here to use, enjoy, and express yourself through the body. You are here to aid in the great expansion of consciousness. You are not here to cry about the miseries of the human condition, but to change them when you find them not to your liking through the joy, strength and vitality that is within you; to create the spirit as faithfully and beautifully as you can in flesh.
quotes.translation: 你的灵与血肉结合，借由血肉体验一个无比丰富的世界，协助创造色彩与形态的实相维度。你的灵生于血肉之中，是为了丰富感官觉知的奇妙领域，感受能量化为物质形态。你在此是为了通过身体去运用、享受并表达自己。你在此是为了协助意识进行伟大的扩展。你并非在此哀叹人类处境的苦难，而是要在发现它们不合你意时，借由内在的喜悦、力量与活力去改变它们；尽可能忠实而优美地在血肉中创造灵性。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality

quotes.text: I surely understand that you want to make the knowledge practical in the physical world, and to help people as much as you can, but that cannot be the only goal -- for that goal must always be the high personal exploration of consciousness, the creative and artistic pursuit for which there may be no name. You do not make shoes to put on people's feet. You do not make deodorants to stop perspiration. If you did either of these things, you could see material results -- material results -- at once: people with shoes of your making, and people who did not sweat (with some humor). (In parentheses: Such deodorants are highly disadvantageous, incidentally.)
quotes.translation: 我完全理解你希望让知识在物质世界中变得实用，并尽可能帮助他人，但这不能是唯一的目标——因为那个目标始终必须是意识的高层次个人探索，是那可能无名无分的创造性与艺术性追求。你不是在做鞋子给人穿，也不是在制造止汗剂来抑制出汗。如果你做的是这些事，你立刻就能看到物质结果——物质结果——：人们穿上你做的鞋子，人们不再流汗（带着些许幽默）。（括号内：顺便一提，这类止汗剂非常不利。）
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: Consciousness at different levels or stages perceives different kinds of events. In order to perceive some of these you have only to learn to change the focus of your attention from one level to another. There are minute chemical and electromagnetic alterations that accompany these stages of consciousness, and certain physical changes within the body itself in hormone production and pineal activity.
quotes.translation: 不同层次或阶段的意识感知到不同种类的事件。要感知其中某些事件，你只需学会将注意力焦点从一个层次转换到另一个层次。伴随这些意识阶段的是细微的化学与电磁变化，以及身体内部激素分泌与松果体活动的特定生理改变。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: Energy constantly changes. Form is not a characteristic of energy, any more than it is a characteristic of matter. As a rule no form imposed upon energy can long endure. The form cannot long endure. The consciousness behind the form, that imposes the form, does endure; because while energy continually changes and does not retain imposed form, it is in itself conscious, and its consciousness endures.
quotes.translation: 能量持续变化。形态并非能量的特性，正如它并非物质的特性。通常，任何强加于能量之上的形态都无法长久存续。形态无法长久存续。而形态背后的意识——那个施加形态的意识——确实存续；因为尽管能量持续变化且不保留被强加的形态，能量本身即具有意识，其意识永续不灭。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)

quotes.text: The emergence of consciousness into those physical conditions automatically alters them -- a fact not recognized by astrologers. Each child born alters the entire universe,7 and changes the world of its time and birth by bringing into it action not there earlier, in your terms, and by impressing the universe with the stamp -- the indelible stamp -- of its reality. Each child chooses its own probable version of any given birthdate. Such dates are obviously not just points in time, pinpointed in space. In the first place, since all time is simultaneous, you are always dying and being born, and your later experience affects the time of your birth.
quotes.translation: 意识进入那些物质条件会自动改变它们——这是占星家没有认识到的事实。每个出生的孩子都会改变整个宇宙，并改变其出生时代的世界，通过将之前不存在的行动带入其中（按你们的说法），并通过用其现实的印记——不可磨灭的印记——铭刻在宇宙上。每个孩子都会选择任何给定出生日期的自己可能版本。这样的日期显然不仅仅是空间中的时间点。首先，由于所有时间都是同时存在的，你始终在死去和出生，而你后来的经验会影响你出生时的时刻。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: When you want to go downtown, you know that destination exists, though you may be miles away from it. When you want to find a mate you take it for granted that a potential mate exists, though where in space and time you do not know. Your intent to find a mate sends out "strands of consciousness," however, composed of desire and intent. Like detectives, these search the world, looking in a completely different way than a physical sleuth. The world is probed with your characteristics in mind, seeking for someone else with characteristics that will best suit your own. And whatever your purpose is, the same procedure on a psychic level is involved.
quotes.translation: 当你想去市中心时，你知道那个目的地存在，尽管你可能离它还有数英里之遥。当你想寻找伴侣时，你理所当然地认为潜在伴侣存在，尽管你不知道他在时空中的何处。然而，你寻找伴侣的意图会发出“意识之线”，由欲望和意图组成。像侦探一样，这些线搜索世界，以与物理侦探完全不同的方式寻找。世界被以你的特征为考量进行探查，寻找具有最适合你特征的人。无论你的目的是什么，在心灵层面都涉及同样的过程。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: We will continue our discussion concerning action and identities. I have said that identity is a part of action, and basically inseparable from action. Identity attempts to form meaningful patterns and relationships from action. Consciousness is action that perceives itself. The ego is action's attempt to stand off from itself.
quotes.translation: 我们将继续讨论关于行动与身份认同的议题。我曾说过，身份认同是行动的一部分，且本质上与行动不可分割。身份认同试图从行动中构建有意义的模式与关系。意识是觉察到自身的行动。自我则是行动试图与自身保持距离的尝试。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)

quotes.text: Look at the image in your mind as it exists in the snapshot, and see it as being aware only of those other objects that surround it. Its world is bounded by the four edges of the picture. Try to put your consciousness into that image of yourself. Your world view is limited to the photograph itself. Now in your mind see that image walking out of the snapshot, onto the desk or table. (Pause.) The environment of the physical room will seem gigantic to that small self. The scale and proportion alone will be far different. Imagine that miniature image navigating in the physical room, then going outside, and quite an expanded world view will result.
quotes.translation: 请审视你脑海中那张快照里的影像，想象它只能感知周围的其他物体。它的世界被照片的四条边界所局限。试着将你的意识投入那个影像中的自己——你的世界观将仅限于这张照片本身。现在，在脑海中让那个影像走出快照，来到书桌或桌面上。（停顿）物理房间的环境对这个微小的自我而言将显得无比巨大。仅比例尺度就会截然不同。想象这个微缩影像在物理房间中穿行，继而走向户外——由此将产生一个极为扩展的世界观。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: You have set up the problem for yourselves within the framework of your reference. You will not understand your part within the framework of nature until you actually see yourselves in danger of tearing it apart. You will not destroy consciousness. You will not annihilate the consciousness of even one leaf, but in your context, if the problem were not solved, these would fade from your experience.
quotes.translation: 你们在自身参照框架内为自己设定了问题。除非你们真正目睹自己濒临撕裂自然的危险，否则无法理解自身在自然框架中的角色。你们无法摧毁意识——甚至无法湮灭一片叶子的意识——但在你们的语境中，若问题未能解决，这些将从你们的体验中消逝。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: This consciousness of organs, atoms and molecules, cells and other components, is left undiminished with whatever degree of consciousness and vitality they originally possessed, not enough of course to hold form or organization to any degree. However, the image is also maintained partially by those others who view it. An explanation for this you will find in our discussion of the nature of matter.
quotes.translation: 器官、原子、分子、细胞及其他组件的意识，其原本拥有的意识与活力程度丝毫未减，当然不足以维持任何程度的形体或组织。然而，影像也部分由观看它的其他人所维持。关于此点的解释，你将在我们对物质本质的讨论中找到。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)

quotes.text: Ruburt has allowed a portion of his this-life consciousness to go off on a tangent, so to speak, on another path into another system of actuality (i.e., into his psychic library). His life there is as valid as his existence in your world. In the waking state he is able, now, to alter the direction of his focus precisely enough to bring about a condition in which he perceives both realities simultaneously. He is just beginning, so as yet he is only occasionally conscious of that other experience. He is, however, aware of it now in the back of his mind more or less constantly. It does not intrude upon the world that he knows, but enriches it.
quotes.translation: 可以说，鲁柏允许他此生意识的一部分沿着切线偏离，进入另一条通往另一实相系统的路径（即进入他的心灵图书馆）。他在那里的生活与他在你们世界的存在同样真实。在清醒状态下，他现在能够足够精确地调整其焦点的方向，以达成一种同时感知两种实相的状态。他刚开始这么做，因此目前只是偶尔意识到那另一经验。然而，他现在或多或少持续地在脑海深处意识到它。它并不侵扰他所知的世界，反而丰富了它。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: All possible entities that can ever be actualized always exist. They [have] always existed and they always will exist. All That Is must, by its characteristics, be all that it can ever be, and so there can be no end to existence--and, in those terms, no beginning. But in terms of your world the units of consciousness, acting both as forces and as psychological entities of massive power, planted the seeds of your world in a dimension of imaginative power that gave birth to physical form. In your terms those entities are your ancestors--and yet [they are] not yours alone, but the ancestors of all the consciousnesses that make up your world.
quotes.translation: 所有可能被具体化的潜在存有始终存在。它们[一直]存在，也将永远存在。一切万有，按其特性，必须成为它所能成为的一切，因此存在不可能有终点——而且，就那些术语而言，也没有起点。但就你们的世界而言，意识单位既作为力量，也作为具有巨大力量的心理实体，在想象力的维度中播下了你们世界的种子，从而孕育出物质形体。按你们的说法，那些实体是你们的祖先——然而[它们]并非你们独有，而是构成你们世界的所有意识的祖先。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1

quotes.text: Now let us start with Jung for a bit. He presumes that consciousness must be organized about an ego structure. And what he calls the unconscious, not so egotistically organized, he therefore considers without consciousness, without consciousness of a self.
quotes.translation: 现在让我们先谈谈荣格。他假定意识必须围绕一个自我结构来组织。而他所谓的无意识，由于组织方式不那么以自我为中心，因此他认为无意识是没有意识的，没有自我意识。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)

quotes.text: Not only do your metaphysics and sciences suffer, but your daily experience as a human being is far less than it could be. There are, then, probabilities quite present, and for that matter biologically practical, that would allow for a change in individual consciousness so great as literally to propel the race into another level of experience entirely. As in your terms the cavemen ventured out into the daylight of the earth, there is a time for man to venture out into a greater knowledge of his subjective reality, comma, to explore the dimensions of selfhood and go beyond the small areas of himself in which he has thus far found shelter.
quotes.translation: 不仅你们的形而上学和科学深受其害，你们作为人类的日常体验也远未达到应有的丰富程度。因此，存在着相当明显且生物学上切实可行的概率，这些概率将允许个体意识发生如此巨大的改变，以至于实际上将人类推向一个全新的体验层面。正如在你们的时间概念中，穴居人冒险进入地球的白昼世界，现在也到了人类冒险进入对自身主观现实更深刻认知的时刻，去探索自我本体的维度，并超越迄今为止赖以栖身的那一小片自我领域。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1

quotes.text: (Maybe, I thought, the experience was a delayed reaction to the projection suggestions. Just before the session I asked my pendulum a few questions -- keeping in mind that I wasn't so sure this technique should be used to explore other than physically oriented data. Usually I get excellent results with the pendulum; I learned now that the visual-interference effects had been caused by my unsuccessful attempts to perceive a thought-form sent out by a survival personality. This backed up the reasons given by Seth, then, for the eye phenomenon. Usually we use the pendulum to reach information that is just below normal waking consciousness.
quotes.translation: （我心想，也许这种体验是对投射建议的延迟反应。就在课程开始前，我用灵摆问了几个问题——同时提醒自己，我不太确定这种技巧是否应该用于探索非物理导向的数据。通常我用灵摆能得到极好的结果；我现在明白了，视觉干扰效应是由于我试图感知某个幸存人格发出的思想形态但未能成功所致。这证实了赛斯随后对眼睛现象给出的解释。我们通常用灵摆来获取刚好低于正常清醒意识层面的信息。）
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: Now unconsciously you do many of these things. You often go back in time, so to speak, and "relive" a particular event so that it has a different ending, or say things that you wish you had said. A knowledge of one state of consciousness can help you in other states. In a light trance the meaning of dream symbols will be given if you ask for them. The symbols may then be used as methods of suggestion that will be tailored for you personally. If you discover, say, that a fountain in a dream represents refreshment, then when you are tired or depressed, think of a fountain. In another layer of reality, of course, you will be creating one.
quotes.translation: 现在你无意识地做了许多这类事。你常常可以说回到过去，"重演"某个特定事件，让它有不同结局，或说出你希望自己说过的话。对一种意识状态的认识可以帮助你进入其他状态。在轻度出神状态中，如果你要求，梦的象征意义便会显现。这些象征随后可被用作为你量身定制的暗示方法。例如，如果你发现梦中的喷泉代表提神，那么当你疲倦或沮丧时，就想象喷泉。当然，在现实的另一层面，你正在创造一座喷泉。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: Symbols can be called psychic codes that are interpreted in infinite fashion according to the circumstances in which consciousness finds itself. Dream events "come together" in the same way that the universe does. Events, therefore, cannot be precisely defined. You can explore your own experience of an event, and that exploration itself alters the nature of the seemingly separate event that you began to investigate. You share, then, a mass dream experience as you share a mass waking world. Your daily experience is private and uniquely yours, yet it happens within the context of a shared environment. The same applies to the dream state.
quotes.translation: 象征可被称为心灵密码，它们根据意识所处的环境以无限方式被解读。梦的事件"聚合"的方式与宇宙的聚合方式相同。因此，事件无法被精确定义。你可以探索自己对某个事件的体验，而这种探索本身会改变你最初开始调查的那个看似独立事件的性质。因此，你共享着集体梦境体验，就像你共享集体清醒世界一样。你的日常体验是私密且独一无二的，但它发生在共享环境的背景下。梦境状态也是如此。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche

quotes.text: There are what I will call "intervals of perception." (Pause.) You are usually conscious of events that are significant neurologically, and that neurological timing is the end result of an [almost]2 infinite series of sequences. (Pause.) Those sequences are areas in which activities happen. Each consciousness within each area is tuned into its proper sequence. Each area builds on the others. The invisible particles are the framework upon which your body is formed, for example--they (underlined) move faster than the speed of light, yet you are not dizzy. You are aware of no such motion. You are tuned into a different sequence of action.
quotes.translation: 存在我称之为"感知间隔"的东西。（停顿）你通常只意识到对神经系统有重大意义的事件，而这种神经时间节奏是几乎无限序列的最终结果。（停顿）这些序列是活动发生的领域。每个领域中的每个意识都调谐到其适当的序列。每个领域都建立在其他领域之上。例如，不可见粒子是你身体形成的框架——它们（强调）以超光速运动，但你并不感到眩晕。你察觉不到这种运动。你调谐到了不同的行动序列中。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2

quotes.text: Now: All of this sounds very complicated. Yet in a different way the same processes occur at other levels, as in its way cellular consciousness perceives all of the probabilities concerned with physical survival in its most far-reaching complications. At this moment the cells within your body know the life conditions of any place on the planet, and compute these, ascertaining the ways in which they require action on the body's part. Your cells are aware of the motion of the planets and of all circumstances regarding the body's equilibrium, stability, and survival. The body is then formed constantly as the result of these computations.
quotes.translation: 现在：这一切听起来非常复杂。然而，在另一种层面上，同样的过程也发生在其他层次——就像细胞意识以其方式感知所有与物质生存相关的概率，涵盖其最深远复杂的层面。此刻，你体内的细胞知晓地球上任何地方的生命条件，并计算这些条件，确定它们需要身体采取何种行动。你的细胞意识到行星的运动，以及所有关乎身体平衡、稳定与生存的状况。身体正是这些计算结果的持续形成。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche

quotes.text: You are bombarded with such "alien intrusions" constantly. The focus of your consciousness blots these out while you are in the normal waking state. There are falling stars everywhere tumbling through the heavens, for example, though you only see some of these in the night sky. It is important during the day that a screening process be used, so that the precision of your actions can be maintained. Again, however, that fine precision rests upon an endless amount of information that impinges onto other levels of your psychological reality. Those data then become the raw material, so to speak, from which your physical events are formed.
quotes.translation: 你不断受到此类"异质入侵"的轰炸。当你处于正常清醒状态时，意识的焦点会屏蔽这些。例如，流星处处划过天际，尽管你只在夜空中看到其中一些。白天使用筛选过程至关重要，这样你行动的精确性才能得以维持。然而，再次强调，这种精微的精确性依赖于无尽的信息，这些信息冲击着你心理现实的其他层面。那些数据随后成为——可以说——形成你物质事件的原始素材。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche

quotes.text: The first Seth excerpt is in keeping with the idea of creating bridges between the two volumes of "Unknown" Reality by lifting something out of one for inclusion in the other. Once more from the 743rd session in Volume 2: "No book entitled The "Unknown" Reality can hope to make that reality entirely known. It remains nebulous because it is consciously unrealized. The best I can do is to point out areas that have been relatively invisible, to help you explore, actually, different facets of your own consciousness ... I am well aware that the book raises many more questions than it presents answers for, and this has been my intent...."
quotes.translation: 第一段赛斯摘录符合在《未知的实相》两卷之间搭建桥梁的理念，即从一卷中提取内容纳入另一卷。再次引用第二卷第743节：“任何题为《未知的实相》的书，都无法指望让那实相完全为人所知。它依然朦胧，因为它在意识层面尚未被实现。我所能做的，是指出那些相对不可见的领域，实际上是为了帮助你探索自身意识的不同面向……我很清楚，这本书提出的问题远多于它给出的答案，而这正是我的意图……”
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1

quotes.text: The imagination has always dealt with creativity, and as man began to settle upon a kind of consciousness that dealt with cause and effect, he no longer physically perceived the products of his imagination directly in the old manner. He realized in those earlier times that illness, for instance, was initially as much the result of the imagination as health was, for he experienced far more directly the brilliant character of his own imagination. The lines between imaginative and physical experience have blurred for you, and of course they have also become tempered by other beliefs and the experiences that those beliefs then engender.
quotes.translation: 想象力始终与创造力相关，当人类开始定居于一种处理因果关系的意识时，他不再以旧方式直接感知想象力的产物。在更早的时代，他意识到，例如疾病最初与健康一样，很大程度上是想象力的结果，因为他更直接地体验到自己想象力的鲜明特质。对你们而言，想象与身体体验之间的界限已变得模糊，当然，它们也因其他信念以及这些信念随后引发的体验而变得缓和。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: The experiences at these various levels may be interpreted symbolically. They may appear in the form of fantasy, fiction, or art work, without the conscious self realizing their origin. Now at any of these various stages of consciousness, other phenomena may also be perceived -- thought-forms for example, energy manifestations, projections from the personal subconscious, and projections from the collective unconscious. Any or all of these may take symbolic form, and may appear beneficial or threatening according to the attitude of the personality involved. They should be regarded as quite natural phenomena, often neutral in intent.
quotes.translation: 这些不同层面的经验可能以象征形式被解读。它们可能以幻想、虚构或艺术作品的形式呈现，而意识自我却不知其来源。在意识的任何这些不同阶段，也可能感知到其他现象——例如思想形态、能量显化、个人潜意识的投射，以及集体无意识的投射。其中任何一种或全部都可能采取象征形式，并根据相关人格的态度而显得有益或具有威胁性。它们应被视为相当自然的现象，通常意图中立。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: (A one-minute pause at 11:21.) Learned behavior therefore alters not only present and future but also past conduct. Your power as a rational consciousness focused in the present provides you with opportunities for creativity that you are but vaguely learning to understand. As you do learn, you will automatically begin to appreciate the multidimensional nature of not only your own species but of others as well. The moment as you think of it, then, is the creative framework through which you, the nonphysical self, constantly form corporeal reality; and through that window into earthly existence you form both its future and its past.
quotes.translation: （11点21分停顿一分钟）因此，习得行为不仅改变现在和未来，也改变过去的行为。你作为聚焦于当下的理性意识，其力量为你提供了你仅模糊开始理解的创造力机会。随着你逐渐学习，你将自动开始欣赏不仅是你自己物种，还有其他物种的多维本质。因此，你所认为的当下时刻，正是你——非物质的自己——不断形成物质实相的创造性框架；而通过那扇通往尘世存在的窗口，你同时形成其未来与过去。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality

quotes.text: Theoretically, a thoroughly educated space traveler in your time, landing upon a strange planet, would be able to adjust his own consciousness so that he could perceive the planet in various "sequences" of time. If you land upon a planet in a spaceship and find volcanoes, you would, perhaps, realize that other portions of that planet might show different faces. You have confidence in your ability to move through space, so you might then explore the terrain that you could not see from your original landing point. If you did not understand the change in qualities of space, you might imagine that the whole planet was a giant volcano.
quotes.translation: 理论上，你们时代一位受过充分教育的太空旅行者，若降落在一颗陌生行星上，将能够调整自己的意识，以便在时间的各种"序列"中感知该行星。如果你乘坐宇宙飞船降落在一颗行星上并发现火山，你或许会意识到该行星的其他部分可能呈现不同面貌。你对自己在空间中移动的能力有信心，因此你可能会探索从最初降落点无法看到的地形。如果你不了解空间性质的变化，你可能会想象整颗行星是一座巨大的火山。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: (All emphatically and joyously:) In your terms, the world is intensely different from one moment to another, with each smallest portion of consciousness choosing its reality from a field of infinite probabilities.3 Immense calculations, far beyond your conscious decisions as you think of them, are possible only because of the unutterable freedom that resides within minute worlds inside your skull -- patterns of interrelationships, counterparts so cunningly woven that each is unique, freewheeling, and involved in an infinite cooperative venture so powerful that the atoms stay in certain forms, and the same stars shine in the sky.
quotes.translation: （全部强调且欢快地：）用你们的话来说，世界每一刻都截然不同，每个最微小的意识部分都从无限可能性的场域中选择自己的实相。³ 巨大的计算——远超你们所认为的意识决定——之所以可能，只是因为存在于你们头骨内微观世界中的难以言喻的自由——相互关系的模式，如此巧妙编织的对等物，以至于每个都是独特的、自由运转的，并参与到一个如此强大的无限合作事业中，使得原子保持特定形态，同样的星辰在天空中闪耀。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: Reality changes its form in order to become known to itself. It constructs varieties of consciousness with varying kinds of sense apparatus, or perceiving apparatus. Each consciousness then perceives reality in a different fashion. Yet all fashions you see are legitimate, as far as physical sense apparatus is concerned. But the mental or psychic energy behind all this is the one basic reality.
quotes.translation: 实相改变其形态以认识自身。它构建了各种意识形态，配备不同种类的感官装置或感知装置。每种意识便以不同方式感知实相。就物质感官装置而言，你所见的一切方式都是合理的。但这一切背后的心灵或灵性能量才是唯一的基本实相。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 6 of The Seth Material (Sessions 240-280)

quotes.text: Remember, the normal consciousness is not synonymous with the ego. The ego is only a portion of consciousness. For Ruburt's benefit yes, indeed, the cure is permanent, as long as he utilizes what he has learned, and it seems fairly certain that he will.
quotes.translation: 请记住，正常意识并不等同于自我。自我只是意识的一部分。对鲁柏而言，是的，确实如此，只要他运用所学到的，疗愈就是永久的，而且看来他相当确定会这么做。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)

quotes.text: This book is Seth's way of demonstrating that human personality is multidimensional, that we exist in many realities at once, that the soul or inner self is not something apart from us, but the very medium in which we exist. He emphasizes that "truth" is not found by going from teacher to teacher, church to church, or discipline to discipline, but by looking within the self. The intimate knowledge of consciousness, the "secrets of the universe," are not esoteric truths to be hidden from the people, then. Such information is as natural to man as air, and as available to those who honestly seek it by looking to the source within.
quotes.translation: 这本书是赛斯展示人类人格具有多重维度的方式，证明我们同时存在于许多实相中，灵魂或内在自我并非与我们分离之物，而是我们存在的媒介本身。他强调"真理"并非通过从一个老师到另一个老师、从一个教会到另一个教会、或从一个法门到另一个法门而找到，而是通过向内观照自我。因此，对意识的深层认知、"宇宙的奥秘"并非需要向人们隐藏的秘传真理。这类信息对人类而言如同空气般自然，并且对那些诚实地向内寻求源头的人而言唾手可得。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: (10:16.) Now: In almost all instances, demons in dreams represent the dreamer's belief in evil, instantly materialized. They are not the inhabitants of some nether world, then, or underground. We will be giving some instructions that will enable readers to experiment with the projection of consciousness at least to some extent. It is very important for you to realize that even in dreams you form your own reality. Your state of mind, freed from its usual physical focus, creatively expresses itself in all of its power and brilliance. The state of mind itself serves as an intent, propelling you into realities of like conditions.
quotes.translation: （10:16。）现在：在几乎所有情况下，梦中的恶魔代表做梦者对邪恶的信念，并立即具象化。它们并非某个地下世界或地底的居民。我们将提供一些指导，使读者至少能在某种程度上体验意识的投射。认识到即使在梦中你也创造自己的实相，这一点非常重要。你的心智状态，从通常的物理焦点中解放出来，以其全部力量和光辉创造性地表达自身。心智状态本身作为一种意图，将你推向具有相似条件的实相。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: (10:18.) Nevertheless, encounters between you occur frequently--in the dream state as stated, in alterations of your usual focus, and in your arts, where you are less arbitrary in your definitions. As you began to bring your own physical reality into harder, clearer focus, you stopped with your own view of human consciousness, shutting off completely and rather arbitrarily those other elements in order to more clearly frame and define the boundaries of physical order. It seems to you now that such personalities (long pause) are not physically perceivable, but at one time you could bring them into the range of your perception.
quotes.translation: （10:18。）尽管如此，你们之间的相遇频繁发生——如前所述在梦境状态中，在你通常焦点的改变中，以及在你的艺术中，在那里你的定义不那么武断。当你开始将自己的物理实相带入更清晰、更明确的焦点时，你便止步于自己对人类意识的看法，相当武断地完全关闭了其他那些元素，以便更清晰地勾勒和界定物理秩序的边界。如今对你而言，这类人格（长停顿）似乎无法被物理感知，但曾几何时，你能将它们带入你的感知范围。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2

quotes.text: Then pretend you are having a dream that begins with the image of an orange. Follow the dream in your mind. Next, pretend that you are waking from the dream to realize that another dream was simultaneously occurring, and ask yourself quickly what that dream was. Followed in the same sequence given, the exercise will allow you to make loops with your own consciousness, so to speak, to catch it "coming and going." And the last question -- what else were you dreaming of? -- should bring an entirely new sequence of images and thoughts into your mind that were indeed happening at the same time as your daydream about the orange.
quotes.translation: 接着，假装你正在做一个以橙子意象开场的梦。在脑海中跟随这个梦境。然后，假装你正从梦中醒来，却意识到同时还有另一个梦正在发生，并迅速问自己那是什么梦。按照同样的顺序进行，这个练习将让你能够用意识绕圈——可以说，捕捉它“来去”的轨迹。最后一个问题——你当时还在梦到什么？——应该会为你带来一整套全新的意象与思绪，这些确实与你关于橙子的白日梦同时发生。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche

quotes.text: ("Your world is formed out of the vast unpredictability of consciousness. From it you form your own ideas of significance and of yourself ... You must stop thinking in terms of ordinary progression. It is bad enough when you worry about keeping up with the Joneses. It is something else, however, when you start worrying about which kind of self [or consciousness] is superior to another kind."
quotes.translation: （“你的世界是由意识那浩瀚的不可预测性所形成。从中你构建出自己对意义与自我的概念……你必须停止以寻常的进展方式来思考。当你担心赶不上邻居时，这已经够糟了。然而，当你开始担心哪一种自我[或意识]优于另一种时，那又是另一回事了。”）
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1

quotes.text: You must allow the normal waking consciousness to become transparent, so to speak, without however disturbing its flow. When this is accomplished you can also become aware of the projection conditions. This is almost like working backward, for directly beneath the thoughts and impressions of waking consciousness, you will glimpse dream images like those that appear just as you fall to sleep.
quotes.translation: 你必须让正常的清醒意识变得透明，可以说，同时又不干扰其流动。当这一点达成时，你也能意识到投射的条件。这几乎像是逆向运作，因为在清醒意识的思想和印象正下方，你会瞥见梦境影像，就像你刚入睡时出现的那种。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)

quotes.text: Instead you only perceive in this fashion. In alternate focus you can dispense with the root assumptions that usually guard, direct, and limit your perception. You are able to step aside from the moment as you know it, and return to it and find it there. Consciousness only pretends to bow to the idea of time. At other levels it enjoys playing with such concepts and perceiving great unity from events that occur outside of a time context -- mixing, for example, events from various centuries, finding harmony and points of contact by examining both historical and private environments, plucking them out of the time framework.
quotes.translation: 相反，你只是以这种方式感知。在交替焦点中，你可以摒弃通常守护、引导和限制你感知的根本假设。你能够从你所知的当下抽身，再回到它，并发现它依然在那里。意识只是假装向时间观念低头。在其他层面，它乐于玩弄这些概念，并从时间背景之外发生的事件中感知到伟大的统一——例如，混合来自不同世纪的事件，通过审视历史与私人环境，将它们从时间框架中抽离出来，找到和谐与接触点。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: When women give birth in a crowded world they also know, and with a portion of their conscious minds, that a violation is involved. When your species sees that it is destroying other species and disrupting the natural balance, then it is consciously aware of its violation. When such natural guilt is not faced there are other mechanisms that must be employed. Again at the risk of repeating myself: Many of your problems result from the fact that you do not accept the responsibility of your own consciousness. It is meant to assess the reality that is unconsciously formed in direct replica of your thoughts and expectations.
quotes.translation: 当女性在这个拥挤的世界里生育时，她们也以部分意识心智知晓其中涉及一种侵犯。当你们物种看到自己在毁灭其他物种、破坏自然平衡时，便会有意识地觉察到自身的侵犯。当这种自然的罪恶感不被面对时，就必须采用其他机制。再次冒着重复的风险：你们的许多问题源于你们不接受自身意识的责任。意识本应评估那直接复制你们思想与期待而无意识形成的实相。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality

quotes.text: "It seems to be an easy natural state for me to take; I go into it 'like a duck takes to water,' I guess, but it's difficult to explain. It's a state in which hardly any resistance is encountered; answers are 'just there.' The only problem is in getting the information across to another person in terms of his or her vocabulary. I enjoy this particular 'alteration of consciousness,' although I don't really recognize it as alien to my regular one; it's just different. It's an accelerated condition mixed with passivity, poised. If [our scientist's] attitude had been critical, I probably wouldn't have done as well, though."
quotes.translation: "这对我来说似乎是一种轻松自然的状态；我进入它'如鱼得水'，但很难解释。这是一种几乎没有任何阻力的状态；答案'就在那里'。唯一的问题是如何用对方能理解的词汇将信息传达给另一个人。我很享受这种特定的'意识改变'，尽管我并不真的认为它与我平常的意识格格不入；它只是不同而已。这是一种加速状态与被动性混合的状态，平衡而稳定。如果（我们那位科学家）的态度是批判性的，我可能就不会表现得这么好了。"
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: When we make contact his personality and consciousness in concentrated form makes its journey, in your terms like a speck in space, the consciousness reduced to its essence, and from this experience we then let him fall back into the physical system. (Pause.) Children's blocks then become massive by contrast. It was an experience in concepts, and therefore an advance on your parts. (Smile.)
quotes.translation: 当我们建立联系时，他的人格和意识以浓缩的形式进行旅程，用你们的话说就像空间中的一个微粒，意识被还原到其本质，从这种体验中我们让他落回物质系统。（停顿。）相比之下，儿童的障碍就变得巨大了。这是一次概念上的体验，因此是你们层面的进步。（微笑。）
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)

quotes.text: In the dream state the actors become aware to some extent of the parts they play, and sense the true personal identity that is behind the artist's craft. I have spoken of this before, but it is important to remember that you impose a certain kind of "artificial" sense of exaggerated continuity even to the self you know. Your experience changes constantly, and so does the intimate context of your life -- but you concentrate upon points of order, in your terms, that actually serve to scale down the context of your experience to make it more comprehensible. There are no such limits naturally set about your consciousness.
quotes.translation: 在梦境状态中，演员们开始在一定程度上意识到自己所扮演的角色，并感受到艺术家技艺背后真实的人格身份。我之前曾谈到过这一点，但重要的是要记住，即使对你所认知的自我，你也施加了一种特定"人为"的、夸张的连续性感知。你的体验不断变化，你生活的内在脉络也随之改变——但你会聚焦于秩序点，用你的话说，这些秩序点实际上会缩小你体验的脉络，使其更易于理解。你的意识并没有被自然设定这样的限制。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche

quotes.text: There is presently no science, religion, or psychology that comes close to even approaching a conceptual framework that could explain, or even indirectly describe, the dimensions of that kind of universe. (Pause.) Its properties are psychological, following the logic of the psyche, and all of the physical properties that you understand are reflections of those deeper issues. Again, each atom and molecule -- and any particle that you can imagine -- possesses, and would possess, a consciousness. Unless you accept that statement at least as a theory upon which to build, then much of my material would appear meaningless.
quotes.translation: 目前没有任何科学、宗教或心理学能接近甚至间接描述那种宇宙维度的概念框架。（停顿）它的属性是心理性的，遵循心灵的逻辑，你所理解的所有物理属性都是那些更深层议题的反映。再次强调，每个原子和分子——以及你能想象的任何粒子——都拥有且将拥有意识。除非你至少将这个陈述作为构建理论的基础，否则我的许多材料将显得毫无意义。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: In many instances you travel outside of three-dimensional reality while dreaming, but your experiences must then be recalled in physical terms or you would have no memory of them. Even your dreams, you see, must come through that point in the present -- of spirit's intersection with flesh. Dreaming does represent an open channel through which the material environment is transcended. There are as yet undiscovered, bizarre changes in the brain during certain dream states, an acceleration that quite literally propels the consciousness out of its usual space-time continuum into those other realities from which it comes.
quotes.translation: 在许多情况下，你在做梦时会离开三维现实，但你的经历必须用物理术语来回忆，否则你将无法记住它们。你看，就连你的梦境也必须通过当下那个点——即精神与肉体的交汇点——才能呈现。做梦确实代表着一个开放通道，通过它物质环境被超越。在某些梦境状态中，大脑会发生尚未被发现的奇异变化，这种加速会真正将意识推出其惯常的时空连续体，进入它本源的另类实相。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality

quotes.text: Anything of which you are aware in three-dimensional existence is only a projection of a greater reality into that dimension. The events of which you are conscious are only those fragments of activities that intrude or appear to your normal waking consciousness. Other portions of these events are quite clear to you both in the dreaming state and beneath waking consciousness during the day.
quotes.translation: 你在三维存在中所觉察的任何事物，只是更大实相投射到该维度中的影像。你所意识到的那些事件，不过是闯入或显现于你正常清醒意识的零碎活动。这些事件的其他部分，在梦境状态以及白天的清醒意识之下，对你而言都相当清晰。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: While the cells required future and past data, and used it to form from that invisible tension the body's present corporal reality, the same kind of information could be a threat then to the ego consciousness, which could be overwhelmed. Within the corporal structure, however, there are indeed messages that leap too quickly or too slowly2 from your viewpoint to allow for any physical response. In that way cellular comprehension is allowed its free flow; but the selectivity mentioned (in sessions 682-3) bypasses such information, so that it does not conflict with present sense data requiring physical action in time.
quotes.translation: 虽然细胞需要过去与未来的数据，并运用这些数据从那种无形的张力中构建出身体当下的物质实相，但这类信息对自我意识而言可能构成威胁，使其不堪重负。然而在物质结构内部，确实存在某些讯息——从你的视角来看，它们传递得太快或太慢，以至于无法引发任何生理反应。如此，细胞的理解得以自由流动；但（第682-3节中提到的）选择性过滤机制会绕过这类信息，使其不与需要及时物理行动的当下感官数据产生冲突。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1

quotes.text: When the process began, however, the deep power of nature had to be "controlled" so that the growing consciousness could see itself as apart from this natural source. Yet children, so necessary to the species, continued to spring from women's wombs. Therefore the natural source was most flagrant, observable, and undeniable. For that reason the species -- and not the male alone -- placed so many taboos about female behavior and sexuality. In "subduing" its own female elements, the species tried to gain some psychological distance from the great natural source from which it was, for its own reasons, trying to emerge.
quotes.translation: 然而当这个过程开始时，自然界的深层力量必须被"控制"，以便成长中的意识能将自己视为独立于这一自然源头。但物种延续所必需的孩童，仍持续从女性子宫中诞生。因此自然源头以最昭彰、可观察且无可否认的方式显现。正因如此，该物种——并非仅限男性——对女性行为与性征施加了诸多禁忌。在"驯服"自身阴性元素的过程中，该物种试图与那个它出于自身理由正试图脱离的伟大自然源头，保持某种心理距离。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche

quotes.text: To some extent this is natural. You are focused in a daily life for a reason. You have adopted it as a challenge. But within its framework you are also meant to grow and develop, and to extend the limits of your consciousness. It is very difficult to admit that you are in many ways more effective and creative in the sleep state than the waking state, and somewhat shattering to admit that the dream body can indeed fly, defying both time and space. It is much easier to pretend that all such experiences are symbolic and not literal, to evolve complicated psychological theories, for example, to explain flying dreams.
quotes.translation: 在某种程度上，这是自然的。你聚焦于日常生活是有原因的。你已将其视为一项挑战。但在其框架内，你也应成长和发展，并扩展你意识的界限。很难承认你在睡眠状态中比清醒状态更有效率、更有创造力，而承认梦体确实能飞翔，无视时空，则更令人震惊。假装所有这些经历都是象征性的而非字面意义的，并发展出复杂的心理学理论来解释飞翔的梦，要容易得多。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: If you are in a world not yours, with your consciousness drifting, you are in free gear, so to speak, your feelings and thoughts flowing into experience. You have to learn how to distinguish your psychological state from the reality in which you find yourself, if you want to maintain your alertness and explore that environment. Many of my readers find themselves in just such situations while they are sleeping. While still dreaming they seem to come suddenly awake in an environment that appears to make no sense. Demons may be chasing them. The world may seem topsy-turvy. The dead and the living may meet and speak.
quotes.translation: 如果你身处一个不属于你的世界，你的意识飘忽不定，那么你就处于自由档位，可以说，你的感受和思想流入体验中。如果你想保持警觉并探索那个环境，就必须学会如何区分你的心理状态和你所处的现实。我的许多读者在睡眠时发现自己正处于这样的情境中。在梦中，他们似乎突然在一个毫无意义的环境中醒来。恶魔可能正在追赶他们。世界可能天翻地覆。死者和生者可能相遇交谈。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: People were not nearly as isolated as it now appears, for in their dreams early men communicated their various locations, the symbols of their cultures and understanding, the nature of their arts. All of the inventions that you often think now happened quite by chance--the discovery of anything from the first tool to the importance of fire, or the coming of the Iron Age or whatever--all of that inventiveness was the result of the inspiration and communication of the dream world. Man dreamed his world and then created it, and the units of consciousness first dreamed man and all of the other species that you know.
quotes.translation: 早期人类并不像现在看起来那样彼此隔绝，因为在他们的梦境中，他们相互沟通各自的位置、文化与理解的象征符号，以及他们艺术的本质。你们现在常认为纯属偶然的所有发明——从第一件工具到火的重要性，再到铁器时代的来临或其他任何事物——所有这些创造力都是梦世界灵感与沟通的结果。人类先梦见自己的世界，然后创造了它，而意识单位则先梦见了人类以及你们所知的所有其他物种。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1

quotes.text: (A one-minute pause.) Each natural element had its own key system that interlocked with others, forming channels through which consciousness could flow from one kind of life to another. Man understood himself to be a separate entity, but one that was connected to all of nature. The emotional reaches of his subjective life, then, leapt far beyond what you think of as private experience. Each person participating fully in a storm, for example, still participated in his or her own individual way. Yet the grandeur of the emotions was allowed full sway, and the seasons of the earth and the world were jointly felt.
quotes.translation: （停顿一分钟。）每个自然元素都有其自身的关键系统，与其他系统相互连锁，形成通道，让意识得以从一种生命形态流向另一种。人类明白自己是独立的个体，但也是与整个自然相连的。因此，其主观生命的情感领域远远超越你们所谓的私人经验。例如，每个人在充分参与一场风暴时，仍以各自独特的方式参与其中。然而，情感的壮阔得以全然展现，大地与世界的季节被共同感知。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche

quotes.text: Now: Each reader is a portion of his or her own entity, and is developing toward the same kind of existence that I know. In childhood and in the dream state, each personality is aware to some extent of the true freedom that belongs to its own inner consciousness. These abilities of which I speak, therefore, are inherent characteristics of consciousness as a whole and of each personality.
quotes.translation: 现在：每位读者都是其自身存有的一部分，正朝着我所知晓的同一类存在发展。在童年与梦境状态中，每个个性都在某种程度上意识到属于其内在意识的真正自由。因此，我所说的这些能力，是意识整体以及每个个性固有的特性。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: The two-sex division was adopted, separating and balancing these most necessary but seemingly opposing tendencies. Only beginning consciousness needs these kind of controls. The anima and the animus, therefore, are embedded deeply with their necessary complementary but apparently opposed tendencies, and they are highly important in maintaining the very nature of your human consciousness.
quotes.translation: 两性划分被采用，以分离并平衡这些最必要却看似对立倾向。唯有初生的意识才需要这类控制。因此，阿尼玛与阿尼姆斯深植于其必要互补却明显对立的倾向中，对维持你们人类意识的本质至关重要。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: We will end the session but continue this material on Wednesday. My heartiest wishes to you both. Bear in mind also that as individuals, you are creating new dimensions through which consciousness can know itself, both in physical experience and within other systems. As a part of consciousness other systems are not closed to you, but as physical organisms other systems are closed to you.
quotes.translation: 我们将结束本次课程，但周三继续此材料。衷心祝福你们二位。同时请谨记，作为个体，你们正在创造新的维度，让意识得以在物质经验与其他系统中认识自身。作为意识的一部分，其他系统对你们并非封闭；但作为物质有机体，其他系统对你们是封闭的。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)

quotes.text: (9:47.) God, therefore, became male. The love and devotion that might otherwise be connected with the facets of nature and the female principle had to be "snatched away from" any natural attraction to sexuality. In such a way, religion, echoing your state of consciousness, was able to harness the powers of love and use them for purposes of domination. They became state-oriented. A man's love and devotion was a political gain. Fervor was as important as a government's treasury, for a state could count upon the devotion of its lieutenants in the same way that many fanatics will work without money for a cause.
quotes.translation: （9:47）于是，上帝变成了男性。本可能与自然面向及女性原则相连的爱与奉献，不得不被"从对性欲的自然吸引中夺走"。如此，宗教呼应着你们意识的状态，得以驾驭爱的力量，并将其用于统治目的。它们变得以国家为导向。男人的爱与奉献成了政治收益。狂热与国库同等重要，因为国家可以像许多狂热者无偿为事业效力那样，依赖其将领的奉献。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche

quotes.text: Your experiments with the pendulum are quite helpful to you, in that the subconscious is allowed, through its own action, to make itself more readily available. Your own conscious awareness is increased because you are then aware of inner actions with which the conscious mind had not been familiar. Here we have a coming together of actions, a joining and an immersion of one action within another: the action of the subconscious in answering questions put to it by the conscious mind, （use brain rather than mind）, and the acceptance, which is itself action on the part of consciousness, of the answers received.
quotes.translation: 你进行的钟摆实验对你颇有助益，因为潜意识得以通过自身行动，让自己更易于被触及。你自身的意识觉察也随之增强，因为你开始意识到那些意识心智原本不熟悉的内在行动。在此，我们看到了行动的交汇、融合与沉浸：潜意识回应意识心智（此处用大脑而非心智）所提问题的行动，以及意识本身作为行动对接收到的答案的接纳。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)

quotes.text: The simple fact is that when you dream you are flying, you often are. In the dream state you operate under the same conditions, more or less, that are native to a consciousness not focused in physical reality. Many of your experiences, therefore, are precisely those you may meet after death. You may speak with dead friends or relatives, revisit the past, greet old classmates, walk down streets that existed fifty years earlier in physical time, travel through space without taking any physical time to do so, be met by guides, be instructed, teach others, perform meaningful work, solve problems, hallucinate.
quotes.translation: 一个简单的事实是：当你梦见自己在飞翔时，你往往确实在飞翔。在梦境状态中，你或多或少是在与不聚焦于物质实相的意识所固有的相同条件下运作。因此，你的许多体验恰恰是你在死后可能遭遇的。你或许能与已故的朋友或亲人交谈，重访过去，问候老同学，漫步于物理时间中五十年前就已存在的街道，不耗费任何物理时间便穿越太空，被向导迎接，接受指导，教导他人，从事有意义的工作，解决问题，或产生幻觉。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: Your ego gains assurance from what seems to be the memory of its immediate past. A man who loses memory of past events feels insecure and lost, but other types of personality gestalts operate far differently. Instead of a time sequence that governs or seems to govern thought, mental activity of any kind, and overt action, you have associative processes, offshoots, and possibilities. To some extent you can gain an idea of this through an examination of your own stream of consciousness. But the comparison is highly superficial, for here too continuity reigns, even though associations are given greater play.
quotes.translation: 你的自我从看似对直接过去的记忆中获取安全感。一个失去过往事件记忆的人会感到不安与迷失，但其他类型的人格完形运作方式则大相径庭。取代支配或看似支配思想、任何心智活动及外在行为的时间序列的，是联想过程、分支与可能性。在某种程度上，你可以通过检视自己的意识流来获得对此的理解。但这种比较极为肤浅，因为即便联想得到更充分的发挥，连续性在此依然占据主导。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)

quotes.text: The "you" who is capable of such expansion must be a far more creative and multidimensional personality than you earlier imagined. Many of the suggested small exercises given earlier in the book will also help you become acquainted with your own reality, will give you direct experience with the nature of your own soul or entity, and will put you in contact with those portions of your being from which your own vitality springs. You may or may not have your own encounters with past reincarnational selves or probable selves. You may or may not catch yourselves in the act of changing levels of consciousness.
quotes.translation: 那个能够进行如此扩展的“你”，必然是你先前所想象不到的、更具创造力与多维度的自己。本书前文建议的许多小型练习，也将帮助你认识自身的实相，让你直接体验自己灵魂或存有的本质，并让你接触到自己生命力所源出的那些部分。你或许会、或许不会与过去的转世自我或可能自我相遇；你或许会、或许不会在意识层次转换的当下捕捉到自己的身影。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: ("Your consciousness attracts the consciousness that is already connected with the material. That is one of my goodies for the evening! Information, then, becomes new and is reborn as it is interpreted through a new consciousness, as Seagull was.
quotes.translation: （“你的意识会吸引已经与物质相连的意识。这是我今晚的‘小礼物’之一！于是，信息变得崭新，并在通过新意识被诠释时获得重生——就像《海鸥》那样。”
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality

quotes.text: "Cherish the gifts of the gods. Don't be so anxious to throw your individuality back into their faces, saying, 'I'm sick to death of myself and of my individuality; it burdens me.' Even one squirrel's consciousness, suddenly thrown into the body of another of its kind, would feel a sense of loss, encounter a strangeness, and know in the sacredness of its being that something was wrong. Wear your individuality proudly. It is the badge of your godhood. You are a god living a life -- being, desiring, creating. Through honoring yourself, you honor whatever it is God is, and become a conscious co-creator."
quotes.translation: "珍视诸神的馈赠。不要急于将你的个体性掷回他们面前，说：'我厌倦透了自己和我的个体性；它令我负重。'即便是一只松鼠的意识，若突然被投入同类另一具躯体，也会感到失落，遭遇陌生感，并在其存在的神圣性中知晓某些事物出了差错。骄傲地穿戴你的个体性。那是你神性的徽章。你是一位正在经历生命的神——存在、渴望、创造。通过荣耀自己，你荣耀了神之本质，并成为有意识的共同创造者。"
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: In your terms, however, you dream whether you are living or dead. When you are alive, corporally speaking, what you think of as dreaming becomes subordinate to what you refer to as your conscious waking life.
