You always examine your dreams then from an "alien" standpoint, one prejudiced in favor of the ordinary waking state. However, the dreaming condition is consequently experienced in distorted form. Often it does not seem clear. By contrast to waking consciousness it can appear hazy, not precise, or off-focus. This does not always apply, because in some dreams the state of alertness is undeniable.
quotes.translation: 然而，就你们而言，无论你们是活着还是死了，你们都在做梦。当你们活着时，从肉体角度来说，你们所谓的做梦会从属于你们所谓的清醒意识生活。因此，你们总是从一个“异己”的角度来检视你们的梦，这个角度偏向于平常的清醒状态。结果，做梦的状态便以扭曲的形式被体验。它常常显得不清晰。与清醒意识相比，它可能显得模糊、不精确或失焦。但这并非总是如此，因为在某些梦中，警觉状态是无可否认的。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1

quotes.text: There will be all kinds of different spatial relationships for you to explore, and then to attempt to reproduce in your work if you prefer. The disciplines involved in these alterations of consciousness will help you in all other areas, will acquaint you quite personally with other realities, and be a preparation for the time when you will not have a physical body in which to manipulate. There is personal evidence to be obtained here that can be obtained in no other way. Ruburt has talked of some renewed work, and Heartily suggest that you concentrate strongly at doing joint out-of-body explorations.
quotes.translation: 将会有各种不同的空间关系供你们探索，如果你们愿意，还可以尝试在你们的工作中重现它们。这些意识转变所涉及的训练将在所有其他领域帮助你们，让你们亲身熟悉其他实相，并为你们不再拥有可操控的肉体之时做好准备。这里可以获得个人证据，而这些证据无法通过其他方式获得。鲁柏曾谈到一些新的工作，我衷心建议你们强烈专注于共同进行出体探索。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)

quotes.text: I hesitate in many instances to say what I might, because it is so easy to misinterpret meanings; but when you ask what is the purpose of consciousness you take it for granted there must be one purpose -- where the greater truth and creativity must be that consciousness itself cannot be aware of all of its own purposes, but ever discovers its own nature through its own manifestations.
quotes.translation: 在许多情况下，我犹豫着是否该说出我所知道的，因为含义太容易被曲解了；但当你问起意识的目的时，你理所当然地认为它必然有一个目的——而更大的真相与创造力在于，意识本身无法知晓其所有目的，而是通过自身的显化不断发现自己的本质。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche

quotes.text: The powers of consciousness are clearly not understood then, nor its multidimensional aspects. As I have told you, the self is literally unlimited. Each individual has his part to play in projecting these EE units into physical actuality. Therefore physical matter can be legitimately described as an extension of the self, as much as the physical body is a projection of the inner self.
quotes.translation: 显然，意识的力量及其多维面向尚未被理解。正如我告诉过你的，自我本质上是无限的。每个人在将这些EE单位投射为物质实相时都扮演着自己的角色。因此，物质可以合理地描述为自我的延伸，正如肉体是内在自我的投射。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)

quotes.text: ("There were three men whose lives became confused in history and merged, and whose composite history became known as the life of Christ.... Each was highly gifted psychically, knew of his role, and accepted it willingly. The three men were a part of one entity, gaining physical existence in one time. They were not born on the same date, however. There are reasons why the entity did not return as one person. For one thing, the full consciousness of an entity would be too strong for one physical vehicle. For another, the entity wanted a more diversified environment than could otherwise be provided.
quotes.translation: （"曾有三位男子，他们的生命在历史中混淆并融合，其复合历史被称为基督的一生……三人均具有高度灵性天赋，知晓自身角色并欣然接受。这三人属于同一个存有，在同一时代获得物质存在，但并非同日出生。该存有不以单一身份返回是有原因的：其一，存有的完整意识对单一肉身载体而言过于强烈；其二，存有希望获得比单一身份所能提供的更多元的环境。"）
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: It is somewhat humorous that such a vital consciousness could even suppose itself to be the end product of inert elements that were themselves lifeless, but somehow managed to combine in such a way that your species attained fantasy, logic, vast organizational power, technologies, and civilizations. Your myths tell you that nature itself has no intent except survival. It cares little for the individual -- only insofar as the individual helps the species to endure. In its workings, nature then appears to be impersonal, even though it so consists of individuals that it cannot be regarded otherwise.
quotes.translation: 有点讽刺的是，如此重要的意识竟会认为自己是惰性元素的最终产物——这些元素本身毫无生命，却不知何故以某种方式组合，使得你们的物种获得了幻想、逻辑、庞大的组织能力、技术和文明。你们的神话告诉你们，自然本身除了生存之外别无意图。它几乎不关心个体——只有当个体有助于物种延续时才会在意。因此，在其运作中，自然显得非人格化，尽管它由如此多的个体组成，以至于无法以其他方式看待。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: In many philosophies this sort of idea is retained -- the soul being returned to a primal giver, or being dissolved in a nebulous state somewhere between being and nonbeing. The soul is, however, first of all creative. It can be discussed from many viewpoints. Its characteristics can be given to some degree, and indeed most of my readers could find out these characteristics for themselves if they were highly enough motivated, and if this was their main concern. The soul or entity is itself the most highly motivated, most highly energized, and most potent consciousness-unit known in any universe.
quotes.translation: 在许多哲学中，这种想法被保留——灵魂被送回最初的给予者，或溶解在介于存在与非存在之间的模糊状态中。然而，灵魂首先是创造性的。它可以从许多角度讨论。它的特性可以在一定程度上被赋予，事实上，如果我的读者有足够强烈的动机，并且这是他们的主要关注点，他们中的大多数可以自己发现这些特性。灵魂或存有本身是任何宇宙中已知的最具动机、最充满能量、最强大的意识单位。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: (And from the 40th session:) The tissue capsule is actually an energy field boundary ... At the same time it protects the whole self from certain radiations which do not here concern you. No living consciousness exists on any plane without this tissue capsule enclosing it ... To some inhabitants of other planes [realities] that have access to your plane, all that can be seen of you is this capsule, since such inhabitants have had no experience in your particular type of camouflage [physical] construction. Therefore your camouflage patterns are invisible to them, but the tissue capsules are not.1
quotes.translation: （摘自第40节：）组织囊实际上是一个能量场边界……同时它保护整个自我免受某些辐射的影响，这些辐射在此与你无关。没有活着的意识存在于任何层面而不被这个组织囊所包围……对于能够进入你层面的其他层面[实相]的一些居民来说，他们所能看到的你只是这个囊，因为这些居民在你特定的伪装[物质]构造中没有经验。因此，你的伪装模式对他们来说是不可见的，但组织囊却是可见的。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1

quotes.text: Using your conscious mind as a threshold, however, you can discover still more. Figuratively speaking, stand where you are. Think of that moment of conscious awareness as a path. Imagine many other such paths, all converging; again, imaginatively take one of them in your mind and follow it. Accept what you experience uncritically. To some small extent you are "altering" your consciousness. (Half humorously:) Of course, you are not "altering" it at all. You are simply using it in a different fashion, and focusing it -- however briefly -- in another direction. This is the simplest of exercises.
quotes.translation: 然而，以你的意识心为门槛，你还能发现更多。打个比方，请站在原地。将那个有意识的觉知时刻视为一条路径。想象许多其他这样的路径在此汇聚；再次发挥想象，在脑海中选取其中一条并跟随它。不加评判地接纳你所体验的一切。在某种程度上，你正在"改变"你的意识。（半开玩笑地：）当然，你根本没有在"改变"它。你只是以不同的方式运用它，并将它聚焦——哪怕只是短暂地——朝向另一个方向。这是最简单的练习。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1

quotes.text: Much information does not even reach the brain (the mind is aware of such data, however). In man, the psychic-physical structure has at every moment a complete up-to-date picture of pertinent information about all events that will in any way affect the organism. All actions are taken with this information available. In the dream state such data becomes transformed, again, into pseudophysical pictures -- reflections of events that might occur, previews of probable sequences. These are flashed before a consciousness that momentarily focuses upon the inner rather than the outer arena of reality.
quotes.translation: 许多信息甚至不会抵达大脑（然而心灵知晓这些数据）。在人类身上，心灵-生理结构每时每刻都拥有关于所有可能影响有机体事件的相关信息的完整即时图景。所有行动都在掌握这些信息的前提下进行。在梦境状态中，这类数据再次转化为拟似物理画面——可能发生事件的映照，对可能序列的预视。这些画面在意识暂时聚焦于内在而非外在实相舞台时闪现。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche

quotes.text: Your cellular structure is innately able to follow such sequences. Believing such clues to be meaningless, the conscious mind does not perceive them, or calls them coincidences. Such clues in your intimate daily life, however, looked at in a different way, can tell you much about the potentials of the species, and give you glimpses of other systems of reality in which human consciousness can respond. I am here using an incident from the experience of Ruburt and Joseph, but the reader can make his or her own correlations, and discover like events from which the same conclusions can be drawn.
quotes.translation: 你的细胞结构天生就能跟随这样的序列。由于相信这些线索毫无意义，有意识的心智便不去感知它们，或称之为巧合。然而，在你亲密的日常生活中，若以不同的方式看待这些线索，它们能告诉你关于物种潜能的许多信息，并让你瞥见人类意识能够回应的其他实相系统。我在此引用鲁柏和约瑟经历中的一个事件，但读者可以自行找出关联，并发现能得出同样结论的类似事件。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1

quotes.text: In the same way, if you are overly concerned about the nature of your own reality, and if you are looking to others to justify your existence, you will not be able to abandon your own world view successfully, for you will feel too threatened. Or, traveling in psychic exercises even slightly away from your own home station, you will still try to take your familiar paraphernalia with you, and interpret even entirely new situations of consciousness in the light of your own world view. You will transpose your own set of assumptions, then, into conditions in which they may not really fit at all.
quotes.translation: 同理，若你过度执着于自身实相的本质，并依赖他人来证明你的存在价值，你将无法顺利放下自己的世界观——因为你会感到过于受威胁。即便在心灵练习中稍稍偏离自身本位，你仍会试图携带熟悉的装备，并用自己的世界观来诠释全然陌生的意识情境。于是你会将自己的预设条件转译到那些可能完全不适用的情境中。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: In the main physical existence means a translation of inner mood and psychological climate into physical terms, and no consciousness is constantly in a state of bliss. There is always effort involved, for this is the meaning of consciousness.
quotes.translation: 主要而言，物质存在意味着将内在情绪与心理氛围转译为物质语言，没有任何意识能恒常处于极乐状态。其中永远需要付出努力，因为这就是意识的本质。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 6 of The Seth Material (Sessions 240-280)

quotes.text: The type of telepathy which is most commonly thought of as telepathy is of course not the elemental type of which I spoke earlier, but a different variety. This particular, more advanced telepathy could not and did not occur in its most complicated form until the development within your plane of psychically developed personalities. This development involved separations and concentrations of energy into self-conscious identities. This development was an advancement, yet this advancement led also to a separation and objectifying of consciousness, into dual segments of subject and object.
quotes.translation: 通常被视作心灵感应的那种类型，当然不是我先前提到的基本类型，而是另一种变体。这种特定、更高级的心灵感应，在你们层面发展出具有灵性成熟人格之前，无法也不可能以最复杂的形式出现。这种发展涉及将能量分离并凝聚成具有自我意识的本体。这发展是一种进步，然而这种进步也导致了意识的分离与客体化，分裂成主体与客体的二元片段。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)

quotes.text: All of them cast certain "atmospheric conditions" or reflections that color physical events as you know them. Some of your own feelings are propelled into a reality within such systems, adopting within that framework their own mass and form. In the creation and maintenance of your normal reality, you focus your daily waking consciousness so that it becomes effective within the ranges necessary. Ideas and feelings that you want made physical carry within them the mechanisms that will put them in the proper range, well within the electromagnetic field necessary for physical development.
quotes.translation: 它们全都投射出某种"氛围条件"或反射，为你们所知的物质事件染上色彩。你们自身的某些感受会被推进此类系统中的实相，在该架构内获得自身的质量与形态。在创造与维持你们正常实相的过程中，你们聚焦日常清醒意识，使其在必要范围内产生效力。你们想要物质化的想法与感受，其内在携带着将之置于适当范围的机制，完全处于物质发展所需的电磁场之内。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: Much of the remainder of "Unknown" Reality, then, will deal with an inside look at the nature of reality, and with some exercises that will allow you to see yourself and your world from another perspective. Later I intend to say far more about some civilizations that, in your terms, came before your own (but see the last sentence in Note 4). Before you can understand their orientation, we will have to speak about various alternate kinds of consciousness and out-of-body experience. These will help you to understand how other kinds of cultures could operate in ways so alien to your own.
quotes.translation: 因此，《未知的实相》剩余的大部分内容，将探讨实相本质的内在观察，并提供一些练习，让诸位得以从另一视角审视自身与你们的世界。稍后我打算更详细地谈论某些文明——按你们的说法，它们存在于你们文明之前（但请参阅注4最后一句）。在诸位能理解其取向之前，我们必须先讨论各种交替类型的意识与出体经验。这些将帮助你们理解其他类型的文化何以能以如此迥异于你们的方式运作。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: (10:50.) If consciousness vacated the body for the same amount of time from a normally physically awake state, it would consider itself dead, for it could not rationalize the gap of dimension and experience. Therefore in the sleep state, each of you have undergone -- to some degree -- the same kind of absence of consciousness from physical reality that you experience during death.
quotes.translation: （十点五十分。）如果意识在正常的肉体清醒状态下，以相同的时间长度离开身体，它会认为自己已经死亡，因为它无法合理化这个维度与经验的断层。因此，在睡眠状态中，你们每个人都经历过——在某种程度上——与死亡时相同的、意识从物质实相中缺席的状态。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: These ideas and concepts obviously came from consciousness. However, they represent incipient latent developments that may or may not occur in physical reality. They may or may not be perceived by any given individual. The characteristic interest and abilities of the personality involved will have much to do with his recognition of the realities within this layer of consciousness.
quotes.translation: 这些观念与概念显然来自意识。然而，它们代表的是初期的潜在发展，可能在物质实相中发生，也可能不会发生。它们可能被任何特定个体感知到，也可能不会。人格所涉及的特有兴趣与能力，将与他对此意识层面中实相的认知有很大关系。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: Action is therefore a part of all structure. Here again is an apparent dilemma, an exquisite imbalance whose result is consciousness and existence. For consciousness and existence do not exist because of delicate balances, so much as they are made possible by lacks of balances, so richly creative there would be no reality as it is understood to be, if balance were ever maintained.
quotes.translation: 因此，行动是所有结构的一部分。这里又出现了一个明显的两难困境，一种精妙的不平衡，其结果是意识与存在。因为意识与存在并非因精妙的平衡而存在，反而更因不平衡而成为可能——这种不平衡如此富于创造力，以至于如果平衡得以维持，就不会有我们所理解的实相存在。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)

quotes.text: "You do not perceive the consciousness within the self. You do not perceive the consciousness within a star, either--yet the star is the physical materialization in your reality of another kind of consciousness, and all you perceive of it."
quotes.translation: 你并未感知到自我之内的意识。你也未感知到星辰之内的意识——然而星辰是你实相中另一种意识的物质化显现，你所见的不过是它的一部分。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2

quotes.text: This phenomena is not meant to explain legitimate instances in which the personality and consciousness itself returns for purposes of communication. This image trace is a lingering manifestation, an imprint within your system, a part of its reality, and is held within it as you might for example retain an idea in your mind long after the idea has been expressed physically. As a painting that is destroyed physically may still be retained in your mind, so in the reality of the physical system a trace remains, an image trace of the camouflage structure that enclosed a given personality.
quotes.translation: 这种现象并非用于解释人格与意识本身为沟通目的而回归的合法案例。此影像痕迹是一种持续存在的显化，是你系统内的印记，属于其现实的一部分，如同你可能在某个想法被物质表达后仍长久保留在脑海中。正如一幅被物理摧毁的画作仍可存留于你心中，在物质系统的现实中，曾包裹特定人格的伪装结构也会留下痕迹——一种影像痕迹。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)

quotes.text: You have never seen what design is until you see a spider's web when you are out of the body. You have never seen what design is until you see a spider's web from the spider's viewpoint. Now you can learn to do this. You can use your psychic abilities, to throw your consciousness into the spider. Your body is a very handy mechanism and I do not suggest that you step out of it too long. However, as the earth's atmosphere hides the conditions in the universe from your scientists, so also do the conditions of the body blind you to true color and design. Now you can develop in this way.
quotes.translation: 除非你脱离身体时看见蜘蛛网，否则你从未真正见过什么是设计。除非你从蜘蛛的视角看见蜘蛛网，否则你从未真正见过什么是设计。现在你可以学会这样做。你可以运用你的心灵能力，将意识投射进蜘蛛体内。你的身体是个非常便利的机制，但我并不建议你长时间脱离它。然而，正如地球大气层向科学家隐藏了宇宙的真实状况，身体的条件也蒙蔽了你对真实色彩与设计的感知。现在你可以朝这个方向发展。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)

quotes.text: A mountain exists. It is composed of rocks and trees, grass and hills, and in your terms of time you can look at it, see it as such, give it a name, and ignore its equally independent parts. Without those parts the mountain would not exist. It is not invaded by the trees or rocks that compose it, and while trees grow and die the mountain itself, at least in your terms of time, exists despite the changes. It is also dependent upon the changes. In a manner of speaking, your own identities as you think of them are dependent upon the same kinds of living organizations of consciousness.
quotes.translation: 山是存在的。它由岩石、树木、草地与丘陵构成，以你们的时间观念来看，你们可以注视它、视其为山、赋予其名，却忽略其同样独立的组成部分。没有这些部分，山便不复存在。它并非被构成它的树木或岩石所侵占，尽管树木生长凋零，而山本身——至少以你们的时间观念来看——历经变迁依然存在。它同样依赖于这些变化。从某种角度说，你们所认知的自我身份，也依赖于同样类型的意识活组织。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: Now I am giving all of this material here because it will help you understand and use your present abilities. You are asking too much of normal waking consciousness, smoothing out the valleys and peaks of its activity, demanding in some cases that it go full blast ahead when it is actually at a minimal period, denying yourself the great mobility of consciousness that is possible.
quotes.translation: 现在我在此提供所有这些资料，是因为它将帮助你们理解并运用当前的能力。你们对正常清醒意识要求过多，抹平其活动的低谷与高峰，在某些情况下要求它在实际处于最低周期时全速运转，从而否定了自身可能拥有的意识极大流动性。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: (12:19.) In the sleep state after our last session, then, he allowed his consciousness to expand enough so that it became aware of information and experience usually censored automatically through mental and neurological habit. In Adventures Ruburt uses the term "prejudiced perception" -- an excellent one -- that is applicable here. For you have prejudiced yourself spiritually, mentally, and physically in those terms. In the sleep state Ruburt became unprejudiced, at least to some degree, so that he encountered information that seemed alien or out of context with usual experience.
quotes.translation: （12:19分）在我们上次课程后的睡眠状态中，他允许自己的意识充分扩展，以致能觉察到通常因心智与神经习惯而被自动过滤的信息与经验。在《冒险》一书中，鲁柏使用了"偏见性感知"这个术语——极佳的用词——在此处恰如其分。因为你们在灵性、心智与身体层面都已形成这种偏见。在睡眠状态中，鲁柏变得至少在一定程度上无偏见，因此他遭遇了看似陌生或与日常经验脱节的信息。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1

quotes.text: Your alterations of consciousness frequently occur in the dream state, therefore, where it seems to you at least that your experiences do not have any practical application. You imagine that only hallucinations are involved. Many of your best snapshots of other realities are taken in your dreams.5 They may be over-or-underdeveloped, and the focus may be blurred, but your dreams present you with far more information about the unknown reality than you suppose. In the most intimate of terms your body is your home station, so when you leave it you often hide this fact from yourselves.
quotes.translation: 你们对意识的改变经常发生在梦境状态中，因此至少对你们而言，这些经历似乎没有任何实际应用。你们以为那只是幻觉。你们对其他实相的最佳快照，有许多是在梦中拍摄的。它们可能过度曝光或曝光不足，焦点也可能模糊不清，但你们的梦提供给你们关于未知实相的信息，远比你们所认为的要多得多。用最亲密的说法，你们的身体是你们的大本营，所以当你们离开它时，常常对自己隐瞒这个事实。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: The word "unconscious" is in a fashion meaningless. There are endless versions [of consciousness], of course, with their own worlds, forming organizations of meaning and purpose. Some of these mingle with your own and vice versa. The "inner structure" is one of consciousness, and the deeper questions can eventually only be approached by granting the existence of inner references.
quotes.translation: “无意识”这个词在某种意义上是无意义的。当然，有无数种意识版本，各有其世界，形成意义与目的的组织。其中一些与你们的意识交织，反之亦然。“内在结构”是意识的结构，而更深层的问题最终只能通过承认内在参考的存在来接近。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2

quotes.text: The traveler must leave his own camouflage paraphernalia completely behind however, or he will go nowhere. It is possible, theoretically, to travel to any system in this manner, and bypass others you see. Such a traveler would not age physically. His physical body would be in a suspended state. The traveling consciousness would lose all physical conception of time however. A very few individuals have traveled in this manner to any extensive degree. Most of the knowledge gained escapes the physical organism however, for the experiences could not be translated by the physical brain.
quotes.translation: 然而，旅行者必须完全放下自己的伪装装备，否则他将寸步难行。理论上，以这种方式可以旅行到任何系统，并绕过你们所见的其他系统。这样的旅行者不会生理上老化。他的肉体将处于一种悬浮状态。然而，旅行的意识会失去所有对时间的具体概念。极少数人曾以这种方式进行过广泛的旅行。然而，所获得的大部分知识都逃脱了肉体组织，因为肉体大脑无法翻译这些经验。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)

quotes.text: However, nowhere in tonight's 682nd session does Seth refer to EE units by name -- and for a reason, as will be seen late in the next session. In his earlier material he left himself plenty of room to add to his data on such units of consciousness. "They are one form [my emphasis] that emotional energy takes," he told us in the 504th session. And in the 581st session: "There are many ranges and great varieties of such EE units, all existing beyond your perceivable reach. To lump them together in such a way, however, is misleading, for within all of this there is great order."
quotes.translation: 然而在今晚第682节课程中，赛斯始终未提及"EE单位"之名——这自有其因，下节课程将见分晓。在早期资料中，他为自己预留了充分空间来补充这类意识单位的数据。"它们是情绪能量呈现的形态之一（着重号为我所加），"他在第504节课程中告诉我们。而在第581节课程中："此类EE单位存在诸多层级与变体，皆超乎你们可感知的范围。但如此笼统归类实属误导，因其中蕴含着精密的秩序。"
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1

quotes.text: The inner references of reality involve a different kind of experience entirely, with organizational patterns that mix and merge at every conceivable point. You tune your consciousness while you sleep as one might tune a piano, so that in waking reality, it clearly perceives the proper notes and values that build up into physical experience. Those inner fields of reference in which you have your existence are completely changing themselves as your experience is added to them, and your own (long pause) identity was couched in those references before birth as you understand it.
quotes.translation: 实相的内在参照涉及完全不同的经验类型，其组织模式在每一个可想象的点上混合交融。你在睡眠中调整意识，如同调校钢琴，使它在清醒实相中清晰感知那些构成物质经验的恰当音符与价值。你赖以存在的那些内在参照场域，正随着你经验的加入而彻底改变自身；而你的（长停顿）身份在你所理解的出生之前，就已蕴藏于那些参照之中。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2

quotes.text: Action accepts all stimuli in an affirmative manner. It is only when action becomes compartmented, so to speak, in the development of highly differentiated consciousness, that such refinement occurs. I am not here saying that unpleasant stimuli will not be felt as unpleasant, and reacted against, by less self-conscious organisms. I am saying that less self-conscious organisms will rejoice even in their automatic reaction against such stimuli, because any stimuli and reaction represents sensation, and sensation is another method by which such action knows and expresses itself.
quotes.translation: 行动以肯定方式接纳所有刺激。只有当行动在高度分化意识的发展中被“区隔化”时，才会产生这种精炼。我并非说较无自我意识的生物不会感到不愉快刺激，或不会对此反抗。我是说，较无自我意识的生物即使在对这类刺激的自动反应中也会欢欣，因为任何刺激与反应都代表感觉，而感觉正是此类行动认识并表达自身的另一种方式。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)

quotes.text: Science delegates the world of nature as the realm of exterior natural events. Its view of nature is therefore mechanistic. The natural self, however, like the rest of nature, possesses a rich dimension of inside psychological depth, that science, because of its own definitions, cannot perceive. Telepathy and clairvoyance, for example, are a part of natural effects, but they belong to a nature so much more expansive than science's definitions that they have been made to appear as highly unnatural eccentricities of behavior, rather than as natural components of consciousness.
quotes.translation: 科学将自然世界视为外在自然事件的领域，因此它对自然的看法是机械论的。然而，自然自体，如同自然的其余部分，拥有丰富的内在心理深度维度，而科学因其自身的定义无法察觉这一点。例如，心灵感应和千里眼是自然效应的一部分，但它们属于一个远比科学定义更广阔的自然，以至于它们被呈现为高度非自然的异常行为，而非意识的自然组成部分。
quotes.book: The Magical Approach

quotes.text: Even within that context, however, there are surprises and enchantments waiting, if you simply learn to expand your awareness, exploring not only the dream state, but your waking reality in more adventuresome ways. Your dreaming psyche is awake. Many of you have allowed your normal waking consciousness to become blurred -- inactive, relatively speaking, so that you are only half aware of the life that you have. You are your psyche's living expression, its human manifestation. (Pause.) Yet you allow yourselves often to become blind to brilliant aspects of your own existence.
quotes.translation: 然而，即使在这个背景下，如果你只是学会扩展你的觉知，不仅探索梦境状态，还以更冒险的方式探索你的清醒实相，那么惊喜和迷醉正等待着你。你做梦的心灵是清醒的。你们许多人允许自己正常的清醒意识变得模糊——相对而言不活跃，以至于你只对自己所过的生活有一半的觉知。你是你心灵的活生生的表达，是其人类显现。（停顿。）然而，你们常常让自己对自己存在的辉煌面向视而不见。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche

quotes.text: Your conscious mind is meant to give your body an assessment of what I will call cultural conditions, for there are sophistications and specifications that in your terms consciousness alone can assess. If, under conditions naturally safe in the terms of primary experience, you become overwhelmed by unsafe signals from secondary experience -- that is, from your reading or whatever -- you show a lack of discrimination. You are not able to differentiate between the physically safe present situation, and the imagined, which is perhaps unsafe, calling forth the alarms of danger.
quotes.translation: 你的意识心智本应让你对身体做出我称之为文化条件的评估，因为只有意识才能评估那些复杂而具体的状况。如果在原始经验中自然安全的情况下，你被来自次级经验（即阅读或其他途径）的不安全信号所淹没，就表明你缺乏辨别力。你无法区分当下身体安全的情境与可能不安全的想象情境，从而引发危险的警报。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche

quotes.text: "Nothing in the universe is ever lost, or mislaid, or wasted, so the energy of your own thoughts, while they are still your own thoughts, helps to form the natural attributes of physical realities that you do not perceive. So is your own world formed by units of consciousness. Its natural elements are the glistening remnants of other units of consciousness that you do not see."
quotes.translation: "宇宙中没有任何事物会遗失、错放或浪费，因此你自身思想的能量，当它们仍属于你时，有助于形成你未曾感知的物质实相的自然属性。你的世界正是由意识单位所构成。其自然元素是你看不见的其他意识单位闪烁的残余。"
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1

quotes.text: You will remember that we said that atoms and molecules contained consciousness, a generalized consciousness first of all, in which data is suspended in condensed mental genetic code, and also a self-consciousness to some limited degree.
quotes.translation: 你们会记得我们说过，原子和分子都包含意识，首先是一种概括性的意识，其中数据悬浮于浓缩的心灵遗传密码中，同时也包含某种有限程度的自我意识。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)

quotes.text: When you leave this dimension, then you concentrate upon the knowledge that you have gained. If you still do not realize that you create the reality that you know, then you return and again you learn to manipulate and again and again you see the results of your own inner reality as you meet it objectified. You teach yourself the lesson until you have learned it. And when you have learned it, then you have begun to learn how to handle the consciousness that is yours intelligently and well. And then you can form images for the benefit of others, and lead them and guide them.
quotes.translation: 当你离开这个维度时，你会专注于你所获得的知识。如果你仍未意识到你创造了自己所知的实相，那么你就会返回，再次学习操控，并一次又一次地看到自己内在实相的结果，以客观化的形式呈现。你教导自己这个课题，直到你学会它为止。而当你学会它时，你才开始学会如何明智而妥善地运用属于你的意识。然后，你就能为他人形成影像，引领并指导他们。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)

quotes.text: (Slower now): All of this material now given must be considered along with the fact that beneath these developments there are the eternal aspects and creative characteristics of a force that is both undeniable and intimate. All That Is, in other words, represents the reality from which all of us spring. (Pause, one of several.) All That Is, by its nature, transcends all dimensions of activity, consciousness, or reality, while being a part of each.
quotes.translation: （现在语速放缓）：所有这些资料必须与一个事实一同考量——在这些发展之下，存在着一种既不可否认又亲密无间的力量，它拥有永恒的特质与创造性的特征。换言之，"一切万有"代表着我们所有人从中涌现的实相。（停顿数次）"一切万有"就其本质而言，超越所有活动、意识或实相的维度，同时又是每个维度的一部分。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: Give us a moment ... The next family (Tumold), in the order given, is primarily devoted to healing. This does not mean that these people may not be creative, or organizers, or teachers, but the primary slant of their consciousness will be directed to healing. You might find them as doctors and nurses, while not usually as hospital administrators. However, they may be psychics, social workers, psychologists, artists, or in the religions. They may work in flower shops. They may work on assembly lines, for that matter, but if so they will be healers by intent or temperament.
quotes.translation: 请稍等片刻……按既定顺序，下一个家族（图莫尔德）主要致力于疗愈。这并不意味着这些人可能不具创造力、组织力或教学能力，但他们的意识主要倾向将指向疗愈。你可能会发现他们担任医生和护士，但通常不会成为医院行政人员。然而，他们可能是通灵者、社会工作者、心理学家、艺术家，或从事宗教工作。他们可能在花店工作。就这点而言，他们也可能在流水线上工作，但若是如此，他们仍会以意图或性情成为疗愈者。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: I do not speak through Ruburt as one might through a telephone. Instead there is a psychological extension, a projection of characteristics on both of our parts, and this I use for our communications. Later I will explain how this psychological framework is created and maintained, for it is like a road that must be kept clear of debris. You would be much better off in reading this book if you asked yourself who you are, rather than asked who I am, for you cannot understand what I am unless you understand the nature of personality and the characteristics of consciousness.
quotes.translation: 我并非像通过电话那样透过鲁柏说话。相反，存在一种心理延伸，一种我们双方特质投射的产物，我利用这个来进行我们的沟通。稍后我会解释这个心理架构如何被创造与维持，因为它就像一条必须保持畅通无阻的道路。如果你在阅读本书时问自己"你是谁"，而不是问"我是谁"，你会受益更多，因为除非你理解人格的本质与意识的特性，否则你无法理解我是什么。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: It has some energy of its own, but needs additional energy from a perceiver for any interrelationship to take place. If such a materialization appears threatening, then simply wish it peace and withdraw your attention from it. It draws its main activating energy from your focusing, and according to the intensity and nature of your focus. You must not take the root assumptions of physical existence with you as you journey through these levels of consciousness. Divest yourself of as many of them as possible, for they can cause you to misinterpret your experiences. (Pause.)
quotes.translation: 它本身拥有一些能量，但需要来自感知者的额外能量才能产生任何互动关系。如果这种物质化显现具有威胁性，只需祝福它安宁并撤回你的注意力。它主要从你的聚焦中汲取激活能量，其强度与性质取决于你聚焦的强度与性质。当你穿越这些意识层次时，切勿将物质存在的根本假设随身携带。尽可能摆脱这些假设，因为它们会导致你误解自己的体验。（停顿）
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: ("For those of you who do accompany me, I promise you an adventure, a creative alteration of consciousness, and experiences beyond those that you have known in your terms. You look at the world around you and are amazed at its richness and variety. Do you think that the inner world is not as rich, even more rich, more valid? Do you think there is but one kind of consciousness?
quotes.translation: （“对于愿意与我同行的诸位，我承诺一场冒险，一次创造性的意识转化，以及超越你们认知范畴的体验。你们环顾周遭世界，为其丰饶与多样而惊叹。难道你们认为内在世界不如外在世界丰饶，甚至更为丰饶、更为真实吗？难道你们认为意识只有一种吗？”）
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1

quotes.text: The psychedelic experience is a most fruitful one. Drugs are not a prerequisite however. In an experience without drugs, the ego loses its substantial quality momentarily. It remains however as a protection. In all our work we have been allowing the nonphysical self more and more freedom. We have been and we are expanding consciousness, and this consciousness includes the ego.
quotes.translation: 迷幻体验是一种极为丰硕的体验。然而，药物并非必要条件。在无药物的体验中，自我暂时失去了其实质性。但它仍作为一种保护而存在。在我们所有的工作中，我们一直在给予非我物质自我越来越多的自由。我们一直在扩展意识，且正在扩展意识，而这种意识包含了自我。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)

quotes.text: (There are four recognized [electrical] brain waves, and in speed they range upward from 0 to 26 and more Hertz units, or cycles per second. These rhythms can vary somewhat, and are best thought of as areas of activity. Brain waves overlap. Very simply, delta brain waves are connected with dreamless sleep, theta with creativity and dreams, alpha with a relaxed alertness and changing consciousness; beta -- the fastest -- with concentration, and with an intense focus upon all of the challenges [and anxieties and stresses, many would say] faced in the ordinary daily world.
quotes.translation: （有四种公认的[电]脑波，按速度从0到26赫兹或每秒周期数递增。这些节律可能略有变化，最好将其视为活动区域。脑波是重叠的。简单来说，德尔塔脑波与无梦睡眠相关，西塔脑波与创造力和梦境相关，阿尔法脑波与放松的警觉和变化的意识相关；贝塔脑波——最快的——与专注有关，并与对日常世界中面临的所有挑战[以及焦虑和压力，许多人会这么说]的强烈聚焦有关。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: Give us a moment ... Such gadgets can be useful only if they show you that such alterations are naturally possible. Otherwise, with your ideas of applied science and technology, the gadgets will be the pivoting point, and the ideas of manipulation will be stressed. In other words, unless the ideas behind objective science are altered, then gadget-produced altered states will almost certainly be used to manipulate, rather than free, consciousness.
quotes.translation: 请稍等……这些小工具只有在它们向你展示这种改变是自然可能的情况下才有用。否则，以你对应用科学和技术的观念，这些小工具将成为支点，操控的观念将被强调。换句话说，除非客观科学背后的观念被改变，否则由小工具产生的改变状态几乎肯定会被用来操控意识，而非解放意识。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1

quotes.text: A certain portion of Adam was: the version was... You had a body consciousness of a protective nature, but this was only one portion of an entire personality who has yet to appear. Now there is a reason for the bridge framework. The bridge framework takes exercise, understanding, and knowledge on both of your parts, therefore there would be no communication of a strong valid nature until your personality is able to maintain it, for the bridge will not be strong enough to hold. Otherwise there would be too many implications and personality difficulties that could arise.
quotes.translation: 亚当的某个部分是：那个版本是……你拥有一个具有保护性质的身体意识，但这只是整个尚未显现的人格的一部分。现在，这个桥梁架构自有其道理。桥梁架构需要你们双方都付出努力、理解与知识，因此在你们的人格能够维持它之前，不会有任何强烈而有效的沟通，因为桥梁还不够坚固。否则，可能会引发过多的暗示和人格上的困难。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)

quotes.text: (10:30.) You do the same with each of the other choices, all from the framework of that state of consciousness. The methods in each case are the same. You make the decision. You then become aware in whatever way you choose of the physical effects within your body. You enter the body as you did in the way I gave earlier for healing. With great sensitivity you are able to see what physical effect the decision will have -- whether the state of the body remains the same, whether there is a great sense of health within it, or the incipient beginning of great difficulties.
quotes.translation: （10:30。）你们对每一个其他选择都做同样的事，全部从那个意识状态的框架出发。每种情况下的方法都是相同的。你们做出决定。然后，你们以自己选择的任何方式，意识到身体内的物理效应。你们像我在前面关于疗愈的讲解中所说的那样进入身体。凭借高度的敏感性，你们能够看到这个决定将产生什么物理效应——身体状态是否保持不变，体内是否有强烈的健康感，还是重大困难的初期开端。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: Any such communications coming through the inner senses will exist in your psychological time. I have said also that this psychological time operates during sleep and during quiet times of consciousness. Now in dreams and in the dream framework you have the feeling of experiencing many hours, or even days. These days or hours that you seem to experience in dreams are not recorded by the physical body, and are outside of your physical time camouflage. If in a dream for example you experience within the framework two days, physically you do not age for these two days.
quotes.translation: 任何通过内在感官而来的此类沟通，都将存在于你们的心理时间中。我也说过，这种心理时间在睡眠期间和意识的宁静时刻运作。现在，在梦境和梦的架构中，你们会有经历许多小时甚至许多天的感觉。这些你们在梦中似乎经历的日或时，并不被肉体所记录，并且是在你们物理时间伪装之外。例如，如果在梦中你们在架构内经历了两天，肉体上你们并不会为这两天而衰老。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)

quotes.text: We will study these effects in a most detailed fashion at a later time because of their importance. This may sound unbelievable to you; nevertheless the same effects that cause emotional outbursts also cause physical storms. The basis is the same. The manifestation is different. The particles of air being themselves composed of molecular structures having, as all molecules do, a generalized sub-consciousness, and in condensed form a comprehension of the inner laws of the universe, are also then psychic as well as physical structures, as you should by now understand.
quotes.translation: 我们稍后将极其详尽地研究这些效应，因为它们的重要性。这对你们来说可能听起来难以置信；然而，引起情绪爆发的相同效应也引起物理风暴。基础是相同的。显化是不同的。空气粒子本身由分子结构组成，如同所有分子一样，具有一种广义的潜意识，并以浓缩形式理解宇宙的内在法则，那么它们也是心灵结构以及物理结构，正如你们现在应该了解的那样。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)

quotes.text: The complicated organism which is the human personality with its physical structure, has evolved, along with many other structures, a highly differentiated "I" consciousness, whose very nature is such that it attempts to preserve the apparent boundaries of identity. To do so it chooses between actions, for the very choice, or act of choosing, and ability to do so, represents the nature of identity. But beneath this sophisticated gestalt are the simpler foundations of its being, and indeed the very acceptance of all stimuli without which identity would be impossible.
quotes.translation: 构成人类人格及其生理结构的复杂有机体，与其他众多结构一同演化，发展出高度分化的“我”意识，其本质如此，以至于它试图维系身份认同的明显边界。为此，它在行动之间做出选择，因为选择本身、选择的行为以及选择的能力，正代表了身份认同的本质。但在这精密的完形之下，存在着其存在更简单的基础，事实上，正是对所有刺激的全然接纳——若无此接纳，身份认同便不可能存在。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)

quotes.text: It made no difference to your history. The three men were part of one entity, gaining physical existence in one time. They were not born at the same date however. There are reasons why the entity did not return as one person. For one thing the full consciousness of an entity would be too strong for one physical vehicle. For another the entity wanted a more diversified environment than could otherwise be provided. The entity was born once as John the Baptist, and then he was born in two other forms. One of these is a personality that most stories of Christ refer to.
quotes.translation: 这对你们的历史毫无影响。那三人属于同一个存有，在同一时间获得物质存在，但并非同日出生。该存有不以单一人物回归自有其因：其一，存有的完整意识对单一物质载具而言过于强烈；其二，存有渴望比单一形式所能提供的更多元化的环境。该存有曾以施洗约翰的身份降生，随后又以另外两种形态降生——其中一种人格，正是大多数基督故事所指涉的对象。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)

quotes.text: In the beginning CU's, then, units of consciousness, existing within a divine psychological gestalt, endowed with the unimaginable creativity of that sublime identity, began themselves to create, to explore, and to fulfill those innate values by which they were characterized. Operating both as waves and particles, directed in part by their own creative restlessness, and directed in part by the unquenchable creativity of All That Is, they embarked upon the project that brought time and space and your entire [universe] into being. They were the first entities, then.
quotes.translation: 起初，意识单元（CU）存在于神圣心理完形之内，被那崇高身份难以想象的创造力所赋予，它们自身开始创造、探索，并实现那些构成其本质的内在价值。它们既以波的形式运作，也以粒子的形式运作，部分受自身创造性的不安所引导，部分受一切万有无可遏制的创造力所引导，由此开启了那项工程——正是这工程使时间、空间及你们整个[宇宙]得以诞生。它们，便是最初的存有。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1

quotes.text: When you paint the land, do so thinking in terms having to do with its past as well as present and future. Its inner development that has resulted in its present form, the energy behind the form. Remember in your painting the relationship of one object to another, not in terms of space necessarily, the interrelationship of the vitality that forms the objects; the vibrating always changing reality within, say, the skin of the apple or the orange, the quite living consciousness within the molecules that make up what seems to be the solid surface of the fruits' skin.
quotes.translation: 当你描绘大地时，请以涵盖其过去、现在与未来的角度来思考。其内在发展导致了它现在的形态，以及形态背后的能量。在绘画中记住物体之间的关联，不必然以空间的角度，而是构成物体的生命力之间的相互关系；苹果或橘子表皮内那不断振动的、永远变化的实相，以及构成看似固体果皮表面的分子中那活生生的意识。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)

quotes.text: I also mentioned that chemicals alone, in whatever form and with whatever mixtures, will not give you consciousness. The fact is that this statement that I have just given you is true and false, in that chemicals alone will give you consciousness, simply because we know now that every molecule exists on your plane is there as the physical materialization of conscious energy.
quotes.translation: 我也曾提到，单凭化学物质，无论以何种形式或何种混合，都无法赋予你意识。事实上，我刚才给你的这个陈述既正确又错误，因为单凭化学物质确实能赋予你意识，这仅仅是因为我们现在知道，存在于你们层面的每一个分子，都是意识能量的物质化具体呈现。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)

quotes.text: You do experience time, but not time as it is bound by your camouflage patterns. As I have mentioned you can in a dream or daydream or through conscious use of psychological time experience many hours in a few clock minutes. This experience comes very close to the third inner sense. If you will remember again our imaginary experience through the inner senses as we looked down at the street, you will remember that I spoke of sensing not only the present essence of the living consciousness within a certain scope, but I also mentioned sensing their past and futures.
quotes.translation: 你确实体验时间，但并非受限于你们伪装模式的时间。如我曾提及，你可以在梦境、白日梦或通过有意识地运用心理时间，在几分钟的时钟时间内体验数小时。这种体验非常接近第三内在感官。如果你还记得我们通过内在感官俯视街道的想象体验，你会记得我曾提到不仅感知到某个范围内活生生意识的当下本质，也提到感知到它们的过去与未来。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)

quotes.text: Give us a moment ... You are a part of nature that has learned to make choices, a part of nature that naturally and automatically produces dreams and beliefs about which you then organize your reality. There are many effects which you do not like, but you possess a unique kind of consciousness, in which each individual has a hand in the overall formation of a world reality, and you are participating at a level of existence in which you are learning how to transform the imaginative realm of probabilities into a more or less specific, physically experienced world.
quotes.translation: 请稍等片刻……你是自然中学会做出选择的那部分，是自然中自然而然产生梦境与信念的那部分，随后你根据这些信念组织你的实相。有许多你不喜欢的效果，但你拥有一种独特的意识——每个个体都参与着世界实相的整体形成，而你正存在于一个学习如何将想象领域的可能性转化为相对具体、可被物理体验的世界的存在层面。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: Now when he does so disconnect his own stream of consciousness in this manner, any correct associations on his part will automatically show themselves in the data through attraction. They will become incorporated with my data in a constructive fashion. This is fairly important, and we shall follow it up by examples when possible. For I will perceive certain types of information that are basic, and when Ruburts abilities, his own abilities as apart from mine, are fully developed, then he will be able to add certain specifics that have direct personal application.
quotes.translation: 当他以这种方式切断自己的意识流时，任何他自身的正确联想都会通过吸引力自动显现在资料中。这些联想将以建设性的方式与我的资料融合。这一点相当重要，我们将在可能时通过实例进一步说明。因为我会感知某些基础类型的信息，而当鲁柏的能力——他自身与我相分离的能力——完全发展后，他将能添加某些具有直接个人应用性的具体细节。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 5 of The Seth Material (Sessions 199-239)

quotes.text: There is one God, but within that God are many. There is one self, but within that self are many. There is one body, in one time, but the self has other bodies in other times. All "times" exist at once. (Long pause.) Historically speaking, mankind chose a certain line of development. In it his consciousness specialized, focusing upon sharp particulars of experience. But inherent always, psychologically and biologically, there has been the possibility of a change in that pattern, an alteration that would effectively lift the race into another kind of weather.
quotes.translation: 有一位神，但在这位神之内有许多。有一个自己，但在这个自己之内有许多。有一个身体，存在于一个时间，但自己在其他时间拥有其他身体。所有"时间"同时存在。（长停顿。）从历史角度来看，人类选择了某条特定的发展路线。在这条路线中，他的意识变得专门化，聚焦于经验的尖锐细节。但无论在心理上还是生物上，始终存在着改变这种模式的可能性——一种能有效将种族提升到另一种气候中的转变。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1

quotes.text: The physical universe serves then as a threshold for probabilities, and all possible species find their greatest fulfillment within that system, each of them neurologically tuned into their own reality and their own "time." So the body itself, as it presently exists, is innately equipped with other neurological responses that to you would seem to be biologically invisible. Nevertheless, your consciousness and your beliefs are what direct this neurological recognition. At birth, and before structured learning processes begin, you are far freer in that regard.
quotes.translation: 物质宇宙因此充当了可能性的门槛，所有可能的物种都在这个系统中找到最大的圆满，每个物种的神经系统都调谐到它们自己的实相和它们自己的"时间"。因此，身体本身——以它目前存在的状态——天生配备着其他神经反应，这些反应对你们而言在生物学上似乎是不可见的。然而，你们的意识和信念正是引导这种神经认知的方向。在出生时，在结构化的学习过程开始之前，你们在这方面要自由得多。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1

quotes.text: If the stages of waking consciousness were examined as sleep stages are presently being examined, for example, you would find a much greater range of activity than is suspected. Certain transition stages are completely ignored. In many ways it can be said that consciousness does indeed flicker, and varies in intensities. It is not like a steady beam of light, for instance.
quotes.translation: 如果清醒意识的状态能像目前研究睡眠状态那样被检视，你们会发现其活动范围远比想象中广阔。某些过渡阶段完全被忽略了。从许多方面来说，意识确实在闪烁不定，强度也各有不同。它并非像一道稳定的光束。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: All consciousness exists first and primarily in electromagnetic terms, although consciousness may project itself into many various forms. It is never completely imprisoned within those forms however. You see, I have somewhat explained this to you. （Smile.） As physical beings your perception, your physical perception, is slowed down to a necessary but very extensive degree.
quotes.translation: 所有意识最初且主要存在于电磁场中，尽管意识可能投射自身进入许多不同形态。但它从未完全被这些形态所禁锢。你看，我曾向你们解释过这一点。（微笑。）作为物质存在，你们的感知——你们的物质感知——被减缓到必要但非常广泛的程度。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 6 of The Seth Material (Sessions 240-280)

quotes.text: So the psychic families, or the families of consciousness, can be thought of as natives of inner countries of the mind, sharing heritages, purposes, and intents that may have little to do with the physical countries in which you live your surface lives. People are born in any month of the year in every country. All those in Norway are not born in January or August. In the same way, all the members of any given psychic family are spread across the earth, following inner patterns that may or may not relate to other issues as they are currently understood.
quotes.translation: 因此，心灵家族或意识家族可以被视为内在心灵国度的原住民，共享着可能与你们表面生活所居的物理国度无关的传承、目的和意图。人们出生在各国一年中的任何月份。并非所有挪威人都出生在一月或八月。同样，任何特定心灵家族的所有成员都分布在地球各处，遵循着可能与当前理解的其他议题相关或不相关的内在模式。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: Now any physical event is something like the impact of a rocket ship entering your world from "somewhere else." Thoughts often seem to swim in and out of your system of consciousness, and you barely notice. Events often appear and disappear in the same manner, yet they have impressed your reality. You have attracted them to one extent or another, and they have been attracted to you. Momentarily a field of relatedness is set up that is highly charged, one that provides an inner path by which probable events can flow into your area of recognized events.
quotes.translation: 任何物理事件都如同火箭从"别处"撞击进入你的世界。思想常看似游入游出你的意识系统，而你几乎未曾察觉。事件也以同样方式出现与消失，却已在你实相中留下印记。你在某种程度上吸引了它们，它们也被你所吸引。刹那间，一个高度带电的关联场被建立起来，提供一条内在路径，让可能事件得以流入你认知事件的领域。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche

quotes.text: The very nature of the ego and of the personality is formed by the ability to choose between actions or stimuli; but life as it is not connected to a highly differentiated ego, rejoices in all stimuli, as sensation, whether it is pleasurable or painful, for these distinctions do not exist in your terms. In the beginning of our sessions I spoke in a general manner, for example, saying that trees and plant life had a consciousness, but not a developed ego system. The tree, therefore, is conscious of the pain connected with, say, the severing of a limb.
quotes.translation: 自我与人格的本质，正是通过在不同行动或刺激之间做出选择的能力所塑造；但生命若未与高度分化的自我相连，便会欣然接纳所有刺激——无论是愉悦还是痛苦，因为在你看来，这些区别并不存在。在课程初期，我曾泛泛而谈，例如说树木与植物拥有意识，但缺乏发达的自我体系。因此，树木能感知到与断枝相关的痛苦。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)

quotes.text: Your actions take place with such seeming smoothness that you do not realize the order involved. A volcanic eruption in one corner of the world will ultimately affect the entire earth in varying degrees. An emotional eruption will do the same thing on another level, altering the local area primarily but also sending out its ripples into the mass psychological environment. The psyche's picture of reality, then, would be equally incomprehensible to the conscious mind because of the intense focus upon singularity that your usual consciousness requires.
quotes.translation: 你的行动看似如此流畅，以至于你未能察觉其中蕴含的秩序。世界某处的火山喷发，终将不同程度地影响整个地球。情感上的爆发在另一层面亦会产生同样效应：主要改变局部区域，同时将其涟漪扩散至集体心理环境。因此，心灵对现实的图景，对意识心而言同样难以理解——因为你惯常的意识需要极度聚焦于单一性。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche

quotes.text: "This point is extremely important. As you know, animals dream. What you do not know is that all consciousnesses dream. We have said that to some degree even atoms and molecules have consciousness, and each one of those minute consciousnesses forms its own dreams, even as on the other hand each one forms its own physical image. Now, as in the physical field individual atoms combine for their own benefit into more complicated structure gestalts, so do they also combine to form such gestalts, though of a somewhat different nature, in the dream world.
quotes.translation: 这一点极为重要。如你所知，动物会做梦。你所不知道的是，所有意识都会做梦。我们曾说过，甚至原子和分子在某种程度上也拥有意识，而每一个微小的意识都会形成自己的梦境，正如另一方面每一个意识都会形成自己的物质形象。现在，如同在物质领域中，个体原子为了自身利益结合成更复杂的结构完形，它们在梦境世界中也会结合成这样的完形，尽管性质略有不同。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1

quotes.text: The potentials of the true self are so multidimensional that they cannot be expressed in one space or time. Any person who loves another recognizes the infinite potential within that other person. That potential needs infinite opportunity; the true self's reality needs an ever-new, changing situation, for each experience enriches it and, therefore, enhances its own possibilities. En masse, in your terms, the same is true of the race of man. Mama and Papa, in our analogy, represent the infinite potential within one basic unit (CU) of consciousness.
quotes.translation: 真正自我的潜能是如此多维，以至于无法在一个空间或时间中完全展现。任何爱他人的人都会认识到那个人内在的无限潜能。这种潜能需要无限的机会；真正自我的实相需要不断更新、变化的情境，因为每一次体验都会丰富它，从而增强其自身的可能性。用你们的话来说，人类整体也是如此。在我们的类比中，妈妈和爸爸代表了意识基本单位（CU）内的无限潜能。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1

quotes.text: The self that you are aware of represents only one "position" in which those invisible particles happen to intersect, gain mass, build up form. Scientists can only perceive an electron as it is to them. They cannot really track it. They cannot be certain of its position and its speed at the same time, and to some extent the same applies to your consciousness. The speed of your own thoughts takes those thoughts away from you even as you think them--and you can never really examine a thought, but only the thought of a thought (with quiet amusement).
quotes.translation: 你所觉察到的自我，只代表那些无形粒子恰好交汇、获得质量、构建形态的一个"位置"。科学家只能感知到电子在他们面前呈现的样子，无法真正追踪它。他们无法同时确定电子的位置和速度，某种程度上你的意识也是如此。你思想的速度使那些思想在你思考时便离你而去——你永远无法真正检视一个思想，只能检视思想的影子（带着安静的莞尔）。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2

quotes.text: If in your waking hours you playfully make up a dream for yourself, and then playfully interpret it without worrying about implications, but for itself only, you will unwittingly touch upon the nature of your own nightly dreaming. Your regular dreams and your "manufactured" ones will have much in common, and the process of manufacturing dreams will acquaint you with the alterations of consciousness that to a greater degree happen nightly. This is an excellent exercise. It is particularly beneficial for those who have a too-rigid mental framework.
quotes.translation: 若你在清醒时刻为自己戏谑地编造一个梦，再戏谑地解读它——不为探究深意，只为梦境本身——你便会在无意中触及自己夜间梦境的本质。你惯常的梦境与"人造"的梦境将有许多共通之处，而造梦的过程会让你熟悉意识的变化——这种变化在夜间更为显著地发生。这是极佳的练习，尤其适合那些思维框架过于僵化的人。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche

quotes.text: When the inverted time system is understood clearly, then the inadequacy of the cause and effect theory will be clear. If your psychologists and scientists would begin a study of their own dreams, they would learn much through personal experience. They would tackle realities that cannot be measured with physical instruments. Since psychologists deal with psychological activity, it is all the more amazing that they have not concentrated upon psychological activity, observing it first hand as it exists in their own various stages of consciousness.
quotes.translation: 当颠倒的时间系统被清楚理解时，因果理论的不足便会昭然若揭。如果你们的心理学家和科学家开始研究自己的梦境，他们就能通过亲身经历学到很多。他们将触及那些无法用物理仪器测量的实相。既然心理学家研究的是心理活动，那么他们竟没有专注于心理活动，没有亲身观察存在于自身不同意识阶段中的心理活动，这就更加令人惊讶了。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 5 of The Seth Material (Sessions 199-239)

quotes.text: (10:27.) You do not have the same ego now that you had five years ago, but you are not aware of the change. Ego rises out of what you are, in other words. It is a part of the action of your being and consciousness, but as the eye cannot see its own shifting colors and expressions, as it is not aware that it lives and dies constantly as its atomic structure changes, so you are not aware that the ego continually changes, dies, and is reborn.
quotes.translation: （10点27分）你现在拥有的自我与五年前并不相同，但你并未察觉这种变化。换言之，自我是从你的本质中升起的。它是你存在与意识行动的一部分，但正如眼睛无法看见自身变幻的色彩与表情，正如它意识不到自己的原子结构不断变化、持续生死，你也同样意识不到自我在持续变化、死亡与重生。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality

quotes.text: (10:07.) Your own waking consciousness deals specifically with certain kinds of distinctions. These help form the very structure of physical existence. They highlight your lives, providing them also with a kind of frame. Quite simply, you want to experience a certain kind of reality, so you put boundaries about events, that allow you to concentrate upon them. When an artist paints a picture, he uses discrimination. He or she chooses one area of concentration. Everything within the painting fits; so in your physical lives, you do the same thing.
quotes.translation: （10点07分）你自身的清醒意识专门处理特定类型的区分。这些区分帮助构建物质存在的结构本身。它们凸显你们的生活，同时也为其提供某种框架。简单来说，你们想要体验某种特定的实相，于是为事件设置界限，使你们能够专注其上。当艺术家作画时，他会运用辨别力。他或她选择某个专注领域。画作中的一切相互契合；因此在你们的物质生活中，你们也做着同样的事。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche

quotes.text: If that language I speak of had been verbal, man never would have said: "The water flows through the valley." Instead, the sentence would have read something like this: "Running over the rocks, my water self flows together with others in slippery union." That translation is not the best, either. Man did not designate his own as the only kind of consciousness by any means. He graciously thanked the tree that gave him shade, for example, and he understood that the tree retained its own identity even when it allowed his awareness to join with it.
quotes.translation: 若我所说的那种语言是言语性的，人类绝不会说："水流过山谷。" 相反，这句话可能会写成这样："我的水自我在岩石上奔流，与其他水自我在滑润的结合中一同流动。" 这个翻译也并非最佳。人类绝没有将自己的意识视为唯一的一种。例如，他会感激地感谢那棵为他遮荫的树，他明白即使树允许他的意识与之融合，树仍保有其自身的身份。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche

quotes.text: The purpose of the Speakers is to help you correlate and understand this multidimensional existence, and to bring as much as possible of it to your conscious attention. Only by learning to feel, or sense, or intuitively perceive the depths of your own experience can you glimpse the nature of All That Is. By becoming more aware of your consciousness as it operates in physical life, you can learn to watch it as it manipulates through these other less familiar areas. Probable realities are only probable to you because you are not aware of them.
quotes.translation: 传述者的目的是帮助你们关联并理解这多维度的存在，并尽可能将其带入你们的意识觉知中。只有通过学习感受、感知或直觉地体验你们自身经验的深度，你们才能一窥一切万有的本质。通过更清楚地觉察你们的意识在物质生活中的运作，你们可以学会观察它如何操纵穿越这些其他较不熟悉的领域。可能的实相对你们而言只是可能，因为你们尚未意识到它们。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: (Slowly:) In a world in which individuals were confined in space in a tribe or clan (a one-minute pause), action was immediate. The environment presented a framework in which consciousness learned to deal with stimuli in a direct fashion. It learned how to focus. The necessary specialization meant that only so much data could be handled at once, emotionally or otherwise. The formation of different tribes allowed man to behave cooperatively, in small numbers. This meant that those on the outside were selectively ignored, considered strangers.
quotes.translation: （缓慢地：）在一个个体被局限于部落或氏族空间的世界里（停顿一分钟），行动是即时的。环境提供了一个框架，意识在其中学会以直接的方式处理刺激。它学会了如何聚焦。必要的专门化意味着每次只能处理有限的数据，无论是情感上还是其他方面。不同部落的形成使人类能够以少量人数合作。这意味着外部的人被有选择地忽略，被视为陌生人。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1

quotes.text: The individuals continue to exist, and express themselves according to their development in other fields not connected with yours, when they are finished on your plane. No particular identity is ever lost. If so the whole process would be meaningless. And in other ways to lesser degrees, through abstract thought, through art of any kind, the physical human being, having been formed by consciousness, in his own way then working through and with matter, constructs other fields or planes of attraction, which according to their abilities expand.
quotes.translation: 当个体在你们层面完成体验后，他们会继续存在，并根据他们在与你们无关的其他领域中的发展来表达自己。没有任何特定的身份会永远消失。如果那样，整个过程将毫无意义。而在其他方面，通过抽象思维、任何形式的艺术，由意识形成的物质人类，以自身方式通过物质运作并与之合作，构建出其他吸引力的场域或层面，这些场域或层面根据其能力而扩展。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)

quotes.text: I tell you this, and at the same time remind you that your present personality is never lost. Now another word for the soul is entity. You see it is not a simple matter of giving you a definition of a soul or entity, for even to have a glimpse in logical terms you would have to understand it in spiritual, psychic, and electromagnetic terms, and understand the basic nature of consciousness and action as well. But you can intuitively discover the nature of the soul or entity, and in many ways intuitive knowledge is superior to any other kind.
quotes.translation: 我告诉你们这些，同时提醒你们，你们当前的人格永远不会消失。现在，灵魂的另一个词是存有。你们看，给你们一个灵魂或存有的定义并非简单之事，因为即使要逻辑性地瞥见它，你们也必须从灵性、心灵和电磁的角度来理解它，并且还要理解意识与行动的基本本质。但你们可以直觉地发现灵魂或存有的本质，而在许多方面，直觉知识优于任何其他知识。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: The hill house neighborhood is composed of a rather beneficial balance: No particular family of consciousness predominates. Instead, a love of woods and trees transcends such classifications. The area has brought together diverse kinds of people, united by love of nature, some airy spaces, and some privacy ... The people are also achievers of one kind or another, and while [your goals may be different] you appreciate the fact that they are trying to do something with their lives. Many are aware of their limitations. Many dabble in the arts.
quotes.translation: 山丘住宅区由相当有益的平衡构成：没有特定意识家族占据主导。相反，对林木的热爱超越了这类分类。这片区域聚集了形形色色的人，因热爱自然、开阔空间与私密性而联结...这里的居民各有所成，虽然[你们的目标可能不同]，但你们欣赏他们努力活出生命价值的事实。许多人清楚自己的局限，许多人涉猎艺术。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: The exercises will not work, however, in the way they are meant to if they are embarked upon with too serious an air or intent. They should be considered as creative play, though of a mental nature, and they actually consist of mental endeavors tried quite spontaneously by children. So they are not to be regarded as esoteric accomplishments. They represent the intent to discover once again the true transparent delight that you once felt in the manipulation of your own consciousness, as you looped and unlooped it like a child's jumping rope.
quotes.translation: 然而，若以过于严肃的态度或意图进行这些练习，它们便无法达到预期效果。这些练习应被视为创造性游戏——尽管属于心智层面——实际上正是孩童自发尝试的心智活动。因此不应视其为深奥的成就。它们代表着重新发现那份真实透明喜悦的意图——那份你曾如孩童跳绳般收放意识时体验过的喜悦。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche

quotes.text: First, quite simply, there are many who do not see those failings and shortcomings and trends of which you spoke earlier. They cannot be told; they learn. Now alone they would not be permitted to destroy an entire reality. The mixture of consciousness within your system gives some control. The child must mature, and your system is a maturing ground, a very primary one. A beginning school for those who are trying out for particular kinds of experience. Some simply will not succeed. They will continue instead along other lines of development.
quotes.translation: 首先，很简单，有许多人并未看到你先前所说的那些失败、缺陷与趋势。他们无法被告知；他们自行学习。现在，他们独自一人不会被允许摧毁整个实相。你们系统内的意识混合提供了某种控制。孩子必须成熟，而你们的系统是一个成熟之地，一个非常初级的场所。一所为那些尝试特定经验类型者开设的启蒙学校。有些人根本不会成功。他们反而会沿着其他发展路线继续前进。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)

quotes.text: Trance states, daydreaming, hypnotism -- these give you some hint of the various differences that can occur from the standpoint of waking consciousness. In each, reality appears in another fashion, and for that matter, different rules apply. In the dream state far greater variations occur. The key to the dream state, however, lies in the waking one as far as you are concerned. You must change your ideas about dreaming, alter your concepts about it, before you can begin to explore it. Otherwise your own waking prejudice will close the door.
quotes.translation: 出神状态、白日梦、催眠——这些给了你一些暗示，表明从清醒意识的角度可能发生的各种差异。在每种状态中，实相以另一种方式显现，而且就此而言，不同的规则适用。在梦境状态中，会发生远为更大的变化。然而，就你而言，梦境状态的关键在于清醒状态。你必须改变你对做梦的想法，改变你对它的概念，然后才能开始探索它。否则，你自己的清醒偏见会关上那扇门。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche

quotes.text: You are either conscious of self or you are not. On your plane self-consciousness exists as a rule. A tree is conscious of itself as a tree. It does not think of itself as a rock. A dog knows it is not a cat. What I am trying to point out to you here is this supreme egotistical presumption that self-consciousness must of necessity involve humanity per se. It does not.
quotes.translation: 你要么具有自我意识，要么没有。在你们的层面上，自我意识通常存在。一棵树意识到自己是一棵树，它不会把自己当作岩石。狗知道它不是猫。我在这里想向你们指出的是，这种极端的自我中心假设——认为自我意识必然涉及人类本身——是错误的。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)

quotes.text: I have been speaking of your present system only, yet there is always progression to other systems. The growth of each(underlined) seed requires a separate act of creation. There is no mass creative act, for all portions of consciousness have their part to play in creativity. This is the meaning of action, of consciousness, and of individuality: the freedom to create.
quotes.translation: 我一直在谈论你们当前的系统，但始终存在着向其他系统的演进。每颗（加下划线）种子的成长都需要独立的创造行为。不存在大规模的集体创造行为，因为意识的所有部分都在创造中扮演着各自的角色。这就是行动、意识与个体性的意义：创造的自由。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)

quotes.text: She's been most intrigued by Seth's referrals to the concept of "master events" ever since he gave them in the final session for Chapter 8--preparations, she hoped, for his material this evening. She came to play her own conscious part in those preparations, too: Yesterday she'd received many insights from Seth on "master overlays," and made notes. She typed some of her information after supper tonight, and I'm presenting this in Note 1, just to show how closely at times the body of the Seth material lies to her "ordinary" consciousness.)
quotes.translation: 自从赛斯在第八章最后一节中提出“主事件”概念以来，她对此一直深感兴趣——她希望这能为他今晚的资料做准备。她也主动参与了这些准备：昨天她从赛斯关于“主覆盖层”的论述中获得了许多洞见，并做了笔记。今晚晚饭后，她整理了一些自己的信息，我将其放在注释1中，仅为了展示赛斯资料有时与她“普通”意识之间的紧密联系。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2

quotes.text: (A one-minute pause at 9:23.) You may need some time before the old beliefs become less prominent, and finally fall into their proper decay--a decay, incidentally, that does indeed have its own kind of majesty, energy, and beauty. But the inner natural leanings of all of consciousness within the realms of your being now yearn for constructive change, clearer vision, to experience again their inherent sense of corporal spirituality, physical and psychic grace. They want to sense again the effortless motion that is their natural birthright.
quotes.translation: （9:23分停顿一分钟。）你可能需要一些时间，才能让旧信念逐渐淡化，最终归于它们应有的衰败——顺便说一句，这种衰败确实有其自身的庄严、能量与美感。但你们存在领域内所有意识的内在自然倾向，此刻正渴求建设性的改变、更清晰的视野，以及重新体验它们与生俱来的肉体灵性、身体与心灵的优雅。它们渴望再次感受那与生俱来的不费力运动。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2

quotes.text: I mentioned somewhat earlier that oftentimes the consciousness becomes the subconscious and vice versa. This should certainly come as no surprise. You are familiar with it in your everyday existence. It is not some isolated occurrence that happens once in a lifetime, and yet as a rule mankind has ignored this completely. In sleep the conscious becomes actually the subconscious and the subconscious, in the most real manner, becomes conscious. Every man instinctively knows this simple fact, and yet every man stubbornly refuses to admit it.
quotes.translation: 我稍早曾提到，意识常常会变成潜意识，反之亦然。这当然不足为奇。你在日常生活中对此已相当熟悉。这并非一生仅有一次的孤立事件，然而人类通常完全忽视了这一点。在睡眠中，意识实际上变成了潜意识，而潜意识则以最真实的方式变成了意识。每个人本能地知道这个简单事实，却固执地拒绝承认它。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)

quotes.text: In traveling through the states of consciousness, these other personalities would try to attain some focus and perceive your environment, trying to make sense of data with which they are largely unacquainted. Since many of them are unaware of your idea of time, they would find it difficult to understand that you perceive events with intervals between, and would not perceive the inner organization that you thrust upon your normal environment. Yours is obviously a probable system to other fields also touched by the field of probabilities.
quotes.translation: 在穿越意识状态的过程中，这些其他人格会试图找到某种焦点并感知你的环境，试图理解他们大多不熟悉的数据。由于他们中许多人不知道你的时间概念，他们会发现很难理解你感知事件时存在间隔，并且无法感知你强加于正常环境的内在组织。你的系统显然也是其他被可能性场触及的领域中的一个可能系统。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: When you consider that behind all matter there is a conscious energy, then you will see where the pattern comes from. It is not the material that composes man, that gives him his identity. No physical nerve structure, or combination of purely chemical and material properties, will ever result in consciousness. The consciousness gives meaning to the physical material.
quotes.translation: 当你明白所有物质背后都存在着有意识的能量时，你就会看见模式的来源。并非物质构成了人，赋予其身份认同。没有任何神经结构或纯粹的化学物质组合能产生意识。意识赋予物质材料意义。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)

quotes.text: The church ignored Christ's physical birth, for example, and made his mother an immaculate virgin, which meant that the consciousness of the species would for a longer time ignore its relationship with nature and its feminine aspects. I am speaking now of mainline Western civilization. God the Father would be recognized and the Earth Goddess forgotten. There would be feudal lords, therefore, not seeresses. Period. Man would believe he did indeed have dominion over the earth as a separate species, for God the Father had given it to him.
quotes.translation: 例如教会忽略了基督的肉身诞生，将他的母亲塑造成无玷童贞女，这意味着物种意识将在更长时间内忽视其与自然及其阴性面向的关系。我现在谈论的是西方主流文明。天父将被认可，而大地女神将被遗忘。因此会有封建领主，而非女先知。句号。人类会相信自己确实作为独立物种统治地球，因为天父已将地球赐予他。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1

quotes.text: I want you to try and imagine a situation in which (long pause) there exists a psychological force that includes within its capabilities the ability to act simultaneously on the most microscopic and the most macroscopic levels; that can form within itself (long pause, eyes closed) a million separate inviolate unique identities, and that can still operate as a part of those identities, and as a larger unit that is their source--in which case it is a wave from which the particles emerge. That description fits our units of consciousness.
quotes.translation: 我要你们试着想象这样一种情境：（长停顿）存在一种心理力量，其能力包含同时在最微观与最宏观层面运作；能在自身内部（长停顿，闭眼）形成百万个独立完整且不可侵犯的独特身份，同时仍能作为这些身份的一部分运作，并作为它们的源头——那个更大的单位——运作；在这种情况下，它是一道波，粒子从中涌现。这个描述符合我们的意识单位。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1

quotes.text: Any biologist who is a true Darwinist would find these statements of Seth's to be anathema: "Psychic and religious ideas, then, despite many drawbacks ... are far more important in terms of 'evolution' than is recognized." And: "I am telling you that so-called evolution and religion are closely connected." (From the 690th session, in Volume 1.) Such a scientist would have the same reaction to Seth's statement that "Consciousness always creates form, and not the other way around." (From the 513th session, in Chapter 2 of Seth Speaks.)
quotes.translation: 任何真正的达尔文主义生物学家都会认为赛斯的这些陈述是令人深恶痛绝的："灵性与宗教观念，尽管存在诸多缺陷……在'进化'层面上的重要性远超世人所认知。"以及："我告诉你们，所谓的进化与宗教是紧密相连的。"（摘自第690节，第一卷。）这类科学家对赛斯"意识始终创造形态，而非形态创造意识"的陈述（摘自《赛斯说》第二章第513节）也会产生同样的反应。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: Above all, individuals who receive such information in states of expanded consciousness are already those who feel deeply within themselves connections not only with the earth itself, but with deeper realities. Consciously they may often be unaware of this basic quality within themselves. They do not accept answers given by others, but insist upon finding their own.
quotes.translation: 最重要的是，在意识扩展状态下接收此类信息的个体，早已在内心深处感受到与地球本身及更深层实相的联系。他们可能常常在意识层面未能察觉自身这种基本特质。他们拒绝接受他人给予的答案，坚持自行探索。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: Then see this image exploding into your own consciousness, which is unbelievably bright. Realize that it is a portion of a far greater multidimensional structure, spread out in an even richer dimension. Feel the entity sending you energy as you send energy to your cells. Let it fill your being and then direct it physically any place within your body that you choose.
quotes.translation: 接着观想这个意象迸发进入你难以置信明亮的意识中。要明白这是某个更宏大多维结构的一部分，在更丰富的维度中延展。感受本体将能量传递给你，如同你将能量传递给细胞。让这能量充满你的存在，然后将其导向你身体内任何你选择的物理位置。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality

quotes.text: I have an example in point. A young man I will call Joe wrote Ruburt a letter. He left his home in San Francisco to travel to India to study with a guru. He has been told that sexual desire mitigates against spiritual illumination. His home program involves him with no sex whatsoever. Joe tries desperately to abstain. At the same time, when he meditates and alters his consciousness, he immediately finds himself with a blinding headache, images of nude women, and fantasies of female goddesses out to tempt him from his celibate state.
quotes.translation: 我有个现成的例子。有个我称他为乔的年轻人给鲁柏写了封信。他从旧金山离家前往印度跟随一位上师修行。有人告诉他性欲会阻碍灵性开悟。他的修行计划要求他完全禁欲。乔拼命克制自己。然而当他冥想并改变意识状态时，立刻就会头痛欲裂，眼前浮现裸女影像，还有女神幻象试图引诱他破戒。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: 4. This material reminds me of Seth in the 681st session in Volume 1: "The deeper explanations, however [in this case of probabilities], demand a further expansion of ideas of consciousness ... It is not so much a matter of Ruburt's vocabulary, incidentally, since even a specialized scientific one would only present these ideas in its own distorted fashion. It is more a problem of basic language itself, as you are acquainted with it. Words do not exist, for example, for some of the ideas I hope to convey. We will at any rate begin."
quotes.translation: 4. 这段资料让我想起赛斯在第一卷第六八一节 session 中的话："然而更深层的解释（在此指或然性的案例）需要进一步扩展意识的概念...这倒不完全是鲁柏词汇量的问题，因为即便是专业科学用语也只能以扭曲的方式呈现这些概念。更根本的问题在于你们所熟悉的语言本身。例如，我想传达的某些概念在现有词汇中根本不存在。无论如何，我们这就开始。"
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: Give us a moment ... Some readers have brothers or sisters, or both. Others are only children. Your ideas of individuality hamper you to a large extent. To one extent or another, again, each portion of consciousness, while itself, contains [the] potentials of all consciousnesses. Your private information about the world is not nearly as private as you suppose, therefore, for behind the experience of any one event, each of you possesses information pertaining to other dimensions of that same event that you do not ordinarily perceive.
quotes.translation: 请稍等片刻...有些读者有兄弟姐妹，有些则是独生子女。你们对个体性的认知在很大程度上限制了你们。再次强调，意识的每个部分在保持自身特性的同时，都蕴含着所有意识的潜能。你们对世界的私人信息远没有想象中那么私密，因为在经历任何事件时，每个人背后都掌握着该事件其他维度的信息——只是你们通常感知不到罢了。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events

quotes.text: "February 26, 1974. I'm getting something like this ... that data comes through to us multidimensionally, then is sifted through neural connections, where it's transformed into time-segmentation or strung-out experience. Next it flows into our probable (physical) reality (which itself changes all the 'time.') We inherently possess separate pockets or pools of experience (biologically valid among the cells' characteristics), sidepools where information collects for processing before flowing into the 'official pool of consciousness.'
quotes.translation: “1974年2月26日。我接收到类似这样的信息……数据以多维方式传递给我们，然后通过神经连接进行筛选，在那里被转化为时间分段或延展的经验。接着它流入我们可能的（物质）实相（这个实相本身在不断变化）。我们天生拥有分离的经验口袋或池子（在细胞特性中具有生物学有效性），这些侧池中汇集了信息，在流入‘官方意识池’之前进行处理。”
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1

quotes.text: When the outer ego, from the surface of its consciousness, reflects the outer world, it sees reflections of the inner ego which are the images within its own eye; and as the self creates matter subconsciously within its own eye, and as the self creates matter subconsciously and not consciously, and as the self creates matter in line with inner and not outer expectations, so then does the ego, in viewing the material universe, come face to face with the face of its own inner ego; and the outer ego cannot escape from this inner self.
quotes.translation: “当外在自我从其意识表面反映外在世界时，它看到的是内在自我的倒影，这些倒影正是它自己眼中的影像；由于自我在潜意识中而非显意识中创造物质，并且自我根据内在而非外在的期望创造物质，因此当自我审视物质宇宙时，它实际上与自己的内在自我面对面相遇；外在自我无法逃离这个内在自我。”
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)

quotes.text: (Very intently:) In the same manner, the self knows ahead of time the best conditions for its own development, in light of the time and the place of its chosen birth. It has, however, literally endless probabilities to choose from, to fulfill its abilities while maintaining a workable selfhood.6 Consciousness chooses the best overall conditions available for its own purposes of growth. It then preconditions its own organism to respond or not to respond to the time and place of birth, to exaggerate or minimize, to negate or accept.
quotes.translation: （非常专注地：）同样地，自我在出生前就已预知最适合自身发展的条件，并依据所选出生时间与地点进行考量。然而，它拥有几乎无穷无尽的概率可供选择，以便在维持可行自我身份的同时实现自身能力。意识会为自身成长目的选择最佳的整体可用条件，随后预先设定其有机体对出生时间与地点做出或不做出反应，选择夸大或弱化、否定或接受。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: Theoretically, certain dreams can give you a lifetime's experience to draw upon, though the dream itself can take less than an hour in your time. In a way, dreams are the invisible thickness of your normal consciousness. They involve both portions of the brain. Many dreams do activate the brain in a ghostly fashion, sparking responses that are not practically pertinent in ordinary terms. That is, they do not require direct action but serve as anticipators of action, reminders to the brain to initiate certain actions in its future.
quotes.translation: 理论上，某些梦境能赋予你可供汲取的毕生经验，尽管梦境本身可能仅占你时间中的不到一小时。从某种角度看，梦境是你正常意识不可见的厚度。它们同时涉及大脑的两个部分。许多梦境确实以幽灵般的方式激活大脑，引发在常规意义上并不实用的反应——即它们不要求直接行动，而是作为行动的预演，提醒大脑在未来启动特定行动。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche

quotes.text: You could see a phosphorescentlike glow, the aura of electromagnetic "structures" that compose the molecules themselves. You could, if you wished, condense your consciousness until it was small enough to travel through a single molecule, and from the molecule's own world look out and survey the universe of the room and the gigantic galaxy of interrelated, ever-moving starlike shapes. Now all of these possibilities represent a legitimate reality. Yours is no more legitimate than any other, but it is the only one that you perceive.
quotes.translation: 你会看到一种磷光般的光晕，那是构成分子本身的电磁"结构"的光晕。如果你愿意，可以浓缩自己的意识，直到它小到足以穿越单个分子，从分子自身的世界向外眺望，审视房间的宇宙以及由相互关联、不断运动的星状形体构成的巨大星系。所有这些可能性都代表一种合理的实相。你的实相并不比其他任何实相更合理，但它是你唯一能感知到的实相。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: The settings in your physical environment, the sometimes lovely paraphernalia, the physical aspects of life as you know it, are all camouflages, and so I call your physical reality a camouflage. Yet these camouflages are composed of the vitality of the universe. The rocks and stones and mountains and earth are living camouflage, interlocking psychic webs formed by minute consciousnesses that you cannot perceive as such. The atoms and molecules within them have their own consciousness, as do the atoms and molecules with your body.
quotes.translation: 你物质环境中的场景、有时可爱的附属物、你所知的物质生活面向，全都是伪装，因此我称你的物质实相为一种伪装。然而这些伪装由宇宙的生命力构成。岩石、石头、山脉和大地是活生生的伪装，是由你无法直接感知的微小意识交织而成的心理网络。它们内部的原子和分子拥有自己的意识，正如你身体内的原子和分子一样。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: Now initially Ruburt and Joseph and I were a part of the same entity, or overall identity, and so symbolically speaking, there are psychic currents that unite us. All of these merge into what has often been compared to as an ocean of consciousness, a well from which all actuality springs. Start with any one consciousness, and theoretically you will find all others.
quotes.translation: 最初，鲁柏、约瑟和我都是同一个存有或整体身份的一部分，因此象征性地来说，存在着联结我们的心理流。所有这些都汇入常被比作意识之海的东西——一口涌出一切实相的井。从任何一个意识出发，理论上你会找到所有其他意识。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: Within your system, for the "first time", in quotes, individualized consciousness is strongly-enough organized to do, in quotes, "good or evil." Before that it was protected, somewhat coddled, with instinctual behavior holding it in bounds. It is not able to utilize enough energy to maintain a system of its own, but operates as an adjunct to other stronger systems.
quotes.translation: 在你们的系统内，所谓"首次"（引号内）个体化的意识已足够强烈地组织起来，得以做出所谓"善或恶"的行为。在此之前，它受到保护，某种程度上被娇惯，本能行为将其约束在界限内。它尚无法运用足够能量维持自身系统，只能作为其他更强系统的附属运作。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)

quotes.text: There is also a natural tension, then, between sexes that is based on far deeper causes than physical ones. The tension results from the nature of your consciousness that arises from the anima, but depends for its continuation upon the "aggressiveness" of the animus. I have to some extent explained the fascination that one has for the other, as resulting from the inner knowledge of the whole self, that strives to attain true identity as it struggles to combine and fulfill the seemingly opposite tendencies that are a part of it.
quotes.translation: 两性之间还存在着一种基于远非物质原因的天然张力。这种张力源于你们意识的本质——它从阿尼玛中升起，却依赖阿尼姆斯的"侵略性"来维持延续。我已在某种程度上解释了一方对另一方的着迷，这源于对完整自我的内在认知——当它努力结合并实现那些看似对立却属于自身一部分的倾向时，便奋力追寻真正的身份认同。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: Consciousness forms the genes, and not the other way around, and the about-to-be-born infant is the agency that adds new material through the chromosomal structure.2 The child is from birth far more aware of all kinds of physical events than is realized also. But beside that, the child uses the early years to explore -- particularly in the dream state -- other kinds of material that suit its own fancies and intents, and it constantly receives a stream of information that is not at all dependent upon its heredity or environment.
quotes.translation: 意识形成基因，而非相反。即将出生的婴儿正是通过染色体结构添加新材料的媒介。2 婴儿从出生起对各种物理事件的觉察远比人们所认知的更为敏锐。但除此之外，婴儿利用早年时光——尤其在梦境状态中——探索其他符合自身喜好与意图的素材，并持续接收着完全不依赖遗传或环境的信息流。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
